{
  "description": "Explore listener filter chain selection",
  "max_turns": 30,
  "prompt": "I need to understand how Envoy's listener hands off TCP connections to the HTTP connection manager. Explore thoroughly:\n\n1. In `source/common/listener_manager/`, find:\n   - How `FilterChainManager` or similar class selects which network filter chain to use for an incoming connection\n   - The filter chain matching logic (look for FilterChainManagerImpl, filter_chain_manager_impl.cc/h)\n   - How listener_impl.cc handles new connections\n\n2. In `source/common/http/`, find:\n   - How `ConnectionManagerImpl` is installed as a network filter\n   - The `ConnectionManagerImpl::onData()` method - what happens when first bytes arrive\n   - The factory that creates the connection manager\n\n3. Look at `source/extensions/filters/network/http_connection_manager/` for the HCM factory/config\n\nProvide specific file paths, class names, function names, and line numbers for all findings.",
  "subagent_type": "Explore"
}
Output
{
  "agentId": "a45fd8b",
  "content": [
    {
      "text": "Perfect! Now I have all the information needed. Let me create a comprehensive summary document:\n\n## Summary: How Envoy's Listener Hands Off TCP Connections to the HTTP Connection Manager\n\nBased on my thorough exploration of the Envoy codebase, here is the complete flow from TCP connection acceptance to HTTP processing:\n\n---\n\n### **PART 1: LISTENER FILTER CHAIN SELECTION**\n\n#### **FilterChainManager - Filter Chain Matching Logic**\n\n**File:** `/workspace/source/common/listener_manager/filter_chain_manager_impl.h` (lines 127-334)  \n**File:** `/workspace/source/common/listener_manager/filter_chain_manager_impl.cc` (lines 125-797)\n\n**Key Class:** `FilterChainManagerImpl` \n- Implements `Network::FilterChainManager` interface\n- Owns and manages all filter chains for a listener\n- Uses hierarchical data structures to efficiently match incoming connections to filter chains\n\n**Main Method - `findFilterChain()`** (lines 513-546 in .cc)\n```cpp\nconst Network::FilterChain*\nFilterChainManagerImpl::findFilterChain(const Network::ConnectionSocket& socket,\n                                        const StreamInfo::StreamInfo& info) const {\n  if (matcher_) {\n    return findFilterChainUsingMatcher(socket, info);  // Uses xDS matcher\n  }\n  // Otherwise uses hierarchical lookup by:\n  // 1. Destination port (lines 523-536)\n  // 2. Destination IP using LcTrie (lines 537-546)\n  // 3. Server name (SNI)\n  // 4. Transport protocol (TLS/plain)\n  // 5. Application protocols (ALPN)\n  // 6. Direct source IP\n  // 7. Source IP and port\n}\n```\n\n**Filter Chain Matching Hierarchy** (lines 186-216 in .h):\n- **DestinationPortsMap** \u2192 **DestinationIPsMap** \u2192 **ServerNamesMap** \u2192 **TransportProtocolsMap** \u2192 **ApplicationProtocolsMap** \u2192 **DirectSourceIPsMap** \u2192 **SourceTypesArray** \u2192 **SourcePortsMap**\n\n**Key Matching Functions:**\n1. **`findFilterChainForDestinationIP()`** (lines 563-578) - Uses LcTrie for CIDR matching\n2. **`findFilterChainForServerName()`** (lines 580-609) - Supports exact and wildcard domains\n3. **`findFilterChainForTransportProtocol()`** (lines 611-629) - Matches TLS/plain protocols\n4. **`findFilterChainForApplicationProtocols()`** (lines 631-649) - Matches ALPN (h2, http/1.1)\n5. **`findFilterChainForDirectSourceIP()`** (lines 651-666) - CIDR matching for source IPs\n6. **`findFilterChainForSourceTypes()`** (lines 668-700) - Local vs external source classification\n7. **`findFilterChainForSourceIpAndPort()`** (lines 702-734) - Final port matching\n\n---\n\n### **PART 2: TCP CONNECTION ACCEPTANCE & LISTENER FILTER PROCESSING**\n\n#### **Connection Acceptance Path**\n\n**File:** `/workspace/source/common/listener_manager/active_tcp_listener.cc` (lines 80-130)\n\n**Entry Point - `ActiveTcpListener::onAccept()`** (lines 80-91)\n- Called when kernel accepts a new TCP connection\n- Checks listener connection limits\n- Calls `onAcceptWorker()` for further processing\n\n**`ActiveTcpListener::onAcceptWorker()`** (lines 108-130)\n- Balances connection across worker threads\n- Creates `ActiveTcpSocket` instance\n- Calls `onSocketAccepted()` to initiate listener filter chain\n\n#### **Listener Filter Chain Execution**\n\n**File:** `/workspace/source/common/listener_manager/active_tcp_socket.cc` (lines 1-225)\n\n**Key Methods:**\n- **`continueFilterChain()`** (lines 111-173) - Iterates through listener filters\n- **`createListenerFilterBuffer()`** (lines 73-109) - Creates buffer for listener filters needing data inspection\n- **`newConnection()`** (lines 185-222) - Called after all listener filters complete\n\n**Listener Filter Loop (lines 114-159 in `continueFilterChain()`):**\n```cpp\nfor (; iter_ != accept_filters_.end(); iter_++) {\n  Network::FilterStatus status = (*iter_)->onAccept(*this);\n  if (status == Network::FilterStatus::StopIteration) {\n    // Filter needs more data - create listener filter buffer\n    if (listener_filter_buffer_ == nullptr) {\n      createListenerFilterBuffer();\n    }\n    return;\n  }\n}\n// All filters passed - create new connection\nlistener_.newConnection(std::move(socket_), std::move(stream_info_));\n```\n\n---\n\n### **PART 3: FILTER CHAIN SELECTION AND NETWORK FILTER INSTALLATION**\n\n#### **Filter Chain Selection Point**\n\n**File:** `/workspace/source/common/listener_manager/active_stream_listener_base.cc` (lines 38-76)\n\n**Method - `ActiveStreamListenerBase::newConnection()`** (lines 38-76)\n```cpp\nvoid ActiveStreamListenerBase::newConnection(Network::ConnectionSocketPtr&& socket,\n                                             std::unique_ptr<StreamInfo::StreamInfo> stream_info) {\n  // CRITICAL HANDOFF: Find matching filter chain for this connection\n  const auto filter_chain = config_->filterChainManager().findFilterChain(*socket, *stream_info);\n  \n  if (filter_chain == nullptr) {\n    // No matching filter chain - close connection\n    ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"closing connection from {}: no matching filter chain found\",\n              socket->connectionInfoProvider().remoteAddress()->asString());\n    socket->close();\n    return;\n  }\n  \n  // Create transport socket (TLS context setup)\n  auto transport_socket = filter_chain->transportSocketFactory().createDownstreamTransportSocket();\n  \n  // Create underlying network connection\n  auto server_conn_ptr = dispatcher().createServerConnection(\n      std::move(socket), std::move(transport_socket), *stream_info);\n  \n  // INSTALL NETWORK FILTERS: Add network filters from this filter chain\n  const bool empty_filter_chain = !config_->filterChainFactory().createNetworkFilterChain(\n      *server_conn_ptr, filter_chain->networkFilterFactories());\n  \n  if (empty_filter_chain) {\n    // No filters configured - close connection\n    server_conn_ptr->close(Network::ConnectionCloseType::NoFlush, \"no_filters\");\n    return;\n  }\n  \n  // Create active connection object to track this connection's lifetime\n  newActiveConnection(*filter_chain, std::move(server_conn_ptr), std::move(stream_info));\n}\n```\n\n**Key Data Types Involved (lines 41 in .h):**\n- `const auto filter_chain` \u2192 `const Network::FilterChain*`\n- Contains:\n  - Transport socket factory (for TLS setup)\n  - Network filter factories list\n  - Filter chain name and metadata\n\n#### **Network Filter Installation**\n\n**File:** `/workspace/source/common/listener_manager/listener_impl.cc` (lines 942-952)\n\n**Method - `ListenerImpl::createNetworkFilterChain()`** (lines 942-952)\n```cpp\nbool ListenerImpl::createNetworkFilterChain(\n    Network::Connection& connection, \n    const Filter::NetworkFilterFactoriesList& filter_factories) {\n  if (!Configuration::FilterChainUtility::buildFilterChain(connection, filter_factories)) {\n    ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"New connection accepted while missing configuration. \"\n                     \"Close socket and stop the iteration onAccept.\");\n    missing_listener_config_stats_.network_extension_config_missing_.inc();\n    return false;\n  }\n  return true;\n}\n```\n\nThis calls the factory functions to instantiate each network filter on the connection.\n\n---\n\n### **PART 4: HTTP CONNECTION MANAGER INSTALLATION & ACTIVATION**\n\n#### **HCM Factory - Creating the Filter Instance**\n\n**File:** `/workspace/source/extensions/filters/network/http_connection_manager/config.h` (lines 53-71)\n\n**Class:** `HttpConnectionManagerFilterConfigFactory`\n- Inherits from `Common::ExceptionFreeFactoryBase`\n- Registered as `NamedNetworkFilterConfigFactory`\n- Creates `Network::FilterFactoryCb` (callback that instantiates the filter)\n\n#### **HCM Factory Implementation**\n\n**File:** `/workspace/source/extensions/filters/network/http_connection_manager/config.cc` (lines 278-320)\n\n**Method - `createFilterFactoryFromProtoTyped()`** (lines 279-284)\n```cpp\nabsl::StatusOr<Network::FilterFactoryCb>\nHttpConnectionManagerFilterConfigFactory::createFilterFactoryFromProtoTyped(\n    const envoy::extensions::filters::network::http_connection_manager::v3::HttpConnectionManager&\n        proto_config,\n    Server::Configuration::FactoryContext& context) {\n  return createFilterFactoryFromProtoAndHopByHop(proto_config, context, true);\n}\n```\n\n**Method - `createFilterFactoryFromProtoAndHopByHop()`** (lines 287-320)\n```cpp\nabsl::StatusOr<Network::FilterFactoryCb>\nHttpConnectionManagerFilterConfigFactory::createFilterFactoryFromProtoAndHopByHop(...) {\n  // Create singletons (route config manager, tracer manager, etc.)\n  Utility::Singletons singletons = Utility::createSingletons(context);\n  \n  // Create HttpConnectionManagerConfig\n  auto config_or_error = Utility::createConfig(proto_config, context, ...);\n  auto filter_config = std::move(*config_or_error);\n  \n  // Return lambda that instantiates ConnectionManagerImpl when called\n  return [singletons, filter_config, &context, clear_hop_by_hop_headers](\n      Network::FilterManager& filter_manager) -> void {\n    \n    // Create ConnectionManagerImpl instance\n    auto hcm = std::make_shared<Http::ConnectionManagerImpl>(\n        filter_config,                                    // HCM configuration\n        context.drainDecision(),                          // Drain decision interface\n        server_context.api().randomGenerator(),           // Random generator\n        server_context.httpContext(),                     // HTTP context (codecs, etc.)\n        server_context.runtime(),                         // Runtime for feature flags\n        server_context.localInfo(),                       // Local node info\n        server_context.clusterManager(),                  // Cluster manager for routing\n        overload_manager,                                 // Overload manager for pressure relief\n        server_context.mainThreadDispatcher().timeSource(), // Time source\n        context.listenerInfo().direction()                // Downstream/Upstream\n    );\n    \n    // Add HCM as a read filter on the connection\n    filter_manager.addReadFilter(std::move(hcm));\n  };\n}\n```\n\n**ConnectionManagerImpl Location:** `/workspace/source/common/http/conn_manager_impl.h` (lines 60-73)\n\n---\n\n### **PART 5: TCP DATA ARRIVAL AND HTTP PROCESSING**\n\n#### **HCM Filter Chain Initialization**\n\n**File:** `/workspace/source/common/http/conn_manager_impl.cc` (lines 102-150)\n\n**Constructor - `ConnectionManagerImpl::ConnectionManagerImpl()`** (lines 102-140)\n```cpp\nConnectionManagerImpl::ConnectionManagerImpl(\n    ConnectionManagerConfigSharedPtr config,\n    const Network::DrainDecision& drain_close,\n    Random::RandomGenerator& random_generator,\n    Http::Context& http_context,\n    Runtime::Loader& runtime,\n    const LocalInfo::LocalInfo& local_info,\n    Upstream::ClusterManager& cluster_manager,\n    Server::OverloadManager& overload_manager,\n    TimeSource& time_source,\n    envoy::config::core::v3::TrafficDirection direction)\n    : config_(std::move(config)),\n      stats_(config_->stats()),\n      drain_close_(drain_close),\n      // ... initialization\n```\n\n**Method - `initializeReadFilterCallbacks()`** (lines 148-177)\n```cpp\nvoid ConnectionManagerImpl::initializeReadFilterCallbacks(Network::ReadFilterCallbacks& callbacks) {\n  read_callbacks_ = &callbacks;                    // Save connection reference\n  dispatcher_ = &callbacks.connection().dispatcher();\n  \n  // Register as connection callbacks to monitor connection events\n  read_callbacks_->connection().addConnectionCallbacks(*this);\n  \n  // Initialize statistics\n  stats_.named_.downstream_cx_total_.inc();\n  stats_.named_.downstream_cx_active_.inc();\n  \n  // Handle proxy protocol if configured\n  if (config_->addProxyProtocolConnectionState()) {\n    // ... setup proxy protocol filter state\n  }\n}\n```\n\n#### **Network Filter Activation - onNewConnection()**\n\n**File:** `/workspace/source/common/http/conn_manager_impl.cc` (lines 544-556)\n\n**Method - `ConnectionManagerImpl::onNewConnection()`** (lines 544-556)\n```cpp\nNetwork::FilterStatus ConnectionManagerImpl::onNewConnection() {\n  if (!read_callbacks_->connection().streamInfo().protocol()) {\n    // Non-QUIC traffic: codec will be created lazily in onData()\n    return Network::FilterStatus::Continue;\n  }\n  // QUIC traffic: codec created immediately\n  Buffer::OwnedImpl dummy;\n  createCodec(dummy);\n  ASSERT(codec_->protocol() == Protocol::Http3);\n  return Network::FilterStatus::StopIteration;\n}\n```\n\n#### **TCP Data Arrival - onData() - THE HANDOFF MOMENT**\n\n**File:** `/workspace/source/common/http/conn_manager_impl.cc` (lines 486-542)\n\n**Method - `ConnectionManagerImpl::onData()`** (lines 486-542)\n```cpp\nNetwork::FilterStatus ConnectionManagerImpl::onData(Buffer::Instance& data, bool) {\n  requests_during_dispatch_count_ = 0;\n  \n  if (!codec_) {\n    // Check for overload conditions (memory pressure)\n    if (hcm_ondata_creating_codec_ != nullptr && hcm_ondata_creating_codec_->shouldShedLoad()) {\n      stats_.named_.downstream_rq_overload_close_.inc();\n      handleCodecOverloadError(\"onData codec creation overload\");\n      return Network::FilterStatus::StopIteration;\n    }\n    \n    // CRITICAL: Create HTTP codec on first data arrival\n    createCodec(data);\n  }\n  \n  bool redispatch;\n  do {\n    redispatch = false;\n    \n    // Dispatch incoming data through the HTTP codec\n    const Status status = codec_->dispatch(data);\n    \n    // Handle protocol errors\n    if (isBufferFloodError(status) || isInboundFramesWithEmptyPayloadError(status)) {\n      handleCodecError(status.message());\n      return Network::FilterStatus::StopIteration;\n    } else if (isCodecProtocolError(status)) {\n      stats_.named_.downstream_cx_protocol_error_.inc();\n      handleCodecError(status.message());\n      return Network::FilterStatus::StopIteration;\n    }\n    \n    // Check for deferred close (graceful shutdown)\n    checkForDeferredClose(false);\n    \n    // For HTTP/1.1: redispatch if more data and streams complete\n    if (codec_->protocol() < Protocol::Http2) {\n      if (read_callbacks_->connection().state() == Network::Connection::State::Open &&\n          data.length() > 0 && streams_.empty()) {\n        redispatch = true;\n      }\n    }\n  } while (redispatch);\n  \n  // Record protocol for this connection if not already set\n  if (!read_callbacks_->connection().streamInfo().protocol()) {\n    read_callbacks_->connection().streamInfo().protocol(codec_->protocol());\n  }\n  \n  return Network::FilterStatus::StopIteration;\n}\n```\n\n#### **Codec Creation**\n\n**File:** `/workspace/source/common/http/conn_manager_impl.cc` (lines 465-484)\n\n**Method - `ConnectionManagerImpl::createCodec()`** (lines 465-484)\n```cpp\nvoid ConnectionManagerImpl::createCodec(Buffer::Instance& data) {\n  ASSERT(!codec_);\n  \n  // Delegate to config to create appropriate codec (H1, H2, H3)\n  codec_ = config_->createCodec(\n      read_callbacks_->connection(),  // Network connection\n      data,                            // First bytes to analyze\n      *this,                           // ServerConnectionCallbacks\n      overload_manager_);              // For load shedding\n  \n  // Record codec statistics\n  switch (codec_->protocol()) {\n  case Protocol::Http3:\n    stats_.named_.downstream_cx_http3_total_.inc();\n    stats_.named_.downstream_cx_http3_active_.inc();\n    break;\n  case Protocol::Http2:\n    stats_.named_.downstream_cx_http2_total_.inc();\n    stats_.named_.downstream_cx_http2_active_.inc();\n    break;\n  case Protocol::Http11:\n  case Protocol::Http10:\n    stats_.named_.downstream_cx_http1_total_.inc();\n    stats_.named_.downstream_cx_http1_active_.inc();\n    break;\n  }\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### **KEY HANDOFF POINTS SUMMARY**\n\n| Stage | File | Class/Method | Line Numbers | Purpose |\n|-------|------|-------------|--------------|---------|\n| **1. TCP Accept** | `active_tcp_listener.cc` | `ActiveTcpListener::onAccept()` | 80-91 | Kernel accepts TCP connection |\n| **2. Listener Filters** | `active_tcp_socket.cc` | `continueFilterChain()` | 111-173 | Run listener filters (e.g., proxy-protocol) |\n| **3. Filter Chain Selection** | `active_stream_listener_base.cc` | `newConnection()` | 38-76 | **SELECT** matching filter chain |\n| **4. Network Filter Install** | `listener_impl.cc` | `createNetworkFilterChain()` | 942-952 | **INSTALL** HCM & other filters |\n| **5. HCM Instantiation** | `config.cc` | `createFilterFactoryFromProtoAndHopByHop()` | 287-320 | **CREATE** ConnectionManagerImpl |\n| **6. HCM Initialization** | `conn_manager_impl.cc` | `initializeReadFilterCallbacks()` | 148-177 | Register HCM with connection |\n| **7. First Data Arrival** | `conn_manager_impl.cc` | `onData()` | 486-542 | **CREATE CODEC** & parse HTTP |\n| **8. Codec Creation** | `conn_manager_impl.cc` | `createCodec()` | 465-484 | Detect protocol & create codec |\n| **9. Protocol Detection** | `conn_manager_config.h` | `createCodec()` (config) | 166-169 | Delegate to config for codec factory |\n\n---\n\n### **DATA FLOW DIAGRAM**\n\n```\n1. TCP SYN\n   \u2193\n2. onAccept(socket) \u2192 ActiveTcpSocket\n   \u2193\n3. Run listener filters (TLS detection, proxy-proto)\n   \u2193\n4. newConnection() \u2192 findFilterChain(socket, stream_info)\n   \u2193\n5. createNetworkFilterChain(connection, [HCM factory, ...])\n   \u2193\n6. HCM Factory Lambda \u2192 ConnectionManagerImpl instance\n   \u2193\n7. initializeReadFilterCallbacks(callbacks)\n   \u2193\n8. TCP packets arrive \u2192 onData(buffer)\n   \u2193\n9. createCodec(buffer) \u2192 detect H1/H2/H3\n   \u2193\n10. codec_->dispatch(buffer) \u2192 HTTP request parsing\n   \u2193\n11. newStream() \u2192 Router \u2192 Clusters\n```\n\n---\n\n### **KEY CLASSES AND INTERFACES**\n\n| Component | Location | Purpose |\n|-----------|----------|---------|\n| `FilterChainManagerImpl` | `filter_chain_manager_impl.h/cc` | Matches sockets to filter chains |\n| `FilterChainImpl` | `filter_chain_manager_impl.h` | Owns transport socket factory & network filters |\n| `ActiveTcpSocket` | `active_tcp_socket.cc` | Pre-connection filter execution |\n| `ActiveStreamListenerBase` | `active_stream_listener_base.cc` | Filter chain selection & network filter installation |\n| `ListenerImpl` | `listener_impl.h/cc` | Listener configuration & filter chain factory |\n| `HttpConnectionManagerFilterConfigFactory` | `config.h/cc` | Creates HCM filter instances |\n| `HttpConnectionManagerConfig` | `config.h/cc` | HCM configuration & codec factory |\n| `ConnectionManagerImpl` | `conn_manager_impl.h/cc` | HTTP protocol handling, stream management |\n\n---\n\nThis detailed exploration shows that the listener hands off connections through a carefully orchestrated sequence: TCP acceptance \u2192 listener filters \u2192 filter chain selection (matching destination IP, port, SNI, ALPN) \u2192 network filter installation (creating HCM) \u2192 TCP data arrival \u2192 lazy codec creation \u2192 HTTP request processing.",
      "type": "text"
    }
  ],

In `source/common/listener_manager/`, find:\n   - How `FilterChainManager` or similar class selects which network filter chain to use for an incoming connection\n   - The filter chain matching logic (look for FilterChainManagerImpl, filter_chain_manager_impl.cc/h)\n   - How listener_impl.cc handles new connections\n\n2. In `source/common/http/`, find:\n   - How `ConnectionManagerImpl` is installed as a network filter\n   - The `ConnectionManagerImpl::onData()` method - what happens when first bytes arrive\n   - The factory that creates the connection manager\n\n3. Look at `source/extensions/filters/network/http_connection_manager/` for the HCM factory/config\n\nProvide specific file paths, class names, function names, and line numbers for all findings.",

\"source/common/config/utility.h\"\n     9\u2192#include \"source/common/matcher/matcher.h\"\n    10\u2192#include \"source/common/network/matching/data_impl.h\"\n    11\u2192#include \"source/common/network/matching/inputs.h\"\n    12\u2192#include \"source/common/network/socket_interface.h\"\n    13\u2192#include \"source/common/network/utility.h\"\n    14\u2192#include \"source/common/protobuf/utility.h\"\n    15\u2192#include \"source/server/configuration_impl.h\"\n    16\u2192\n    17\u2192#include \"absl/container/node_hash_map.h\"\n    18\u2192#include \"absl/strings/match.h\"\n    19\u2192#include \"absl/strings/str_cat.h\"\n    20\u2192\n    21\u2192namespace Envoy {\n    22\u2192namespace Server {\n    23\u2192\n    24\u2192namespace FilterChain {\n    25\u2192\n    26\u2192// Return a fake address for use when either the source or destination is unix domain socket.\n    27\u2192// This address will only match the fallback matcher of 0.0.0.0/0, which is the default\n    28\u2192// when no IP matcher is configured.\n    29\u2192Network::Address::InstanceConstSharedPtr fakeAddress() {\n    30\u2192  CONSTRUCT_ON_FIRST_USE(Network::Address::InstanceConstSharedPtr,\n    31\u2192                         Network::Utility::parseInternetAddressNoThrow(\"255.255.255.255\"));\n    32\u2192}\n    33\u2192\n    34\u2192struct FilterChainNameAction\n    35\u2192    : public Matcher::ActionBase<ProtobufWkt::StringValue, Configuration::FilterChainBaseAction> {\n    36\u2192  explicit FilterChainNameAction(const std::string& name) : name_(name) {}\n    37\u2192  const Network::FilterChain* get(const FilterChainsByName& filter_chains_by_name,\n    38\u2192                                  const StreamInfo::StreamInfo&) const override {\n    39\u2192    const auto chain_match = filter_chains_by_name.find(name_);\n    40\u2192    if (chain_match != filter_chains_by_name.end()) {\n    41\u2192      return chain_match->second.get();\n    42\u2192    }\n    43\u2192    return nullptr;\n    44\u2192  }\n    45\u2192  const std::string name_;\n    46\u2192};\n    47\u2192\n    48\u2192class FilterChainNameActionFactory : public Matcher::ActionFactory<FilterChainActionFactoryContext>,\n    49\u2192                                     Logger::Loggable<Logger::Id::config> {\n    50\u2192public:\n    51\u2192  std::string name() const override { return \"filter-chain-name\"; }\n    52\u2192  Matcher::ActionFactoryCb createActionFactoryCb(const Protobuf::Message& config,\n    53\u2192                                                 FilterChainActionFactoryContext&,\n    54\u2192                                                 ProtobufMessage::ValidationVisitor&) override {\n    55\u2192    const auto& name = dynamic_cast<const ProtobufWkt::StringValue&>(config);\n    56\u2192    return [value = name.value()]() { return std::make_unique<FilterChainNameAction>(value); };\n    57\u2192  }\n    58\u2192  ProtobufTypes::MessagePtr createEmptyConfigProto() override {\n    59\u2192    return std::make_unique<ProtobufWkt::StringValue>();\n    60\u2192  }\n    61\u2192};\n    62\u2192\n    63\u2192REGISTER_FACTORY(FilterChainNameActionFactory,\n    64\u2192                 Matcher::ActionFactory<FilterChainActionFactoryContext>);\n    65\u2192\n    66\u2192class FilterChainNameActionValidationVisitor\n    67\u2192    : public Matcher::MatchTreeValidationVisitor<Network::MatchingData> {\n    68\u2192public:\n    69\u2192  absl::Status performDataInputValidation(const Matcher::DataInputFactory<Network::MatchingData>&,\n    70\u2192                                          absl::string_view) override {\n    71\u2192    return absl::OkStatus();\n    72\u2192  }\n    73\u2192};\n    74\u2192\n    75\u2192} // namespace FilterChain\n    76\u2192\n    77\u2192PerFilterChainFactoryContextImpl::PerFilterChainFactoryContextImpl(\n    78\u2192    Configuration::FactoryContext& parent_context, Init::Manager& init_manager)\n    79\u2192    : parent_context_(parent_context), scope_(parent_context_.scope().createScope(\"\")),\n    80\u2192      filter_chain_scope_(parent_context_.listenerScope().createScope(\"\")),\n    81\u2192      init_manager_(init_manager) {}\n    82\u2192\n    83\u2192bool PerFilterChainFactoryContextImpl::drainClose(Network::DrainDirection scope) const {\n    84\u2192  return is_draining_.load() || parent_context_.drainDecision().drainClose(scope);\n    85\u2192}\n    86\u2192\n    87\u2192Network::DrainDecision& PerFilterChainFactoryContextImpl::drainDecision() { return *this; }\n    88\u2192\n    89\u2192Init::Manager& PerFilterChainFactoryContextImpl::initManager() { return init_manager_; }\n    90\u2192\n    91\u2192const Network::ListenerInfo& PerFilterChainFactoryContextImpl::listenerInfo() const {\n    92\u2192  return parent_context_.listenerInfo();\n    93\u2192}\n    94\u2192\n    95\u2192ProtobufMessage::ValidationVisitor&\n    96\u2192PerFilterChainFactoryContextImpl::messageValidationVisitor() const {\n    97\u2192  return parent_context_.messageValidationVisitor();\n    98\u2192}\n    99\u2192\n   100\u2192Stats::Scope& PerFilterChainFactoryContextImpl::scope() { return *scope_; }\n   101\u2192\n   102\u2192Configuration::ServerFactoryContext&\n   103\u2192PerFilterChainFactoryContextImpl::serverFactoryContext() const {\n   104\u2192  return parent_context_.serverFactoryContext();\n   105\u2192}\n   106\u2192\n   107\u2192Configuration::TransportSocketFactoryContext&\n   108\u2192PerFilterChainFactoryContextImpl::getTransportSocketFactoryContext() const {\n   109\u2192  return parent_context_.getTransportSocketFactoryContext();\n   110\u2192}\n   111\u2192\n   112\u2192Stats::Scope& PerFilterChainFactoryContextImpl::listenerScope() { return *filter_chain_scope_; }\n   113\u2192\n   114\u2192FilterChainManagerImpl::FilterChainManagerImpl(\n   115\u2192    const std::vector<Network::Address::InstanceConstSharedPtr>& addresses,\n   116\u2192    Configuration::FactoryContext& factory_context, Init::Manager& init_manager,\n   117\u2192    const FilterChainManagerImpl& parent_manager)\n   118\u2192    : addresses_(addresses), parent_context_(factory_context), origin_(&parent_manager),\n   119\u2192      init_manager_(init_manager) {}\n   120\u2192\n   121\u2192bool FilterChainManagerImpl::isWildcardServerName(const std::string& name) {\n   122\u2192  return absl::StartsWith(name, \"*.\");\n   123\u2192}\n   124\u2192\n   125\u2192absl::Status FilterChainManagerImpl::addFilterChains(\n   126\u2192    const xds::type::matcher::v3::Matcher* filter_chain_matcher,\n   127\u2192    absl::Span<const envoy::config::listener::v3::FilterChain* const> filter_chain_span,\n   128\u2192    const envoy::config::listener::v3::FilterChain* default_filter_chain,\n   129\u2192    FilterChainFactoryBuilder& filter_chain_factory_builder,\n   130\u2192    FilterChainFactoryContextCreator& context_creator) {\n   131\u2192  Cleanup cleanup([this]() { origin_ = absl::nullopt; });\n   132\u2192  absl::node_hash_map<envoy::config::listener::v3::FilterChainMatch, std::string, MessageUtil,\n   133\u2192                      MessageUtil>\n   134\u2192      filter_chains;\n   135\u2192  uint32_t new_filter_chain_size = 0;\n   136\u2192  FilterChainsByName filter_chains_by_name;\n   137\u2192\n   138\u2192  for (const auto& filter_chain : filter_chain_span) {\n   139\u2192    const auto& filter_chain_match = filter_chain->filter_chain_match();\n   140\u2192    if (!filter_chain_match.address_suffix().empty() || filter_chain_match.has_suffix_len()) {\n   141\u2192      return absl::InvalidArgumentError(fmt::format(\n   142\u2192          \"error adding listener '{}': filter chain '{}' contains \"\n   143\u2192          \"unimplemented fields\",\n   144\u2192          absl::StrJoin(addresses_, \",\", Network::AddressStrFormatter()), filter_chain->name()));\n   145\u2192    }\n   146\u2192    if (!filter_chain_matcher) {\n   147\u2192      const auto& matching_iter = filter_chains.find(filter_chain_match);\n   148\u2192      if (matching_iter != filter_chains.end()) {\n   149\u2192        std::string error_msg =\n   150\u2192            fmt::format(\"error adding listener '{}': filter chain '{}' has \"\n   151\u2192                        \"the same matching rules defined as '{}'\",\n   152\u2192                        absl::StrJoin(addresses_, \",\", Network::AddressStrFormatter()),\n   153\u2192                        filter_chain->name(), matching_iter->second);\n   154\u2192#ifdef ENVOY_ENABLE_YAML\n   155\u2192        error_msg =\n   156\u2192            absl::StrCat(error_msg, \". duplicate matcher is: \",\n   157\u2192                         MessageUtil::getJsonStringFromMessageOrError(filter_chain_match, false));\n   158\u2192#endif\n   159\u2192\n   160\u2192        return absl::InvalidArgumentError(error_msg);\n   161\u2192      }\n   162\u2192      filter_chains.insert({filter_chain_match, filter_chain->name()});\n   163\u2192    }\n   164\u2192\n   165\u2192    // Reuse created filter chain if possible.\n   166\u2192    // FilterChainManager maintains the lifetime of FilterChainFactoryContext\n   167\u2192    // ListenerImpl maintains the dependencies of FilterChainFactoryContext\n   168\u2192    auto filter_chain_impl = findExistingFilterChain(*filter_chain);\n   169\u2192    if (filter_chain_impl == nullptr) {\n   170\u2192      auto filter_chain_or_error =\n   171\u2192          filter_chain_factory_builder.buildFilterChain(*filter_chain, context_creator);\n   172\u2192      RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(filter_chain_or_error.status());\n   173\u2192      filter_chain_impl = filter_chain_or_error.value();\n   174\u2192      ++new_filter_chain_size;\n   175\u2192    }\n   176\u2192\n   177\u2192    // If using the matcher, require usage of \"name\" field and skip building the index.\n   178\u2192    if (filter_chain_matcher) {\n   179\u2192      if (filter_chain->name().empty()) {\n   180\u2192        return absl::InvalidArgumentError(fmt::format(\n   181\u2192            \"error adding listener '{}': \\\"name\\\" field is required when using a listener matcher\",\n   182\u2192            absl::StrJoin(addresses_, \",\", Network::AddressStrFormatter())));\n   183\u2192      }\n   184\u2192      auto [_, inserted] =\n   185\u2192          filter_chains_by_name.try_emplace(filter_chain->name(), filter_chain_impl);\n   186\u2192      if (!inserted) {\n   187\u2192        return absl::InvalidArgumentError(fmt::format(\n   188\u2192            \"error adding listener '{}': \\\"name\\\" field is duplicated with value '{}'\",\n   189\u2192            absl::StrJoin(addresses_, \",\", Network::AddressStrFormatter()), filter_chain->name()));\n   190\u2192      }\n   191\u2192      if (filter_chain->has_filter_chain_match()) {\n   192\u2192        ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"filter chain match in chain '{}' is ignored\", filter_chain->name());\n   193\u2192      }\n   194\u2192    } else {\n   195\u2192      auto createAddressVector = [](const auto& prefix_ranges,\n   196\u2192                                    absl::Status& creation_status) -> std::vector<std::string> {\n   197\u2192        std::vector<std::string> ips;\n   198\u2192        ips.reserve(prefix_ranges.size());\n   199\u2192        for (const auto& ip : prefix_ranges) {\n   200\u2192          auto cidr_range_or_error = Network::Address::CidrRange::create(ip);\n   201\u2192          if (cidr_range_or_error.status().ok()) {\n   202\u2192            ips.push_back(cidr_range_or_error->asString());\n   203\u2192          } else {\n   204\u2192            creation_status = cidr_range_or_error.status();\n   205\u2192          }\n   206\u2192        }\n   207\u2192        return ips;\n   208\u2192      };\n   209\u2192\n   210\u2192      absl::Status creation_status = absl::OkStatus();\n   211\u2192      // Validate IP addresses.\n   212\u2192      std::vector<std::string> destination_ips =\n   213\u2192          createAddressVector(filter_chain_match.prefix_ranges(), creation_status);\n   214\u2192      RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(creation_status);\n   215\u2192      std::vector<std::string> source_ips =\n   216\u2192          createAddressVector(filter_chain_match.source_prefix_ranges(), creation_status);\n   217\u2192      RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(creation_status);\n   218\u2192      std::vector<std::string> direct_source_ips =\n   219\u2192          createAddressVector(filter_chain_match.direct_source_prefix_ranges(), creation_status);\n   220\u2192      RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(creation_status);\n   221\u2192\n   222\u2192      std::vector<std::string> server_names;\n   223\u2192      // Reject partial wildcards, we don't match on them.\n   224\u2192      for (const auto& server_name : filter_chain_match.server_names()) {\n   225\u2192        if (absl::StrContains(server_name, '*') && !isWildcardServerName(server_name)) {\n   226\u2192          return absl::InvalidArgumentError(\n   227\u2192              fmt::format(\"error adding listener '{}': partial wildcards are not supported in \"\n   228\u2192                          \"\\\"server_names\\\"\",\n   229\u2192                          absl::StrJoin(addresses_, \",\", Network::AddressStrFormatter())));\n   230\u2192        }\n   231\u2192        server_names.push_back(absl::AsciiStrToLower(server_name));\n   232\u2192      }\n   233\u2192\n   234\u2192      RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(addFilterChainForDestinationPorts(\n   235\u2192          destination_ports_map_,\n   236\u2192          PROTOBUF_GET_WRAPPED_OR_DEFAULT(filter_chain_match, destination_port, 0), destination_ips,\n   237\u2192          server_names, filter_chain_match.transport_protocol(),\n   238\u2192          filter_chain_match.application_protocols(), direct_source_ips,\n   239\u2192          filter_chain_match.source_type(), source_ips, filter_chain_match.source_ports(),\n   240\u2192          filter_chain_impl));\n   241\u2192    }\n   242\u2192\n   243\u2192    fc_contexts_[*filter_chain] = filter_chain_impl;\n   244\u2192  }\n   245\u2192  RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(convertIPsToTries());\n   246\u2192  RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(copyOrRebuildDefaultFilterChain(default_filter_chain,\n   247\u2192                                                   filter_chain_factory_builder, context_creator));\n   248\u2192  // Construct matcher if it is present in the listener configuration.\n   249\u2192  if (filter_chain_matcher) {\n   250\u2192    filter_chains_by_name_ = filter_chains_by_name;\n   251\u2192    FilterChain::FilterChainNameActionValidationVisitor validation_visitor;\n   252\u2192    Matcher::MatchTreeFactory<Network::MatchingData, FilterChainActionFactoryContext> factory(\n   253\u2192        parent_context_.serverFactoryContext(), parent_context_.serverFactoryContext(),\n   254\u2192        validation_visitor);\n   255\u2192    matcher_ = factory.create(*filter_chain_matcher)();\n   256\u2192  }\n   257\u2192  ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"new fc_contexts has {} filter chains, including {} newly built\",\n   258\u2192            fc_contexts_.size(), new_filter_chain_size);\n   259\u2192  return absl::OkStatus();\n   260\u2192}\n   261\u2192\n   262\u2192absl::Status FilterChainManagerImpl::copyOrRebuildDefaultFilterChain(\n   263\u2192    const envoy::config::listener::v3::FilterChain* default_filter_chain,\n   264\u2192    FilterChainFactoryBuilder& filter_chain_factory_builder,\n   265\u2192    FilterChainFactoryContextCreator& context_creator) {\n   266\u2192  // Default filter chain is built exactly once.\n   267\u2192  ASSERT(!default_filter_chain_message_.has_value());\n   268\u2192\n   269\u2192  // Save the default filter chain message. This message could be used in next listener update.\n   270\u2192  if (default_filter_chain == nullptr) {\n   271\u2192    return absl::OkStatus();\n   272\u2192  }\n   273\u2192  default_filter_chain_message_ = absl::make_optional(*default_filter_chain);\n   274\u2192\n   275\u2192  // Origin filter chain manager could be empty if the current is the ancestor.\n   276\u2192  const auto* origin = getOriginFilterChainManager();\n   277\u2192  if (origin == nullptr) {\n   278\u2192    auto filter_chain_or_error =\n   279\u2192        filter_chain_factory_builder.buildFilterChain(*default_filter_chain, context_creator);\n   280\u2192    RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(filter_chain_or_error.status());\n   281\u2192    default_filter_chain_ = *filter_chain_or_error;\n   282\u2192    return absl::OkStatus();\n   283\u2192  }\n   284\u2192\n   285\u2192  // Copy from original filter chain manager, or build new filter chain if the default filter chain\n   286\u2192  // is not equivalent to the one in the original filter chain manager.\n   287\u2192  MessageUtil eq;\n   288\u2192  if (origin->default_filter_chain_message_.has_value() &&\n   289\u2192      eq(origin->default_filter_chain_message_.value(), *default_filter_chain)) {\n   290\u2192    default_filter_chain_ = origin->default_filter_chain_;\n   291\u2192  } else {\n   292\u2192    auto filter_chain_or_error =\n   293\u2192        filter_chain_factory_builder.buildFilterChain(*default_filter_chain, context_creator);\n   294\u2192    RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(filter_chain_or_error.status());\n   295\u2192    default_filter_chain_ = *filter_chain_or_error;\n   296\u2192  }\n   297\u2192  return absl::OkStatus();\n   298\u2192}\n   299\u2192\n   300\u2192absl::Status FilterChainManagerImpl::addFilterChainForDestinationPorts(\n   301\u2192    DestinationPortsMap& destination_ports_map, uint16_t destination_port,\n   302\u2192    const std::vector<std::string>& destination_ips,\n   303\u2192    const absl::Span<const std::string> server_names, const std::string& transport_protocol,\n   304\u2192    const absl::Span<const std::string* const> application_protocols,\n   305\u2192    const std::vector<std::string>& direct_source_ips,\n   306\u2192    const envoy::config::listener::v3::FilterChainMatch::ConnectionSourceType source_type,\n   307\u2192    const std::vector<std::string>& source_ips,\n   308\u2192    const absl::Span<const Protobuf::uint32> source_ports,\n   309\u2192    const Network::FilterChainSharedPtr& filter_chain) {\n   310\u2192  if (destination_ports_map.find(destination_port) == destination_ports_map.end()) {\n   311\u2192    destination_ports_map[destination_port] =\n   312\u2192        std::make_pair<DestinationIPsMap, DestinationIPsTriePtr>(DestinationIPsMap{}, nullptr);\n   313\u2192  }\n   314\u2192  return addFilterChainForDestinationIPs(destination_ports_map[destination_port].first,\n   315\u2192                                         destination_ips, server_names, transport_protocol,\n   316\u2192                                         application_protocols, direct_source_ips, source_type,\n   317\u2192                                         source_ips, source_ports, filter_chain);\n   318\u2192}\n   319\u2192\n   320\u2192absl::Status FilterChainManagerImpl::addFilterChainForDestinationIPs(\n   321\u2192    DestinationIPsMap& destination_ips_map, const std::vector<std::string>& destination_ips,\n   322\u2192    const absl::Span<const std::string> server_names, const std::string& transport_protocol,\n   323\u2192    const absl::Span<const std::string* const> application_protocols,\n   324\u2192    const std::vector<std::string>& direct_source_ips,\n   325\u2192    const envoy::config::listener::v3::FilterChainMatch::ConnectionSourceType source_type,\n   326\u2192    const std::vector<std::string>& source_ips,\n   327\u2192    const absl::Span<const Protobuf::uint32> source_ports,\n   328\u2192    const Network::FilterChainSharedPtr& filter_chain) {\n   329\u2192  if (destination_ips.empty()) {\n   330\u2192    RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(addFilterChainForServerNames(\n   331\u2192        destination_ips_map[EMPTY_STRING], server_names, transport_protocol, application_protocols,\n   332\u2192        direct_source_ips, source_type, source_ips, source_ports, filter_chain));\n   333\u2192  } else {\n   334\u2192    for (const auto& destination_ip : destination_ips) {\n   335\u2192      RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(\n   336\u2192          addFilterChainForServerNames(destination_ips_map[destination_ip], server_names,\n   337\u2192                                       transport_protocol, application_protocols, direct_source_ips,\n   338\u2192                                       source_type, source_ips, source_ports, filter_chain));\n   339\u2192    }\n   340\u2192  }\n   341\u2192  return absl::OkStatus();\n   342\u2192}\n   343\u2192\n   344\u2192absl::Status FilterChainManagerImpl::addFilterChainForServerNames(\n   345\u2192    ServerNamesMapSharedPtr& server_names_map_ptr, const absl::Span<const std::string> server_names,\n   346\u2192    const std::string& transport_protocol,\n   347\u2192    const absl::Span<const std::string* const> application_protocols,\n   348\u2192    const std::vector<std::string>& direct_source_ips,\n   349\u2192    const envoy::config::listener::v3::FilterChainMatch::ConnectionSourceType source_type,\n   350\u2192    const std::vector<std::string>& source_ips,\n   351\u2192    const absl::Span<const Protobuf::uint32> source_ports,\n   352\u2192    const Network::FilterChainSharedPtr& filter_chain) {\n   353\u2192  if (server_names_map_ptr == nullptr) {\n   354\u2192    server_names_map_ptr = std::make_shared<ServerNamesMap>();\n   355\u2192  }\n   356\u2192  auto& server_names_map = *server_names_map_ptr;\n   357\u2192\n   358\u2192  if (server_names.empty()) {\n   359\u2192    RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(addFilterChainForApplicationProtocols(\n   360\u2192        server_names_map[EMPTY_STRING][transport_protocol], application_protocols,\n   361\u2192        direct_source_ips, source_type, source_ips, source_ports, filter_chain));\n   362\u2192  } else {\n   363\u2192    for (const auto& server_name : server_names) {\n   364\u2192      if (isWildcardServerName(server_name)) {\n   365\u2192        // Add mapping for the wildcard domain, i.e. \".example.com\" for \"*.example.com\".\n   366\u2192        RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(addFilterChainForApplicationProtocols(\n   367\u2192            server_names_map[server_name.substr(1)][transport_protocol], application_protocols,\n   368\u2192            direct_source_ips, source_type, source_ips, source_ports, filter_chain));\n   369\u2192      } else {\n   370\u2192        RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(addFilterChainForApplicationProtocols(\n   371\u2192            server_names_map[server_name][transport_protocol], application_protocols,\n   372\u2192            direct_source_ips, source_type, source_ips, source_ports, filter_chain));\n   373\u2192      }\n   374\u2192    }\n   375\u2192  }\n   376\u2192  return absl::OkStatus();\n   377\u2192}\n   378\u2192\n   379\u2192absl::Status FilterChainManagerImpl::addFilterChainForApplicationProtocols(\n   380\u2192    ApplicationProtocolsMap& application_protocols_map,\n   381\u2192    const absl::Span<const std::string* const> application_protocols,\n   382\u2192    const std::vector<std::string>& direct_source_ips,\n   383\u2192    const envoy::config::listener::v3::FilterChainMatch::ConnectionSourceType source_type,\n   384\u2192    const std::vector<std::string>& source_ips,\n   385\u2192    const absl::Span<const Protobuf::uint32> source_ports,\n   386\u2192    const Network::FilterChainSharedPtr& filter_chain) {\n   387\u2192  if (application_protocols.empty()) {\n   388\u2192    RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(addFilterChainForDirectSourceIPs(application_protocols_map[EMPTY_STRING].first,\n   389\u2192                                                      direct_source_ips, source_type, source_ips,\n   390\u2192                                                      source_ports, filter_chain));\n   391\u2192  } else {\n   392\u2192    for (const auto& application_protocol_ptr : application_protocols) {\n   393\u2192      RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(addFilterChainForDirectSourceIPs(\n   394\u2192          application_protocols_map[*application_protocol_ptr].first, direct_source_ips,\n   395\u2192          source_type, source_ips, source_ports, filter_chain));\n   396\u2192    }\n   397\u2192  }\n   398\u2192  return absl::OkStatus();\n   399\u2192}\n   400\u2192\n   401\u2192absl::Status FilterChainManagerImpl::addFilterChainForDirectSourceIPs(\n   402\u2192    DirectSourceIPsMap& direct_source_ips_map, const std::vector<std::string>& direct_source_ips,\n   403\u2192    const envoy::config::listener::v3::FilterChainMatch::ConnectionSourceType source_type,\n   404\u2192    const std::vector<std::string>& source_ips,\n   405\u2192    const absl::Span<const Protobuf::uint32> source_ports,\n   406\u2192    const Network::FilterChainSharedPtr& filter_chain) {\n   407\u2192  if (direct_source_ips.empty()) {\n   408\u2192    RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(addFilterChainForSourceTypes(direct_source_ips_map[EMPTY_STRING], source_type,\n   409\u2192                                                  source_ips, source_ports, filter_chain));\n   410\u2192  } else {\n   411\u2192    for (const auto& direct_source_ip : direct_source_ips) {\n   412\u2192      RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(addFilterChainForSourceTypes(direct_source_ips_map[direct_source_ip],\n   413\u2192                                                    source_type, source_ips, source_ports,\n   414\u2192                                                    filter_chain));\n   415\u2192    }\n   416\u2192  }\n   417\u2192  return absl::OkStatus();\n   418\u2192}\n   419\u2192\n   420\u2192absl::Status FilterChainManagerImpl::addFilterChainForSourceTypes(\n   421\u2192    SourceTypesArraySharedPtr& source_types_array_ptr,\n   422\u2192    const envoy::config::listener::v3::FilterChainMatch::ConnectionSourceType source_type,\n   423\u2192    const std::vector<std::string>& source_ips,\n   424\u2192    const absl::Span<const Protobuf::uint32> source_ports,\n   425\u2192    const Network::FilterChainSharedPtr& filter_chain) {\n   426\u2192  if (source_types_array_ptr == nullptr) {\n   427\u2192    source_types_array_ptr = std::make_shared<SourceTypesArray>();\n   428\u2192  }\n   429\u2192\n   430\u2192  SourceTypesArray& source_types_array = *source_types_array_ptr;\n   431\u2192  if (source_ips.empty()) {\n   432\u2192    RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(addFilterChainForSourceIPs(source_types_array[source_type].first, EMPTY_STRING,\n   433\u2192                                                source_ports, filter_chain));\n   434\u2192  } else {\n   435\u2192    for (const auto& source_ip : source_ips) {\n   436\u2192      RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(addFilterChainForSourceIPs(source_types_array[source_type].first, source_ip,\n   437\u2192                                                  source_ports, filter_chain));\n   438\u2192    }\n   439\u2192  }\n   440\u2192  return absl::OkStatus();\n   441\u2192}\n   442\u2192\n   443\u2192absl::Status FilterChainManagerImpl::addFilterChainForSourceIPs(\n   444\u2192    SourceIPsMap& source_ips_map, const std::string& source_ip,\n   445\u2192    const absl::Span<const Protobuf::uint32> source_ports,\n   446\u2192    const Network::FilterChainSharedPtr& filter_chain) {\n   447\u2192  if (source_ports.empty()) {\n   448\u2192    RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(addFilterChainForSourcePorts(source_ips_map[source_ip], 0, filter_chain));\n   449\u2192  } else {\n   450\u2192    for (auto source_port : source_ports) {\n   451\u2192      RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(\n   452\u2192          addFilterChainForSourcePorts(source_ips_map[source_ip], source_port, filter_chain));\n   453\u2192    }\n   454\u2192  }\n   455\u2192  return absl::OkStatus();\n   456\u2192}\n   457\u2192\n   458\u2192absl::Status FilterChainManagerImpl::addFilterChainForSourcePorts(\n   459\u2192    SourcePortsMapSharedPtr& source_ports_map_ptr, uint32_t source_port,\n   460\u2192    const Network::FilterChainSharedPtr& filter_chain) {\n   461\u2192  if (source_ports_map_ptr == nullptr) {\n   462\u2192    source_ports_map_ptr = std::make_shared<SourcePortsMap>();\n   463\u2192  }\n   464\u2192  auto& source_ports_map = *source_ports_map_ptr;\n   465\u2192\n   466\u2192  if (!source_ports_map.try_emplace(source_port, filter_chain).second) {\n   467\u2192    // If we got here and found already configured branch, then it means that this FilterChainMatch\n   468\u2192    // is a duplicate, and that there is some overlap in the repeated fields with already processed\n   469\u2192    // FilterChainMatches.\n   470\u2192    return absl::InvalidArgumentError(\n   471\u2192        fmt::format(\"error adding listener '{}': multiple filter chains with \"\n   472\u2192                    \"overlapping matching rules are defined\",\n   473\u2192                    absl::StrJoin(addresses_, \",\", Network::AddressStrFormatter())));\n   474\u2192  }\n   475\u2192  return absl::OkStatus();\n   476\u2192}\n   477\u2192\n   478\u2192namespace {\n   479\u2192\n   480\u2192// Template function for creating a CIDR list entry for either source or destination address.\n   481\u2192template <class T>\n   482\u2192std::pair<T, std::vector<Network::Address::CidrRange>>\n   483\u2192makeCidrListEntry(const std::string& cidr, const T& data, absl::Status& creation_status) {\n   484\u2192  std::vector<Network::Address::CidrRange> subnets;\n   485\u2192  absl::StatusOr<Network::Address::CidrRange> range;\n   486\u2192  if (cidr == EMPTY_STRING) {\n   487\u2192    if (Network::SocketInterfaceSingleton::get().ipFamilySupported(AF_INET)) {\n   488\u2192      range = Network::Address::CidrRange::create(Network::Utility::getIpv4CidrCatchAllAddress());\n   489\u2192      creation_status = range.status();\n   490\u2192      if (range.status().ok()) {\n   491\u2192        subnets.push_back(*range);\n   492\u2192      }\n   493\u2192    }\n   494\u2192    if (Network::SocketInterfaceSingleton::get().ipFamilySupported(AF_INET6)) {\n   495\u2192      range = Network::Address::CidrRange::create(Network::Utility::getIpv6CidrCatchAllAddress());\n   496\u2192      creation_status = range.status();\n   497\u2192      if (range.status().ok()) {\n   498\u2192        subnets.push_back(*range);\n   499\u2192      }\n   500\u2192    }\n   501\u2192  } else {\n   502\u2192    range = Network::Address::CidrRange::create(cidr);\n   503\u2192    creation_status = range.status();\n   504\u2192    if (range.status().ok()) {\n   505\u2192      subnets.push_back(*range);\n   506\u2192    }\n   507\u2192  }\n   508\u2192  return std::make_pair<T, std::vector<Network::Address::CidrRange>>(T(data), std::move(subnets));\n   509\u2192}\n   510\u2192\n   511\u2192}; // namespace\n   512\u2192\n   513\u2192const Network::FilterChain*\n   514\u2192FilterChainManagerImpl::findFilterChain(const Network::ConnectionSocket& socket,\n   515\u2192                                        const StreamInfo::StreamInfo& info) const {\n   516\u2192  if (matcher_) {\n   517\u2192    return findFilterChainUsingMatcher(socket, info);\n   518\u2192  }\n   519\u2192\n   520\u2192  const auto& address = socket.connectionInfoProvider().localAddress();\n   521\u2192\n   522\u2192  const Network::FilterChain* best_match_filter_chain = nullptr;\n   523\u2192  // Match on destination port (only for IP addresses).\n   524\u2192  if (address->type() == Network::Address::Type::Ip) {\n   525\u2192    const auto port_match = destination_ports_map_.find(address->ip()->port());\n   526\u2192    if (port_match != destination_ports_map_.end()) {\n   527\u2192      best_match_filter_chain = findFilterChainForDestinationIP(*port_match->second.second, socket);\n   528\u2192      if (best_match_filter_chain != nullptr) {\n   529\u2192        return best_match_filter_chain;\n   530\u2192      } else {\n   531\u2192        // There is entry for specific port but none of the filter chain matches. Instead of\n   532\u2192        // matching catch-all port 0, the fallback filter chain is returned.\n   533\u2192        return default_filter_chain_.get();\n   534\u2192      }\n   535\u2192    }\n   536\u2192  }\n   537\u2192  // Match on catch-all port 0 if there is no specific port sub tree.\n   538\u2192  const auto port_match = destination_ports_map_.find(0);\n   539\u2192  if (port_match != destination_ports_map_.end()) {\n   540\u2192    best_match_filter_chain = findFilterChainForDestinationIP(*port_match->second.second, socket);\n   541\u2192  }\n   542\u2192  return best_match_filter_chain != nullptr\n   543\u2192             ? best_match_filter_chain\n   544\u2192             // Neither exact port nor catch-all port matches. Use fallback filter chain.\n   545\u2192             : default_filter_chain_.get();\n   546\u2192}\n   547\u2192\n   548\u2192const Network::FilterChain*\n   549\u2192FilterChainManagerImpl::findFilterChainUsingMatcher(const Network::ConnectionSocket& socket,\n   550\u2192                                                    const StreamInfo::StreamInfo& info) const {\n   551\u2192  Network::Matching::MatchingDataImpl data(socket, info.filterState(), info.dynamicMetadata());\n   552\u2192  const auto& match_result = Matcher::evaluateMatch<Network::MatchingData>(*matcher_, data);\n   553\u2192  ASSERT(match_result.match_state_ == Matcher::MatchState::MatchComplete,\n   554\u2192         \"Matching must complete for network streams.\");\n   555\u2192  if (match_result.result_) {\n   556\u2192    const auto result = match_result.result_();\n   557\u2192    return result->getTyped<Configuration::FilterChainBaseAction>().get(filter_chains_by_name_,\n   558\u2192                                                                        info);\n   559\u2192  }\n   560\u2192  return default_filter_chain_.get();\n   561\u2192}\n   562\u2192\n   563\u2192const Network::FilterChain* FilterChainManagerImpl::findFilterChainForDestinationIP(\n   564\u2192    const DestinationIPsTrie& destination_ips_trie, const Network::ConnectionSocket& socket) const {\n   565\u2192  auto address = socket.connectionInfoProvider().localAddress();\n   566\u2192  if (address->type() != Network::Address::Type::Ip) {\n   567\u2192    address = FilterChain::fakeAddress();\n   568\u2192  }\n   569\u2192\n   570\u2192  // Match on both: exact IP and wider CIDR ranges using LcTrie.\n   571\u2192  const auto& data = destination_ips_trie.getData(address);\n   572\u2192  if (!data.empty()) {\n   573\u2192    ASSERT(data.size() == 1);\n   574\u2192    return findFilterChainForServerName(*data.back(), socket);\n   575\u2192  }\n   576\u2192\n   577\u2192  return nullptr;\n   578\u2192}\n   579\u2192\n   580\u2192const Network::FilterChain* FilterChainManagerImpl::findFilterChainForServerName(\n   581\u2192    const ServerNamesMap& server_names_map, const Network::ConnectionSocket& socket) const {\n   582\u2192  ASSERT(absl::AsciiStrToLower(socket.requestedServerName()) == socket.requestedServerName());\n   583\u2192  const std::string server_name(socket.requestedServerName());\n   584\u2192\n   585\u2192  // Match on exact server name, i.e. \"www.example.com\" for \"www.example.com\".\n   586\u2192  const auto server_name_exact_match = server_names_map.find(server_name);\n   587\u2192  if (server_name_exact_match != server_names_map.end()) {\n   588\u2192    return findFilterChainForTransportProtocol(server_name_exact_match->second, socket);\n   589\u2192  }\n   590\u2192\n   591\u2192  // Match on all wildcard domains, i.e. \".example.com\" and \".com\" for \"www.example.com\".\n   592\u2192  size_t pos = server_name.find('.', 1);\n   593\u2192  while (pos < server_name.size() - 1 && pos != std::string::npos) {\n   594\u2192    const std::string wildcard = server_name.substr(pos);\n   595\u2192    const auto server_name_wildcard_match = server_names_map.find(wildcard);\n   596\u2192    if (server_name_wildcard_match != server_names_map.end()) {\n   597\u2192      return findFilterChainForTransportProtocol(server_name_wildcard_match->second, socket);\n   598\u2192    }\n   599\u2192    pos = server_name.find('.', pos + 1);\n   600\u2192  }\n   601\u2192\n   602\u2192  // Match on a filter chain without server name requirements.\n   603\u2192  const auto server_name_catchall_match = server_names_map.find(EMPTY_STRING);\n   604\u2192  if (server_name_catchall_match != server_names_map.end()) {\n   605\u2192    return findFilterChainForTransportProtocol(server_name_catchall_match->second, socket);\n   606\u2192  }\n   607\u2192\n   608\u2192  return nullptr;\n   609\u2192}\n   610\u2192\n   611\u2192const Network::FilterChain* FilterChainManagerImpl::findFilterChainForTransportProtocol(\n   612\u2192    const TransportProtocolsMap& transport_protocols_map,\n   613\u2192    const Network::ConnectionSocket& socket) const {\n   614\u2192  const std::string transport_protocol(socket.detectedTransportProtocol());\n   615\u2192\n   616\u2192  // Match on exact transport protocol, e.g. \"tls\".\n   617\u2192  const auto transport_protocol_match = transport_protocols_map.find(transport_protocol);\n   618\u2192  if (transport_protocol_match != transport_protocols_map.end()) {\n   619\u2192    return findFilterChainForApplicationProtocols(transport_protocol_match->second, socket);\n   620\u2192  }\n   621\u2192\n   622\u2192  // Match on a filter chain without transport protocol requirements.\n   623\u2192  const auto any_protocol_match = transport_protocols_map.find(EMPTY_STRING);\n   624\u2192  if (any_protocol_match != transport_protocols_map.end()) {\n   625\u2192    return findFilterChainForApplicationProtocols(any_protocol_match->second, socket);\n   626\u2192  }\n   627\u2192\n   628\u2192  return nullptr;\n   629\u2192}\n   630\u2192\n   631\u2192const Network::FilterChain* FilterChainManagerImpl::findFilterChainForApplicationProtocols(\n   632\u2192    const ApplicationProtocolsMap& application_protocols_map,\n   633\u2192    const Network::ConnectionSocket& socket) const {\n   634\u2192  // Match on exact application protocol, e.g. \"h2\" or \"http/1.1\".\n   635\u2192  for (const auto& application_protocol : socket.requestedApplicationProtocols()) {\n   636\u2192    const auto application_protocol_match = application_protocols_map.find(application_protocol);\n   637\u2192    if (application_protocol_match != application_protocols_map.end()) {\n   638\u2192      return findFilterChainForDirectSourceIP(*application_protocol_match->second.second, socket);\n   639\u2192    }\n   640\u2192  }\n   641\u2192\n   642\u2192  // Match on a filter chain without application protocol requirements.\n   643\u2192  const auto any_protocol_match = application_protocols_map.find(EMPTY_STRING);\n   644\u2192  if (any_protocol_match != application_protocols_map.end()) {\n   645\u2192    return findFilterChainForDirectSourceIP(*any_protocol_match->second.second, socket);\n   646\u2192  }\n   647\u2192\n   648\u2192  return nullptr;\n   649\u2192}\n   650\u2192\n   651\u2192const Network::FilterChain* FilterChainManagerImpl::findFilterChainForDirectSourceIP(\n   652\u2192    const DirectSourceIPsTrie& direct_source_ips_trie,\n   653\u2192    const Network::ConnectionSocket& socket) const {\n   654\u2192  auto address = socket.connectionInfoProvider().directRemoteAddress();\n   655\u2192  if (address->type() != Network::Address::Type::Ip) {\n   656\u2192    address = FilterChain::fakeAddress();\n   657\u2192  }\n   658\u2192\n   659\u2192  const auto& data = direct_source_ips_trie.getData(address);\n   660\u2192  if (!data.empty()) {\n   661\u2192    ASSERT(data.size() == 1);\n   662\u2192    return findFilterChainForSourceTypes(*data.back(), socket);\n   663\u2192  }\n   664\u2192\n   665\u2192  return nullptr;\n   666\u2192}\n   667\u2192\n   668\u2192const Network::FilterChain* FilterChainManagerImpl::findFilterChainForSourceTypes(\n   669\u2192    const SourceTypesArray& source_types, const Network::ConnectionSocket& socket) const {\n   670\u2192\n   671\u2192  const auto& filter_chain_local =\n   672\u2192      source_types[envoy::config::listener::v3::FilterChainMatch::SAME_IP_OR_LOOPBACK];\n   673\u2192\n   674\u2192  const auto& filter_chain_external =\n   675\u2192      source_types[envoy::config::listener::v3::FilterChainMatch::EXTERNAL];\n   676\u2192\n   677\u2192  // isSameIpOrLoopback can be expensive. Call it only if LOCAL or EXTERNAL have entries.\n   678\u2192  const bool is_local_connection =\n   679\u2192      (!filter_chain_local.first.empty() || !filter_chain_external.first.empty())\n   680\u2192          ? Network::Utility::isSameIpOrLoopback(socket.connectionInfoProvider())\n   681\u2192          : false;\n   682\u2192\n   683\u2192  if (is_local_connection) {\n   684\u2192    if (!filter_chain_local.first.empty()) {\n   685\u2192      return findFilterChainForSourceIpAndPort(*filter_chain_local.second, socket);\n   686\u2192    }\n   687\u2192  } else {\n   688\u2192    if (!filter_chain_external.first.empty()) {\n   689\u2192      return findFilterChainForSourceIpAndPort(*filter_chain_external.second, socket);\n   690\u2192    }\n   691\u2192  }\n   692\u2192\n   693\u2192  const auto& filter_chain_any = source_types[envoy::config::listener::v3::FilterChainMatch::ANY];\n   694\u2192\n   695\u2192  if (!filter_chain_any.first.empty()) {\n   696\u2192    return findFilterChainForSourceIpAndPort(*filter_chain_any.second, socket);\n   697\u2192  } else {\n   698\u2192    return nullptr;\n   699\u2192  }\n   700\u2192}\n   701\u2192\n   702\u2192const Network::FilterChain* FilterChainManagerImpl::findFilterChainForSourceIpAndPort(\n   703\u2192    const SourceIPsTrie& source_ips_trie, const Network::ConnectionSocket& socket) const {\n   704\u2192  auto address = socket.connectionInfoProvider().remoteAddress();\n   705\u2192  if (address->type() != Network::Address::Type::Ip) {\n   706\u2192    address = FilterChain::fakeAddress();\n   707\u2192  }\n   708\u2192\n   709\u2192  // Match on both: exact IP and wider CIDR ranges using LcTrie.\n   710\u2192  const auto& data = source_ips_trie.getData(address);\n   711\u2192  if (data.empty()) {\n   712\u2192    return nullptr;\n   713\u2192  }\n   714\u2192\n   715\u2192  ASSERT(data.size() == 1);\n   716\u2192  const auto& source_ports_map = *data.back();\n   717\u2192  const uint32_t source_port = address->ip()->port();\n   718\u2192  const auto port_match = source_ports_map.find(source_port);\n   719\u2192\n   720\u2192  // Did we get a direct hit on port.\n   721\u2192  if (port_match != source_ports_map.end()) {\n   722\u2192    return port_match->second.get();\n   723\u2192  }\n   724\u2192\n   725\u2192  // Try port 0 if we didn't already try it (UDS).\n   726\u2192  if (source_port != 0) {\n   727\u2192    const auto any_match = source_ports_map.find(0);\n   728\u2192    if (any_match != source_ports_map.end()) {\n   729\u2192      return any_match->second.get();\n   730\u2192    }\n   731\u2192  }\n   732\u2192\n   733\u2192  return nullptr;\n   734\u2192}\n   735\u2192\n   736\u2192absl::Status FilterChainManagerImpl::convertIPsToTries() {\n   737\u2192  for (auto& [destination_port, destination_ips_pair] : destination_ports_map_) {\n   738\u2192    UNREFERENCED_PARAMETER(destination_port);\n   739\u2192    // These variables are used as we build up the destination CIDRs used for the trie.\n   740\u2192    auto& [destination_ips_map, destination_ips_trie] = destination_ips_pair;\n   741\u2192    std::vector<std::pair<ServerNamesMapSharedPtr, std::vector<Network::Address::CidrRange>>>\n   742\u2192        destination_ips_list;\n   743\u2192    destination_ips_list.reserve(destination_ips_map.size());\n   744\u2192\n   745\u2192    for (const auto& [destination_ip, server_names_map_ptr] : destination_ips_map) {\n   746\u2192      absl::Status creation_status = absl::OkStatus();\n   747\u2192      destination_ips_list.push_back(\n   748\u2192          makeCidrListEntry(destination_ip, server_names_map_ptr, creation_status));\n   749\u2192      RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(creation_status);\n   750\u2192\n   751\u2192      // This hugely nested for loop greatly pains me, but I'm not sure how to make it better.\n   752\u2192      // We need to get access to all of the source IP strings so that we can convert them into\n   753\u2192      // a trie like we did for the destination IPs above.\n   754\u2192      for (auto& [server_name, transport_protocols_map] : *server_names_map_ptr) {\n   755\u2192        UNREFERENCED_PARAMETER(server_name);\n   756\u2192        for (auto& [transport_protocol, application_protocols_map] : transport_protocols_map) {\n   757\u2192          UNREFERENCED_PARAMETER(transport_protocol);\n   758\u2192          for (auto& [application_protocol, direct_source_ips_pair] : application_protocols_map) {\n   759\u2192            UNREFERENCED_PARAMETER(application_protocol);\n   760\u2192            auto& [direct_source_ips_map, direct_source_ips_trie] = direct_source_ips_pair;\n   761\u2192\n   762\u2192            std::vector<\n   763\u2192                std::pair<SourceTypesArraySharedPtr, std::vector<Network::Address::CidrRange>>>\n   764\u2192                direct_source_ips_list;\n   765\u2192            direct_source_ips_list.reserve(direct_source_ips_map.size());\n   766\u2192\n   767\u2192            for (auto& [direct_source_ip, source_arrays_ptr] : direct_source_ips_map) {\n   768\u2192              direct_source_ips_list.push_back(\n   769\u2192                  makeCidrListEntry(direct_source_ip, source_arrays_ptr, creation_status));\n   770\u2192              RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(creation_status);\n   771\u2192\n   772\u2192              for (auto& [source_ips_map, source_ips_trie] : *source_arrays_ptr) {\n   773\u2192                std::vector<\n   774\u2192                    std::pair<SourcePortsMapSharedPtr, std::vector<Network::Address::CidrRange>>>\n   775\u2192                    source_ips_list;\n   776\u2192                source_ips_list.reserve(source_ips_map.size());\n   777\u2192\n   778\u2192                for (auto& [source_ip, source_port_map_ptr] : source_ips_map) {\n   779\u2192                  source_ips_list.push_back(\n   780\u2192                      makeCidrListEntry(source_ip, source_port_map_ptr, creation_status));\n   781\u2192                  RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(creation_status);\n   782\u2192                }\n   783\u2192\n   784\u2192                source_ips_trie = std::make_unique<SourceIPsTrie>(source_ips_list, true);\n   785\u2192              }\n   786\u2192            }\n   787\u2192            direct_source_ips_trie =\n   788\u2192                std::make_unique<DirectSourceIPsTrie>(direct_source_ips_list, true);\n   789\u2192          }\n   790\u2192        }\n   791\u2192      }\n   792\u2192    }\n   793\u2192\n   794\u2192    destination_ips_trie = std::make_unique<DestinationIPsTrie>(destination_ips_list, true);\n   795\u2192  }\n   796\u2192  return absl::OkStatus();\n   797\u2192}\n   798\u2192\n   799\u2192Network::DrainableFilterChainSharedPtr FilterChainManagerImpl::findExistingFilterChain(\n   800\u2192    const envoy::config::listener::v3::FilterChain& filter_chain_message) {\n   801\u2192  // Origin filter chain manager could be empty if the current is the ancestor.\n   802\u2192  const auto* origin = getOriginFilterChainManager();\n   803\u2192  if (origin == nullptr) {\n   804\u2192    return nullptr;\n   805\u2192  }\n   806\u2192  auto iter = origin->fc_contexts_.find(filter_chain_message);\n   807\u2192  if (iter != origin->fc_contexts_.end()) {\n   808\u2192    // copy the context to this filter chain manager.\n   809\u2192    fc_contexts_.emplace(filter_chain_message, iter->second);\n   810\u2192    return iter->second;\n   811\u2192  }\n   812\u2192  return nullptr;\n   813\u2192}\n   814\u2192\n   815\u2192Configuration::FilterChainFactoryContextPtr FilterChainManagerImpl::createFilterChainFactoryContext(\n   816\u2192    const ::envoy::config::listener::v3::FilterChain* const filter_chain) {\n   817\u2192  // TODO(lambdai): add stats\n   818\u2192  UNREFERENCED_PARAMETER(filter_chain);\n   819\u2192  return std::make_unique<PerFilterChainFactoryContextImpl>(parent_context_, init_manager_);\n   820\u2192}\n   821\u2192\n   822\u2192} // namespace Server\n   823\u2192} // namespace Envoy\n   824\u2192"
9. Read @ -

105\u2192  ListenerComponentFactory& factory_;\n   106\u2192  // Initially, its port number might be 0. Is it required only at\n   164\u2192// listener update or during the lifetime of listener?\n   165\u2192class PerListenerFactoryContextImpl : public Configuration::ListenerFactoryContext {\n   166\u2192public:\n   167\u2192  PerListenerFactoryContextImpl(Envoy::Server::Instance& server,\n   168\u2192                                ProtobufMessage::ValidationVisitor& validation_visitor,\n   169\u2192                                const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& config_message,\n   170\u2192                                ListenerImpl& listener_impl, DrainManagerPtr drain_manager);\n   171\u2192\n   172\u2192  PerListenerFactoryContextImpl(\n   173\u2192      std::shared_ptr<ListenerFactoryContextBaseImpl> listener_factory_context_base,\n   174\u2192      ListenerImpl& listener_impl)\n   175\u2192      : listener_factory_context_base_(listener_factory_context_base),\n   176\u2192        listener_impl_(listener_impl) {}\n   177\u2192\n   178\u2192  // FactoryContext\n   179\u2192  Network::DrainDecision& drainDecision() override;\n   180\u2192  Init::Manager& initManager() override;\n   181\u2192  Stats::Scope& scope() override;\n   182\u2192  const Network::ListenerInfo& listenerInfo() const override;\n   183\u2192  ProtobufMessage::ValidationVisitor& messageValidationVisitor() const override;\n   184\u2192  Configuration::ServerFactoryContext& serverFactoryContext() const override;\n   185\u2192  Configuration::TransportSocketFactoryContext& getTransportSocketFactoryContext() const override;\n   186\u2192\n   187\u2192  Stats::Scope& listenerScope() override;\n   188\u2192\n   189\u2192  ListenerFactoryContextBaseImpl& parentFactoryContext() { return *listener_factory_context_base_; }\n   190\u2192  friend class ListenerImpl;\n   191\u2192\n   192\u2192private:\n   193\u2192  std::shared_ptr<ListenerFactoryContextBaseImpl> listener_factory_context_base_;\n   194\u2192  ListenerImpl& listener_impl_;\n   195\u2192};\n   196\u2192\n   197\u2192/**\n   198\u2192 * Maps proto config to runtime config for a listener with a network filter chain.\n   199\u2192 */\n   200\u2192class ListenerImpl final : public Network::ListenerConfig,\n   201\u2192                           public Network::FilterChainFactory,\n   202\u2192                           Logger::Loggable<Logger::Id::config> {\n   203\u2192public:\n   204\u2192  /**\n   205\u2192   * Create a new listener.\n   206\u2192   * @param config supplies the configuration proto.\n   207\u2192   * @param version_info supplies the xDS version of the listener.\n   208\u2192   * @param parent supplies the owning manager.\n   209\u2192   * @param name supplies the listener name.\n   210\u2192   * @param added_via_api supplies whether the listener can be updated or removed.\n   211\u2192   * @param workers_started supplies whether the listener is being added before or after workers\n   212\u2192   *        have been started. This controls various behavior related to init management.\n   213\u2192   * @param hash supplies the hash to use for duplicate checking.\n   214\u2192   * @param concurrency is the number of listeners instances to be created.\n   215\u2192   */\n   216\u2192  static absl::StatusOr<std::unique_ptr<ListenerImpl>>\n   217\u2192  create(const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& config, const std::string& version_info,\n   218\u2192         ListenerManagerImpl& parent, const std::string& name, bool added_via_api,\n   219\u2192         bool workers_started, uint64_t hash);\n   220\u2192  ~ListenerImpl() override;\n   221\u2192\n   222\u2192  // TODO(lambdai): Explore using the same ListenerImpl object to execute in place filter chain\n   223\u2192  // update.\n   224\u2192  /**\n   225\u2192   * Execute in place filter chain update. The filter chain update is less expensive than full\n   226\u2192   * listener update because connections may not need to be drained.\n   227\u2192   */\n   228\u2192  absl::StatusOr<std::unique_ptr<ListenerImpl>>\n   229\u2192  newListenerWithFilterChain(const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& config,\n   230\u2192                             bool workers_started, uint64_t hash);\n   231\u2192  /**\n   232\u2192   * Determine if in place filter chain update could be executed at this moment.\n   233\u2192   */\n   234\u2192  bool supportUpdateFilterChain(const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& new_config,\n   235\u2192                                bool worker_started);\n   236\u2192\n   237\u2192  /**\n   238\u2192   * Run the callback on each filter chain that exists in this listener but not in the passed\n   239\u2192   * listener config.\n   240\u2192   */\n   241\u2192  void diffFilterChain(const ListenerImpl& another_listener,\n   242\u2192                       std::function<void(Network::DrainableFilterChain&)> callback);\n   243\u2192\n   244\u2192  /**\n   245\u2192   * Helper functions to determine whether a listener is blocked for update or remove.\n   246\u2192   */\n   247\u2192  bool blockUpdate(uint64_t new_hash) { return new_hash == hash_ || !added_via_api_; }\n   248\u2192  bool blockRemove() { return !added_via_api_; }\n   249\u2192\n   250\u2192  const std::vector<Network::Address::InstanceConstSharedPtr>& addresses() const {\n   251\u2192    return addresses_;\n   252\u2192  }\n   253\u2192  const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& config() const { return config_; }\n   254\u2192  const std::vector<Network::ListenSocketFactoryPtr>& getSocketFactories() const {\n   255\u2192    return socket_factories_;\n   256\u2192  }\n   257\u2192  void debugLog(const std::string& message);\n   258\u2192  void initialize();\n   259\u2192  DrainManager& localDrainManager() const {\n   260\u2192    return listener_factory_context_->listener_factory_context_base_->drainManager();\n   261\u2192  }\n   262\u2192  absl::Status addSocketFactory(Network::ListenSocketFactoryPtr&& socket_factory);\n   263\u2192  void setSocketAndOptions(const Network::SocketSharedPtr& socket);\n   264\u2192  const Network::Socket::OptionsSharedPtr& listenSocketOptions(uint32_t address_index) {\n   265\u2192    ASSERT(listen_socket_options_list_.size() > address_index);\n   266\u2192    return listen_socket_options_list_[address_index];\n   267\u2192  }\n   268\u2192  const std::string& versionInfo() const { return version_info_; }\n   269\u2192  bool reusePort() const { return reuse_port_; }\n   270\u2192  static bool getReusePortOrDefault(Server::Instance& server,\n   271\u2192                                    const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& config,\n   272\u2192                                    Network::Socket::Type socket_type);\n   273\u2192\n   274\u2192  // Check whether a new listener can share sockets with this listener.\n   275\u2192  bool hasCompatibleAddress(const ListenerImpl& other) const;\n   276\u2192  // Check whether a new listener has duplicated listening address this listener.\n   277\u2192  bool hasDuplicatedAddress(const ListenerImpl& other) const;\n   278\u2192\n   279\u2192  // Network::ListenerConfig\n   280\u2192  Network::FilterChainManager& filterChainManager() override { return *filter_chain_manager_; }\n   281\u2192  Network::FilterChainFactory& filterChainFactory() override { return *this; }\n   282\u2192  std::vector<Network::ListenSocketFactoryPtr>& listenSocketFactories() override {\n   283\u2192    return socket_factories_;\n   284\u2192  }\n   285\u2192  bool bindToPort() const override { return bind_to_port_; }\n   300\u2192  uint64_t listenerTag() const override { return listener_tag_; }\n   301\u2192  const std::string& name() const override { return name_; }\n   302\u2192  Network::UdpListenerConfigOptRef udpListenerConfig() override {\n   303\u2192    return udp_listener_config_ != nullptr ? *udp_listener_config_\n   304\u2192                                           : Network::UdpListenerConfigOptRef();\n   305\u2192  }\n   306\u2192  Network::InternalListenerConfigOptRef internalListenerConfig() override {\n   307\u2192    return internal_listener_config_ != nullptr ? *internal_listener_config_\n   308\u2192                                                : Network::InternalListenerConfigOptRef();\n   309\u2192  }\n   310\u2192  Network::ConnectionBalancer&\n   311\u2192  connectionBalancer(const Network::Address::Instance& address) override {\n   312\u2192    auto balancer = connection_balancers_.find(address.asString());\n   313\u2192    ASSERT(balancer != connection_balancers_.end());\n   314\u2192    return *balancer->second;\n   315\u2192  }\n   316\u2192  ResourceLimit& openConnections() override { return *open_connections_;
