43\u2192namespace Server {\n    44\u2192\n    45\u2192namespace {\n    46\u2192\n    47\u2192bool anyFilterChain(\n    48\u2192    const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& config,\n    49\u2192    std::function<bool(const envoy::config::listener::v3::FilterChain&)> predicate) {\n    50\u2192  return (config.has_default_filter_chain() && predicate(config.default_filter_chain())) ||\n    51\u2192         std::any_of(config.filter_chains().begin(), config.filter_chains().end(), predicate);\n    52\u2192}\n    53\u2192\n    54\u2192bool usesProxyProto(const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& config) {\n    55\u2192  // Checking only the first or default filter chain is done for backwards compatibility.\n    56\u2192  const bool use_proxy_proto = PROTOBUF_GET_WRAPPED_OR_DEFAULT(\n    57\u2192      config.filter_chains().empty() ? config.default_filter_chain() : config.filter_chains()[0],\n    58\u2192      use_proxy_proto, false);\n    59\u2192  if (use_proxy_proto) {\n    60\u2192    ENVOY_LOG_MISC(warn,\n    61\u2192                   \"using deprecated field 'use_proxy_proto' is dangerous as it does not respect \"\n    62\u2192                   \"listener filter order. Do not use this field and instead configure the proxy \"\n    63\u2192                   \"proto listener filter directly.\");\n    64\u2192  }\n    65\u2192\n    66\u2192  return use_proxy_proto;\n    67\u2192}\n    68\u2192\n    69\u2192bool shouldBindToPort(const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& config) {\n    70\u2192  return PROTOBUF_GET_WRAPPED_OR_DEFAULT(config, bind_to_port, true) &&\n    71\u2192         PROTOBUF_GET_WRAPPED_OR_DEFAULT(config.deprecated_v1(), bind_to_port, true);\n    72\u2192}\n    73\u2192} // namespace\n    74\u2192\n    75\u2192absl::StatusOr<std::unique_ptr<ListenSocketFactoryImpl>> ListenSocketFactoryImpl::create(\n    76\u2192    ListenerComponentFactory& factory, Network::Address::InstanceConstSharedPtr address,\n    77\u2192    Network::Socket::Type socket_type, const Network::Socket::OptionsSharedPtr& options,\n    78\u2192    const std::string& listener_name, uint32_t tcp_backlog_size,\n    79\u2192    ListenerComponentFactory::BindType bind_type,\n    80\u2192    const Network::SocketCreationOptions& creation_options, uint32_t num_sockets) {\n    81\u2192  absl::Status creation_status = absl::OkStatus();\n    82\u2192  auto ret = std::unique_ptr<ListenSocketFactoryImpl>(new ListenSocketFactoryImpl(\n    83\u2192      factory, address, socket_type, options, listener_name, tcp_backlog_size, bind_type,\n    84\u2192      creation_options, num_sockets, creation_status));\n    85\u2192  RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(creation_status);\n    86\u2192  return ret;\n    87\u2192}\n    88\u2192\n    89\u2192ListenSocketFactoryImpl::ListenSocketFactoryImpl(\n    90\u2192    ListenerComponentFactory& factory, Network::Address::InstanceConstSharedPtr address,\n    91\u2192    Network::Socket::Type socket_type, const Network::Socket::OptionsSharedPtr& options,\n    92\u2192    const std::string& listener_name, uint32_t tcp_backlog_size,\n    93\u2192    ListenerComponentFactory::BindType bind_type,\n    94\u2192    const Network::SocketCreationOptions& creation_options, uint32_t num_sockets,\n    95\u2192    absl::Status& creation_status)\n    96\u2192    : factory_(factory), local_address_(address), socket_type_(socket_type), options_(options),\n    97\u2192      listener_name_(listener_name), tcp_backlog_size_(tcp_backlog_size), bind_type_(bind_type),\n    98\u2192      socket_creation_options_(creation_options) {\n    99\u2192\n   100\u2192  if (local_address_->type() == Network::Address::Type::Ip) {\n   101\u2192    if (socket_type == Network::Socket::Type::Datagram) {\n   102\u2192      ASSERT(bind_type_ == ListenerComponentFactory::BindType::ReusePort || num_sockets == 1u);\n   103\u2192    }\n   104\u2192  } else {\n   105\u2192    if (local_address_->type() == Network::Address::Type::Pipe) {\n   106\u2192      // Listeners with Unix domain socket always use shared socket.\n   107\u2192      // TODO(mattklein123): This should be blocked at the config parsing layer instead of getting\n   108\u2192      // here and disabling reuse_port.\n   109\u2192      if (bind_type_ == ListenerComponentFactory::BindType::ReusePort) {\n   110\u2192        bind_type_ = ListenerComponentFactory::BindType::NoReusePort;\n   111\u2192      }\n   112\u2192    } else {\n   113\u2192      ASSERT(local_address_->type() == Network::Address::Type::EnvoyInternal);\n   114\u2192      bind_type_ = ListenerComponentFactory::BindType::NoBind;\n   115\u2192    }\n   116\u2192  }\n   117\u2192\n   118\u2192  auto socket_or_error = createListenSocketAndApplyOptions(factory, socket_type, 0);\n   119\u2192  SET_AND_RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(socket_or_error.status(), creation_status);\n   120\u2192  sockets_.push_back(*socket_or_error);\n   121\u2192\n   122\u2192  if (sockets_[0] != nullptr && local_address_->ip() && local_address_->ip()->port() == 0) {\n   123\u2192    local_address_ = sockets_[0]->connectionInfoProvider().localAddress();\n   124\u2192  }\n   125\u2192  ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"Set listener {} socket factory local address to {}\", listener_name,\n   126\u2192            local_address_->asString());\n   127\u2192\n   128\u2192  // Now create the remainder of the sockets that will be used by the rest of the workers.\n   129\u2192  for (uint32_t i = 1; i < num_sockets; i++) {\n   130\u2192    if (bind_type_ != ListenerComponentFactory::BindType::ReusePort && sockets_[0] != nullptr) {\n   131\u2192      sockets_.push_back(sockets_[0]->duplicate());\n   132\u2192    } else {\n   133\u2192      auto socket_or_error = createListenSocketAndApplyOptions(factory, socket_type, i);\n   134\u2192      SET_AND_RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(socket_or_error.status(), creation_status);\n   135\u2192      sockets_.push_back(*socket_or_error);\n   136\u2192    }\n   137\u2192  }\n   138\u2192  ASSERT(sockets_.size() == num_sockets);\n   139\u2192}\n   140\u2192\n   141\u2192ListenSocketFactoryImpl::ListenSocketFactoryImpl(const ListenSocketFactoryImpl& factory_to_clone)\n   142\u2192    : factory_(factory_to_clone.factory_), local_address_(factory_to_clone.local_address_),\n   143\u2192      socket_type_(factory_to_clone.socket_type_), options_(factory_to_clone.options_),\n   144\u2192      listener_name_(factory_to_clone.listener_name_),\n   145\u2192      tcp_backlog_size_(factory_to_clone.tcp_backlog_size_),\n   146\u2192      bind_type_(factory_to_clone.bind_type_),\n   147\u2192      socket_creation_options_(factory_to_clone.socket_creation_options_) {\n   148\u2192  for (auto& socket : factory_to_clone.sockets_) {\n   149\u2192    // In the cloning case we always duplicate() the socket. This makes sure that during listener\n   150\u2192    // update/drain we don't lose any incoming connections when using reuse_port. Specifically on\n   151\u2192    // Linux the use of SO_REUSEPORT causes the kernel to allocate a separate queue for each socket\n   152\u2192    // on the same address. Incoming connections are immediately assigned to one of these queues.\n   153\u2192    // If connections are in the queue when the socket is closed, they are closed/reset, not sent to\n   154\u2192    // another queue. So avoid making extra queues in the kernel, even temporarily.\n   155\u2192    //\n   156\u2192    // TODO(mattklein123): In the current code as long as the address matches, the socket factory\n   157\u2192    // will be cloned, effectively ignoring any changed socket options. The code should probably\n   158\u2192    // block any updates to listeners that use the same address but different socket options.\n   159\u2192    // (It's possible we could handle changing some socket options, but this would be tricky and\n   160\u2192    // probably not worth the difficulty.)\n   161\u2192    sockets_.push_back(socket->duplicate());\n   162\u2192  }\n   163\u2192}\n   164\u2192\n   165\u2192absl::StatusOr<Network::SocketSharedPtr> ListenSocketFactoryImpl::createListenSocketAndApplyOptions(\n   166\u2192    ListenerComponentFactory& factory, Network::Socket::Type socket_type, uint32_t worker_index) {\n   167\u2192  // Socket might be nullptr when doing server validation.\n   168\u2192  // TODO(mattklein123): See the comment in the validation code. Make that code not return nullptr\n   169\u2192  // so this code can be simpler.\n   170\u2192  absl::StatusOr<Network::SocketSharedPtr> socket_or_error = factory.createListenSocket(\n   171\u2192      local_address_, socket_type, options_, bind_type_, socket_creation_options_, worker_index);\n   172\u2192  RETURN_IF_NOT_OK_REF(socket_or_error.status());\n   173\u2192  Network::SocketSharedPtr socket = std::move(*socket_or_error);\n   174\u2192\n   175\u2192  // Binding is done by now.\n   176\u2192  ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"Create listen socket for listener {} on address {}\", listener_name_,\n   177\u2192            local_address_->asString());\n   178\u2192  if (socket != nullptr && options_ != nullptr) {\n   179\u2192    const bool ok = Network::Socket::applyOptions(\n   180\u2192        options_, *socket, envoy::config::core::v3::SocketOption::STATE_BOUND);\n   181\u2192    const std::string message =\n   182\u2192        fmt::format(\"{}: Setting socket options {}\", listener_name_, ok ? \"succeeded\" : \"failed\");\n   183\u2192    if (!ok) {\n   184\u2192      ENVOY_LOG(warn, \"{}\", message);\n   185\u2192      return absl::InvalidArgumentError(message);\n   186\u2192    } else {\n   187\u2192      ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"{}\", message);\n   188\u2192    }\n   189\u2192\n   190\u2192    // Add the options to the socket_ so that STATE_LISTENING options can be\n   191\u2192    // set after listen() is called and immediately before the workers start running.\n   192\u2192    socket->addOptions(options_);\n   193\u2192  }\n   194\u2192  return socket;\n   195\u2192}\n   196\u2192\n   197\u2192Network::SocketSharedPtr ListenSocketFactoryImpl::getListenSocket(uint32_t worker_index) {\n   198\u2192  // Per the TODO above, sockets at this point can never be null. That only happens in the\n   199\u2192  // config validation path.\n   200\u2192  ASSERT(worker_index < sockets_.size() && sockets_[worker_index] != nullptr);"

\"source/common/stream_info/stream_info_impl.h\"\n    50\u2192#include \"source/common/tracing/http_tracer_impl.h\"\n    51\u2192\n    52\u2192namespace Envoy {\n    53\u2192namespace Http {\n    54\u2192\n    55\u2192/**\n    56\u2192 * Implementation of both ConnectionManager and ServerConnectionCallbacks. This is a\n    57\u2192 * Network::Filter that can be installed on a connection that will perform HTTP protocol agnostic\n    58\u2192 * handling of a connection and all requests/pushes that occur on a connection.\n    59\u2192 */\n    60\u2192class ConnectionManagerImpl : Logger::Loggable<Logger::Id::http>,\n    61\u2192                              public Network::ReadFilter,\n    62\u2192                              public ServerConnectionCallbacks,\n    63\u2192                              public Network::ConnectionCallbacks,\n    64\u2192                              public Http::ApiListener {\n    65\u2192public:\n    66\u2192  ConnectionManagerImpl(ConnectionManagerConfigSharedPtr config,\n    67\u2192                        const Network::DrainDecision& drain_close,\n    68\u2192                        Random::RandomGenerator& random_generator, Http::Context& http_context,\n    69\u2192                        Runtime::Loader& runtime, const LocalInfo::LocalInfo& local_info,\n    70\u2192                        Upstream::ClusterManager& cluster_manager,\n    71\u2192                        Server::OverloadManager& overload_manager, TimeSource& time_system,\n    72\u2192                        envoy::config::core::v3::TrafficDirection direction);\n    73\u2192  ~ConnectionManagerImpl() override;\n    74\u2192\n    75\u2192  static ConnectionManagerStats generateStats(const std::string& prefix, Stats::Scope& scope);\n    76\u2192  static ConnectionManagerTracingStats generateTracingStats(const std::string& prefix,\n    77\u2192                                                            Stats::Scope& scope);\n    78\u2192  static void chargeTracingStats(const Tracing::Reason& tracing_reason,\n    79\u2192                                 ConnectionManagerTracingStats& tracing_stats);\n    80\u2192  static ConnectionManagerListenerStats generateListenerStats(const std::string& prefix,\n    81\u2192                                                              Stats::Scope& scope);\n    82\u2192  static const ResponseHeaderMap& continueHeader();\n    83\u2192\n    84\u2192  // Currently the ConnectionManager creates a codec lazily when either:\n    85\u2192  //   a) onConnection for H3.\n    86\u2192  //   b) onData for H1 and H2.\n    87\u2192  // With the introduction of ApiListeners, neither event occurs. This function allows consumer code\n    88\u2192  // to manually create a codec.\n    89\u2192  // TODO(junr03): consider passing a synthetic codec instead of creating once. The codec in the\n    90\u2192  // ApiListener case is solely used to determine the protocol version.\n    91\u2192  void createCodec(Buffer::Instance& data);\n    92\u2192\n    93\u2192  // Network::ReadFilter\n    94\u2192  Network::FilterStatus onData(Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream) override;\n    95\u2192  Network::FilterStatus onNewConnection() override;\n    96\u2192  void initializeReadFilterCallbacks(Network::ReadFilterCallbacks& callbacks) override;\n    97\u2192\n    98\u2192  // Http::ConnectionCallbacks\n    99\u2192  void onGoAway(GoAwayErrorCode error_code) override;\n   100\u2192\n   101\u2192  // Http::ServerConnectionCallbacks\n   102\u2192  RequestDecoder& newStream(ResponseEncoder& response_encoder,\n   103\u2192                            bool is_internally_created = false) override;\n   104\u2192\n   105\u2192  RequestDecoderHandlePtr newStreamHandle(ResponseEncoder& response_encoder,\n   106\u2192                                          bool is_internally_created = false) override;\n   107\u2192\n   108\u2192  // Network::ConnectionCallbacks\n   109\u2192  void onEvent(Network::ConnectionEvent event) override;\n   110\u2192  // Pass connection watermark events on to all the streams associated with that connection.\n   111\u2192  void onAboveWriteBufferHighWatermark() override {\n   112\u2192    codec_->onUnderlyingConnectionAboveWriteBufferHighWatermark();\n   113\u2192  }\n   114\u2192  void onBelowWriteBufferLowWatermark() override {\n   115\u2192    codec_->onUnderlyingConnectionBelowWriteBufferLowWatermark();\n   116\u2192  }\n   117\u2192\n   118\u2192  TimeSource& timeSource() { return time_source_; }\n   119\u2192\n   120\u2192  void setClearHopByHopResponseHeaders(bool value) { clear_hop_by_hop_response_headers_ = value; }\n   121\u2192  bool clearHopByHopResponseHeaders() const { return clear_hop_by_hop_response_headers_; }\n   122\u2192\n   123\u2192  // This runtime key configures the number of streams which must be closed on a connection before\n   124\u2192  // envoy will potentially drain a connection due to excessive prematurely reset streams.\n   125\u2192  static const absl::string_view PrematureResetTotalStreamCountKey;\n   126\u2192\n   127\u2192  // The minimum lifetime of a stream, in seconds, in order not to be considered\n   128\u2192  // prematurely closed.\n   129\u2192  static const absl::string_view PrematureResetMinStreamLifetimeSecondsKey;\n   130\u2192  static const absl::string_view MaxRequestsPerIoCycle;\n   131\u2192  static const absl::string_view OptionallyDelayClose;\n   132\u2192\n   133\u2192private:\n   134\u2192  struct ActiveStream;\n   135\u2192  class MobileConnectionManagerImpl;\n   136\u2192\n   137\u2192  /**\n   138\u2192   * Wraps a single active stream on the connection. These are either full request/response pairs\n   139\u2192   * or pushes.\n   140\u2192   */\n   141\u2192  struct ActiveStream final : LinkedObject<ActiveStream>,\n   142\u2192                              public Event::DeferredDeletable,\n   143\u2192                              public StreamCallbacks,\n   144\u2192                              public CodecEventCallbacks,\n   145\u2192                              public RequestDecoder,\n   146\u2192                              public Tracing::Config,\n   147\u2192                              public ScopeTrackedObject,\n   148\u2192                              public FilterManagerCallbacks,\n   149\u2192                              public DownstreamStreamFilterCallbacks,\n   150\u2192                              public RouteCache {"

"   486\u2192Network::FilterStatus ConnectionManagerImpl::onData(Buffer::Instance& data, bool) {\n   487\u2192  requests_during_dispatch_count_ = 0;\n   488\u2192  if (!codec_) {\n   489\u2192    // Close connections if Envoy is under pressure, typically memory, before creating codec.\n   490\u2192    if (hcm_ondata_creating_codec_ != nullptr && hcm_ondata_creating_codec_->shouldShedLoad()) {\n   491\u2192      stats_.named_.downstream_rq_overload_close_.inc();\n   492\u2192      handleCodecOverloadError(\"onData codec creation overload\");\n   493\u2192      return Network::FilterStatus::StopIteration;\n   494\u2192    }\n   495\u2192    // Http3 codec should have been instantiated by now.\n   496\u2192    createCodec(data);\n   497\u2192  }\n   498\u2192\n   499\u2192  bool redispatch;\n   500\u2192  do {\n   501\u2192    redispatch = false;\n   502\u2192\n   503\u2192    const Status status = codec_->dispatch(data);\n   504\u2192\n   505\u2192    if (isBufferFloodError(status) || isInboundFramesWithEmptyPayloadError(status)) {\n   506\u2192      handleCodecError(status.message());\n   507\u2192      return Network::FilterStatus::StopIteration;\n   508\u2192    } else if (isCodecProtocolError(status)) {\n   509\u2192      stats_.named_.downstream_cx_protocol_error_.inc();\n   510\u2192      handleCodecError(status.message());\n   511\u2192      return Network::FilterStatus::StopIteration;\n   512\u2192    } else if (isEnvoyOverloadError(status)) {\n   513\u2192      // The other codecs aren't wired to send this status.\n   514\u2192      ASSERT(codec_->protocol() < Protocol::Http2,\n   515\u2192             \"Expected only HTTP1.1 and below to send overload error.\");\n   516\u2192      stats_.named_.downstream_rq_overload_close_.inc();\n   517\u2192      handleCodecOverloadError(status.message());\n   518\u2192      return Network::FilterStatus::StopIteration;\n   519\u2192    }\n   520\u2192    ASSERT(status.ok());\n   521\u2192\n   522\u2192    // Processing incoming data may release outbound data so check for closure here as well.\n   523\u2192    checkForDeferredClose(false);\n   524\u2192\n   525\u2192    // The HTTP/1 codec will pause dispatch after a single message is complete. We want to\n   526\u2192    // either redispatch if there are no streams and we have more data. If we have a single\n   527\u2192    // complete non-WebSocket stream but have not responded yet we will pause socket reads\n   528\u2192    // to apply back pressure.\n   529\u2192    if (codec_->protocol() < Protocol::Http2) {\n   530\u2192      if (read_callbacks_->connection().state() == Network::Connection::State::Open &&\n   531\u2192          data.length() > 0 && streams_.empty()) {\n   532\u2192        redispatch = true;\n   533\u2192      }\n   534\u2192    }\n   535\u2192  } while (redispatch);\n   536\u2192\n   537\u2192  if (!read_callbacks_->connection().streamInfo().protocol()) {\n   538\u2192    read_callbacks_->connection().streamInfo().protocol(codec_->protocol());\n   539\u2192  }\n   540\u2192\n   541\u2192  return Network::FilterStatus::StopIteration;\n   542\u2192}\n   543\u2192\n   544\u2192Network::FilterStatus ConnectionManagerImpl::onNewConnection() {\n   545\u2192  if (!read_callbacks_->connection().streamInfo().protocol()) {\n   546\u2192    // For Non-QUIC traffic, continue passing data to filters.\n   547\u2192    return Network::FilterStatus::Continue;\n   548\u2192  }\n   549\u2192  // Only QUIC connection's stream_info_ specifies protocol.\n   550\u2192  Buffer::OwnedImpl dummy;\n   551\u2192  createCodec(dummy);\n   552\u2192  ASSERT(codec_->protocol() == Protocol::Http3);\n   553\u2192  // Stop iterating through network filters for QUIC. Currently QUIC connections bypass the\n   554\u2192  // onData() interface because QUICHE already handles de-multiplexing.\n   555\u2192  return Network::FilterStatus::StopIteration;\n   556\u2192}\n   557\u2192\n   558\u2192void ConnectionManagerImpl::resetAllStreams(\n   559\u2192    absl::optional<StreamInfo::CoreResponseFlag> response_flag, absl::string_view details) {\n   560\u2192  while (!streams_.empty()) {\n   561\u2192    // Mimic a downstream reset in this case. We must also remove callbacks here. Though we are\n   562\u2192    // about to close the connection and will disable further reads, it is possible that flushing\n   563\u2192    // data out can cause stream callbacks to fire (e.g., low watermark callbacks).\n   564\u2192    //\n   565\u2192    // TODO(mattklein123): I tried to actually reset through the codec here, but ran into issues\n   566\u2192    // with nghttp2 state and being unhappy about sending reset frames after the connection had\n   567\u2192    // been terminated via GOAWAY. It might be possible to do something better here inside the h2\n   568\u2192    // codec but there are no easy answers and this seems simpler.\n   569\u2192    auto& stream = *streams_.front();\n   570\u2192    stream.response_encoder_->getStream().removeCallbacks(stream);\n   571\u2192    if (!stream.response_encoder_->getStream().responseDetails().empty()) {\n   572\u2192      stream.filter_manager_.streamInfo().setResponseCodeDetails(\n   573\u2192          stream.response_encoder_->getStream().responseDetails());\n   574\u2192    } else if (!details.empty()) {\n   575\u2192      stream.filter_manager_.streamInfo().setResponseCodeDetails(details);\n   576\u2192    }\n   577\u2192    if (response_flag.has_value()) {\n   578\u2192      stream.filter_manager_.streamInfo().setResponseFlag(response_flag.value());\n   579\u2192    }\n   580\u2192    stream.onResetStream(StreamResetReason::ConnectionTermination, absl::string_view());\n   581\u2192  }\n   582\u2192}\n   583\u2192\n   584\u2192void ConnectionManagerImpl::onEvent(Network::ConnectionEvent event) {\n   585\u2192  if (event == Network::ConnectionEvent::LocalClose) {"

Round Trip Time\n   112\u2192  absl::optional<std::chrono::milliseconds> t = socket->lastRoundTripTime();\n   113\u2192  if (t.has_value()) {\n   114\u2192 119\u2192        connection_balancer_.pickTargetHandler(*this);\n   120\u2192    if (&target_handler != this) {\n   121\u2192      target_handler.post(std::move(socket));\n   122\u2192      return;\n ActiveTcpListener::pauseListening() {\n   133\u2192  if (listener_ != nullptr) {\n   134\u2192    listener_->disable();\n   135\u2192  }\n   136\u2192}\n   137\u2192\n   138\u2192void ActiveTcpListener::resumeListening() {\n   139\u2192  if (listener_ != nullptr) {\n   140\u2192    listener_->enable();\n   141\u2192  }\n   142\u2192}\n   143\u2192\n   144\u2192Network::BalancedConnectionHandlerOptRef\n   145\u2192ActiveTcpListener::getBalancedHandlerByAddress(const Network::Address::Instance& address) {\n   146\u2192  return tcp_conn_handler_.getBalancedHandlerByAddress(address);\n   147\u2192}\n   148\u2192\n   149\u2192void ActiveTcpListener::newActiveConnection(const Network::FilterChain& filter_chain,\n   150\u2192  152\u2192  auto& active_connections = getOrCreateActiveConnections(filter_chain);\n   153\u2192  auto active_connection =\n   154\u2192      std::make_unique<ActiveTcpConnection>(active_connections, std::move(server_conn_ptr),\n   155\u2192                                            dispatcher().timeSource(), std::move(stream_info));\n   156\u2192  // If the connection is already closed, we can just let this connection immediately die.\n   157\u2192  if (active_connection->connection_->state() != Network::Connection::State::Closed) {\n   158\u2192    ENVOY_CONN_LOG(\n   159\u2192        debug, \"new connection from {}\", *active_connection->connection_,\n   160\u2192        active_connection->connection_->connectionInfoProvider().remoteAddress()->asString());\n   161\u2192    active_connection->connection_->addConnectionCallbacks(*active_connection);\n   162\u2192    LinkedList::moveIntoList(std::move(active_connection), active_connections.connections_);\n   163\u2192  }\n   164\u2192}\n   165\u2192\n   166\u2192void ActiveTcpListener::post(Network::ConnectionSocketPtr&& socket) {\n   167\u2192  // It is not possible to capture a unique_ptr because the post() API copies the lambda, so we must\n   168\u2192  // bundle the socket inside a shared_ptr that can be captured.\n   169\u2192  // TODO(mattklein123): It may be possible to change the post() API such that the lambda is only\n   170\u2192  // moved, but this is non-trivial and needs investigation.\n   171\u2192  RebalancedSocketSharedPtr socket_to_rebalance = std::make_shared<RebalancedSocket>();\n   172\u2192  socket_to_rebalance->socket = std::move(socket);\n   173\u2192\n   174\u2192  dispatcher().post([socket_to_rebalance, address = listen_address_, tag = config_->listenerTag(),\n   175\u2192                     &tcp_conn_handler = tcp_conn_handler_,\n   176\u2192                     handoff = config_->handOffRestoredDestinationConnections()]() {\n   177\u2192    auto balanced_handler = tcp_conn_handler.getBalancedHandlerByTag(tag, *address);\n   178\u2192    if (balanced_handler.has_value()) {\n   179\u2192      balanced_handler->get().onAcceptWorker(std::move(socket_to_rebalance->socket), handoff, true);\n   180\u2192      return;\n   181\u2192    }\n   182\u2192  });\n   183\u2192}\n   184\u2192\n "     1\u2192#include \"source/common/listener_manager/active_tcp_socket.h\"\n     2\u2192\n     3\u2192#include \"envoy/network/filter.h\"\n     4\u2192\n     5\u2192#include \"source/common/listener_manager/active_stream_listener_base.h\"\n     6\u2192#include \"source/common/stream_info/stream_info_impl.h\"\n     7\u2192\n     8\u2192namespace Envoy {\n     9\u2192namespace Server {\n    10\u2192\n    11\u2192ActiveTcpSocket::ActiveTcpSocket(ActiveStreamListenerBase& listener,\n    12\u2192                                 Network::ConnectionSocketPtr&& socket,\n    13\u2192                                 bool hand_off_restored_destination_connections)\n    14\u2192    : listener_(listener), socket_(std::move(socket)),\n    15\u2192      hand_off_restored_destination_connections_(hand_off_restored_destination_connections),\n    16\u2192      iter_(accept_filters_.end()),\n    17\u2192      stream_info_(std::make_unique<StreamInfo::StreamInfoImpl>(\n    18\u2192          listener_.dispatcher().timeSource(), socket_->connectionInfoProviderSharedPtr(),\n    19\u2192          StreamInfo::FilterState::LifeSpan::Connection)) {\n    20\u2192  listener_.stats_.downstream_pre_cx_active_.inc();\n    21\u2192}\n    22\u2192\n    23\u2192ActiveTcpSocket::~ActiveTcpSocket() {\n    24\u2192  accept_filters_.clear();\n    25\u2192\n    26\u2192  listener_.stats_.downstream_pre_cx_active_.dec();\n    27\u2192  // If the underlying socket is no longer attached, it means that it has been transferred to\n    28\u2192  // an active connection. In this case, the active connection will decrement the number\n    29\u2192  // of listener connections.\n    30\u2192  // TODO(mattklein123): In general the way we account for the number of listener connections\n    31\u2192  // is incredibly fragile. Revisit this by potentially merging ActiveTcpSocket and\n    32\u2192  // ActiveTcpConnection, having a shared object which does accounting (but would require\n    33\u2192  // another allocation, etc.).\n    34\u2192  if (socket_ != nullptr) {\n    35\u2192    listener_.decNumConnections();\n    36\u2192  }\n    37\u2192}\n    38\u2192\n    39\u2192Event::Dispatcher& ActiveTcpSocket::dispatcher() { return listener_.dispatcher(); }\n    40\u2192\n    41\u2192void ActiveTcpSocket::onTimeout() {\n    42\u2192  listener_.stats_.downstream_pre_cx_timeout_.inc();\n    43\u2192  ASSERT(inserted());\n    44\u2192  ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"listener filter times out after {} ms\",\n    45\u2192            listener_.listener_filters_timeout_.count());\n    46\u2192\n    47\u2192  if (listener_.continue_on_listener_filters_timeout_) {\n    48\u2192    ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"fallback to default listener filter\");\n    49\u2192    newConnection();\n    50\u2192  }\n    51\u2192  unlink();\n    52\u2192}\n    53\u2192\n    54\u2192void ActiveTcpSocket::startTimer() {\n    55\u2192  if (listener_.listener_filters_timeout_.count() > 0) {\n    56\u2192    timer_ = listener_.dispatcher().createTimer([this]() -> void { onTimeout(); });\n    57\u2192    timer_->enableTimer(listener_.listener_filters_timeout_);\n    58\u2192  }\n    59\u2192}\n    60\u2192\n    61\u2192void ActiveTcpSocket::unlink() {\n    62\u2192  auto removed = listener_.removeSocket(std::move(*this));\n    63\u2192  if (removed->timer_ != nullptr) {\n    64\u2192    removed->timer_->disableTimer();\n    65\u2192  }\n    66\u2192  // Emit logs if a connection is not established.\n    67\u2192  if (!connected_ && stream_info_ != nullptr) {\n    68\u2192    ActiveStreamListenerBase::emitLogs(*listener_.config_, *stream_info_);\n    69\u2192  }\n    70\u2192  listener_.dispatcher().deferredDelete(std::move(removed));\n    71\u2192}\n    72\u2192\n    73\u2192void ActiveTcpSocket::createListenerFilterBuffer() {\n    74\u2192  listener_filter_buffer_ = std::make_unique<Network::ListenerFilterBufferImpl>(\n    75\u2192      socket_->ioHandle(), listener_.dispatcher(),\n    76\u2192      [this](bool error) {\n    77\u2192        socket_->ioHandle().close();\n    78\u2192        if (error) {\n    79\u2192          listener_.stats_.downstream_listener_filter_error_.inc();\n    80\u2192        } else {\n    81\u2192          listener_.stats_.downstream_listener_filter_remote_close_.inc();\n    82\u2192        }\n    83\u2192        continueFilterChain(false);\n    84\u2192      },\n    85\u2192      [this](Network::ListenerFilterBufferImpl& filter_buffer) {\n    86\u2192        Network::FilterStatus status = (*iter_)->onData(filter_buffer);\n    87\u2192        if (status == Network::FilterStatus::StopIteration) {\n    88\u2192          if (socket_->ioHandle().isOpen()) {\n    89\u2192            // The listener filter should not wait for more data when it has already received\n    90\u2192            // all the data it requested.\n    91\u2192            ASSERT(filter_buffer.rawSlice().len_ < (*iter_)->maxReadBytes());\n    92\u2192            // Check if the maxReadBytes is changed or not. If change,\n    93\u2192            // reset the buffer capacity.\n    94\u2192            if ((*iter_)->maxReadBytes() > filter_buffer.capacity()) {\n    95\u2192              filter_buffer.resetCapacity((*iter_)->maxReadBytes());\n    96\u2192              // Activate `Read` event manually in case the data already\n    97\u2192              // available in the socket buffer.\n    98\u2192              filter_buffer.activateFileEvent(Event::FileReadyType::Read);\n    99\u2192            }\n   100\u2192          } else {\n   101\u2192            // The filter closed the socket.\n   102\u2192            continueFilterChain(false);\n   103\u2192          }\n   104\u2192          return;\n   105\u2192        }\n   106\u2192        continueFilterChain(true);\n   107\u2192      },\n   108\u2192      (*iter_)->maxReadBytes() == 0, (*iter_)->maxReadBytes());\n   109\u2192}\n   110\u2192\n   111\u2192void ActiveTcpSocket::continueFilterChain(bool success) {\n   112\u2192  if (success) {\n   113\u2192    bool no_error = true;\n   114\u2192    if (iter_ == accept_filters_.end()) {\n   115\u2192      iter_ = accept_filters_.begin();\n   116\u2192    } else {\n   117\u2192      iter_ = std::next(iter_);\n   118\u2192    }\n   119\u2192\n   120\u2192    for (; iter_ != accept_filters_.end(); iter_++) {\n   121\u2192      Network::FilterStatus status = (*iter_)->onAccept(*this);\n   122\u2192      if (status == Network::FilterStatus::StopIteration) {\n   123\u2192        // The filter is responsible for calling us again at a later time to continue the filter\n   124\u2192        // chain from the next filter.\n   125\u2192        if (!socket().ioHandle().isOpen()) {\n   126\u2192          // Break the loop but should not create new connection.\n   127\u2192          no_error = false;\n   128\u2192          break;\n   129\u2192        } else {\n   130\u2192          // There are two cases for returning StopIteration. One is listener filter\n   131\u2192          // needs to wait for data. The other is listener filter with 0 maxReadBytes()\n   132\u2192          // doesn't inspect data, but needs to wait for some asynchronous callback and\n   133\u2192          // listener filter buffer needs to listen for socket close event.\n   134\u2192          if (listener_filter_buffer_ == nullptr) {\n   135\u2192            createListenerFilterBuffer();\n   136\u2192          }\n   137\u2192          if ((*iter_)->maxReadBytes() > 0) {\n   138\u2192            listener_filter_buffer_->disableOnDataCallback(false);\n   139\u2192            // If the current filter expect more data than previous filters, then\n   140\u2192            // increase the filter buffer's capacity.\n   141\u2192            if (listener_filter_buffer_->capacity() < (*iter_)->maxReadBytes()) {\n   142\u2192              listener_filter_buffer_->resetCapacity((*iter_)->maxReadBytes());\n   143\u2192            }\n   144\u2192            if (listener_filter_buffer_ != nullptr) {\n   145\u2192              // There are two cases for activate event manually: One is\n   146\u2192              // the data is already available when connect, activate the read event to peek\n   147\u2192              // data from the socket . Another one is the data already\n   148\u2192              // peeked into the buffer when previous filter processing the data, then activate the\n   149\u2192              // read event to trigger the current filter callback to process the data.\n   150\u2192              listener_filter_buffer_->activateFileEvent(Event::FileReadyType::Read);"

filter chain iteration is needed.\n   165\u2192      iter_ = accept_filters_.end();\n   166\u2192    }\n   167\u2192  }\n   168\u2192\n   169\u2192  // Filter execution concluded, unlink and delete this ActiveTcpSocket if it was linked.\n   170\u2192  if (inserted()) {\n   171\u2192    unlink();\n   172\u2192  }\n   173\u2192}\n   174\u2192\n   175\u2192void stream_info_->setDynamicMetadata(name, value);\n   178\u2192}\n   179\u2192\n   180\u2192void ActiveTcpSocket::setDynamicTypedMetadata(const std::string& name,\n   181\u2192                                              const ProtobufWkt::Any& value) {\n   182\u2192  stream_info_->setDynamicTypedMetadata(name, value);\n   183\u2192}\n   184\u2192\n   185\u2192void ActiveTcpSocket::newConnection() {\n   186\u2192  connected_ = true;\n   187\u2192\n   188\u2192  // Check if the socket may need to be redirected to another listener.\n   189\u2192  Network::BalancedConnectionHandlerOptRef new_listener;\n   190\u2192\n   191\u2192  if (hand_off_restored_destination_connections_ &&\n   192\u2192      socket_->connectionInfoProvider().localAddressRestored()) {\n   193\u2192    // Find a listener associated with the original destination address.\n   194\u2192    new_listener =\n   195\u2192        listener_.getBalancedHandlerByAddress(*socket_->connectionInfoProvider().localAddress());\n   196\u2192  }\n   197\u2192\n   198\u2192  // Reset the file events which are registered by listener filter.\n   199\u2192  // reference https://github.com/envoyproxy/envoy/issues/8925.\n   200\u2192  if (listener_filter_buffer_ != nullptr) {\n   201\u2192    listener_filter_buffer_->reset();\n   202\u2192  }\n   203\u2192\n   204\u2192  if (new_listener.has_value()) {\n   205\u2192    // Hands off connections redirected by iptables to the listener associated with the\n   206\u2192    // original destination address. Pass 'hand_off_restored_destination_connections' as false to\n   207\u2192    // prevent further redirection.\n   208\u2192    // Leave the new listener to decide whether to execute re-balance.\n   209\u2192    // Note also that we must account for the number of connections properly across both listeners.\n   210\u2192    // TODO(mattklein123): See note in ~ActiveTcpSocket() related to making this accounting better.\n   211\u2192    listener_.decNumConnections();\n   212\u2192    new_listener.value().get().onAcceptWorker(std::move(socket_), false, false);\n   213\u2192  } else {\n   214\u2192    // Set default transport protocol if none of the listener filters did it.\n   215\u2192    if (socket_->detectedTransportProtocol().empty()) {\n   216\u2192      socket_->setDetectedTransportProtocol(\"raw_buffer\");\n   217\u2192    }\n   218\u2192    accept_filters_.clear();\n   219\u2192    // Create a new connection on this listener.\n   220\u2192    listener_.newConnection(std::move(socket_), std::move(stream_info_));\n   221\u2192  }\n   222\u2192}\n   223\u2192} // namespace Server\n   224\u2192} // namespace Envoy\n   225\u2192"

"    38\u2192void ActiveStreamListenerBase::newConnection(Network::ConnectionSocketPtr&& socket,\n    39\u2192                                             std::unique_ptr<StreamInfo::StreamInfo> stream_info) {\n    40\u2192  // Find matching filter chain.\n    41\u2192  const auto filter_chain = config_->filterChainManager().findFilterChain(*socket, *stream_info);\n    42\u2192  if (filter_chain == nullptr) {\n    43\u2192    RELEASE_ASSERT(socket->connectionInfoProvider().remoteAddress() != nullptr, \"\");\n    44\u2192    ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"closing connection from {}: no matching filter chain found\",\n    45\u2192              socket->connectionInfoProvider().remoteAddress()->asString());\n    46\u2192    stats_.no_filter_chain_match_.inc();\n    47\u2192    stream_info->setResponseFlag(StreamInfo::CoreResponseFlag::NoRouteFound);\n    48\u2192    stream_info->setResponseCodeDetails(StreamInfo::ResponseCodeDetails::get().FilterChainNotFound);\n    49\u2192    emitLogs(*config_, *stream_info);\n    50\u2192    socket->close();\n    51\u2192    return;\n    52\u2192  }\n    53\u2192\n    54\u2192 59\u2192  auto server_conn_ptr = dispatcher().createServerConnection(\n    60\u2192      std::move(socket), std::move(transport_socket), *stream_info);\n    61\u2192  if (const auto timeout = filter_chain->transportSocketConnectTimeout();\n    62\u2192      timeout != std::chrono::milliseconds::zero()) {\n    63\u2192    server_conn_ptr->setTransportSocketConnectTimeout(\n    64\u2192        timeout, stats_.downstream_cx_transport_socket_connect_timeout_);\n    65\u2192  }\n    66\u2192  server_conn_ptr->setBufferLimits(config_->perConnectionBufferLimitBytes());\n    67\u2192  RELEASE_ASSERT(server_conn_ptr->connectionInfoProvider().remoteAddress() != nullptr, \"\");\n    68\u2192  const bool empty_filter_chain = !config_->filterChainFactory().createNetworkFilterChain(\n    69\u2192      *server_conn_ptr, filter_chain->networkFilterFactories());\n    70\u2192  if (empty_filter_chain) {\n    71\u2192    ENVOY_CONN_LOG(debug, \"closing connection from {}: no filters\", *server_conn_ptr,\n    72\u2192                   server_conn_ptr->connectionInfoProvider().remoteAddress()->asString());\n    73\u2192    server_conn_ptr->close(Network::ConnectionCloseType::NoFlush, \"no_filters\");\n    74\u2192  }\n    75\u2192  newActiveConnection(*filter_chain, std::move(server_conn_ptr), std::move(stream_info));\n    76\u2192}\n    77\u2192\n    78\u2192ActiveConnections::ActiveConnections(OwnedActiveStreamListenerBase& listener,\n    79\u2192                                     const Network::FilterChain& filter_chain)\n    80\u2192    : listener_(listener), filter_chain_(filter_chain) {}\n    81\u2192\n    82\u2192ActiveConnections::~ActiveConnections() {\n    83\u2192  // connections should be defer deleted already.\n    84\u2192  ASSERT(connections_.empty());\n    85\u2192}\n    86\u2192\n    87\u2192ActiveTcpConnection::ActiveTcpConnection(ActiveConnections& active_connections,\n    88\u2192                                         Network::ConnectionPtr&& new_connection,\n    89\u2192                                         TimeSource& time_source,\n    90\u2192                                         std::unique_ptr<StreamInfo::StreamInfo>&& stream_info)\n    91\u2192    : stream_info_(std::move(stream_info)), active_connections_(active_connections),\n    92\u2192      connection_(std::move(new_connection)),\n    93\u2192      conn_length_(new Stats::HistogramCompletableTimespanImpl(\n    94\u2192          active_connections_.listener_.stats_.downstream_cx_length_ms_, time_source)) {\n    95\u2192  // We just universally set no delay on connections. Theoretically we might at some point want\n    96\u2192  // to make this configurable.\n    97\u2192  connection_->noDelay(true);\n    98\u2192  auto& listener = active_connections_.listener_;\n    99\u2192  listener.stats_.downstream_cx_total_.inc();\n   100\u2192  listener.stats_.downstream_cx_active_.inc();\n   101\u2192  listener.per_worker_stats_.downstream_cx_total_.inc();\n   102\u2192  listener.per_worker_stats_.downstream_cx_active_.inc();\n   103\u2192\n   104\u2192  // Active connections on the handler (not listener). The per listener connections have already\n   105\u2192  // been incremented at this point either via the connection balancer or in the socket accept\n   106\u2192  // path if there is no configured balancer.\n   107\u2192  listener.parent_.incNumConnections();\n   108\u2192}\n   109\u2192\n   110\u2192ActiveTcpConnection::~ActiveTcpConnection() {\n   111\u2192  ActiveStreamListenerBase::emitLogs(*active_connections_.listener_.config_, *stream_info_);\n   112\u2192  auto& listener = active_connections_.listener_;\n   113\u2192  listener.stats_.downstream_cx_active_.dec();\n   114\u2192  listener.stats_.downstream_cx_destroy_.inc();\n   115\u2192  listener.per_worker_stats_.downstream_cx_active_.dec();\n   116\u2192  conn_length_->complete();\n   117\u2192"

{

  "prompt": "I need to understand how Envoy builds and iterates through the HTTP filter chain once HTTP request headers are parsed. Explore thoroughly:\n\n1. In `source/common/http/`, find:\n   - How `ActiveStream` or similar is created when a new HTTP stream begins (look in conn_manager_impl.cc)\n   - The `FilterManager` class - where it's defined, how it manages decoder/encoder filters\n   - The `filter_manager.cc/h` files - specifically:\n     - How the HTTP filter chain is created (look for `createFilterChain` or similar)\n     - How decoder filters are iterated (decodeHeaders, decodeData, etc.)\n     - How encoder filters are iterated (encodeHeaders, encodeData, etc.)\n     - What return values filters can return (FilterHeadersStatus, FilterDataStatus, etc.)\n     - The order of iteration for decoder vs encoder filters\n\n2. Look at how `newStream()` callback from the codec triggers stream/filter creation\n\n3. Find the FilterHeadersStatus, FilterDataStatus, FilterTrailersStatus enums - what values they can return and what each means\n\nProvide specific file paths, class names, function names, and line numbers for all findings.",
  "subagent_type": "Explore"
}

  "agentId": "a9831c6",
  "content": [
    {
      "text": "Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me create a comprehensive summary of my findings.\n\n## Summary: Envoy HTTP Filter Chain Architecture\n\nBased on my thorough exploration of the Envoy codebase, here is a detailed analysis of how HTTP request headers are parsed and the filter chain is iterated:\n\n### 1. **ActiveStream Creation on New HTTP Stream**\n\n**File**: `/workspace/source/common/http/conn_manager_impl.cc`\n\n**Entry Point - newStream() Callback** (Lines 387-442):\n- When the HTTP codec detects a new stream, it calls `ConnectionManagerImpl::newStream(ResponseEncoder& response_encoder, bool is_internally_created)` (line 387)\n- This creates a new `ActiveStream` object using `std::make_unique<ActiveStream>()` (line 407)\n- The `ActiveStream` constructor initializes the `DownstreamFilterManager` which manages the filter chain\n\n**ActiveStream Constructor** (Lines 812-876):\n- Located at line 812 in `conn_manager_impl.cc`\n- Creates a `DownstreamFilterManager` object (lines 821-828) with these parameters:\n  - `filter_manager_callbacks_` (the ActiveStream itself implements this)\n  - Dispatcher, connection, stream_id, buffer account\n  - Filter chain factory and local reply handler from config\n  - Protocol, time source, and parent filter state\n\n### 2. **FilterManager Class Definition and Structure**\n\n**File**: `/workspace/source/common/http/filter_manager.h`\n\n**FilterManager Class** (Lines 681-1163):\n- Base class for managing decoder and encoder filter chains\n- Key members:\n  - `StreamDecoderFilters decoder_filters_` (line 1109): Holds decoder filter wrappers\n  - `StreamEncoderFilters encoder_filters_` (line 1110): Holds encoder filter wrappers\n  - `std::vector<StreamFilterBase*> filters_` (line 1111): Flat list of all filters\n\n**StreamDecoderFilters** (Lines 73-81):\n- Iterator type: `std::vector<ActiveStreamDecoderFilterPtr>::iterator`\n- Iterates **forward** through filters (A, B, C)\n- Created by `createFilterChain()` and iterated in order\n\n**StreamEncoderFilters** (Lines 92-100):\n- Iterator type: `std::vector<ActiveStreamEncoderFilterPtr>::reverse_iterator`\n- Iterates **backward/reverse** through filters (C, B, A)\n- Explanation in comments (lines 84-91): \"Unlike the decoder filter, the encoder filter chain will iterate with the reverse order of the configured filters\"\n- This is important for proper request/response symmetry\n\n### 3. **Filter Chain Creation**\n\n**FilterManager::createFilterChain()** (Lines 1660-1703 in `/workspace/source/common/http/filter_manager.cc`):\n```\n1. Check if chain already created (line 1662)\n2. Cleanup handler sets iterator entries after creation (lines 1668-1675)\n3. Try upgrade filter chain if downstream (lines 1689-1695)\n4. Call filter_chain_factory.createFilterChain() (line 1701)\n   - This factory invokes FilterChainFactoryCallbacksImpl\n   - Each filter is wrapped in ActiveStreamDecoderFilter or ActiveStreamEncoderFilter\n5. Store result in state_.create_chain_result_\n```\n\n**FilterChainFactoryCallbacksImpl** (Lines 983-1020 in filter_manager.h):\n- `addStreamDecoderFilter()` (lines 988-993): Creates ActiveStreamDecoderFilter wrapper, adds to decoder_filters_.entries_\n- `addStreamEncoderFilter()` (lines 995-1000): Creates ActiveStreamEncoderFilter wrapper, adds to encoder_filters_.entries_\n- `addStreamFilter()` (lines 1002-1009): Adds same filter to both decoder and encoder chains\n\n**When is filter chain created?**\n- Called from `DownstreamFilterManager::createDownstreamFilterChain()` (line 1086-1087)\n- Invoked in `ActiveStream::decodeHeaders()` at line 1403 in conn_manager_impl.cc\n- Triggered when first request headers arrive from downstream\n\n### 4. **Decoder Filter Chain Iteration**\n\n**FilterManager::decodeHeaders()** (Lines 537-636 in filter_manager.cc):\n```\n1. Get starting iterator via commonDecodePrefix() (line 541)\n   - If first call: decoder_filters_.begin()\n   - If resuming: next filter or current if iterate_from_current_filter_ set\n2. Loop through decoder_filters_ (line 547: for (; entry != decoder_filters_.end(); entry++)\n3. For each filter:\n   - Set FilterCallState::DecodeHeaders flag (line 550)\n   - Set end_stream_ on filter (line 551)\n   - Call (*entry)->decodeHeaders(headers, end_stream) (line 555)\n   - Handle return status via commonHandleAfterHeadersCallback() (line 577)\n   - Call decodeComplete() if end_stream (line 585)\n   - Process newly added metadata (line 594)\n   - Check if should continue iteration\n4. Call maybeEndDecode() at line 635\n```\n\n**Return Values for FilterHeadersStatus** (Lines 38-110 in `/workspace/envoy/http/filter.h`):\n- `Continue` (line 40): Continue to next filter in chain\n- `StopIteration` (lines 42-48): Stop iteration, wait for continueDecoding()\n- `ContinueAndDontEndStream` (lines 50-86): Convert to header+body, don't end stream yet\n- `StopAllIterationAndBuffer` (lines 88-98): Stop all, buffer body data\n- `StopAllIterationAndWatermark` (lines 99-109): Stop all, buffer until watermark\n\n**FilterManager::decodeData()** (Lines 638-762 in filter_manager.cc):\n```\n1. Get starting iterator via commonDecodePrefix() (line 653)\n2. Check if decoder filter chain already stopped (line 646)\n3. Loop through decoder_filters_ (line 658)\n4. For each filter:\n   - Call handleDataIfStopAll() (line 661)\n   - Check if filter's end_stream_ already set (line 694)\n   - Set FilterCallState::DecodeData flag (line 708)\n   - Call (*entry)->handle_->decodeData(data, end_stream) (line 710)\n   - Handle return status via commonHandleAfterDataCallback() (line 743)\n   - Call decodeComplete() if end_stream (line 712)\n5. If trailers were added during decodeData, call decodeTrailers() (line 755)\n6. Call maybeEndDecode() at line 761\n```\n\n**Return Values for FilterDataStatus** (Lines 129-165 in filter.h):\n- `Continue` (lines 130-134): Continue to next filter\n- `StopIterationAndBuffer` (lines 135-144): Stop, buffer for later\n- `StopIterationAndWatermark` (lines 145-155): Stop, buffer until watermark\n- `StopIterationNoBuffer` (lines 156-164): Stop, don't buffer\n\n**FilterManager::decodeTrailers()** (Lines 796-844 in filter_manager.cc):\n```\n1. Get starting iterator via commonDecodePrefix() (line 804)\n2. Check if decoder filter chain already stopped (line 799)\n3. Loop through decoder_filters_ (line 809)\n4. For each filter:\n   - Check if filter has stopped all (line 812)\n   - Set FilterCallState::DecodeTrailers flag (line 816)\n   - Call (*entry)->handle_->decodeTrailers(trailers) (line 817)\n   - Call decodeComplete() (line 818)\n   - Set end_stream_ = true (line 819)\n   - Handle return status via commonHandleAfterTrailersCallback() (line 835)\n5. Call maybeEndDecode()\n```\n\n**Return Values for FilterTrailersStatus** (Lines 171-177 in filter.h):\n- `Continue` (line 173): Continue to next filter\n- `StopIteration` (lines 174-176): Stop iteration, wait for continueDecoding()\n\n### 5. **Encoder Filter Chain Iteration**\n\n**FilterManager::encodeHeaders()** (Lines 1225-1314 in filter_manager.cc):\n```\n1. Get starting iterator via commonEncodePrefix() (line 1234)\n   - REVERSE iteration starts at encoder_filters_.begin() (line 922)\n   - Because encoder_filters_ uses reverse_iterator (line 94)\n   - So forward iteration goes backwards through filter list\n2. Loop through encoder_filters_ in REVERSE order (line 1238)\n3. For each filter (in reverse):\n   - Set FilterCallState::EncodeHeaders flag (line 1241)\n   - Set end_stream_ on filter (line 1242)\n   - Call (*entry)->handle_->encodeHeaders(headers, end_stream) (line 1246)\n   - Handle return status via commonHandleAfterHeadersCallback() (line 1267)\n   - Call encodeComplete() if end_stream (line 1271)\n4. Check required response headers (line 1293)\n5. Call filter_manager_callbacks_.encodeHeaders() (line 1305)\n6. Call maybeEndEncode() at line 1309\n```\n\n**FilterManager::encodeData()** (Lines 1401-1474 in filter_manager.cc):\n```\n1. Get starting iterator via commonEncodePrefix() (line 1407)\n2. Loop through encoder_filters_ in REVERSE order (line 1412)\n3. For each filter:\n   - Call handleDataIfStopAll() (line 1415)\n   - Check if filter's end_stream_ already set (line 1420)\n   - Set FilterCallState::EncodeData flag (line 1428)\n   - Call (*entry)->handle_->encodeData(data, end_stream) (line 1436)\n   - Handle return status via commonHandleAfterDataCallback() (line 1457)\n   - Call encodeComplete() if end_stream (line 1443)\n4. If trailers were added, call encodeTrailers() (line 1472)\n5. Call maybeEndEncode()\n```\n\n**FilterManager::encodeTrailers()** (Lines 1476-1507 in filter_manager.cc):\n```\n1. Get starting iterator via commonEncodePrefix() (line 1481)\n2. Loop through encoder_filters_ in REVERSE order (line 1483)\n3. For each filter:\n   - Check if filter has stopped all (line 1486)\n   - Set FilterCallState::EncodeTrailers flag (line 1490)\n   - Call (*entry)->handle_->encodeTrailers(trailers) (line 1491)\n   - Call encodeComplete() (line 1492)\n   - Set end_stream_ = true (line 1493)\n   - Handle return status via commonHandleAfterTrailersCallback() (line 1497)\n4. Call filter_manager_callbacks_.encodeTrailers() (line 1502)\n5. Call maybeEndEncode() at line 1506\n```\n\n### 6. **Iteration Control and State Management**\n\n**ActiveStreamFilterBase::IterationState** (Lines 211-218 in filter_manager.h):\n- `Continue`: Iteration has not stopped for any frame type\n- `StopSingleIteration`: Iteration has stopped for headers, 100-continue, or data\n- `StopAllBuffer`: Iteration stopped for all frame types, buffer data\n- `StopAllWatermark`: Iteration stopped for all frame types, buffer until watermark\n\n**FilterCallState Flags** (Lines 1137-1161 in filter_manager.h):\n```\nDecodeHeaders   = 0x01\nDecodeData      = 0x02\nDecodeMetadata  = 0x04\nDecodeTrailers  = 0x08\nEncodeHeaders   = 0x10\nEncodeData      = 0x20\nEncodeMetadata  = 0x40\nEncodeTrailers  = 0x80\nEncode1xxHeaders = 0x100\nEndOfStream     = 0x200\nIsDecodingMask  = DecodeHeaders | DecodeData | DecodeMetadata | DecodeTrailers\nIsEncodingMask  = EncodeHeaders | Encode1xxHeaders | EncodeData | EncodeMetadata | EncodeTrailers\n```\n\n**commonDecodePrefix()** and **commonEncodePrefix()** (Lines 901-947 in filter_manager.cc):\n- Determine starting iterator for filter iteration\n- Handle resumption from StopAllBuffer/Watermark states\n- Support `FilterIterationStartState::AlwaysStartFromNext` (skip current filter)\n- Support `FilterIterationStartState::CanStartFromCurrent` (may resume from current)\n\n### 7. **Response Status Handlers**\n\n**ActiveStreamFilterBase::commonHandleAfterHeadersCallback()** (Lines 130-162 in filter_manager.cc):\n- Maps FilterHeadersStatus to IterationState\n- Returns `false` if iteration stopped, `true` if continuing\n- Handles ContinueAndDontEndStream specially\n\n**ActiveStreamFilterBase::commonHandleAfterDataCallback()** (Lines 180-213 in filter_manager.cc):\n- Maps FilterDataStatus to IterationState\n- Buffers data if needed via commonHandleBufferData()\n- Calls commonContinue() recursively if data continues after stop\n\n**ActiveStreamFilterBase::commonHandleAfterTrailersCallback()** (Lines 215-232 in filter_manager.cc):\n- Maps FilterTrailersStatus to IterationState\n- Returns `false` if iteration stopped, `true` if continuing\n\n### 8. **Key Files and Line References**\n\n| File | Content |\n|------|---------|\n| `/workspace/source/common/http/filter_manager.h` | FilterManager class, ActiveStreamDecoderFilter, ActiveStreamEncoderFilter, filter containers |\n| `/workspace/source/common/http/filter_manager.cc` | decodeHeaders/Data/Trailers, encodeHeaders/Data/Trailers, iteration logic, status handlers |\n| `/workspace/source/common/http/conn_manager_impl.h` | ConnectionManagerImpl, ActiveStream struct definition |\n| `/workspace/source/common/http/conn_manager_impl.cc` | newStream(), ActiveStream constructor, filter chain creation trigger |\n| `/workspace/envoy/http/filter.h` | FilterHeadersStatus, FilterDataStatus, FilterTrailersStatus enums |\n| `/workspace/source/common/http/filter_chain_helper.h` | Filter chain factory and configuration |\n\nThis architecture ensures request headers flow through filters sequentially (A\u2192B\u2192C) while responses flow backward (C\u2192B\u2192A), enabling filters to properly wrap and transform both request and response data.",
      "type": "text"
    }
  ],

\"envoy.filters.network.http_connection_manager.local_reply_owner\";\n    45\u2192class LocalReplyOwnerObject : public StreamInfo::FilterState::Object {\n    46\u2192public:\n    47\u2192  LocalReplyOwnerObject(const std::string& filter_config_name)\n    48\u2192      : filter_config_name_(filter_config_name) {}\n    49\u2192\n    50\u2192  ProtobufTypes::MessagePtr serializeAsProto() const override {\n    51\u2192    auto message = std::make_unique<ProtobufWkt::StringValue>();\n    52\u2192    message->set_value(filter_config_name_);\n    53\u2192    return message;\n    54\u2192
