filter_config_name_;\n    60\u2192};\n    61\u2192\n    62\u2192// TODO(wbpcode): Rather than allocating every filter with an unique pointer, we could\n    63\u2192// construct the filter in place in the vector. This should reduce the heap allocation and\n    64\u2192// memory fragmentation.\n    65\u2192\n    66\u2192// HTTP decoder filters. If filters are configured in the following order (assume all three\n    67\u2192// filters are both decoder/encoder filters):\n    68\u2192//   http_filters:\n    69\u2192//     - A\n    70\u2192//     - B\n    71\u2192//     - C\n    72\u2192// The decoder filter chain will iterate through filters A, B, C.\n    73\u2192struct StreamDecoderFilters {\n    74\u2192  using Element = ActiveStreamDecoderFilter;\n    75\u2192  using Iterator = std::vector<ActiveStreamDecoderFilterPtr>::iterator;\n    76\u2192\n    77\u2192  Iterator begin() { return entries_.begin(); }\n    78\u2192  Iterator end() { return entries_.end(); }\n    79\u2192\n    80\u2192  std::vector<ActiveStreamDecoderFilterPtr> entries_;\n    81\u2192};\n    82\u2192\n    83\u2192// HTTP encoder filters. If filters are configured in the following order (assume all three\n    84\u2192// filters are both decoder/encoder filters):\n    85\u2192//   http_filters:\n    86\u2192//     - A\n    87\u2192//     - B\n    88\u2192//     - C\n    89\u2192// Unlike the decoder filter, the encoder filter chain will iterate with the\n    90\u2192// reverse order of the configured filters, i.e., C, B, A. This is why we use reverse_iterator\n    91\u2192// here.\n    92\u2192struct StreamEncoderFilters {\n    93\u2192  using Element = ActiveStreamEncoderFilter;\n    94\u2192  using Iterator = std::vector<ActiveStreamEncoderFilterPtr>::reverse_iterator;\n    95\u2192\n    96\u2192  Iterator begin() { return entries_.rbegin(); }\n    97\u2192  Iterator end() { return entries_.rend(); }\n    98\u2192\n    99\u2192  std::vector<ActiveStreamEncoderFilterPtr> entries_;\n   100\u2192};\n   101\u2192\n   102\u2192/**\n   103\u2192 * Base class wrapper for both stream encoder and decoder filters.\n   104\u2192 *\n   105\u2192 * This class is responsible for performing matching and updating match data when a match tree is\n   106\u2192 * configured for the associated filter. When not using a match tree, only minimal overhead (i.e.\n   107\u2192 * memory overhead of unused fields) should apply.\n   108\u2192 */\n   109\u2192struct ActiveStreamFilterBase : public virtual StreamFilterCallbacks,\n   110\u2192                                Logger::Loggable<Logger::Id::http> {\n   111\u2192  ActiveStreamFilterBase(FilterManager& parent, FilterContext filter_context)\n   112\u2192      : parent_(parent), iteration_state_(IterationState::Continue),\n   113\u2192        filter_context_(std::move(filter_context)) {}\n   114\u2192\n   115\u2192  // Functions in the following block are called after the filter finishes processing\n   116\u2192  // corresponding data. Those functions handle state updates and data storage (if needed)\n   117\u2192  // according to the status returned by filter's callback functions.\n   118\u2192  bool commonHandleAfter1xxHeadersCallback(Filter1xxHeadersStatus status);\n   119\u2192  bool commonHandleAfterHeadersCallback(FilterHeadersStatus status, bool& end_stream);\n   120\u2192  bool commonHandleAfterDataCallback(FilterDataStatus status, Buffer::Instance& provided_data,\n   121\u2192                                     bool& buffer_was_streaming);\n   122\u2192  bool commonHandleAfterTrailersCallback(FilterTrailersStatus status);\n   123\u2192\n   124\u2192  // Buffers provided_data.\n   125\u2192  void commonHandleBufferData(Buffer::Instance& provided_data);\n   126\u2192\n   127\u2192  // If iteration has stopped for all frame types, calls this function to buffer the data before\n   128\u2192  // the filter processes data. The function also updates streaming state.\n   129\u2192  void commonBufferDataIfStopAll(Buffer::Instance& provided_data, bool& buffer_was_streaming);\n   130\u2192\n   131\u2192  void commonContinue();\n   132\u2192  virtual bool canContinue() PURE;\n   133\u2192  virtual Buffer::InstancePtr createBuffer() PURE;\n   134\u2192  virtual Buffer::InstancePtr& bufferedData() PURE;\n   135\u2192  virtual bool observedEndStream() PURE;\n   136\u2192  virtual bool has1xxHeaders() PURE;\n   137\u2192  virtual void do1xxHeaders() PURE;\n   138\u2192  virtual void doHeaders(bool end_stream) PURE;\n   139\u2192  virtual void doData(bool end_stream) PURE;\n   140\u2192  virtual void doTrailers() PURE;\n   141\u2192  virtual bool hasTrailers() PURE;\n   142\u2192  virtual void doMetadata() PURE;\n   143\u2192  // TODO(soya3129): make this pure when adding impl to encoder filter.\n   144\u2192  virtual void handleMetadataAfterHeadersCallback() PURE;\n   145\u2192\n   146\u2192  virtual void onMatchCallback(const Matcher::Action& action) PURE;\n   147\u2192\n   148\u2192  // Http::StreamFilterCallbacks\n   149\u2192  OptRef<const Network::Connection> connection() override;\n   150\u2192  Event::Dispatcher& dispatcher() override;\n   151\u2192  Router::RouteConstSharedPtr route() override;\n   152\u2192  void resetStream(Http::StreamResetReason reset_reason,\n   153\u2192                   absl::string_view transport_failure_reason) override;\n   154\u2192  Upstream::ClusterInfoConstSharedPtr clusterInfo() override;\n   155\u2192  uint64_t streamId() const override;\n   156\u2192  StreamInfo::StreamInfo& streamInfo() override;\n   157\u2192  Tracing::Span& activeSpan() override;\n   158\u2192  OptRef<const Tracing::Config> tracingConfig() const override;\n   159\u2192  const ScopeTrackedObject& scope() override;\n   160\u2192  void restoreContextOnContinue(ScopeTrackedObjectStack& tracked_object_stack) override;\n   161\u2192  void resetIdleTimer() override;\n   162\u2192  const Router::RouteSpecificFilterConfig* mostSpecificPerFilterConfig() const override;\n   163\u2192  Router::RouteSpecificFilterConfigs perFilterConfigs() const override;\n   164\u2192  Http1StreamEncoderOptionsOptRef http1StreamEncoderOptions() override;\n   165\u2192  OptRef<DownstreamStreamFilterCallbacks> downstreamCallbacks() override;\n   166\u2192  OptRef<UpstreamStreamFilterCallbacks> upstreamCallbacks() override;\n   167\u2192  absl::string_view filterConfigName() const override { return filter_context_.config_name; }\n   168\u2192  RequestHeaderMapOptRef requestHeaders() override;\n   169\u2192  RequestTrailerMapOptRef requestTrailers() override;\n   170\u2192  ResponseHeaderMapOptRef informationalHeaders() override;\n   171\u2192  ResponseHeaderMapOptRef responseHeaders() override;\n   172\u2192  ResponseTrailerMapOptRef responseTrailers() override;\n   173\u2192\n   174\u2192  // Functions to set or get iteration state.\n   175\u2192  bool canIterate() { return iteration_state_ == IterationState::Continue; }\n   176\u2192  bool stoppedAll() {\n   177\u2192    return iteration_state_ == IterationState::StopAllBuffer ||\n   178\u2192           iteration_state_ == IterationState::StopAllWatermark;\n   179\u2192  }\n   180\u2192  void allowIteration() {\n   181\u2192    ASSERT(iteration_state_ != IterationState::Continue);\n   182\u2192    iteration_state_ = IterationState::Continue;\n   183\u2192  }\n   184\u2192  MetadataMapVector* getSavedRequestMetadata() {\n   185\u2192    if (saved_request_metadata_ == nullptr) {\n   186\u2192      saved_request_metadata_ = std::make_unique<MetadataMapVector>();\n   187\u2192    }\n   188\u2192    return saved_request_metadata_.get();\n   189\u2192  }\n   190\u2192  MetadataMapVector* getSavedResponseMetadata() {\n   191\u2192    if (saved_response_metadata_ == nullptr) {\n   192\u2192      saved_response_metadata_ = std::make_unique<MetadataMapVector>();\n   193\u2192    }\n   194\u2192    return saved_response_metadata_.get();\n   195\u2192  }\n   196\u2192\n   197\u2192  Router::RouteConstSharedPtr getRoute() const;\n   198\u2192\n   199\u2192  void sendLocalReply(Code code, absl::string_view body,\n   200\u2192                      std::function<void(ResponseHeaderMap& headers)> modify_headers,\n   201\u2192                      const absl::optional<Grpc::Status::GrpcStatus> grpc_status,\n   202\u2192                      absl::string_view details);\n   203\u2192\n   204\u2192  // A vector to save metadata when the current filter's [de|en]codeMetadata() can not be called,\n   205\u2192  // either because [de|en]codeHeaders() of the current filter returns StopAllIteration or because\n   206\u2192  // [de|en]codeHeaders() adds new metadata to [de|en]code, but we don't know\n   207\u2192  // [de|en]codeHeaders()'s return value yet. The storage is created on demand.\n   208\u2192  std::unique_ptr<MetadataMapVector> saved_request_metadata_{nullptr};\n   209\u2192  std::unique_ptr<MetadataMapVector> saved_response_metadata_{nullptr};\n   210\u2192  // The state of iteration.\n   211\u2192  enum class IterationState : uint8_t {\n   212\u2192    Continue,            // Iteration has not stopped for any frame type.\n   213\u2192    StopSingleIteration, // Iteration has stopped for headers, 100-continue, or data.\n   214\u2192    StopAllBuffer,       // Iteration has stopped for all frame types, and following data should\n   215\u2192                         // be buffered.\n   216\u2192    StopAllWatermark,    // Iteration has stopped for all frame types, and following data should\n   217\u2192                         // be buffered until high watermark is reached.\n   218\u2192  };\n   219\u2192  FilterManager& parent_;\n   220\u2192  IterationState iteration_state_{};\n   221\u2192\n   222\u2192  const FilterContext filter_context_;\n   223\u2192\n   224\u2192  // If the filter resumes iteration from a StopAllBuffer/Watermark state, the current filter\n   225\u2192  // hasn't parsed data and trailers. As a result, the filter iteration should start with the\n   226\u2192  // current filter instead of the next one. If true, filter iteration starts with the current\n   227\u2192  // filter. Otherwise, starts with the next filter in the chain.\n   228\u2192  bool iterate_from_current_filter_{};\n   229\u2192  bool headers_continued_{};\n   230\u2192  bool continued_1xx_headers_{};\n   231\u2192  // If true, end_stream is called for this filter.\n   232\u2192  bool end_stream_{};\n   233\u2192  // If true, the filter has processed headers.\n   234\u2192  bool processed_headers_{};\n   235\u2192};\n   236\u2192\n   237\u2192/**\n   238\u2192 * Wrapper for a stream decoder filter.\n   239\u2192 */\n   240\u2192struct ActiveStreamDecoderFilter : public ActiveStreamFilterBase,\n   241\u2192                                   public StreamDecoderFilterCallbacks {\n   242\u2192  ActiveStreamDecoderFilter(FilterManager& parent, StreamDecoderFilterSharedPtr filter,\n   243\u2192                            FilterContext filter_context)\n   244\u2192      : ActiveStreamFilterBase(parent, std::move(filter_context)), handle_(std::move(filter)) {\n   245\u2192    handle_->setDecoderFilterCallbacks(*this);\n   246\u2192  }\n   247\u2192\n   248\u2192  // ActiveStreamFilterBase\n   249\u2192  bool canContinue() override;\n   250\u2192  Buffer::InstancePtr createBuffer() override;\n   251\u2192  Buffer::InstancePtr& bufferedData() override;\n   252\u2192  bool observedEndStream() override;\n   253\u2192  bool has1xxHeaders() override { return false; }\n   254\u2192  void do1xxHeaders() override { IS_ENVOY_BUG(\"unexpected 1xx headers\"); }\n   255\u2192  void doHeaders(bool end_stream) override;\n   256\u2192  void doData(bool end_stream) override;\n   257\u2192  void doMetadata() override {\n   258\u2192    if (saved_request_metadata_ != nullptr) {\n   259\u2192      drainSavedRequestMetadata();\n   260\u2192    }\n   261\u2192  }\n   262\u2192  void doTrailers() override;\n   263\u2192  bool hasTrailers() override;\n   264\u2192\n   265\u2192  void drainSavedRequestMetadata();\n   266\u2192  // This function is called after the filter calls decodeHeaders() to drain accumulated metadata.\n   267\u2192  void handleMetadataAfterHeadersCallback() override;\n   268\u2192  void onMatchCallback(const Matcher::Action& action) override {\n   269\u2192    handle_->onMatchCallback(std::move(action));\n   270\u2192  }\n   271\u2192\n   272\u2192  // Http::StreamDecoderFilterCallbacks\n   273\u2192  void addDecodedData(Buffer::Instance& data, bool streaming) override;\n   274\u2192  void injectDecodedDataToFilterChain(Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream) override;\n   275\u2192  RequestTrailerMap& addDecodedTrailers() override;\n   276\u2192  MetadataMapVector& addDecodedMetadata() override;\n   277\u2192  void continueDecoding() override;\n   278\u2192  const Buffer::Instance* decodingBuffer() override;\n   279\u2192\n   280\u2192  void modifyDecodingBuffer(std::function<void(Buffer::Instance&)> callback) override;\n   281\u2192\n   282\u2192  void sendLocalReply(Code code, absl::string_view body,\n   283\u2192                      std::function<void(ResponseHeaderMap& headers)> modify_headers,\n   284\u2192                      const absl::optional<Grpc::Status::GrpcStatus> grpc_status,\n   285\u2192                      absl::string_view details) override;\n   286\u2192  void encode1xxHeaders(ResponseHeaderMapPtr&& headers) override;\n   287\u2192  void encodeHeaders(ResponseHeaderMapPtr&& headers, bool end_stream,\n   288\u2192                     absl::string_view details) override;\n   289\u2192  void encodeData(Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream) override;\n   290\u2192  void encodeTrailers(ResponseTrailerMapPtr&& trailers) override;\n   291\u2192  void encodeMetadata(MetadataMapPtr&& metadata_map_ptr) override;\n   292\u2192  void onDecoderFilterAboveWriteBufferHighWatermark() override;\n   293\u2192  void onDecoderFilterBelowWriteBufferLowWatermark() override;\n   294\u2192  void addDownstreamWatermarkCallbacks(DownstreamWatermarkCallbacks& watermark_callbacks) override;\n   295\u2192  void\n   296\u2192  removeDownstreamWatermarkCallbacks(DownstreamWatermarkCallbacks& watermark_callbacks) override;\n   297\u2192  void setDecoderBufferLimit(uint32_t limit) override;\n   298\u2192  uint32_t decoderBufferLimit() override;\n   299\u2192  bool recreateStream(const Http::ResponseHeaderMap* original_response_headers) override;\n   300\u2192\n   301\u2192  void addUpstreamSocketOptions(const Network::Socket::OptionsSharedPtr& options) override;\n   302\u2192\n   303\u2192  Network::Socket::OptionsSharedPtr getUpstreamSocketOptions() const override;\n   304\u2192  Buffer::BufferMemoryAccountSharedPtr account() const override;\n   305\u2192  void setUpstreamOverrideHost(Upstream::LoadBalancerContext::OverrideHost) override;\n   306\u2192  absl::optional<Upstream::LoadBalancerContext::OverrideHost> upstreamOverrideHost() const override;\n   307\u2192  bool shouldLoadShed() const override;\n   308\u2192  void sendGoAwayAndClose() override;\n   309\u2192\n   310\u2192  // Each decoder filter instance checks if the request passed to the filter is gRPC\n   311\u2192  // so that we can issue gRPC local responses to gRPC requests. Filter's decodeHeaders()\n   312\u2192  // called here may change the content type, so we must check it before the call.\n   313\u2192  FilterHeadersStatus decodeHeaders(RequestHeaderMap& headers, bool end_stream) {\n   314\u2192    is_grpc_request_ = Grpc::Common::isGrpcRequestHeaders(headers);\n   315\u2192    FilterHeadersStatus status = handle_->decodeHeaders(headers, end_stream);\n   316\u2192    return status;\n   317\u2192  }\n   318\u2192\n   319\u2192  void requestDataTooLarge();\n   320\u2192  void requestDataDrained();\n   321\u2192  // Encoding end_stream by a non-terminal filters (i.e. cache filter) always causes the decoding to\n   322\u2192  // be stopped even if independent half-close is enabled. For simplicity, independent half-close is\n   323\u2192  // enabled only when the terminal filter is encoding the response.\n   324\u2192  void stopDecodingIfNonTerminalFilterEncodedEndStream(bool encoded_end_stream);\n   325\u2192  StreamDecoderFilters::Iterator entry() const { return entry_; }\n   326\u2192\n   327\u2192  StreamDecoderFilterSharedPtr handle_;\n   328\u2192  StreamDecoderFilters::Iterator entry_{};\n   329\u2192  bool is_grpc_request_{};\n   330\u2192};\n   331\u2192\n   332\u2192/**\n   333\u2192 * Wrapper for a stream encoder filter.\n   334\u2192 */\n   335\u2192struct ActiveStreamEncoderFilter : public ActiveStreamFilterBase,\n   336\u2192                                   public StreamEncoderFilterCallbacks {\n   337\u2192  ActiveStreamEncoderFilter(FilterManager& parent, StreamEncoderFilterSharedPtr filter,\n   338\u2192                            FilterContext filter_context)\n   339\u2192      : ActiveStreamFilterBase(parent, std::move(filter_context)), handle_(std::move(filter)) {\n   340\u2192    handle_->setEncoderFilterCallbacks(*this);\n   341\u2192  }\n   342\u2192\n   343\u2192  // ActiveStreamFilterBase\n   344\u2192  bool canContinue() override;\n   345\u2192  Buffer::InstancePtr createBuffer() override;\n   346\u2192  Buffer::InstancePtr& bufferedData() override;\n   347\u2192  bool observedEndStream() override;\n   348\u2192  bool has1xxHeaders() override;\n   349\u2192  void do1xxHeaders() override;\n   350\u2192  void doHeaders(bool end_stream) override;\n   351\u2192  void doData(bool end_stream) override;\n   352\u2192  void drainSavedResponseMetadata();\n   353\u2192  void handleMetadataAfterHeadersCallback() override;\n   354\u2192  void onMatchCallback(const Matcher::Action& action) override { handle_->onMatchCallback(action); }\n   355\u2192\n   356\u2192  void doMetadata() override {\n   357\u2192    if (saved_response_metadata_ != nullptr) {\n   358\u2192      drainSavedResponseMetadata();\n   359\u2192    }\n   360\u2192  }\n   361\u2192  void doTrailers() override;\n   362\u2192  bool hasTrailers() override;\n   363\u2192\n   364\u2192  // Http::StreamEncoderFilterCallbacks\n   365\u2192  void addEncodedData(Buffer::Instance& data, bool streaming) override;\n   366\u2192  void injectEncodedDataToFilterChain(Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream) override;\n   367\u2192  ResponseTrailerMap& addEncodedTrailers() override;\n   368\u2192  void addEncodedMetadata(MetadataMapPtr&& metadata_map) override;\n   369\u2192  void onEncoderFilterAboveWriteBufferHighWatermark() override;\n   370\u2192  void onEncoderFilterBelowWriteBufferLowWatermark() override;\n   371\u2192  void setEncoderBufferLimit(uint32_t limit) override;\n   372\u2192  uint32_t encoderBufferLimit() override;\n   373\u2192  void continueEncoding() override;\n   374\u2192  const Buffer::Instance* encodingBuffer() override;\n   375\u2192  void modifyEncodingBuffer(std::function<void(Buffer::Instance&)> callback) override;\n   376\u2192  void sendLocalReply(Code code, absl::string_view body,\n   377\u2192                      std::function<void(ResponseHeaderMap& headers)> modify_headers,\n   378\u2192                      const absl::optional<Grpc::Status::GrpcStatus> grpc_status,\n   379\u2192                      absl::string_view details) override;\n   380\u2192\n   381\u2192  void responseDataTooLarge();\n   382\u2192  void responseDataDrained();\n   383\u2192  StreamEncoderFilters::Iterator entry() const { return entry_; }\n   384\u2192\n   385\u2192  StreamEncoderFilterSharedPtr handle_;\n   386\u2192  StreamEncoderFilters::Iterator entry_{};\n   387\u2192};\n   388\u2192\n   389\u2192/**\n   390\u2192 * Callbacks invoked by the FilterManager to pass filter data/events back to the caller.\n   391\u2192 */\n   392\u2192class FilterManagerCallbacks {\n   393\u2192public:\n   394\u2192  virtual ~FilterManagerCallbacks() = default;\n   395\u2192\n   396\u2192  /**\n   397\u2192   * Called when the provided headers have been encoded by all the filters in the chain.\n   398\u2192   * @param response_headers the encoded headers.\n   399\u2192   * @param end_stream whether this is a header only response.\n   400\u2192   */\n   401\u2192  virtual void encodeHeaders(ResponseHeaderMap& response_headers, bool end_stream) PURE;\n   402\u2192\n   403\u2192  /**\n   404\u2192   * Called when the provided 100 Continue headers have been encoded by all the filters in the\n   405\u2192   * chain.\n   406\u2192   * @param response_headers the encoded headers.\n   407\u2192   */\n   408\u2192  virtual void encode1xxHeaders(ResponseHeaderMap& response_headers) PURE;\n   409\u2192\n   410\u2192  /**\n   411\u2192   * Called when the provided data has been encoded by all filters in the chain.\n   412\u2192   * @param data the encoded data.\n   413\u2192   * @param end_stream whether this is the end of the response.\n   414\u2192   */\n   415\u2192  virtual void encodeData(Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream) PURE;\n   416\u2192\n   417\u2192  /**\n   418\u2192   * Called when the provided trailers have been encoded by all filters in the chain.\n   419\u2192   * @param trailers the encoded trailers.\n   420\u2192   */\n   421\u2192  virtual void encodeTrailers(ResponseTrailerMap& trailers) PURE;\n   422\u2192\n   423\u2192  /**\n   424\u2192   * Called when the provided metadata has been encoded by all filters in the chain.\n   425\u2192   * @param trailers the encoded trailers.\n   426\u2192   */\n   427\u2192  virtual void encodeMetadata(MetadataMapPtr&& metadata) PURE;\n   428\u2192\n   429\u2192  /**\n   430\u2192   * Injects request trailers into a stream that originally did not have request trailers.\n   431\u2192   */\n   432\u2192  virtual void setRequestTrailers(RequestTrailerMapPtr&& request_trailers) PURE;\n   433\u2192\n   434\u2192  /**\n   435\u2192   * Passes ownership of received informational headers to the parent. This may be called multiple\n   436\u2192   * times in the case of multiple upstream calls.\n   437\u2192   */\n   438\u2192  virtual void setInformationalHeaders(ResponseHeaderMapPtr&& response_headers) PURE;\n   439\u2192\n   440\u2192  /**\n   441\u2192   * Passes ownership of received response headers to the parent. This may be called multiple times\n   442\u2192   * in the case of multiple upstream calls.\n   443\u2192   */\n   444\u2192  virtual void setResponseHeaders(ResponseHeaderMapPtr&& response_headers) PURE;\n   445\u2192\n   446\u2192  /**\n   447\u2192   * Passes ownership of received response trailers to the parent. This may be called multiple times\n   448\u2192   * in the case of multiple upstream calls.\n   449\u2192   */\n   450\u2192  virtual void setResponseTrailers(ResponseTrailerMapPtr&& response_trailers) PURE;\n   451\u2192\n   452\u2192  /**\n   453\u2192   * Optionally updates response code stats based on the details in the headers.\n   454\u2192   */\n   455\u2192  virtual void chargeStats(const ResponseHeaderMap& /*headers*/) {}\n   456\u2192\n   457\u2192  // TODO(snowp): We should consider moving filter access to headers/trailers to happen via the\n   458\u2192  // callbacks instead of via the encode/decode callbacks on the filters.\n   459\u2192\n   460\u2192  /**\n   461\u2192   * The downstream request headers if set.\n   462\u2192   */\n   463\u2192  virtual RequestHeaderMapOptRef requestHeaders() PURE;\n   464\u2192\n   465\u2192  /**\n   466\u2192   * The downstream request trailers if present.\n   467\u2192   */\n   468\u2192  virtual RequestTrailerMapOptRef requestTrailers() PURE;\n   469\u2192\n   470\u2192  /**\n   471\u2192   * Retrieves a pointer to the continue headers set via the call to setInformationalHeaders.\n   472\u2192   */\n   473\u2192  virtual ResponseHeaderMapOptRef informationalHeaders() PURE;\n   474\u2192\n   475\u2192  /**\n   476\u2192   * Retrieves a pointer to the response headers set via the last call to setResponseHeaders.\n   477\u2192   * Note that response headers might be set multiple times (e.g. if a local reply is issued after\n   478\u2192   * headers have been received but before headers have been encoded), so it is not safe in general\n   479\u2192   * to assume that any set of headers will be valid for the duration of a stream.\n   480\u2192   */\n   481\u2192  virtual ResponseHeaderMapOptRef responseHeaders() PURE;\n   482\u2192\n   483\u2192  /**\n   484\u2192   * Retrieves a pointer to the last response trailers set via setResponseTrailers.\n   485\u2192   * Note that response trailers might be set multiple times, so it is not safe in general to assume\n   486\u2192   * that any set of trailers will be valid for the duration of the stream.\n   487\u2192   */\n   488\u2192  virtual ResponseTrailerMapOptRef responseTrailers() PURE;\n   489\u2192\n   490\u2192  /**\n   491\u2192   * Called after encoding has completed.\n   492\u2192   */\n   493\u2192  virtual void endStream() PURE;\n   494\u2192\n   495\u2192  /**\n   496\u2192   * Attempt to send GOAWAY and close the connection.\n   497\u2192   */\n   498\u2192  virtual void sendGoAwayAndClose() PURE;\n   499\u2192\n   500\u2192  /**\n   501\u2192   * Called when the stream write buffer is no longer above the low watermark.\n   502\u2192   */\n   503\u2192  virtual void onDecoderFilterBelowWriteBufferLowWatermark() PURE;\n   504\u2192\n   505\u2192  /**\n   506\u2192   * Called when the stream write buffer is above above the high watermark.\n   507\u2192   */\n   508\u2192  virtual void onDecoderFilterAboveWriteBufferHighWatermark() PURE;\n   509\u2192\n   510\u2192  /**\n   511\u2192   * Called when request activity indicates that the request timeout should be disarmed.\n   512\u2192   */\n   513\u2192  virtual void disarmRequestTimeout() PURE;\n   514\u2192\n   515\u2192  /**\n   516\u2192   * Called when stream activity indicates that the stream idle timeout should be reset.\n   517\u2192   */\n   518\u2192  virtual void resetIdleTimer() PURE;\n   519\u2192\n   520\u2192  /**\n   521\u2192   * Called when the stream should be re-created, e.g. for an internal redirect.\n   522\u2192   */\n   523\u2192  virtual void recreateStream(StreamInfo::FilterStateSharedPtr filter_state) PURE;\n   524\u2192\n   525\u2192  /**\n   526\u2192   * Called when the stream should be reset.\n   527\u2192   */\n   528\u2192  virtual void\n   529\u2192  resetStream(Http::StreamResetReason reset_reason = Http::StreamResetReason::LocalReset,\n   530\u2192              absl::string_view transport_failure_reason = \"\") PURE;\n   531\u2192\n   532\u2192  /**\n   533\u2192   * Returns the upgrade map for the current route entry.\n   534\u2192   */\n   535\u2192  virtual const Router::RouteEntry::UpgradeMap* upgradeMap() PURE;\n   536\u2192\n   537\u2192  /**\n   538\u2192   * Returns the cluster info for the current route entry.\n   539\u2192   */\n   540\u2192  virtual Upstream::ClusterInfoConstSharedPtr clusterInfo() PURE;\n   541\u2192\n   542\u2192  /**\n   543\u2192   * Returns the current active span.\n   544\u2192   */\n   545\u2192  virtual Tracing::Span& activeSpan() PURE;\n   546\u2192\n   547\u2192  // TODO(snowp): It might make more sense to pass (optional?) counters to the FM instead of\n   548\u2192  // calling back out to the AS to record them.\n   549\u2192  /**\n   550\u2192   * Called when a stream fails due to the response data being too large.\n   551\u2192   */\n   552\u2192  virtual void onResponseDataTooLarge() PURE;\n   553\u2192\n   554\u2192  /**\n   555\u2192   * Called when a stream fails due to the request data being too large.\n   556\u2192   */\n   557\u2192  virtual void onRequestDataTooLarge() PURE;\n   558\u2192\n   559\u2192  /**\n   560\u2192   * Returns the Http1StreamEncoderOptions associated with the response encoder.\n   561\u2192   */\n   562\u2192  virtual Http1StreamEncoderOptionsOptRef http1StreamEncoderOptions() PURE;\n   563\u2192\n   564\u2192  /**\n   565\u2192   * Returns the UpstreamStreamFilterCallbacks for upstream HTTP filters.\n   566\u2192   */\n   567\u2192  virtual OptRef<UpstreamStreamFilterCallbacks> upstreamCallbacks() { return {}; }\n   568\u2192\n   569\u2192  /**\n   570\u2192   * Called when a local reply is made by the filter manager.\n   571\u2192   * @param code the response code of the local reply.\n   572\u2192   */\n   573\u2192  virtual void onLocalReply(Code code) PURE;\n   574\u2192\n   575\u2192  /**\n   576\u2192   * Returns the tracing configuration to use for this stream.\n   577\u2192   */\n   578\u2192  virtual OptRef<const Tracing::Config> tracingConfig() const PURE;\n   579\u2192\n   580\u2192  /**\n   581\u2192   * Returns the tracked scope to use for this stream.\n   582\u2192   */\n   583\u2192  virtual const ScopeTrackedObject& scope() PURE;\n   584\u2192\n   585\u2192  /**\n   586\u2192   * Returns the DownstreamStreamFilterCallbacks for downstream HTTP filters.\n   587\u2192   */\n   588\u2192  virtual OptRef<DownstreamStreamFilterCallbacks> downstreamCallbacks() { return {}; }\n   589\u2192  /**\n   590\u2192   * Returns if close from the upstream is to be handled with half-close semantics.\n   591\u2192   * This is used for HTTP/1.1 codec.\n   592\u2192   */\n   593\u2192  virtual bool isHalfCloseEnabled() PURE;\n   594\u2192};\n   595\u2192\n   596\u2192/**\n   597\u2192 * This class allows the remote address to be overridden for HTTP stream info. This is used for\n   598\u2192 * XFF handling. This is required to avoid providing stream info with a non-const connection info\n   599\u2192 * provider. Private inheritance from ConnectionInfoProvider is used to make sure users get the\n   600\u2192 * address provider via the normal getter.\n   601\u2192 */\n   602\u2192class OverridableRemoteConnectionInfoSetterStreamInfo : public StreamInfo::StreamInfoImpl,\n   603\u2192                                                        private Network::ConnectionInfoProvider {\n   604\u2192public:\n   605\u2192  using StreamInfoImpl::StreamInfoImpl;\n   606\u2192\n   607\u2192  void setDownstreamRemoteAddress(\n   608\u2192      const Network::Address::InstanceConstSharedPtr& downstream_remote_address) {\n   609\u2192    // TODO(rgs1): we should assert overridden_downstream_remote_address_ is nullptr,\n   610\u2192    // but we are currently relaxing this as a workaround to:\n   611\u2192    //\n   612\u2192    // https://github.com/envoyproxy/envoy/pull/14432#issuecomment-758167614\n   613\u2192    overridden_downstream_remote_address_ = downstream_remote_address;\n   614\u2192  }\n   615\u2192\n   616\u2192  // StreamInfo::StreamInfo\n   617\u2192  const Network::ConnectionInfoProvider& downstreamAddressProvider() const override {\n   618\u2192    return *this;\n   619\u2192  }\n   620\u2192\n   621\u2192  // Network::ConnectionInfoProvider\n   622\u2192  const Network::Address::InstanceConstSharedPtr& localAddress() const override {\n   623\u2192    return StreamInfoImpl::downstreamAddressProvider().localAddress();\n   624\u2192  }\n   625\u2192  const Network::Address::InstanceConstSharedPtr& directLocalAddress() const override {\n   626\u2192    return StreamInfoImpl::downstreamAddressProvider().directLocalAddress();\n   627\u2192  }\n   628\u2192  bool localAddressRestored() const override {\n   629\u2192    return StreamInfoImpl::downstreamAddressProvider().localAddressRestored();\n   630\u2192  }\n   631\u2192  const Network::Address::InstanceConstSharedPtr& remoteAddress() const override {\n   632\u2192    return overridden_downstream_remote_address_ != nullptr\n   633\u2192               ? overridden_downstream_remote_address_\n   634\u2192               : StreamInfoImpl::downstreamAddressProvider().remoteAddress();\n   635\u2192  }\n   636\u2192  const Network::Address::InstanceConstSharedPtr& directRemoteAddress() const override {\n   637\u2192    return StreamInfoImpl::downstreamAddressProvider().directRemoteAddress();\n   638\u2192  }\n   639\u2192  absl::string_view requestedServerName() const override {\n   640\u2192    return StreamInfoImpl::downstreamAddressProvider().requestedServerName();\n   641\u2192  }\n   642\u2192  absl::optional<uint64_t> connectionID() const override {\n   643\u2192    return StreamInfoImpl::downstreamAddressProvider().connectionID();\n   644\u2192  }\n   645\u2192  absl::optional<absl::string_view> interfaceName() const override {\n   646\u2192    return StreamInfoImpl::downstreamAddressProvider().interfaceName();\n   647\u2192  }\n   648\u2192  Ssl::ConnectionInfoConstSharedPtr sslConnection() const override {\n   649\u2192    return StreamInfoImpl::downstreamAddressProvider().sslConnection();\n   650\u2192  }\n   651\u2192  void dumpState(std::ostream& os, int indent_level) const override {\n   652\u2192    StreamInfoImpl::dumpState(os, indent_level);\n   653\u2192\n   654\u2192    const char* spaces = spacesForLevel(indent_level);\n   655\u2192    os << spaces << \"OverridableRemoteConnectionInfoSetterStreamInfo \" << this\n   656\u2192       << DUMP_MEMBER_AS(remoteAddress(), remoteAddress()->asStringView())\n   657\u2192       << DUMP_MEMBER_AS(directRemoteAddress(), directRemoteAddress()->asStringView())\n   658\u2192       << DUMP_MEMBER_AS(localAddress(), localAddress()->asStringView()) << \"\\n\";\n   659\u2192  }\n   660\u2192  absl::string_view ja3Hash() const override {\n   661\u2192    return StreamInfoImpl::downstreamAddressProvider().ja3Hash();\n   662\u2192  }\n   663\u2192  const absl::optional<std::chrono::milliseconds>& roundTripTime() const override {\n   664\u2192    return StreamInfoImpl::downstreamAddressProvider().roundTripTime();\n   665\u2192  }\n   666\u2192  OptRef<const Network::FilterChainInfo> filterChainInfo() const override {\n   667\u2192    return StreamInfoImpl::downstreamAddressProvider().filterChainInfo();\n   668\u2192  }\n   669\u2192  OptRef<const Network::ListenerInfo> listenerInfo() const override {\n   670\u2192    return StreamInfoImpl::downstreamAddressProvider().listenerInfo();\n   671\u2192  }\n   672\u2192\n   673\u2192private:\n   674\u2192  Network::Address::InstanceConstSharedPtr overridden_downstream_remote_address_;\n   675\u2192};\n   676\u2192\n   677\u2192/**\n   678\u2192 * FilterManager manages decoding a request through a series of decoding filter and the encoding\n   679\u2192 * of the resulting response.\n   680\u2192 */\n   681\u2192class FilterManager : public ScopeTrackedObject,\n   682\u2192                      public FilterChainManager,\n   683\u2192                      Logger::Loggable<Logger::Id::http> {\n   684\u2192public:\n   685\u2192  FilterManager(FilterManagerCallbacks& filter_manager_callbacks, Event::Dispatcher& dispatcher,\n   686\u2192                OptRef<const Network::Connection> connection, uint64_t stream_id,\n   687\u2192                Buffer::BufferMemoryAccountSharedPtr account, bool proxy_100_continue,\n   688\u2192                uint32_t buffer_limit)\n   689\u2192      : filter_manager_callbacks_(filter_manager_callbacks), dispatcher_(dispatcher),\n   690\u2192        connection_(connection), stream_id_(stream_id), account_(std::move(account)),\n   691\u2192        proxy_100_continue_(proxy_100_continue), buffer_limit_(buffer_limit) {}\n   692\u2192\n   693\u2192  ~FilterManager() override {\n   694\u2192    ASSERT(state_.destroyed_);\n   695\u2192    ASSERT(state_.filter_call_state_ == 0);\n   696\u2192  }\n   697\u2192\n   698\u2192  // ScopeTrackedObject\n   699\u2192  OptRef<const StreamInfo::StreamInfo> trackedStream() const override { return streamInfo(); }\n   700\u2192  void dumpState(std::ostream& os, int indent_level = 0) const override {\n   701\u2192    const char* spaces = spacesForLevel(indent_level);\n   702\u2192    os << spaces << \"FilterManager \" << this << DUMP_MEMBER(state_.has_1xx_headers_)\n   703\u2192       << DUMP_MEMBER(state_.decoder_filter_chain_complete_)\n   704\u2192       << DUMP_MEMBER(state_.encoder_filter_chain_complete_)\n   705\u2192       << DUMP_MEMBER(state_.observed_decode_end_stream_)\n   706\u2192       << DUMP_MEMBER(state_.observed_encode_end_stream_) << \"\\n\";\n   707\u2192\n   708\u2192    DUMP_DETAILS(filter_manager_callbacks_.requestHeaders());\n   709\u2192    DUMP_DETAILS(filter_manager_callbacks_.requestTrailers());\n   710\u2192    DUMP_DETAILS(filter_manager_callbacks_.responseHeaders());\n   711\u2192    DUMP_DETAILS(filter_manager_callbacks_.responseTrailers());\n   712\u2192    DUMP_DETAILS(&streamInfo());\n   713\u2192  }\n   714\u2192\n   715\u2192  // FilterChainManager\n   716\u2192  void applyFilterFactoryCb(FilterContext context, FilterFactoryCb& factory) override;\n   717\u2192\n   718\u2192  void log(const Formatter::HttpFormatterContext log_context) {\n   719\u2192    for (const auto& log_handler : access_log_handlers_) {\n   720\u2192      log_handler->log(log_context, streamInfo());\n   721\u2192    }\n   722\u2192  }\n   723\u2192\n   724\u2192  const AccessLog::InstanceSharedPtrVector& accessLogHandlers() { return access_log_handlers_; }\n   725\u2192\n   726\u2192  void onStreamComplete() {\n   727\u2192    for (auto filter : filters_) {\n   728\u2192      filter->onStreamComplete();\n   729\u2192    }\n   730\u2192  }\n   731\u2192\n   732\u2192  void destroyFilters() {\n   733\u2192    state_.destroyed_ = true;\n   734\u2192\n   735\u2192    for (auto filter : filters_) {\n   736\u2192      filter->onDestroy();\n   737\u2192    }\n   738\u2192  }\n   739\u2192\n   740\u2192  /**\n   741\u2192   * Decodes the provided headers starting at the first filter in the chain.\n   742\u2192   * @param headers the headers to decode.\n   743\u2192   * @param end_stream whether the request is header only.\n   744\u2192   */\n   745\u2192  void decodeHeaders(RequestHeaderMap& headers, bool end_stream) {\n   746\u2192    state_.observed_decode_end_stream_ = end_stream;\n   747\u2192    decodeHeaders(nullptr, headers, end_stream);\n   748\u2192  }\n   749\u2192\n   750\u2192  /**\n   751\u2192   * Decodes the provided data starting at the first filter in the chain.\n   752\u2192   * @param data the data to decode.\n   753\u2192   * @param end_stream whether this data is the end of the request.\n   754\u2192   */\n   755\u2192  void decodeData(Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream) {\n   756\u2192    state_.observed_decode_end_stream_ = end_stream;\n   757\u2192    decodeData(nullptr, data, end_stream, FilterIterationStartState::CanStartFromCurrent);\n   758\u2192  }\n   759\u2192\n   760\u2192  /**\n   761\u2192   * Decodes the provided trailers starting at the first filter in the chain.\n   762\u2192   * @param trailers the trailers to decode.\n   763\u2192   */\n   764\u2192  void decodeTrailers(RequestTrailerMap& trailers) {\n   765\u2192    state_.observed_decode_end_stream_ = true;\n   766\u2192    decodeTrailers(nullptr, trailers);\n   767\u2192  }\n   768\u2192\n   769\u2192  /**\n   770\u2192   * Decodes the provided metadata starting at the first filter in the chain.\n   771\u2192   * @param metadata_map the metadata to decode.\n   772\u2192   */\n   773\u2192  void decodeMetadata(MetadataMap& metadata_map) { decodeMetadata(nullptr, metadata_map); }\n   774\u2192\n   775\u2192  void disarmRequestTimeout();\n   776\u2192\n   777\u2192  /**\n   778\u2192   * If end_stream is true, marks encoding as complete. This is a noop if end_stream is false.\n   779\u2192   * @param end_stream whether encoding is complete.\n   780\u2192   */\n   781\u2192  void maybeEndEncode(bool end_stream);\n   782\u2192\n   783\u2192  /**\n   784\u2192   * If terminal_filter_decoded_end_stream is true, marks decoding as complete. This is a noop if\n   785\u2192   * terminal_filter_decoded_end_stream is false.\n   786\u2192   * @param end_stream whether decoding is complete.\n   787\u2192   */\n   788\u2192  void maybeEndDecode(bool terminal_filter_decoded_end_stream);\n   789\u2192\n   790\u2192  void checkAndCloseStreamIfFullyClosed();\n   791\u2192\n   792\u2192  virtual void sendLocalReply(Code code, absl::string_view body,\n   793\u2192                              const std::function<void(ResponseHeaderMap& headers)>& modify_headers,\n   794\u2192                              const absl::optional<Grpc::Status::GrpcStatus> grpc_status,\n   795\u2192                              absl::string_view details) PURE;\n   796\u2192\n   797\u2192  void resetStream(StreamResetReason reason, absl::string_view transport_failure_reason);\n   798\u2192\n   799\u2192  /**\n   800\u2192   * Executes a prepared, but not yet propagated, local reply.\n   801\u2192   * Prepared local replies can occur in the decoder filter chain iteration.\n   802\u2192   */\n   803\u2192  virtual void executeLocalReplyIfPrepared() PURE;\n   804\u2192\n   805\u2192  // Possibly increases buffer_limit_ to the value of limit.\n   806\u2192  void setBufferLimit(uint32_t limit);\n   807\u2192\n   808\u2192  /**\n   809\u2192   * @return bool whether any above high watermark triggers are currently active\n   810\u2192   */\n   811\u2192  bool aboveHighWatermark() { return high_watermark_count_ != 0; }\n   812\u2192\n   813\u2192  // Pass on watermark callbacks to watermark subscribers. This boils down to passing watermark\n   814\u2192  // events for this stream and the downstream connection to the router filter.\n   815\u2192  void callHighWatermarkCallbacks();\n   816\u2192  void callLowWatermarkCallbacks();\n   817\u2192\n   818\u2192  void requestHeadersInitialized() {\n   819\u2192    if (Http::Headers::get().MethodValues.Head ==\n   820\u2192        filter_manager_callbacks_.requestHeaders()->getMethodValue()) {\n   821\u2192      state_.is_head_request_ = true;\n   822\u2192    }\n   823\u2192    state_.is_grpc_request_ =\n   824\u2192        Grpc::Common::isGrpcRequestHeaders(filter_manager_callbacks_.requestHeaders().ref());\n   825\u2192  }\n   826\u2192\n   827\u2192  /**\n   828\u2192   * Marks local processing as complete.\n   829\u2192   * TODO(yanvlasov): deprecate and decommission this function.\n   830\u2192   */\n   831\u2192  void setLocalComplete() {\n   832\u2192    state_.observed_encode_end_stream_ = true;\n   833\u2192    state_.decoder_filter_chain_aborted_ = true;\n   834\u2192  }\n   835\u2192\n   836\u2192  /**\n   837\u2192   * Whether the filters have been destroyed.\n   838\u2192   */\n   839\u2192  bool destroyed() const { return state_.destroyed_; }\n   840\u2192\n   841\u2192  /**\n   842\u2192   * Whether remote processing has been marked as complete.\n   843\u2192   */\n   844\u2192  virtual bool decoderObservedEndStream() const { return state_.observed_decode_end_stream_; }\n   845\u2192\n   846\u2192  /**\n   847\u2192   * Instructs the FilterManager to not create a filter chain. This makes it possible to issue\n   848\u2192   * a local reply without the overhead of creating and traversing the filters.\n   849\u2192   */\n   850\u2192  void skipFilterChainCreation() {\n   851\u2192    ASSERT(!state_.create_chain_result_.created());\n   852\u2192    state_.create_chain_result_ = CreateChainResult(true);\n   853\u2192  }\n   854\u2192\n   855\u2192  virtual StreamInfo::StreamInfo& streamInfo() PURE;\n   856\u2192  virtual const StreamInfo::StreamInfo& streamInfo() const PURE;\n   857\u2192\n   858\u2192  enum class UpgradeResult : uint8_t { UpgradeUnneeded, UpgradeAccepted, UpgradeRejected };\n   859\u2192\n   860\u2192  /**\n   861\u2192   * Filter chain creation result.\n   862\u2192   */\n   863\u2192  class CreateChainResult {\n   864\u2192  public:\n   865\u2192    CreateChainResult() = default;\n   866\u2192\n   867\u2192    /**\n   868\u2192     * @param created whether the filter chain was created.\n   869\u2192     * @param upgrade the upgrade result.\n   870\u2192     */\n   871\u2192    CreateChainResult(bool created, UpgradeResult upgrade = UpgradeResult::UpgradeUnneeded)\n   872\u2192        : created_(created), upgrade_(upgrade) {}\n   873\u2192\n   874\u2192    /**\n   875\u2192     * @return whether the filter chain was created.\n   876\u2192     */\n   877\u2192    bool created() const { return created_; }\n   878\u2192    /**\n   879\u2192     * @return whether the upgrade was accepted.\n   880\u2192     */\n   881\u2192    bool upgradeAccepted() const { return upgrade_ == UpgradeResult::UpgradeAccepted; }\n   882\u2192    /**\n   883\u2192     * @return whether the upgrade was rejected.\n   884\u2192     */\n   885\u2192    bool upgradeRejected() const { return upgrade_ == UpgradeResult::UpgradeRejected; }\n   886\u2192\n   887\u2192  private:\n   888\u2192    bool created_ = false;\n   889\u2192    UpgradeResult upgrade_ = UpgradeResult::UpgradeUnneeded;\n   890\u2192  };\n   891\u2192\n   892\u2192  /**\n   893\u2192   * Set up the Encoder/Decoder filter chain.\n   894\u2192   * @param filter_chain_factory the factory to create the filter chain.\n   895\u2192   */\n   896\u2192  CreateChainResult createFilterChain(const FilterChainFactory& filter_chain_factory);\n   897\u2192\n   898\u2192  OptRef<const Network::Connection> connection() const { return connection_; }\n   899\u2192\n   900\u2192  uint64_t streamId() const { return stream_id_; }\n   901\u2192  Buffer::BufferMemoryAccountSharedPtr account() const { return account_; }\n   902\u2192\n   903\u2192  Buffer::InstancePtr& bufferedRequestData() { return buffered_request_data_; }\n   904\u2192\n   905\u2192  void contextOnContinue(ScopeTrackedObjectStack& tracked_object_stack);\n   906\u2192\n   907\u2192  void onDownstreamReset() { state_.saw_downstream_reset_ = true; }\n   908\u2192  bool sawDownstreamReset() { return state_.saw_downstream_reset_; }\n   909\u2192\n   910\u2192  virtual bool shouldLoadShed() { return false; };\n   911\u2192\n   912\u2192  void sendGoAwayAndClose() {\n   913\u2192    // Stop filter chain iteration by checking encoder or decoder chain.\n   914\u2192    if (state_.filter_call_state_ & FilterCallState::IsDecodingMask) {\n   915\u2192      state_.decoder_filter_chain_aborted_ = true;\n   916\u2192    } else if (state_.filter_call_state_ & FilterCallState::IsEncodingMask) {\n   917\u2192      state_.encoder_filter_chain_aborted_ = true;\n   918\u2192    }\n   919\u2192    filter_manager_callbacks_.sendGoAwayAndClose();\n   920\u2192  }\n   921\u2192\n   922\u2192protected:\n   923\u2192  struct State {\n   924\u2192    State() = default;\n   925\u2192    uint32_t filter_call_state_{0};\n   926\u2192\n   927\u2192    // Set after decoder filter chain has completed iteration. Prevents further calls to decoder\n   928\u2192    // filters. This flag is used to determine stream completion when the independent half-close is\n   929\u2192    // enabled.\n   930\u2192    bool decoder_filter_chain_complete_{};\n   931\u2192\n   932\u2192    // Set after encoder filter chain has completed iteration. Prevents further calls to encoder\n   933\u2192    // filters. This flag is used to determine stream completion when the independent half-close is\n   934\u2192    // enabled.\n   935\u2192    bool encoder_filter_chain_complete_{};\n   936\u2192\n   937\u2192    // Set `true` when the filter manager observes end stream on the decoder path (from downstream\n   938\u2192    // client) before iteration of the decoder filter chain begins. This flag is used for setting\n   939\u2192    // end_stream value when resuming decoder filter chain iteration.\n   940\u2192    bool observed_decode_end_stream_{};\n   941\u2192    // Set `true` when the filter manager observes end stream on the encoder path (from upstream\n   942\u2192    // server or Envoy's local reply) before iteration of the encoder filter chain begins. This flag\n   943\u2192    // is used for setting end_stream value when resuming encoder filter chain iteration.\n   944\u2192    bool observed_encode_end_stream_{};\n   945\u2192\n   946\u2192    // By default, we will assume there are no 1xx. If encode1xxHeaders\n   947\u2192    // is ever called, this is set to true so commonContinue resumes processing the 1xx.\n   948\u2192    bool has_1xx_headers_{};\n   949\u2192    // These two are latched on initial header read, to determine if the original headers\n   950\u2192    // constituted a HEAD or gRPC request, respectively.\n   951\u2192    bool is_head_request_{};\n   952\u2192    bool is_grpc_request_{};\n   953\u2192    // Tracks if headers other than 100-Continue have been encoded to the codec.\n   954\u2192    bool non_100_response_headers_encoded_{};\n   955\u2192    // True under the stack of onLocalReply, false otherwise.\n   956\u2192    bool under_on_local_reply_{};\n   957\u2192    // True when the filter chain iteration was aborted with local reply.\n   958\u2192    bool decoder_filter_chain_aborted_{};\n   959\u2192    bool encoder_filter_chain_aborted_{};\n   960\u2192    bool saw_downstream_reset_{};\n   961\u2192    // True when the stream was recreated.\n   962\u2192    bool recreated_stream_{};\n   963\u2192\n   964\u2192    // The following 3 members are booleans rather than part of the space-saving bitfield as they\n   965\u2192    // are passed as arguments to functions expecting bools. Extend State using the bitfield\n   966\u2192    // where possible.\n   967\u2192    bool encoder_filters_streaming_{true};\n   968\u2192    bool decoder_filters_streaming_{true};\n   969\u2192    bool destroyed_{false};\n   970\u2192\n   971\u2192    // Result of filter chain creation.\n   972\u2192    CreateChainResult create_chain_result_{};\n   973\u2192\n   974\u2192    // Used to track which filter is the latest filter that has received data.\n   975\u2192    ActiveStreamEncoderFilter* latest_data_encoding_filter_{};\n   976\u2192    ActiveStreamDecoderFilter* latest_data_decoding_filter_{};\n   977\u2192  };\n   978\u2192\n   979\u2192  State& state() { return state_; }\n   980\u2192\n   981\u2192private:\n   982\u2192  friend class This state is only used\n  1148\u2192      // to verify we do not encode1xx headers more than once per\n  1149\u2192      // filter.\n  1150\u2192      static constexpr uint32_t Encode1xxHeaders  = 0x100;\n  1151\u2192      // Used to indicate that one of the following conditions is true:\n  1152\u2192      // 1. The filter manager is processing the final [En|De]codeData frame.\n  1153\u2192      // 2. The filter manager is in [En|De]codeHeaders and is processing a\n  1154\u2192      //    header-only request or response.\n  1155\u2192      static constexpr uint32_t EndOfStream = 0x200;\n  1156\u2192\n  1157\u2192      // Masks for filter call state.\n  1158\u2192      static constexpr uint32_t IsDecodingMask = DecodeHeaders | DecodeData | DecodeMetadata | DecodeTrailers;\n  1159\u2192 1163\u2192  State state_;\n  1164\u2192};\n  1165\u2192\n  1166\u2192// The DownstreamFilterManager has explicit handling to send local replies.\n  1167\u2192// The UpstreamFilterManager will not, and overload_manager)\n  1180\u2192      : FilterManager(filter_manager_callbacks, dispatcher, connection, stream_id, account,\n  1181\u2192                      proxy_100_continue, buffer_limit),\n  1182\u2192        stream_info_(protocol, time_source, connection.connectionInfoProviderSharedPtr(),\n  1183\u2192                     StreamInfo::FilterState::LifeSpan::FilterChain,\n  1184\u2192 1190\u2192    ENVOY_LOG_ONCE_IF(\n  1191\u2192        trace, downstream_filter_load_shed_point_ == nullptr,\n  1192\u2192        \"LoadShedPoint envoy.load_shed_points.http_downstream_filter_check is not found. \"\n  1193\u2192        \"Is it configured?\");\n  1194\u2192  }\n  1195\u2192  ~DownstreamFilterManager() override {\n  1196\u2192    ASSERT(prepared_local_reply_ == nullptr,\n  1197\u2192           \"Filter Manager destroyed without executing prepared local reply\");\n  1198\u2192  }\n  1199\u2192\n  1200\u2192  // TODO(snowp): This should probably return a StreamInfo instead of the impl.\n  1201\u2192  StreamInfo::StreamInfoImpl& streamInfo() override { return stream_info_; }\n  1202\u2192  const StreamInfo::StreamInfoImpl& streamInfo() const override { return stream_info_; }\n 1212\u2192   * Called before local reply is made by the filter manager.\n  1213\u2192   * @param data the data associated with the local reply.\n  1214\u2192   */\n  1215\u2192  void onLocalReply(StreamFilterBase::LocalReplyData& data);\n  1216\u2192\n  1217\u2192  void sendLocalReply(Code code, absl::string_view body,\n  1218\u2192                      const std::function<void(ResponseHeaderMap& headers)>& modify_headers,\n  1219\u2192                      const absl::optional<Grpc::Status::GrpcStatus> grpc_status,\n  1220\u2192                      absl::string_view details) override;\n  1221\u2192\n  1222\u2192  /**\n  1223\u2192   * Whether downstream has observed end_stream.\n  1224\u2192   */\n  1225\u2192  bool decoderObservedEndStream() const override {\n  1226\u2192    // Set by the envoy.reloadable_features.use_filter_manager_state_for_downstream_end_stream\n  1227\u2192    // runtime flag.\n  1228\u2192    if (use_filter_manager_state_for_downstream_end_stream_) {\n  1229\u2192      return state_.observed_decode_end_stream_;\n  1230\u2192    }\n  1231\u2192\n  1232\u2192    return hasLastDownstreamByteReceived();\n  1233\u2192  }\n  1234\u2192\n  1235\u2192  /**\n  1236\u2192   * Return true if the timestamp of the downstream end_stream was recorded.\n  1237\u2192   * For the HCM to handle the case of internal redirects, timeout error replies\n  1238\u2192   * and stream resets on premature upstream response.\n  1239\u2192   */\n  1240\u2192  bool hasLastDownstreamByteReceived() const {\n  1241\u2192    return streamInfo().downstreamTiming() &&\n  1242\u2192 downstream_filter_load_shed_point_ != nullptr &&\n  1247\u2192           downstream_filter_load_shed_point_->shouldShedLoad();\n  1248\u2192  }\n  1249\u2192\n  1250\u2192private:\n  1251\u2192  /**\n  1252\u2192   * Sends a local reply by constructing a response and passing it through all the encoder\n  1253\u2192   * filters. The resulting response will be passed out via the FilterManagerCallbacks.\n  1254\u2192   * This will be deprecated in favor of prepareLocalReplyViaFilterChain.\n  1255\u2192   */\n  1256\u2192  void sendLocalReplyViaFilterChain(\n  1257\u2192      bool is_grpc_request, Code code, absl::string_view body,\n  1258\u2192      const std::function<void(ResponseHeaderMap& headers)>& modify_headers, bool is_head_request,\n  1259\u2192      const absl::optional<Grpc::Status::GrpcStatus> grpc_status, absl::string_view details);\n  1260\u2192\n  1261\u2192  /**\n  1262\u2192   * Prepares a local reply that will be sent along the encoder filters in\n  1263\u2192   * executeLocalReplyViaFilterChain.\n  1264\u2192   */\n  1265\u2192  void prepareLocalReplyViaFilterChain(\n  1266\u2192      bool is_grpc_request, Code code, absl::string_view body,\n  1267\u2192      const std::function<void(ResponseHeaderMap& headers)>& modify_headers, bool is_head_request,\n  1268\u2192      const absl::optional<Grpc::Status::GrpcStatus> grpc_status, absl::string_view details);\n  1269\u2192\n  1270\u2192  /**\n  1271\u2192   * Executes a prepared local reply along the encoder filters.\n  1272\u2192   */\n  1273\u2192  void executeLocalReplyIfPrepared() override;\n  1274\u2192\n  1275\u2192  /**\n  1276\u2192   * Sends a local reply by constructing a response and skipping the encoder filters.
