ConnectionManagerTracingStats& tracing_stats);\n    80\u2192  static ConnectionManagerListenerStats generateListenerStats(const std::string& prefix,\n    81\u2192                                                              Stats::Scope& scope);\n    82\u2192  static const ResponseHeaderMap& continueHeader();\n    83\u2192\n    84\u2192  // Currently the ConnectionManager creates a codec lazily when either:\n    85\u2192  //   a) onConnection for H3.\n    86\u2192  //   b) onData for H1 and H2.\n    87\u2192  // With the introduction of ApiListeners, neither event occurs. This function allows consumer code\n    88\u2192  // to manually create a codec.\n    89\u2192  // TODO(junr03): consider passing a synthetic codec instead of creating once. The codec in the\n    90\u2192  // ApiListener case is solely used to determine the protocol version.\n    91\u2192  void createCodec(Buffer::Instance& data);\n    92\u2192\n    93\u2192  // Network::ReadFilter\n    94\u2192  Network::FilterStatus onData(Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream) override;\n    95\u2192  Network::FilterStatus onNewConnection() override;\n    96\u2192  void initializeReadFilterCallbacks(Network::ReadFilterCallbacks& callbacks) override;\n    97\u2192\n    98\u2192  // Http::ConnectionCallbacks\n    99\u2192  void onGoAway(GoAwayErrorCode error_code) override;\n   100\u2192\n   101\u2192  // Http::ServerConnectionCallbacks\n   102\u2192  RequestDecoder& newStream(ResponseEncoder& response_encoder,\n   103\u2192                            bool is_internally_created = false) override;\n   104\u2192\n   105\u2192  RequestDecoderHandlePtr newStreamHandle(ResponseEncoder& response_encoder,\n   106\u2192                                          bool is_internally_created = false) override;\n   107\u2192\n   108\u2192  // Network::ConnectionCallbacks\n   109\u2192  void onEvent(Network::ConnectionEvent event) override;\n   110\u2192  // Pass connection watermark events on to all the streams associated with that connection.\n   111\u2192  void onAboveWriteBufferHighWatermark() override {\n   112\u2192    codec_->onUnderlyingConnectionAboveWriteBufferHighWatermark();\n   113\u2192  }\n   114\u2192  void onBelowWriteBufferLowWatermark() override {\n   115\u2192    codec_->onUnderlyingConnectionBelowWriteBufferLowWatermark();\n   116\u2192  }\n   117\u2192\n   118\u2192  TimeSource& timeSource() { return time_source_; }\n   119\u2192\n   120\u2192  void setClearHopByHopResponseHeaders(bool value) { clear_hop_by_hop_response_headers_ = value; }\n   121\u2192  bool clearHopByHopResponseHeaders() const { return clear_hop_by_hop_response_headers_; }\n   122\u2192\n   123\u2192  // This runtime key configures the number of streams which must be closed on a connection before\n   124\u2192  // envoy will potentially drain a connection due to excessive prematurely reset streams.\n   125\u2192  static const absl::string_view PrematureResetTotalStreamCountKey;\n   126\u2192\n   127\u2192  // The minimum lifetime of a stream, in seconds, in order not to be considered\n   128\u2192  // prematurely closed.\n   129\u2192  static const absl::string_view PrematureResetMinStreamLifetimeSecondsKey;\n   130\u2192  static const absl::string_view MaxRequestsPerIoCycle;\n   131\u2192  static const absl::string_view OptionallyDelayClose;\n   132\u2192\n   133\u2192private:\n   134\u2192  struct ActiveStream;\n   135\u2192  class MobileConnectionManagerImpl;\n   136\u2192\n   137\u2192  /**\n   138\u2192   * Wraps a single active stream on the connection. These are either full request/response pairs\n   139\u2192   * or pushes.\n   140\u2192   */\n   141\u2192  struct ActiveStream final : LinkedObject<ActiveStream>,\n   142\u2192                              public Event::DeferredDeletable,\n   143\u2192                              public StreamCallbacks,\n   144\u2192                              public CodecEventCallbacks,\n   145\u2192                              public RequestDecoder,\n   146\u2192                              public Tracing::Config,\n   147\u2192                              public ScopeTrackedObject,\n   148\u2192                              public FilterManagerCallbacks,\n   149\u2192                              public DownstreamStreamFilterCallbacks,\n   150\u2192                              public RouteCache {\n   151\u2192    ActiveStream(ConnectionManagerImpl& connection_manager, uint32_t buffer_limit,\n   152\u2192                 Buffer::BufferMemoryAccountSharedPtr account);\n   153\u2192\n   154\u2192    // Event::DeferredDeletable\n   155\u2192    void deleteIsPending() override {\n   156\u2192      // The stream should not be accessed once deferred delete has been called.\n   157\u2192      still_alive_.reset();\n   158\u2192    }\n   159\u2192\n   160\u2192    void log(AccessLog::AccessLogType type);\n   161\u2192    void completeRequest();\n   162\u2192\n   163\u2192    const Network::Connection* connection();\n   164\u2192    uint64_t streamId() { return stream_id_; }\n   165\u2192\n   166\u2192    // Http::StreamCallbacks\n   167\u2192    void onResetStream(StreamResetReason reason,\n   168\u2192                       absl::string_view transport_failure_reason) override;\n   169\u2192    void onAboveWriteBufferHighWatermark() override;\n   170\u2192    void onBelowWriteBufferLowWatermark() override;\n   171\u2192\n   172\u2192    // Http::CodecEventCallbacks\n   173\u2192    void onCodecEncodeComplete() override;\n   174\u2192    void onCodecLowLevelReset() override;\n   175\u2192\n   176\u2192    // Http::StreamDecoder\n   177\u2192    void decodeData(Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream) override;\n   178\u2192    void decodeMetadata(MetadataMapPtr&&) override;\n   179\u2192\n   180\u2192    // Record the timestamp of last downstream byte is received.\n   181\u2192    void maybeRecordLastByteReceived(bool end_stream);\n   182\u2192\n   183\u2192    // Http::RequestDecoder\n   184\u2192    void decodeHeaders(RequestHeaderMapSharedPtr&& headers, bool end_stream) override;\n   185\u2192    void decodeTrailers(RequestTrailerMapPtr&& trailers) override;\n   186\u2192    StreamInfo::StreamInfo& streamInfo() override { return filter_manager_.streamInfo(); }\n   187\u2192    void sendLocalReply(Code code, absl::string_view body,\n   188\u2192                        const std::function<void(ResponseHeaderMap& headers)>& modify_headers,\n   189\u2192                        const absl::optional<Grpc::Status::GrpcStatus> grpc_status,\n   190\u2192                        absl::string_view details) override {\n   191\u2192      return filter_manager_.sendLocalReply(code, body, modify_headers, grpc_status, details);\n   192\u2192    }\n   193\u2192\n   194\u2192    void sendGoAwayAndClose() override { return connection_manager_.sendGoAwayAndClose(); }\n   195\u2192\n   196\u2192    AccessLog::InstanceSharedPtrVector accessLogHandlers() override {\n   197\u2192      const AccessLog::InstanceSharedPtrVector& config_log_handlers =\n   198\u2192          connection_manager_.config_->accessLogs();\n   199\u2192      const AccessLog::InstanceSharedPtrVector& filter_log_handlers =\n   200\u2192          filter_manager_.accessLogHandlers();\n   201\u2192\n   202\u2192      AccessLog::InstanceSharedPtrVector combined_log_handlers;\n   203\u2192      combined_log_handlers.reserve(config_log_handlers.size() + filter_log_handlers.size());\n   204\u2192\n   205\u2192      if (!Runtime::runtimeFeatureEnabled(\n   206\u2192              \"envoy.reloadable_features.filter_access_loggers_first\")) {\n   207\u2192        combined_log_handlers.insert(combined_log_handlers.end(), filter_log_handlers.begin(),\n   208\u2192                                     filter_log_handlers.end());\n   209\u2192        combined_log_handlers.insert(combined_log_handlers.end(), config_log_handlers.begin(),\n   210\u2192                                     config_log_handlers.end());\n   211\u2192\n   212\u2192      } else {\n   213\u2192        combined_log_handlers.insert(combined_log_handlers.end(), config_log_handlers.begin(),\n   214\u2192                                     config_log_handlers.end());\n   215\u2192        combined_log_handlers.insert(combined_log_handlers.end(), filter_log_handlers.begin(),\n   216\u2192                                     filter_log_handlers.end());\n   217\u2192      }\n   218\u2192      return combined_log_handlers;\n   219\u2192    }\n   220\u2192    // Hand off headers/trailers and stream info to the codec's response encoder, for logging later\n   221\u2192    // (i.e. possibly after this stream has been destroyed).\n   222\u2192    //\n   223\u2192    // TODO(paulsohn): Investigate whether we can move the headers/trailers and stream info required\n   224\u2192    // for logging instead of copying them (as is currently done in the HTTP/3 implementation) or\n   225\u2192    // using a shared pointer. See\n   226\u2192    // https://github.com/envoyproxy/envoy/pull/23648#discussion_r1066095564 for more details.\n   227\u2192    void deferHeadersAndTrailers() {\n   228\u2192      response_encoder_->setDeferredLoggingHeadersAndTrailers(request_headers_, response_headers_,\n   229\u2192                                                              response_trailers_, streamInfo());\n   230\u2192    }\n   231\u2192\n   232\u2192    // ScopeTrackedObject\n   233\u2192    OptRef<const StreamInfo::StreamInfo> trackedStream() const override {\n   234\u2192      return filter_manager_.trackedStream();\n   235\u2192    }\n   236\u2192    void dumpState(std::ostream& os, int indent_level = 0) const override {\n   237\u2192      const char* spaces = spacesForLevel(indent_level);\n   238\u2192      os << spaces << \"ActiveStream \" << this << DUMP_MEMBER(stream_id_);\n   239\u2192\n   240\u2192      DUMP_DETAILS(&filter_manager_);\n   241\u2192    }\n   242\u2192\n   243\u2192    // FilterManagerCallbacks\n   244\u2192    void encodeHeaders(ResponseHeaderMap& response_headers, bool end_stream) override;\n   245\u2192    void encode1xxHeaders(ResponseHeaderMap& response_headers) override;\n   246\u2192    void encodeData(Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream) override;\n   247\u2192    void encodeTrailers(ResponseTrailerMap& trailers) override;\n   248\u2192    void encodeMetadata(MetadataMapPtr&& metadata) override;\n   249\u2192    void setRequestTrailers(Http::RequestTrailerMapPtr&& request_trailers) override {\n   250\u2192      ASSERT(!request_trailers_);\n   251\u2192      request_trailers_ = std::move(request_trailers);\n   252\u2192    }\n   253\u2192    void setInformationalHeaders(Http::ResponseHeaderMapPtr&& informational_headers) override {\n   254\u2192      ASSERT(!informational_headers_);\n   255\u2192      informational_headers_ = std::move(informational_headers);\n   256\u2192    }\n   257\u2192    void setResponseHeaders(Http::ResponseHeaderMapPtr&& response_headers) override {\n   258\u2192      // We'll overwrite the headers in the case where we fail the stream after upstream headers\n   259\u2192      // have begun filter processing but before they have been sent downstream.\n   260\u2192      response_headers_ = std::move(response_headers);\n   261\u2192    }\n   262\u2192    void setResponseTrailers(Http::ResponseTrailerMapPtr&& response_trailers) override {\n   263\u2192      response_trailers_ = std::move(response_trailers);\n   264\u2192    }\n   265\u2192    void chargeStats(const ResponseHeaderMap& headers) override;\n   266\u2192\n   267\u2192    Http::RequestHeaderMapOptRef requestHeaders() override {\n   268\u2192      return makeOptRefFromPtr(request_headers_.get());\n   269\u2192    }\n   270\u2192    Http::RequestTrailerMapOptRef requestTrailers() override {\n   271\u2192      return makeOptRefFromPtr(request_trailers_.get());\n   272\u2192    }\n   273\u2192    Http::ResponseHeaderMapOptRef informationalHeaders() override {\n   274\u2192      return makeOptRefFromPtr(informational_headers_.get());\n   275\u2192    }\n   276\u2192    Http::ResponseHeaderMapOptRef responseHeaders() override {\n   277\u2192      return makeOptRefFromPtr(response_headers_.get());\n   278\u2192    }\n   279\u2192    Http::ResponseTrailerMapOptRef responseTrailers() override {\n   280\u2192      return makeOptRefFromPtr(response_trailers_.get());\n   281\u2192    }\n   282\u2192\n   283\u2192    void endStream() override {\n   284\u2192      ASSERT(!state_.codec_saw_local_complete_);\n   285\u2192      state_.codec_saw_local_complete_ = true;\n   286\u2192      connection_manager_.doEndStream(*this);\n   287\u2192    }\n   288\u2192    void onDecoderFilterBelowWriteBufferLowWatermark() override;\n   289\u2192    void onDecoderFilterAboveWriteBufferHighWatermark() override;\n   290\u2192    void disarmRequestTimeout() override;\n   291\u2192    void resetIdleTimer() override;\n   292\u2192    void recreateStream(StreamInfo::FilterStateSharedPtr filter_state) override;\n   293\u2192    void resetStream(Http::StreamResetReason reset_reason = Http::StreamResetReason::LocalReset,\n   294\u2192                     absl::string_view transport_failure_reason = \"\") override;\n   295\u2192    const Router::RouteEntry::UpgradeMap* upgradeMap() override;\n   296\u2192    Upstream::ClusterInfoConstSharedPtr clusterInfo() override;\n   297\u2192    Tracing::Span& activeSpan() override;\n   298\u2192    void onResponseDataTooLarge() override;\n   299\u2192    void onRequestDataTooLarge() override;\n   300\u2192    Http1StreamEncoderOptionsOptRef http1StreamEncoderOptions() override;\n   301\u2192    void onLocalReply(Code code) override;\n   302\u2192    OptRef<const Tracing::Config> tracingConfig() const override;\n   303\u2192    const ScopeTrackedObject& scope() override;\n   304\u2192    OptRef<DownstreamStreamFilterCallbacks> downstreamCallbacks() override { return *this; }\n   305\u2192    bool isHalfCloseEnabled() override { return connection_manager_.allow_upstream_half_close_; }\n   306\u2192\n   307\u2192    // DownstreamStreamFilterCallbacks\n   308\u2192    void setRoute(Router::RouteConstSharedPtr route) override;\n   309\u2192    Router::RouteConstSharedPtr route(const Router::RouteCallback& cb) override;\n   310\u2192    void clearRouteCache() override;\n   311\u2192    void requestRouteConfigUpdate(\n   312\u2192        Http::RouteConfigUpdatedCallbackSharedPtr route_config_updated_cb) override;\n   313\u2192\n   314\u2192    // Set cached route. This method should never be called directly. This is only called in the\n   315\u2192    // setRoute(), clearRouteCache(), and refreshCachedRoute() methods.\n   316\u2192    void setCachedRoute(absl::optional<Router::RouteConstSharedPtr>&& route);\n   317\u2192    // Block the route cache and clear the snapped route config. By doing this the route cache will\n   318\u2192    // not be updated. And if the route config is updated by the RDS, the snapped route config may\n   319\u2192    // be freed before the stream is destroyed.\n   320\u2192    // This will be called automatically at the end of handle response headers.\n   321\u2192    void blockRouteCache();\n   322\u2192    // Return true if the cached route is blocked.\n   323\u2192    bool routeCacheBlocked() const {\n   324\u2192      ENVOY_BUG(!route_cache_blocked_, \"Should never try to refresh or clear the route cache when \"\n   325\u2192                                       \"it is blocked!\");\n   326\u2192      return route_cache_blocked_;\n   327\u2192    }\n   328\u2192\n   329\u2192    absl::optional<Router::ConfigConstSharedPtr> routeConfig();\n   330\u2192    void traceRequest();\n   331\u2192\n   332\u2192    // Updates the snapped_route_config_ (by reselecting scoped route configuration), if a scope is\n   333\u2192    // not found, snapped_route_config_ is set to Router::NullConfigImpl.\n   334\u2192    void snapScopedRouteConfig();\n   335\u2192\n   336\u2192    void refreshCachedRoute(const Router::RouteCallback& cb);\n   337\u2192\n   338\u2192    void refreshCachedTracingCustomTags();\n   339\u2192    void refreshDurationTimeout();\n   340\u2192    void refreshIdleTimeout();\n   341\u2192    void refreshAccessLogFlushTimer();\n   342\u2192\n   343\u2192    // All state for the stream. Put here for readability.\n   344\u2192    struct State {\n   345\u2192      State()\n   346\u2192          : codec_saw_local_complete_(false), codec_encode_complete_(false),\n   347\u2192            on_reset_stream_called_(false), is_zombie_stream_(false), successful_upgrade_(false),\n   348\u2192            is_internally_destroyed_(false), is_internally_created_(false), is_tunneling_(false),\n   349\u2192            decorated_propagate_(true), deferred_to_next_io_iteration_(false),\n   350\u2192            deferred_end_stream_(false) {}\n   351\u2192\n   352\u2192      // It's possibly for the codec to see the completed response but not fully\n   353\u2192      // encode it.\n   354\u2192      bool codec_saw_local_complete_ : 1; // This indicates that local is complete as the completed\n   355\u2192                                          // response has made its way to the codec.\n   356\u2192      bool codec_encode_complete_ : 1;    // This indicates that the codec has\n   357\u2192                                          // completed encoding the response.\n   358\u2192      bool on_reset_stream_called_ : 1;   // Whether the stream has been reset.\n   359\u2192      bool is_zombie_stream_ : 1;         // Whether stream is waiting for signal\n   360\u2192                                          // the underlying codec to be destroyed.\n   361\u2192      bool successful_upgrade_ : 1;\n   362\u2192\n   363\u2192      // True if this stream was the original externally created stream, but was\n   364\u2192      // destroyed as part of internal redirect.\n   365\u2192      bool is_internally_destroyed_ : 1;\n   366\u2192      // True if this stream is internally created. Currently only used for\n   367\u2192      // internal redirects or other streams created via recreateStream().\n   368\u2192      bool is_internally_created_ : 1;\n   369\u2192\n   370\u2192      // True if the response headers indicate a successful upgrade or connect\n   371\u2192      // response.\n   372\u2192      bool is_tunneling_ : 1;\n   373\u2192\n   374\u2192      bool decorated_propagate_ : 1;\n   375\u2192\n   376\u2192      // Indicates that sending headers to the filter manager is deferred to the\n   377\u2192      // next I/O cycle. If data or trailers are received when this flag is set\n   378\u2192      // they are deferred too.\n   379\u2192      // TODO(yanavlasov): encapsulate the entire state of deferred streams into a separate\n   380\u2192      // structure, so it can be atomically created and cleared.\n   381\u2192      bool deferred_to_next_io_iteration_ : 1;\n   382\u2192      bool deferred_end_stream_ : 1;\n   383\u2192    };\n   384\u2192\n   385\u2192    bool canDestroyStream() const {\n   386\u2192      return state_.on_reset_stream_called_ || state_.codec_encode_complete_ ||\n   387\u2192             state_.is_internally_destroyed_;\n   388\u2192    }\n   389\u2192\n   390\u2192    // Per-stream idle timeout callback.\n   391\u2192    void onIdleTimeout();\n   392\u2192    // Per-stream request timeout callback.\n   393\u2192    void onRequestTimeout();\n   394\u2192    // Per-stream request header timeout callback.\n   395\u2192    void onRequestHeaderTimeout();\n   396\u2192    // Per-stream alive duration reached.\n   397\u2192    void onStreamMaxDurationReached();\n   398\u2192\n   399\u2192    // RouteCache\n   400\u2192    bool hasCachedRoute() const override {\n   401\u2192      return cached_route_.has_value() && cached_route_.value();\n   402\u2192    }\n   403\u2192    void refreshCachedRoute() override;\n   404\u2192\n   405\u2192    // Return local port of the connection.\n   406\u2192    uint32_t localPort();\n   407\u2192\n   408\u2192    friend std::ostream& operator<<(std::ostream& os, const ActiveStream& s) {\n   409\u2192      s.dumpState(os);\n   410\u2192      return os;\n   411\u2192    }\n   412\u2192\n   413\u2192    Tracing::CustomTagMap& getOrMakeTracingCustomTagMap() {\n   414\u2192      if (tracing_custom_tags_ == nullptr) {\n   415\u2192        tracing_custom_tags_ = std::make_unique<Tracing::CustomTagMap>();\n   416\u2192      }\n   417\u2192      return *tracing_custom_tags_;\n   418\u2192    }\n   419\u2192\n   420\u2192    // Note: this method is a noop unless ENVOY_ENABLE_UHV is defined\n   421\u2192    // Call header validator extension to validate request header map after it was deserialized.\n   422\u2192    // If header map failed validation, it sends an error response and returns false.\n   423\u2192    bool validateHeaders();\n   424\u2192\n   425\u2192    // Note: this method is a noop unless ENVOY_ENABLE_UHV is defined\n   426\u2192    // Call header validator extension to validate the request trailer map after it was\n   427\u2192    // deserialized. If the trailer map failed validation, this method does the following:\n   428\u2192    // 1. For H/1 it sends 400 response and returns false.\n   429\u2192    // 2. For H/2 and H/3 it resets the stream (without error response). Issue #24735 is filed to\n   430\u2192    //    harmonize this behavior with H/1.\n   431\u2192    // 3. If the `stream_error_on_invalid_http_message` is set to `false` (it is by default) in the\n   432\u2192    // HTTP connection manager configuration, then the entire connection is closed.\n   433\u2192    bool validateTrailers(RequestTrailerMap& trailers);\n   434\u2192\n   435\u2192    std::weak_ptr<bool> stillAlive() { return {still_alive_}; }\n   436\u2192\n   437\u2192    // Dispatch deferred headers, body and trailers to the filter manager.\n   438\u2192    // Return true if this stream was deferred and dispatched pending headers, body and trailers (if\n   439\u2192    // present). Return false if this stream was not deferred.\n   440\u2192    bool onDeferredRequestProcessing();\n   441\u2192\n   442\u2192    ConnectionManagerImpl& connection_manager_;\n   443\u2192    OptRef<const TracingConnectionManagerConfig> connection_manager_tracing_config_;\n   444\u2192    // TODO(snowp): It might make sense to move this to the FilterManager to avoid storing it in\n   445\u2192    // both locations, then refer to the FM when doing stream logs.\n   446\u2192    const uint64_t stream_id_;\n   447\u2192\n   448\u2192    RequestHeaderMapSharedPtr request_headers_;\n   449\u2192    RequestTrailerMapPtr request_trailers_;\n   450\u2192\n   451\u2192    ResponseHeaderMapPtr informational_headers_;\n   452\u2192    ResponseHeaderMapSharedPtr response_headers_;\n   453\u2192    ResponseTrailerMapSharedPtr response_trailers_;\n   454\u2192\n   455\u2192    // Note: The FM must outlive the above headers, as they are possibly accessed during filter\n   456\u2192    // destruction.\n   457\u2192    DownstreamFilterManager filter_manager_;\n   458\u2192\n   459\u2192    Tracing::SpanPtr active_span_;\n   460\u2192    ResponseEncoder* response_encoder_{};\n   461\u2192    Stats::TimespanPtr request_response_timespan_;\n   462\u2192    // Per-stream idle timeout. This timer gets reset whenever activity occurs on the stream, and,\n   463\u2192    // when triggered, will close the stream.\n   464\u2192    Event::TimerPtr stream_idle_timer_;\n   465\u2192    // Per-stream request timeout. This timer is enabled when the stream is created and disabled\n   466\u2192    // when the stream ends. If triggered, it will close the stream.\n   467\u2192    Event::TimerPtr request_timer_;\n   468\u2192    // Per-stream request header timeout. This timer is enabled when the stream is created and\n   469\u2192    // disabled when the downstream finishes sending headers. If triggered, it will close the\n   470\u2192    // stream.\n   471\u2192    Event::TimerPtr request_header_timer_;\n   472\u2192    // Per-stream alive duration. This timer is enabled once when the stream is created and, if\n   473\u2192    // triggered, will close the stream.\n   474\u2192    Event::TimerPtr max_stream_duration_timer_;\n   475\u2192    // Per-stream access log flush duration. This timer is enabled once when the stream is created\n   476\u2192    // and will log to all access logs once per trigger.\n   477\u2192    Event::TimerPtr access_log_flush_timer_;\n   478\u2192\n   479\u2192    std::chrono::milliseconds idle_timeout_ms_{};\n   480\u2192    State state_;\n   481\u2192\n   482\u2192    // Snapshot of the route configuration at the time of request is started. This is used to ensure\n   483\u2192    // that the same route configuration is used throughout the lifetime of the request. This\n   484\u2192    // snapshot will be cleared when the cached route is blocked. Because after that we will not\n   485\u2192    // refresh the cached route and release this snapshot can help to release the memory when the\n   486\u2192    // route configuration is updated frequently and the request is long-lived.\n   487\u2192    Router::ConfigConstSharedPtr snapped_route_config_;\n   488\u2192    Router::ScopedConfigConstSharedPtr snapped_scoped_routes_config_;\n   489\u2192    // This is used to track the route that has been cached in the request. And we will keep this\n   490\u2192    // route alive until the request is finished.\n   491\u2192    absl::optional<Router::RouteConstSharedPtr> cached_route_;\n   492\u2192    // This is used to track whether the route has been blocked. If the route is blocked, we can not\n   493\u2192    // clear it or refresh it.\n   494\u2192    bool route_cache_blocked_{false};\n   495\u2192    // This is used to track routes that have been cleared from the request. By this way, all the\n   496\u2192    // configurations that have been used in the processing of the request will be alive until the\n   497\u2192    // request is finished.\n   498\u2192    // For example, if a filter stored a per-route config in the decoding phase and may try to\n   499\u2192    // use it in the encoding phase, but the route is cleared and refreshed by another decoder\n   500\u2192    // filter, we must keep the per-route config alive to avoid use-after-free.\n   501\u2192    // Note that we assume that the number of routes that have been cleared is small. So we use\n   502\u2192    // inline vector to avoid heap allocation. If this assumption is wrong, we should consider using\n   503\u2192    // a list or other data structures.\n   504\u2192    //\n   505\u2192    // TODO(wbpcode): This is a helpless compromise. To avoid exposing the complexity of the route\n   506\u2192    // lifetime management to every HTTP filter, we do a hack here. But if every filter could manage\n   507\u2192    // the lifetime of the route config by itself easily, we could remove this hack.\n   508\u2192    absl::InlinedVector<Router::RouteConstSharedPtr, 3> cleared_cached_routes_;\n   509\u2192\n   510\u2192    absl::optional<Upstream::ClusterInfoConstSharedPtr> cached_cluster_info_;\n   511\u2192    const std::string* decorated_operation_{nullptr};\n   512\u2192    absl::optional<std::unique_ptr<RouteConfigUpdateRequester>> route_config_update_requester_;\n   513\u2192    std::unique_ptr<Tracing::CustomTagMap> tracing_custom_tags_{nullptr};\n   514\u2192    Http::ServerHeaderValidatorPtr header_validator_;\n   515\u2192\n   516\u2192    friend FilterManager;\n   517\u2192\n   518\u2192  private:\n   519\u2192    // Keep these methods private to ensure that these methods are only called by the reference\n   520\u2192    // returned by the public tracingConfig() method.\n   521\u2192    // Tracing::TracingConfig\n   522\u2192    Tracing::OperationName operationName() const override;\n   523\u2192    const Tracing::CustomTagMap* customTags() const override;\n   524\u2192    bool verbose() const override;\n   525\u2192    uint32_t maxPathTagLength() const override;\n   526\u2192    bool spawnUpstreamSpan() const override;\n   527\u2192\n   528\u2192    std::shared_ptr<bool> still_alive_ = std::make_shared<bool>(true);\n   529\u2192    std::unique_ptr<Buffer::OwnedImpl> deferred_data_;\n   530\u2192    std::queue<MetadataMapPtr> deferred_metadata_;\n   531\u2192    RequestTrailerMapPtr deferred_request_trailers_;\n   532\u2192  };\n   533\u2192\n   534\u2192  using ActiveStreamPtr = std::unique_ptr<ActiveStream>;\n   535\u2192\n   536\u2192  class ActiveStreamHandle : public RequestDecoderHandle {\n   537\u2192  public:\n   538\u2192    explicit ActiveStreamHandle(ActiveStream& stream)\n   539\u2192        : valid_(stream.stillAlive()), stream_(stream) {}\n   540\u2192\n   541\u2192    ~ActiveStreamHandle() override = default;\n   542\u2192\n   543\u2192    OptRef<RequestDecoder> get() override {\n   544\u2192      if (valid_.expired()) {\n   545\u2192        return {};\n   546\u2192      }\n   547\u2192      return stream_;\n   548\u2192    }\n   549\u2192\n   550\u2192  private:\n   551\u2192    std::weak_ptr<bool> valid_;\n   552\u2192    ActiveStream& stream_;\n   553\u2192  };\n   554\u2192\n   555\u2192  class HttpStreamIdProviderImpl : public StreamInfo::StreamIdProvider {\n   556\u2192  public:\n   557\u2192    HttpStreamIdProviderImpl(ActiveStream& parent) : parent_(parent) {}\n   558\u2192\n   559\u2192    // StreamInfo::StreamIdProvider\n   560\u2192    absl::optional<absl::string_view> toStringView() const override;\n   561\u2192    absl::optional<uint64_t> toInteger() const override;\n   562\u2192\n   563\u2192    ActiveStream& parent_;\n   564\u2192  };\n   565\u2192\n   566\u2192  /**\n   567\u2192   * Check to see if the connection can be closed after gracefully waiting to send pending codec\n   568\u2192   * data.\n   569\u2192   */\n   570\u2192  void checkForDeferredClose(bool skip_deferred_close);\n   571\u2192\n   572\u2192  /**\n   573\u2192   * Do a delayed destruction of a stream to allow for stack unwind. Also calls onDestroy() for\n   574\u2192   * each filter.\n   575\u2192   */\n   576\u2192  void doDeferredStreamDestroy(ActiveStream& stream);\n   577\u2192\n   578\u2192  /**\n   579\u2192   * Process a stream that is ending due to upstream response or reset.\n   580\u2192   * If check_for_deferred_close is true, the ConnectionManager will check to\n   581\u2192   * see if the connection was drained and should be closed if no streams remain.\n   582\u2192   */\n   583\u2192  void doEndStream(ActiveStream& stream, bool check_for_deferred_close = true);\n   584\u2192\n   585\u2192  void resetAllStreams(absl::optional<StreamInfo::CoreResponseFlag> response_flag,\n   586\u2192                       absl::string_view details);\n   587\u2192  void onIdleTimeout();\n   588\u2192  void onConnectionDurationTimeout();\n   589\u2192  void onDrainTimeout();\n   590\u2192  void startDrainSequence();\n   591\u2192  Tracing::Tracer& tracer() { return *config_->tracer(); }\n   592\u2192  void handleCodecErrorImpl(absl::string_view error, absl::string_view details,\n   593\u2192                            StreamInfo::CoreResponseFlag response_flag);\n   594\u2192  void handleCodecError(absl::string_view error);\n   595\u2192  void handleCodecOverloadError(absl::string_view error);\n   596\u2192  void doConnectionClose(absl::optional<Network::ConnectionCloseType> close_type,\n   597\u2192                         absl::optional<StreamInfo::CoreResponseFlag> response_flag,\n   598\u2192                         absl::string_view details);\n   599\u2192  void sendGoAwayAndClose();\n   600\u2192\n   601\u2192  // Returns true if a RST_STREAM for the given stream is premature. Premature\n   602\u2192  // means the RST_STREAM arrived before response headers were sent and than\n   603\u2192  // the stream was alive for short period of time. This period is specified\n   604\u2192  // by the optional runtime value PrematureResetMinStreamLifetimeSecondsKey,\n   605\u2192  // or one second if that is not present.\n   606\u2192  bool isPrematureRstStream(const ActiveStream& stream) const;\n   607\u2192  // Sends a GOAWAY if both sufficient streams have been closed on a connection\n   608\u2192  // and at least half have been prematurely reset?\n   609\u2192  void maybeDrainDueToPrematureResets();\n   610\u2192\n   611\u2192  bool shouldDeferRequestProxyingToNextIoCycle();\n   612\u2192  void onDeferredRequestProcessing();\n   613\u2192\n   614\u2192  enum class DrainState { NotDraining, Draining, Closing };\n   615\u2192\n   616\u2192  ConnectionManagerConfigSharedPtr config_;\n   617\u2192  ConnectionManagerStats& stats_; // We store a reference here to avoid an extra stats() call on\n   618\u2192                                  // the config in the hot path.\n   619\u2192  ServerConnectionPtr codec_;\n   620\u2192  std::list<ActiveStreamPtr> streams_;\n   621\u2192  Stats::TimespanPtr conn_length_;\n   622\u2192  const Network::DrainDecision& drain_close_;\n   623\u2192  DrainState drain_state_{DrainState::NotDraining};\n   624\u2192  UserAgent user_agent_;\n   625\u2192  // An idle timer for the connection. This is only armed when there are no streams on the\n   626\u2192  // connection. When there are active streams it is disarmed in favor of each stream's\n   627\u2192  // stream_idle_timer_.\n   628\u2192  Event::TimerPtr connection_idle_timer_;\n   629\u2192  // A connection duration timer. Armed during handling new connection if enabled in config.\n   630\u2192  Event::TimerPtr connection_duration_timer_;\n   631\u2192  Event::TimerPtr drain_timer_;\n   632\u2192  // When set to true, add Connection:close response header to nudge downstream client to reconnect.\n   633\u2192  bool soft_drain_http1_{false};\n   634\u2192  Random::RandomGenerator& random_generator_;\n   635\u2192  Runtime::Loader& runtime_;\n   636\u2192  const LocalInfo::LocalInfo& local_info_;\n   637\u2192  Upstream::ClusterManager& cluster_manager_;\n   638\u2192  Network::ReadFilterCallbacks* read_callbacks_{};\n   639\u2192  Event::Dispatcher* dispatcher_{};\n   640\u2192  ConnectionManagerListenerStats& listener_stats_;\n   641\u2192  Server::OverloadManager& overload_manager_;\n   642\u2192  Server::ThreadLocalOverloadState& overload_state_;\n   643\u2192  Server::LoadShedPoint* accept_new_http_stream_{nullptr};\n   644\u2192  Server::LoadShedPoint* hcm_ondata_creating_codec_{nullptr};\n   645\u2192  // References into the overload manager thread local state map. Using these lets us avoid a\n   646\u2192  // map lookup in the hot path of processing each request.\n   647\u2192  const Server::OverloadActionState& overload_stop_accepting_requests_ref_;\n   648\u2192  const Server::OverloadActionState& overload_disable_keepalive_ref_;\n   649\u2192  TimeSource& time_source_;\n   650\u2192  bool go_away_sent_{false};\n   651\u2192  bool remote_close_{};\n   652\u2192  // Hop by hop headers should always be cleared for Envoy-as-a-proxy but will\n   653\u2192  // not be for Envoy-mobile.\n   654\u2192  bool clear_hop_by_hop_response_headers_{true};\n   655\u2192  // The number of requests accumulated on the current connection.\n   656\u2192  uint64_t accumulated_requests_{};\n   657\u2192  // The number of requests closed on the current connection which were\n   658\u2192  // not internally destroyed\n   659\u2192  uint64_t closed_non_internally_destroyed_requests_{};\n   660\u2192  // The number of requests that received a premature RST_STREAM, according to\n   661\u2192  // the definition given in `isPrematureRstStream()`.\n   662\u2192  uint64_t number_premature_stream_resets_{0};\n   663\u2192  const std::string proxy_name_; // for Proxy-Status.\n   664\u2192  uint32_t requests_during_dispatch_count_{0};\n   665\u2192  const uint32_t max_requests_during_dispatch_{UINT32_MAX};\n   666\u2192  Event::SchedulableCallbackPtr deferred_request_processing_callback_;\n   667\u2192  const envoy::config::core::v3::TrafficDirection direction_;\n   668\u2192\n   669\u2192  // If independent half-close is enabled and the upstream protocol is either HTTP/2 or HTTP/3\n   670\u2192  // protocols the stream is destroyed after both request and response are complete i.e. reach their\n   671\u2192  // respective end-of-stream, by receiving trailers or the header/body with end-stream set in both\n   672\u2192  // directions AND response has success (2xx) status code.\n   673\u2192  //\n   674\u2192  // For HTTP/1 upstream protocol or if independent half-close is disabled the stream is destroyed\n   675\u2192  // when the response is complete and reaches its end-of-stream, i.e. when trailers or the response\n   676\u2192  // header/body with end-stream set are received, even if the request has not yet completed. If\n   677\u2192  // request was incomplete at response completion, the stream is reset.\n   678\u2192\n   679\u2192  const bool allow_upstream_half_close_{};\n   680\u2192};\n   681\u2192\n   682\u2192} // namespace Http\n   683\u2192} // namespace Envoy\n   684\u2192"

"     1\u2192#include \"source/common/http/filter_manager.h\"\n     2\u2192\n     3\u2192#include <functional>\n     4\u2192\n     5\u2192#include \"envoy/http/header_map.h\"\n     6\u2192#include \"envoy/matcher/matcher.h\"\n     7\u2192\n     8\u2192#include \"source/common/common/enum_to_int.h\"\n     9\u2192#include \"source/common/common/scope_tracked_object_stack.h\"\n    10\u2192#include \"source/common/common/scope_tracker.h\"\n    11\u2192#include \"source/common/http/codes.h\"\n    12\u2192#include \"source/common/http/header_map_impl.h\"\n    13\u2192#include \"source/common/http/header_utility.h\"\n    14\u2192#include \"source/common/http/utility.h\"\n    15\u2192\n    16\u2192#include \"matching/data_impl.h\"\n    17\u2192\n    18\u2192namespace Envoy {\n    19\u2192namespace Http {\n    20\u2192\n    21\u2192namespace {\n    22\u2192\n    23\u2192// Shared helper for recording the latest filter used.\n    24\u2192template <class Filters>\n    25\u2192void recordLatestDataFilter(typename Filters::Iterator current_filter,\n    26\u2192                            typename Filters::Element*& latest_filter, Filters& filters) {\n    27\u2192  // If this is the first time we're calling onData, just record the current filter.\n    28\u2192  if (latest_filter == nullptr) {\n    29\u2192    latest_filter = current_filter->get();\n    30\u2192    return;\n    31\u2192  }\n    32\u2192\n    33\u2192  // We want to keep this pointing at the latest filter in the filter list that has received the\n    34\u2192  // onData callback. To do so, we compare the current latest with the *previous* filter. If they\n    35\u2192  // match, then we must be processing a new filter for the first time. We omit this check if we're\n    36\u2192  // the first filter, since the above check handles that case.\n    37\u2192  //\n    38\u2192  // We compare against the previous filter to avoid multiple filter iterations from resetting the\n    39\u2192  // pointer: If we just set latest to current, then the first onData filter iteration would\n    40\u2192  // correctly iterate over the filters and set latest, but on subsequent onData iterations\n    41\u2192  // we'd start from the beginning again, potentially allowing filter N to modify the buffer even\n    42\u2192  // though filter M > N was the filter that inserted data into the buffer.\n    43\u2192  if (current_filter != filters.begin() && latest_filter == std::prev(current_filter)->get()) {\n    44\u2192    latest_filter = current_filter->get();\n    45\u2192  }\n    46\u2192}\n    47\u2192\n    48\u2192} // namespace\n    49\u2192\n    50\u2192void ActiveStreamFilterBase::commonContinue() {\n    51\u2192  if (!canContinue()) {\n    52\u2192    ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"cannot continue filter chain: filter={}\", *this,\n    53\u2192                     static_cast<const void*>(this));\n    54\u2192    return;\n    55\u2192  }\n    56\u2192\n    57\u2192  // Set ScopeTrackerScopeState if there's no existing crash context.\n    58\u2192  ScopeTrackedObjectStack encapsulated_object;\n    59\u2192  absl::optional<ScopeTrackerScopeState> state;\n    60\u2192  if (parent_.dispatcher_.trackedObjectStackIsEmpty()) {\n    61\u2192    restoreContextOnContinue(encapsulated_object);\n    62\u2192    state.emplace(&encapsulated_object, parent_.dispatcher_);\n    63\u2192  }\n    64\u2192\n    65\u2192  ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"continuing filter chain: filter={}\", *this,\n    66\u2192                   static_cast<const void*>(this));\n    67\u2192  ASSERT(!canIterate(),\n    68\u2192         \"Attempting to continue iteration while the IterationState is already Continue\");\n    69\u2192  // If iteration has stopped for all frame types, set iterate_from_current_filter_ to true so the\n    70\u2192  // filter iteration starts with the current filter instead of the next one.\n    71\u2192  if (stoppedAll()) {\n    72\u2192    iterate_from_current_filter_ = true;\n    73\u2192  }\n    74\u2192  allowIteration();\n    75\u2192\n    76\u2192  // Only resume with do1xxHeaders() if we've actually seen 1xx headers.\n    77\u2192  if (has1xxHeaders()) {\n    78\u2192    continued_1xx_headers_ = true;\n    79\u2192    do1xxHeaders();\n    80\u2192    // If the response headers have not yet come in, don't continue on with\n    81\u2192    // headers and body. doHeaders expects request headers to exist.\n    82\u2192    if (!parent_.filter_manager_callbacks_.responseHeaders()) {\n    83\u2192      return;\n    84\u2192    }\n    85\u2192  }\n    86\u2192\n    87\u2192  // Make sure that we handle the zero byte data frame case. We make no effort to optimize this\n    88\u2192  // case in terms of merging it into a header only request/response. This could be done in the\n    89\u2192  // future.\n    90\u2192  if (!headers_continued_) {\n    91\u2192    headers_continued_ = true;\n    92\u2192    doHeaders(observedEndStream() && !bufferedData() && !hasTrailers());\n    93\u2192  }\n    94\u2192\n    95\u2192  doMetadata();\n    96\u2192\n    97\u2192  // It is possible for trailers to be added during doData(). doData() itself handles continuation\n    98\u2192  // of trailers for the non-continuation case. Thus, we must keep track of whether we had\n    99\u2192  // trailers prior to calling doData(). If we do, then we continue them here, otherwise we rely\n   100\u2192  // on doData() to do so.\n   101\u2192  const bool had_trailers_before_data = hasTrailers();\n   102\u2192  if (bufferedData()) {\n   103\u2192    doData(observedEndStream() && !had_trailers_before_data);\n   104\u2192  }\n   105\u2192\n   106\u2192  if (had_trailers_before_data) {\n   107\u2192    doTrailers();\n   108\u2192  }\n   109\u2192\n   110\u2192  iterate_from_current_filter_ = false;\n   111\u2192}\n   112\u2192\n   113\u2192bool ActiveStreamFilterBase::commonHandleAfter1xxHeadersCallback(Filter1xxHeadersStatus status) {\n   114\u2192  ASSERT(parent_.state_.has_1xx_headers_);\n   115\u2192  ASSERT(!continued_1xx_headers_);\n   116\u2192  ASSERT(canIterate());\n   117\u2192\n   118\u2192  switch (status) {\n   119\u2192  case Filter1xxHeadersStatus::Continue:\n   120\u2192    continued_1xx_headers_ = true;\n   121\u2192    return true;\n   122\u2192  case Filter1xxHeadersStatus::StopIteration:\n   123\u2192    iteration_state_ = IterationState::StopSingleIteration;\n   124\u2192    return false;\n   125\u2192  }\n   126\u2192\n   127\u2192  PANIC_DUE_TO_CORRUPT_ENUM;\n   128\u2192}\n   129\u2192\n   130\u2192bool ActiveStreamFilterBase::commonHandleAfterHeadersCallback(FilterHeadersStatus status,\n   131\u2192                                                              bool& end_stream) {\n   132\u2192  ASSERT(!headers_continued_);\n   133\u2192  ASSERT(canIterate());\n   134\u2192\n   135\u2192  switch (status) {\n   136\u2192  case FilterHeadersStatus::StopIteration:\n   137\u2192    iteration_state_ = IterationState::StopSingleIteration;\n   138\u2192    break;\n   139\u2192  case FilterHeadersStatus::StopAllIterationAndBuffer:\n   140\u2192    iteration_state_ = IterationState::StopAllBuffer;\n   141\u2192    break;\n   142\u2192  case FilterHeadersStatus::StopAllIterationAndWatermark:\n   143\u2192    iteration_state_ = IterationState::StopAllWatermark;\n   144\u2192    break;\n   145\u2192  case FilterHeadersStatus::ContinueAndDontEndStream:\n   146\u2192    end_stream = false;\n   147\u2192    headers_continued_ = true;\n   148\u2192 headers_continued_ = true;\n   152\u2192    break;\n   153\u2192  }\n   154\u2192\n   155\u2192  handleMetadataAfterHeadersCallback();\n   156\u2192\n   157\u2192  if (stoppedAll() || status == FilterHeadersStatus::StopIteration) {\n   158\u2192    return false;\n   159\u2192  } else {\n   160\u2192    return true;\n   161\u2192  }\n   162\u2192}\n   163\u2192\n   164\u2192void ActiveStreamFilterBase::commonHandleBufferData(Buffer::Instance& provided_data) {\n   165\u2192\n   166\u2192  // The way we do buffering is a little complicated which is why we have this common function\n   167\u2192  // which is used for both encoding and decoding. When data first comes into our filter pipeline,\n   168\u2192  // we send it through. Any filter can choose to stop iteration and buffer or not. If we then\n   169\u2192  // continue iteration in the future, we use the buffered data. A future filter can stop and\n   170\u2192  // buffer again. In this case, since we are already operating on buffered data, we don't\n   171\u2192  // rebuffer, because we assume the filter has modified the buffer as it wishes in place.\n   172\u2192  if (bufferedData().get() != &provided_data) {\n   173\u2192    if (!bufferedData()) {\n   174\u2192      bufferedData() = createBuffer();\n   175\u2192    }\n   176\u2192    bufferedData()->move(provided_data);\n   177\u2192  }\n   178\u2192}\n   179\u2192\n   180\u2192bool ActiveStreamFilterBase::commonHandleAfterDataCallback(FilterDataStatus status,\n   181\u2192                                                           Buffer::Instance& provided_data,\n   182\u2192                                                           bool& buffer_was_streaming) {\n   183\u2192\n   184\u2192  if (status == FilterDataStatus::Continue) {\n   185\u2192    if (iteration_state_ == IterationState::StopSingleIteration) {\n   186\u2192      commonHandleBufferData(provided_data);\n   187\u2192      commonContinue();\n   188\u2192      return false;\n   189\u2192    } else {\n   190\u2192      ASSERT(headers_continued_);\n   191\u2192    }\n   192\u2192  } else {\n   193\u2192    iteration_state_ = IterationState::StopSingleIteration;\n   194\u2192    if (status == FilterDataStatus::StopIterationAndBuffer ||\n   195\u2192        status == FilterDataStatus::StopIterationAndWatermark) {\n   196\u2192      buffer_was_streaming = status == FilterDataStatus::StopIterationAndWatermark;\n   197\u2192      commonHandleBufferData(provided_data);\n   198\u2192    } else if (observedEndStream() && !hasTrailers() && !bufferedData() &&\n   199\u2192               // If the stream is destroyed, no need to handle the data buffer or trailers.\n   200\u2192               // This can occur if the filter calls sendLocalReply.\n   201\u2192               !parent_.state_.destroyed_) {\n   202\u2192      // If this filter is doing StopIterationNoBuffer and this stream is terminated with a zero\n   203\u2192      // byte data frame, we need to create an empty buffer to make sure that when commonContinue\n   204\u2192      // is called, the pipeline resumes with an empty data frame with end_stream = true\n   205\u2192      ASSERT(end_stream_);\n   206\u2192      bufferedData() = createBuffer();\n   207\u2192    }\n   208\u2192\n   209\u2192    return false;\n   210\u2192  }\n   211\u2192\n   212\u2192  return true;\n   213\u2192}\n   214\u2192\n   215\u2192bool ActiveStreamFilterBase::commonHandleAfterTrailersCallback(FilterTrailersStatus status) {\n   216\u2192\n   217\u2192  if (status == FilterTrailersStatus::Continue) {\n   218\u2192    if (iteration_state_ == IterationState::StopSingleIteration) {\n   219\u2192      commonContinue();\n   220\u2192      return false;\n   221\u2192    } else {\n   222\u2192      ASSERT(headers_continued_);\n   223\u2192    }\n   224\u2192  } else if (status == FilterTrailersStatus::StopIteration) {\n   225\u2192    if (canIterate()) {\n   226\u2192      iteration_state_ = IterationState::StopSingleIteration;\n   227\u2192    }\n   228\u2192    return false;\n   229\u2192  }\n   230\u2192\n   231\u2192  return true;\n   232\u2192}\n   233\u2192\n   234\u2192OptRef<const Network::Connection> ActiveStreamFilterBase::connection() {\n   235\u2192  return parent_.connection();\n   236\u2192}\n   237\u2192\n   238\u2192Event::Dispatcher& ActiveStreamFilterBase::dispatcher() { return parent_.dispatcher_; }\n   239\u2192\n   240\u2192StreamInfo::StreamInfo& ActiveStreamFilterBase::streamInfo() { return parent_.streamInfo(); }\n   241\u2192\n   242\u2192Tracing::Span& ActiveStreamFilterBase::activeSpan() {\n   243\u2192  return parent_.filter_manager_callbacks_.activeSpan();\n   244\u2192}\n   245\u2192\n   246\u2192const ActiveStreamFilterBase::restoreContextOnContinue(\n   251\u2192    ScopeTrackedObjectStack& tracked_object_stack) {\n   252\u2192  parent_.contextOnContinue(tracked_object_stack);\n   253\u2192}\n The connection manager bases further\n    36\u2192 * filter invocations on the return code of the previous filter.\n    37\u2192 */\n    38\u2192enum class FilterHeadersStatus {\n    39\u2192  // Continue filter chain iteration.\n    40\u2192  Continue,\n    41\u2192  // Do not iterate for headers on any of the remaining filters in the chain.\n    42\u2192  //\n    43\u2192  // Returning FilterDataStatus::Continue from decodeData()/encodeData() or calling\n    44\u2192  // continueDecoding()/continueEncoding() MUST be called if continued filter iteration is desired.\n    45\u2192  //\n    46\u2192  // Note that if a local reply was sent, no further iteration for headers as well as data and\n    47\u2192  // trailers for the current filter and the filters following will happen. A local reply can be\n    48\u2192  // triggered via sendLocalReply() or encodeHeaders().\n    49\u2192  StopIteration,\n    50\u2192  // Continue headers iteration to remaining filters, but delay ending the stream. This status MUST\n    51\u2192  // NOT be returned when end_stream is already set to false.\n    52\u2192  //\n    53\u2192  // Used when a filter wants to add a body to a headers-only request/response, but this body is not\n    54\u2192  // readily available. Delaying end_stream allows the filter to add the body once it's available\n    55\u2192  // without stopping headers iteration.\n    56\u2192  //\n    57\u2192  // The filter is responsible to continue the stream by providing a body through calling\n    58\u2192  // injectDecodedDataToFilterChain()/injectEncodedDataToFilterChain(), possibly multiple times\n    59\u2192  // if the body needs to be divided into several chunks. The filter may need to handle\n    60\u2192  // watermark events when injecting a body, see:\n    61\u2192  // https://github.com/envoyproxy/envoy/blob/main/source/docs/flow_control.md.\n    62\u2192  //\n    63\u2192  // The last call to inject data MUST have end_stream set to true to conclude the stream.\n    64\u2192  // If the filter cannot provide a body the stream should be reset.\n    65\u2192  //\n    66\u2192  // Adding a body through calling addDecodedData()/addEncodedData() then\n    67\u2192  // continueDecoding()/continueEncoding() is currently NOT supported and causes an assert failure.\n    68\u2192  //\n    69\u2192  // Adding trailers in this scenario is currently NOT supported.\n    70\u2192  //\n    71\u2192  // The filter MUST NOT attempt to continue the stream without providing a body using\n    72\u2192  // continueDecoding()/continueEncoding().\n    73\u2192  //\n    74\u2192  // TODO(yosrym93): Support adding a body in this case by calling addDecodedData()/addEncodedData()\n    75\u2192  // then continueDecoding()/continueEncoding(). To support this a new FilterManager::IterationState\n    76\u2192  // needs to be added and set when a filter returns this status in\n    77\u2192  // FilterManager::decodeHeaders/FilterManager::encodeHeaders()\n    78\u2192  // Currently, when a filter returns this, the IterationState is Continue. This causes ASSERTs in\n    79\u2192  // FilterManager::commonContinue() to fail when continueDecoding()/continueEncoding() is called;\n    80\u2192  // due to trying to continue iteration when the IterationState is already Continue.\n    81\u2192  // In this case, a different ASSERT will be needed to make sure the filter does not try to\n    82\u2192  // continue without adding a body first.\n    83\u2192  //\n    84\u2192  // TODO(yosrym93): Support adding trailers in this case by implementing new functions to inject\n    85\u2192  // trailers, similar to the inject data functions.\n    86\u2192  ContinueAndDontEndStream,\n    87\u2192  // Do not iterate for headers as well as data and trailers for the current filter and the filters\n    88\u2192  // following, and buffer body data for later dispatching. ContinueDecoding() MUST\n    89\u2192  // be called if continued filter iteration is desired.\n    90\u2192  //\n    91\u2192  // Used when a filter wants to stop iteration on data and trailers while waiting for headers'\n    92\u2192  // iteration to resume.\n    93\u2192  //\n    94\u2192  // If buffering the request causes buffered data to exceed the configured buffer limit, a 413 will\n    95\u2192  // be sent to the user. On the response path exceeding buffer limits will result in a 500.\n    96\u2192  //\n    97\u2192  // TODO(soya3129): stop metadata parsing when StopAllIterationAndBuffer is set.\n    98\u2192  StopAllIterationAndBuffer,\n    99\u2192  // Do not iterate for headers as well as data and trailers for the current filter and the filters\n   100\u2192  // following, and buffer body data for later dispatching. continueDecoding() MUST\n   101\u2192  // be called if continued filter iteration is desired.\n   102\u2192  //\n   103\u2192  // Used when a filter wants to stop iteration on data and trailers while waiting for headers'\n   104\u2192  // iteration to resume.\n   105\u2192  //\n   106\u2192  // This will cause the flow of incoming data to cease until continueDecoding() function is called.\n   107\u2192  //\n   108\u2192  // TODO(soya3129): stop metadata parsing when StopAllIterationAndWatermark is set.\n   109\u2192  StopAllIterationAndWatermark,\n   110\u2192};\n   111\u2192\n   112\u2192/**\n   113\u2192 * Return codes for encode on informative headers filter invocations.\n   114\u2192 */\n   115\u2192enum class Filter1xxHeadersStatus {\n   116\u2192  // Continue filter chain iteration.\n   117\u2192  Continue,\n   118\u2192  // Do not iterate for informative headers on any of the remaining filters in the chain.\n   119\u2192  //\n   120\u2192  // Returning FilterDataStatus::Continue from encodeData() or calling\n   121\u2192  // continueEncoding() MUST be called if continued filter iteration is desired.\n   122\u2192  StopIteration\n   123\u2192};\n   124\u2192\n   125\u2192/**\n   126\u2192 * Return codes for encode/decode data filter invocations. The connection manager bases further\n   127\u2192 * filter invocations on the return code of the previous filter.\n   128\u2192 */\n   129\u2192enum class FilterDataStatus {\n   130\u2192  // Continue filter chain iteration. If headers have not yet been sent to the next filter, they\n   131\u2192  // will be sent first via decodeHeaders()/encodeHeaders(). If data has previously been buffered,\n   132\u2192  // the data in this callback will be added to the buffer before the entirety is sent to the next\n   133\u2192  // filter.\n   134\u2192  Continue,\n   135\u2192  // Do not iterate to any of the remaining filters in the chain, and buffer body data for later\n   136\u2192  // dispatching. Returning FilterDataStatus::Continue from decodeData()/encodeData() or calling\n   137\u2192  // continueDecoding()/continueEncoding() MUST be called if continued filter iteration is desired.\n   138\u2192  //\n   139\u2192  // This should be called by filters which must parse a larger block of the incoming data before\n   140\u2192  // continuing processing and so can not push back on streaming data via watermarks.\n   141\u2192  //\n   142\u2192  // If buffering the request causes buffered data to exceed the configured buffer limit, a 413 will\n   143\u2192  // be sent to the user. On the response path exceeding buffer limits will result in a 500.\n   144\u2192  StopIterationAndBuffer,\n   145\u2192  // Do not iterate to any of the remaining filters in the chain, and buffer body data for later\n   146\u2192  // dispatching. Returning FilterDataStatus::Continue from decodeData()/encodeData() or calling\n   147\u2192  // continueDecoding()/continueEncoding() MUST be called if continued filter iteration is desired.\n   148\u2192  //\n   149\u2192  // This will cause the flow of incoming data to cease until one of the continue.*() functions is\n   150\u2192  // called.\n   151\u2192  //\n   152\u2192  // This should be returned by filters which can nominally stream data but have a transient back-up\n   153\u2192  // such as the configured delay of the fault filter, or if the router filter is still fetching an\n   154\u2192  // upstream connection.\n   155\u2192  StopIterationAndWatermark,\n   156\u2192  // Do not iterate to any of the remaining filters in the chain, but do not buffer any of the\n   157\u2192  // body data for later dispatching. Returning FilterDataStatus::Continue from\n   158\u2192  // decodeData()/encodeData() or calling continueDecoding()/continueEncoding() MUST be called if\n   159\u2192  // continued filter iteration is desired.\n   160\u2192  //\n   161\u2192  // Note that if a local reply was sent, no further iteration for either data or trailers\n   162\u2192  // for the current filter and the filters following will happen. A local reply can be\n   163\u2192  // triggered via sendLocalReply() or encodeHeaders().\n   164\u2192  StopIterationNoBuffer\n   165\u2192};\n   166\u2192\n   167\u2192/**\n   168\u2192 * Return codes for encode/decode trailers filter invocations. The connection manager bases further\n   169\u2192 * filter invocations on the return code of the previous filter.\n   170\u2192 */\n   171\u2192enum class FilterTrailersStatus {\n   172\u2192  // Continue filter chain iteration.\n   173\u2192  Continue,\n   174\u2192  // Do not iterate to any of the remaining filters in the chain. Calling\n   175\u2192  // continueDecoding()/continueEncoding() MUST be called if continued filter iteration is desired.\n   176\u2192  StopIteration\n   177\u2192};\n   178\u2192\n   179\u2192/**\n   180\u2192 * Return codes for encode metadata filter invocations. If headers have not yet been sent to the next filter, they\n   189\u2192  // will be sent first via (decode|encode)Headers().\n   190\u2192  ContinueAll,\n   191\u2192\n   192\u2192  // Stops iteration of the entire filter chain. So, we can safely complete the initialization of the filter chain now.\n  1668\u2192  Cleanup initialize_filter_chain([this]() {\n  1669\u2192    for (auto iter = decoder_filters_.begin(); iter != decoder_filters_.end(); ++iter) {\n  1670\u2192      (*iter)->entry_ = iter;\n  1671\u2192    }\n  1672\u2192    for (auto iter = encoder_filters_.begin(); iter != encoder_filters_.end(); ++iter) {\n  1673\u2192      (*iter)->entry_ = iter;\n  1674\u2192    }\n  1675\u2192  });\n  1676\u2192\n  1677\u2192  // TODO(wbpcode): reserve memory for filters to avoid frequent reallocation.\n  1678\u2192\n  1679\u2192  OptRef<DownstreamStreamFilterCallbacks> downstream_callbacks =\n  1680\u2192      filter_manager_callbacks_.downstreamCallbacks();\n  1681\u2192\n  1682\u2192  // This filter chain options is only used for the downstream HTTP filter chains for now. So, try\n  1683\u2192  // to set valid initial route only when the downstream callbacks is available.\n  1684\u2192  FilterChainOptionsImpl options(downstream_callbacks.has_value() ? streamInfo().route() : nullptr);\n  1685\u2192\n  1686\u2192  UpgradeResult upgrade = UpgradeResult::UpgradeUnneeded;\n  1687\u2192\n  1688\u2192  // Only try the upgrade filter chain for downstream filter chains.\n  1689\u2192  if (downstream_callbacks.has_value()) {\n  1690\u2192    upgrade = createUpgradeFilterChain(filter_chain_factory, options);\n  1691\u2192    if (upgrade == UpgradeResult::UpgradeAccepted) {\n  1692\u2192      // Upgrade filter chain is created. Return the result directly.\n  1693\u2192      state_.create_chain_result_ = CreateChainResult(true, upgrade);\n  1694\u2192      return state_.create_chain_result_;\n  1695\u2192    }\n  1696\u2192    // If the upgrade is unnecessary or the upgrade filter chain is rejected, fall through to\n  1697\u2192    // create the default filter chain.\n  1698\u2192  }\n  1699\u2192\n  1700\u2192  state_.create_chain_result_ =\n  1701\u2192      CreateChainResult(filter_chain_factory.createFilterChain(*this, options), upgrade);\n  1702\u2192  return state_.create_chain_result_;\n  1703\u2192}\n  1704\u2192\n  1705\u2192void ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::requestDataDrained() {\n  1706\u2192  // If this is called it means the call to requestDataTooLarge() was a\n  1707\u2192  // streaming call, or a 413 would have been sent.\n  1708\u2192  onDecoderFilterBelowWriteBufferLowWatermark();\n  1709\u2192}\n  1710\u2192\n  1711\u2192void ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::onDecoderFilterBelowWriteBufferLowWatermark() {\n  1712\u2192  parent_.filter_manager_callbacks_.onDecoderFilterBelowWriteBufferLowWatermark();\n  1713\u2192}\n  1714\u2192\n  1715\u2192void ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::addDownstreamWatermarkCallbacks(\n  1716\u2192    DownstreamWatermarkCallbacks& watermark_callbacks) {\n  1717\u2192  // This is called exactly once per upstream-stream, by the router filter. Therefore, we\n  1718\u2192  // expect the same callbacks to not be registered twice.\n  1719\u2192  ASSERT(std::find(parent_.watermark_callbacks_.begin(), parent_.watermark_callbacks_.end(),\n  1720\u2192                   &watermark_callbacks) == parent_.watermark_callbacks_.end());\n  1721\u2192  parent_.watermark_callbacks_.emplace(parent_.watermark_callbacks_.end(), &watermark_callbacks);\n  1722\u2192  for (uint32_t i = 0; i < parent_.high_watermark_count_; ++i) {\n  1723\u2192    watermark_callbacks.onAboveWriteBufferHighWatermark();\n  1724\u2192  }\n  1725\u2192}\n  1726\u2192\n  1727\u2192void ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::removeDownstreamWatermarkCallbacks(\n  1728\u2192    DownstreamWatermarkCallbacks& watermark_callbacks) {\n  1729\u2192  ASSERT(std::find(parent_.watermark_callbacks_.begin(), parent_.watermark_callbacks_.end(),\n  1730\u2192                   &watermark_callbacks) != parent_.watermark_callbacks_.end());\n  1731\u2192  parent_.watermark_callbacks_.remove(&watermark_callbacks);\n  1732\u2192}\n  1733\u2192\n  1734\u2192void ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::setDecoderBufferLimit(uint32_t limit) {\n  1735\u2192  parent_.setBufferLimit(limit);\n  1736\u2192}\n  1737\u2192\n  1738\u2192uint32_t ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::decoderBufferLimit() { return parent_.buffer_limit_; }\n  1739\u2192\n  1740\u2192bool ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::recreateStream(const ResponseHeaderMap* headers) {\n  1741\u2192  // Because the filter's and the HCM view of if the stream has a body and if\n  1742\u2192  // the stream is complete may differ, re-check bytesReceived() to make sure\n  1743\u2192  // there was no body from the HCM's point of view.\n  1744\u2192  if (!observedEndStream()) {\n  1745\u2192    return false;\n  1746\u2192  }\n  1747\u2192\n  1748\u2192  parent_.state_.decoder_filter_chain_aborted_ = true;\n  1749\u2192  parent_.state_.encoder_filter_chain_aborted_ = true;\n  1750\u2192  parent_.state_.recreated_stream_ = true;\n  1751\u2192\n  1752\u2192  parent_.streamInfo().setResponseCodeDetails(\n  1753\u2192      StreamInfo::ResponseCodeDetails::get().InternalRedirect);\n  1754\u2192\n  1755\u2192  if (headers != nullptr) {\n  1756\u2192    // The call to setResponseHeaders is needed to ensure that the headers are properly logged in\n  1757\u2192    // access logs before the stream is destroyed. Since the function expects a\n  1758\u2192    // ResponseHeaderPtr&&, ownership of the headers must be passed. This cannot happen earlier in\n  1759\u2192    // the flow (such as in the call to setupRedirect) because at that point it is still possible"

"   537\u2192void FilterManager::decodeHeaders(ActiveStreamDecoderFilter* filter, RequestHeaderMap& headers,\n   538\u2192                                  bool end_stream) {\n   539\u2192  // Headers filter iteration should always start with the next filter if available.\n   540\u2192  StreamDecoderFilters::Iterator entry =\n   541\u2192      commonDecodePrefix(filter, FilterIterationStartState::AlwaysStartFromNext);\n   542\u2192  StreamDecoderFilters::Iterator continue_data_entry = decoder_filters_.end();\n   543\u2192  bool terminal_filter_decoded_end_stream = false;\n   544\u2192  ASSERT(!state_.decoder_filter_chain_complete_ || entry == decoder_filters_.end() ||\n   545\u2192         (*entry)->end_stream_);\n   546\u2192\n   547\u2192  for (; entry != decoder_filters_.end(); entry++) {\n   548\u2192    ENVOY_EXECUTION_SCOPE(trackedStream(), &(*entry)->filter_context_);\n   549\u2192    ASSERT(!(state_.filter_call_state_ & FilterCallState::DecodeHeaders));\n   550\u2192    state_.filter_call_state_ |= FilterCallState::DecodeHeaders;\n   551\u2192    (*entry)->end_stream_ = (end_stream && continue_data_entry == decoder_filters_.end());\n   552\u2192    if ((*entry)->end_stream_) {\n   553\u2192      state_.filter_call_state_ |= FilterCallState::EndOfStream;\n   554\u2192    }\n   555\u2192 ((*entry)->end_stream_) {\n   558\u2192      state_.filter_call_state_ &= ~FilterCallState::EndOfStream;\n   559\u2192    }\n   560\u2192    if (state_.decoder_filter_chain_aborted_) {\n   561\u2192      executeLocalReplyIfPrepared();\n   562\u2192      ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace,\n   563\u2192                       \"decodeHeaders filter iteration aborted due to local reply: filter={}\",\n   564\u2192                       *this, (*entry)->filter_context_.config_name);\n   565\u2192      status = FilterHeadersStatus::StopIteration;\n   566\u2192    }\n   567\u2192\n   568\u2192    ASSERT(!(status == FilterHeadersStatus::ContinueAndDontEndStream && !(*entry)->end_stream_),\n   569\u2192           \"Filters should not return FilterHeadersStatus::ContinueAndDontEndStream from \"\n   570\u2192           \"decodeHeaders when end_stream is already false\");\n   571\u2192\n   572\u2192    ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"decode headers called: filter={} status={}\", *this,\n   573\u2192                     (*entry)->filter_context_.config_name, static_cast<uint64_t>(status));\n   574\u2192\n   575\u2192    (*entry)->processed_headers_ = true;\n   576\u2192\n   577\u2192    const auto continue_iteration = (*entry)->commonHandleAfterHeadersCallback(status, end_stream);\n   578\u2192    ENVOY_BUG(!continue_iteration || !stopDecoderFilterChain(),\n   579\u2192              fmt::format(\n   580\u2192                  \"filter={} did not return StopAll or StopIteration after sending a local reply.\",\n   581\u2192                  (*entry)->filter_context_.config_name));\n   582\u2192\n   583\u2192    // If this filter ended the stream, decodeComplete() should be called for it.\n   584\u2192    if ((*entry)->end_stream_) {\n   585\u2192      (*entry)->handle_->decodeComplete();\n   586\u2192    }\n   587\u2192\n   588\u2192    // Skip processing metadata after sending local reply\n   589\u2192    if (stopDecoderFilterChain() && std::next(entry) != decoder_filters_.end()) {\n   590\u2192      maybeContinueDecoding(continue_data_entry);\n   591\u2192      return;\n   592\u2192    }\n   593\u2192\n   594\u2192    const bool new_metadata_added = processNewlyAddedMetadata();\n   595\u2192    // If end_stream is set in headers, and a filter adds new metadata, we need to delay end_stream\n   596\u2192    // in headers by inserting an empty data frame with end_stream set. The empty data frame is sent\n   597\u2192    // after the new metadata.\n   598\u2192    if ((*entry)->end_stream_ && new_metadata_added && !buffered_request_data_) {\n   599\u2192      Buffer::OwnedImpl empty_data(\"\");\n   600\u2192      ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(\n   601\u2192          trace, \"inserting an empty data frame for end_stream due metadata being added.\", *this);\n   602\u2192      // Metadata frame doesn't carry end of stream bit. We need an empty data frame to end the\n   603\u2192      // stream.\n   604\u2192      addDecodedData(*(*entry), empty_data, true);\n   605\u2192    }\n   606\u2192\n   607\u2192    if (!continue_iteration && std::next(entry) != decoder_filters_.end()) {\n   608\u2192      // Stop iteration IFF this is not the last filter. If it is the last filter, continue with\n   609\u2192      // processing since we need to handle the case where a terminal filter wants to buffer, but\n   610\u2192      // a previous filter has added body.\n   611\u2192      maybeContinueDecoding(continue_data_entry);\n   612\u2192      return;\n   613\u2192    }\n   614\u2192\n   615\u2192    // Here we handle the case where we have a header only request, but a filter adds a body\n   616\u2192    // to it. We need to not raise end_stream = true to further filters during inline iteration.\n   617\u2192    if (end_stream && buffered_request_data_ && continue_data_entry == decoder_filters_.end()) {\n   618\u2192      continue_data_entry = entry;\n   619\u2192    }\n   620\u2192    // The decoder filter chain is finished here if the following is true:\n   621\u2192    // 1. the last filter has observed the end_stream AND\n   622\u2192    // 2. no filter, including the last filter, has injected body during header iteration.\n   623\u2192    // If body was injected the end_stream will be processed in the `decodeData()` method below.\n   624\u2192    const bool no_body_was_injected = continue_data_entry == decoder_filters_.end();\n   625\u2192    terminal_filter_decoded_end_stream =\n   626\u2192        (std::next(entry) == decoder_filters_.end() && (*entry)->end_stream_) &&\n   627\u2192        no_body_was_injected;\n   628\u2192  }\n   629\u2192\n   630\u2192  maybeContinueDecoding(continue_data_entry);\n   631\u2192\n   632\u2192  if (end_stream) {\n   633\u2192    disarmRequestTimeout();\n   634\u2192  }\n   635\u2192  maybeEndDecode(terminal_filter_decoded_end_stream);\n   636\u2192}\n   637\u2192\n   638\u2192void FilterManager::decodeData(ActiveStreamDecoderFilter* filter, Buffer::Instance& data,\n   639\u2192                               bool end_stream,\n   640\u2192                               FilterIterationStartState filter_iteration_start_state) {\n   641\u2192  ScopeTrackerScopeState scope(this, dispatcher_);\n   642\u2192  filter_manager_callbacks_.resetIdleTimer();\n   643\u2192\n   644\u2192  // If a response is complete or a reset has been sent, filters do not care about further body\n   645\u2192  // data. Just drop it.\n   646\u2192  if (stopDecoderFilterChain()) {\n   647\u2192    return;\n   648\u2192  }\n   649\u2192\n   650\u2192  auto trailers_added_entry = decoder_filters_.end();\n   651\u2192  const bool trailers_exists_at_start = filter_manager_callbacks_.requestTrailers().has_value();\n   652\u2192  // Filter iteration may start at the current filter.\n   653\u2192  StreamDecoderFilters::Iterator entry = commonDecodePrefix(filter, filter_iteration_start_state);\n   654\u2192  bool terminal_filter_decoded_end_stream = false;\n   655\u2192  ASSERT(!state_.decoder_filter_chain_complete_ || entry == decoder_filters_.end() ||\n   656\u2192         (*entry)->end_stream_);\n   657\u2192\n   658\u2192  for (; entry != decoder_filters_.end(); entry++) {\n   659\u2192    ENVOY_EXECUTION_SCOPE(trackedStream(), &(*entry)->filter_context_);\n   660\u2192    // If the filter pointed by entry has stopped for all frame types, return now.\n   661\u2192    if (handleDataIfStopAll(**entry, data, state_.decoder_filters_streaming_)) {\n   662\u2192      return;\n   663\u2192    }\n   664\u2192    // If end_stream_ is marked for a filter, the data is not for this filter and filters after.\n   665\u2192    //\n   666\u2192    // In following case, ActiveStreamFilterBase::commonContinue() could be called recursively and\n   667\u2192    // its doData() is called with wrong data.\n   668\u2192    //\n   669\u2192    //  There are 3 decode filters and \"wrapper\" refers to ActiveStreamFilter object.\n   670\u2192    //\n   671\u2192    //  filter0->decodeHeaders(_, true)\n   672\u2192    //    return STOP\n   673\u2192    //  filter0->continueDecoding()\n   674\u2192    //    wrapper0->commonContinue()\n   675\u2192    //      wrapper0->decodeHeaders(_, _, true)\n   676\u2192    //        filter1->decodeHeaders(_, true)\n   677\u2192    //          filter1->addDecodeData()\n   678\u2192    //          return CONTINUE\n   679\u2192    //        filter2->decodeHeaders(_, false)\n   680\u2192    //          return CONTINUE\n   681\u2192    //        wrapper1->commonContinue() // Detects data is added.\n   682\u2192    //          wrapper1->doData()\n   683\u2192    //            wrapper1->decodeData()\n   684\u2192    //              filter2->decodeData(_, true)\n   685\u2192    //                 return CONTINUE\n   686\u2192    //      wrapper0->doData() // This should not be called"

"   686\u2192    //      wrapper0->doData() // This should not be called\n   687\u2192    //        wrapper0->decodeData()\n   688\u2192    //          filter1->decodeData(_, true)  // It will cause assertions.\n   689\u2192    //\n   690\u2192    // One way to solve this problem is to mark end_stream_ for each filter.\n   691\u2192    // If a filter is already marked as end_stream_ when decodeData() is called, bails out the\n   692\u2192    // whole function. If just skip the filter, the codes after the loop will be called with\n   693\u2192    // wrong data. For encodeData, the response_encoder->encode() will be called.\n   694\u2192    if ((*entry)->end_stream_) {\n   695\u2192      return;\n   696\u2192    }\n   697\u2192    ASSERT(!(state_.filter_call_state_ & FilterCallState::DecodeData));\n   698\u2192\n   699\u2192    // We check the request_trailers_ pointer here in case addDecodedTrailers\n   700\u2192    // is called in decodeData during a previous filter invocation, at which point we communicate to\n   701\u2192    // the current and future filters that the stream has not yet ended.\n   702\u2192    if state_.filter_call_state_ &= ~FilterCallState::EndOfStream;\n   717\u2192    }\n   718\u2192    ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"decode data called: filter={} status={}\", *this,\n   719\u2192                     (*entry)->filter_context_.config_name, static_cast<uint64_t>(status));\n   720\u2192    if (state_.decoder_filter_chain_aborted_) {\n   721\u2192      executeLocalReplyIfPrepared();\n   722\u2192      ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"decodeData filter iteration aborted due to local reply: filter={}\",\n   723\u2192                       *this, (*entry)->filter_context_.config_name);\n   724\u2192      return;\n   725\u2192    }\n   726\u2192\n   727\u2192    processNewlyAddedMetadata();\n   728\u2192\n   729\u2192    if (!trailers_exists_at_start && filter_manager_callbacks_.requestTrailers() &&\n   730\u2192        trailers_added_entry == decoder_filters_.end()) {\n   731\u2192      end_stream = false;\n   732\u2192      trailers_added_entry = entry;\n   733\u2192    }\n   734\u2192\n   735\u2192    // The decoder filter chain is finished here if the following is true:\n   736\u2192    // 1. the last filter has observed the end_stream AND\n   737\u2192    // 2. no filter, including the last filter, has injected trailers during header iteration.\n   738\u2192    //    NOTE: end_stream is set to false above if a filter injected trailers.\n   739\u2192    // If trailers were injected the end_stream will be processed in the `decodeTrailers()` method\n   740\u2192    // below.\n   741\u2192    terminal_filter_decoded_end_stream = end_stream && std::next(entry) == decoder_filters_.end();\n   742\u2192\n   743\u2192    if (!(*entry)->commonHandleAfterDataCallback(status, data, state_.decoder_filters_streaming_) &&\n   744\u2192        std::next(entry) != decoder_filters_.end()) {\n   745\u2192      // Stop iteration IFF this is not the last filter. If it is the last filter, continue with\n   746\u2192      // processing since we need to handle the case where a terminal filter wants to buffer, but\n   747\u2192      // a previous filter has added trailers.\n   748\u2192      break;\n   749\u2192    }\n   750\u2192  }\n   751\u2192\n   752\u2192  // If trailers were adding during decodeData we need to trigger decodeTrailers in order\n   753\u2192  // to allow filters to process the trailers.\n   754\u2192  if (trailers_added_entry != decoder_filters_.end()) {\n   755\u2192    decodeTrailers(trailers_added_entry->get(), *filter_manager_callbacks_.requestTrailers());\n   756\u2192  }\n   757\u2192\n   758\u2192  if (end_stream) {\n   759\u2192    disarmRequestTimeout();\n   760\u2192  }\n   761\u2192  maybeEndDecode(terminal_filter_decoded_end_stream);\n   762\u2192}\n   763\u2192\n   764\u2192RequestTrailerMap& FilterManager::addDecodedTrailers() {\n   765\u2192  // Trailers can only be added during the last data frame (i.e. end_stream = true).\n   766\u2192  ASSERT(state_.filter_call_state_ & FilterCallState::EndOfStream);\n   767\u2192\n   768\u2192  filter_manager_callbacks_.setRequestTrailers(RequestTrailerMapImpl::create());\n   769\u2192  return *filter_manager_callbacks_.requestTrailers();\n   770\u2192}\n   771\u2192\n   772\u2192void FilterManager::addDecodedData(ActiveStreamDecoderFilter& filter, Buffer::Instance& data,\n   773\u2192                                   bool streaming) {\n   774\u2192  if (state_.filter_call_state_ == 0 ||\n   775\u2192      (state_.filter_call_state_ & FilterCallState::DecodeHeaders) ||\n   776\u2192      (state_.filter_call_state_ & FilterCallState::DecodeData) ||\n   777\u2192
