state_.decoder_filters_streaming_ = streaming;\n   780\u2192    // If no call is happening or we are in the decode headers/data callback, buffer the data.\n   781\u2192    // Inline processing happens in the decodeHeaders() callback if necessary.\n   782\u2192    filter.commonHandleBufferData(data);\n   783\u2192  } else if (state_.filter_call_state_ & FilterCallState::DecodeTrailers) {\n   784\u2192    // In this case we need to inline dispatch the data to further filters. If those filters\n   785\u2192    // choose to buffer/stop iteration that's fine.\n   786\u2192    decodeData(&filter, data, false, FilterIterationStartState::AlwaysStartFromNext);\n   787\u2192  } else {\n   788\u2192    IS_ENVOY_BUG(\"Invalid request data\");\n   789\u2192    sendLocalReply(Http::Code::BadGateway, \"Filter error\", nullptr, absl::nullopt,\n   790\u2192                   StreamInfo::ResponseCodeDetails::get().FilterAddedInvalidRequestData);\n   791\u2192  }\n   792\u2192}\n   793\u2192\n   794\u2192MetadataMapVector& FilterManager::addDecodedMetadata() { return *getRequestMetadataMapVector(); }\n   795\u2192\n   796\u2192void FilterManager::decodeTrailers(ActiveStreamDecoderFilter* filter, RequestTrailerMap& trailers) {\n   797\u2192  // If a response is complete or a reset has been sent, filters do not care about further body\n   798\u2192  // data. Just drop it.\n   799\u2192  if (stopDecoderFilterChain()) {\n   800\u2192    return;\n   801\u2192  }\n   802\u2192\n   803\u2192  // Filter iteration may start at the current filter.\n   804\u2192  StreamDecoderFilters::Iterator entry =\n   805\u2192      commonDecodePrefix(filter, FilterIterationStartState::CanStartFromCurrent);\n   806\u2192  bool terminal_filter_reached = false;\n   807\u2192  ASSERT(!state_.decoder_filter_chain_complete_ || entry == decoder_filters_.end());\n   808\u2192\n   809\u2192  for (; entry != decoder_filters_.end(); entry++) {\n   810\u2192    ENVOY_EXECUTION_SCOPE(trackedStream(), &(*entry)->filter_context_);\n   811\u2192    // If the filter pointed by entry has stopped for all frame type, return now.\n   812\u2192    if ((*entry)->stoppedAll()) {\n   813\u2192      return;\n   814\u2192    }\n   815\u2192    ASSERT(!(state_.filter_call_state_ & FilterCallState::DecodeTrailers));\n   816\u2192    state_.filter_call_state_ |= FilterCallState::DecodeTrailers;\n   817\u2192    FilterTrailersStatus status = (*entry)->handle_->decodeTrailers(trailers);\n   818\u2192    (*entry)->handle_->decodeComplete();\n   819\u2192    (*entry)->end_stream_ = true;\n   820\u2192    state_.filter_call_state_ &= ~FilterCallState::DecodeTrailers;\n   821\u2192    ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"decode trailers called: filter={} status={}\", *this,\n   822\u2192                     (*entry)->filter_context_.config_name, static_cast<uint64_t>(status));\n   823\u2192    if (state_.decoder_filter_chain_aborted_) {\n   824\u2192      executeLocalReplyIfPrepared();\n   825\u2192      ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace,\n   826\u2192                       \"decodeTrailers filter iteration aborted due to local reply: filter={}\",\n   827\u2192                       *this, (*entry)->filter_context_.config_name);\n   828\u2192      status = FilterTrailersStatus::StopIteration;\n   829\u2192    }\n   830\u2192\n   831\u2192    processNewlyAddedMetadata();\n   832\u2192    // Check if the last filter has processed trailers\n   833\u2192    terminal_filter_reached = std::next(entry) == decoder_filters_.end();\n   834\u2192\n   835\u2192    if (!(*entry)->commonHandleAfterTrailersCallback(status) && !terminal_filter_reached) {"

((*entry)->end_stream_) {\n  1271\u2192      (*entry)->handle_->encodeComplete();\n  1272\u2192    }\n  1273\u2192\n  1274\u2192    if (!continue_iteration) {\n  1275\u2192      if handle the case where we have a header only response, but a filter adds a body\n  1282\u2192    // to it. We need to not raise end_stream = true to further filters during inline iteration.\n  1283\u2192    if (end_stream && buffered_response_data_ && continue_data_entry == encoder_filters_.end()) {\n values.\n  1289\u2192  // We could do this at the codec in order to prevent other places than the filter chain from\n  1290\u2192  // removing critical headers, but it will come with the implementation complexity.\n  1291\u2192  // See the previous attempt (#15658) for detail, and for now we choose to protect only against\n  1292\u2192  // filter chains.\n  1293\u2192  const auto status = HeaderUtility::checkRequiredResponseHeaders(headers);\n  1294\u2192  if (!status.ok()) {\n  1295\u2192    // If the check failed, then we reply with BadGateway, and stop the further &(*entry)->filter_context_);\n  1325\u2192    // If the filter pointed by entry has stopped for all frame type, stores metadata and returns.\n  1326\u2192    // If the filter pointed by entry hasn't returned from encodeHeaders, stores newly added\n  1327\u2192    // metadata in case encodeHeaders returns StopAllIteration. The latter can happen when headers\n  1328\u2192    // callbacks generate new metadata.\n  1329\u2192    if (!(*entry)->processed_headers_ || (*entry)->stoppedAll()) {\n  1330\u2192      (*entry)->getSavedResponseMetadata()->emplace_back(std::move(metadata_map_ptr));\n  1331\u2192      return;\n  1332\u2192    }\n  1333\u2192\n  1334\u2192    state_.filter_call_state_ |= FilterCallState::EncodeMetadata;\n  1335\u2192\n  1336\u2192    FilterMetadataStatus status = (*entry)->handle_->encodeMetadata(*metadata_map_ptr);\n  1337\u2192\n  1338\u2192    state_.filter_call_state_ &= ~FilterCallState::EncodeMetadata;\n  1339\u2192\n  1340\u2192    ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"encode metadata called: filter={} status={} metadata={}\", *this,\n  1341\u2192                     (*entry)->filter_context_.config_name, static_cast<uint64_t>(status),\n  1342\u2192                     *metadata_map_ptr);\n  1343\u2192\n  1344\u2192    if (status == FilterMetadataStatus::StopIterationForLocalReply) {\n  1345\u2192      // In case of a local reply, we do not continue encoding metadata. local reply)\n  1530\u2192    // we rely on the encoding of end_stream to close the stream.\n  1531\u2192    if (filter_manager_callbacks_.isHalfCloseEnabled() && !stopDecoderFilterChain()) {\n  1532\u2192      checkAndCloseStreamIfFullyClosed();\n  1533\u2192    }\n  1534\u2192  }\n  1535\u2192}\n  1536\u2192\n  1537\u2192void FilterManager::checkAndCloseStreamIfFullyClosed() {\n  1538\u2192  ASSERT(filter_manager_callbacks_.isHalfCloseEnabled());\n  1539\u2192  // When the independent half close is enabled the stream is always closed on error responses\n  1540\u2192  // from the server.\n  1541\u2192  if (filter_manager_callbacks_.responseHeaders().has_value()) {\n  1542\u2192    const uint64_t response_status =\n  1543\u2192        Http::Utility::getResponseStatus(filter_manager_callbacks_.responseHeaders().ref());\n  1544\u2192    const bool error_response =\n  1545\u2192        !(Http::CodeUtility::is2xx(response_status) || Http::CodeUtility::is1xx(response_status));\n  1546\u2192    // Abort the decoder filter if it has not yet been completed.\n  1547\u2192    if (error_response && !state_.decoder_filter_chain_complete_) {\n  1548\u2192      state_.decoder_filter_chain_aborted_ = true;\n  1549\u2192    }\n  1550\u2192  }"

"   901\u2192FilterManager::commonEncodePrefix(ActiveStreamEncoderFilter* filter, bool end_stream,\n   902\u2192                                  FilterIterationStartState filter_iteration_start_state) {\n   903\u2192  // Only do base state setting on the initial call. Subsequent calls for filtering do not touch\n   904\u2192  // the base state.\n   905\u2192  ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"commonEncodePrefix end_stream: {}, isHalfCloseEnabled: {}\", *this,\n   906\u2192                   end_stream, filter_manager_callbacks_.isHalfCloseEnabled());\n   907\u2192  if (filter == nullptr) {\n   908\u2192    if (end_stream) {\n   909\u2192      ASSERT(!state_.observed_encode_end_stream_);\n   910\u2192      state_.observed_encode_end_stream_ = true;\n   911\u2192\n   912\u2192      // When half close semantics are disabled, receiving end stream from the upstream causes\n   913\u2192      // decoder filter to stop, as neither filters nor upstream is interested in downstream data.\n   914\u2192      // half close is enabled in case tcp proxying is done with http1 encoder. In this case, we\n   915\u2192      // should not stop decoder filter chain when end_stream is true, as it will cause any data\n   916\u2192      // sent in the upstream direction to be\n   917\u2192      // dropped.\n   918\u2192      if (!filter_manager_callbacks_.isHalfCloseEnabled()) {\n   919\u2192        state_.decoder_filter_chain_aborted_ = true;\n   920\u2192      }\n   921\u2192    }\n   922\u2192    return encoder_filters_.begin();\n   923\u2192  }\n   924\u2192\n   925\u2192  if (filter_iteration_start_state == FilterIterationStartState::CanStartFromCurrent &&\n   926\u2192      (*(filter->entry()))->iterate_from_current_filter_) {\n   927\u2192    // The filter iteration has been stopped for all frame types, and now the iteration continues.\n   928\u2192    // The current filter's encoding callback has not be called. Call it now.\n   929\u2192    return filter->entry();\n   930\u2192  }\n   931\u2192  return std::next(filter->entry());\n   932\u2192}\n   933\u2192\n   934\u2192StreamDecoderFilters::Iterator\n   935\u2192FilterManager::commonDecodePrefix(ActiveStreamDecoderFilter* filter,\n   936\u2192                                  FilterIterationStartState filter_iteration_start_state) {\n   937\u2192  if (!filter) {\n   938\u2192    return decoder_filters_.begin();\n   939\u2192  }\n   940\u2192  if (filter_iteration_start_state == FilterIterationStartState::CanStartFromCurrent &&\n   941\u2192      (*(filter->entry()))->iterate_from_current_filter_) {\n   942\u2192    // The filter iteration has been stopped for all frame types, and now the iteration continues.\n   943\u2192    // The current filter's callback function has not been called. Call it now.\n   944\u2192    return filter->entry();\n   945\u2192  }\n   946\u2192  return std::next(filter->entry());\n   947\u2192}\n   948\u2192\n   949\u2192void DownstreamFilterManager::onLocalReply(StreamFilterBase::LocalReplyData& data) {\n   950\u2192  state_.under_on_local_reply_ = true;\n   951\u2192  filter_manager_callbacks_.onLocalReply(data.code_);\n   952\u2192\n   953\u2192  for (auto entry : filters_) {\n   954\u2192    if (entry->onLocalReply(data) == LocalErrorStatus::ContinueAndResetStream) {\n   955\u2192      data.reset_imminent_ = true;\n   956\u2192    }\n   957\u2192  }\n   958\u2192  state_.under_on_local_reply_ = false;\n   959\u2192}\n   960\u2192\n   961\u2192void DownstreamFilterManager::sendLocalReply(\n   962\u2192    Code code, absl::string_view body,\n   963\u2192    const std::function<void(ResponseHeaderMap& headers)>& modify_headers,\n   964\u2192    const absl::optional<Grpc::Status::GrpcStatus> grpc_status, (data.reset_imminent_) {\n   985\u2192    ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(debug, \"Resetting stream due to {}. onLocalReply requested reset.\", *this,\n   986\u2192                     details);\n   987\u2192 Logger::Loggable<Logger::Id::config> {\n    75\u2192public:\n    76\u2192  using FilterFactoriesList = FilterChainUtility::FilterFactoriesList;\n    77\u2192  using FilterConfigProviderManager =\n    78\u2192      Filter::FilterConfigProviderManager<Filter::HttpFilterFactoryCb, FilterCtx>;\n    79\u2192\n    80\u2192  FilterChainHelper(FilterConfigProviderManager& filter_config_provider_manager,\n 89\u2192  absl::Status processFilters(const FiltersList& filters, const std::string& prefix,\n    90\u2192                              const std::string& filter_chain_type,\n    91\u2192                              FilterFactoriesList& filter_factories) {\n    92\u2192\n    93\u2192    DependencyManager dependency_manager;\n    94\u2192    filter_factories.reserve(filters.size());\n    95\u2192 98\u2192                        filter_factories, dependency_manager);\n    99\u2192      if (!status.ok()) {\n   100\u2192        return status;\n   101\u2192      }\n   102\u2192    }\n   103\u2192 absl::Status\n   109\u2192  processFilter(const envoy::extensions::filters::network::http_connection_manager::v3::HttpFilter&\n   110\u2192                    proto_config,\n   111\u2192      DependencyManager& dependency_manager) {\n   114\u2192    ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"    {} filter #{}\", prefix, i);\n   115\u2192\n   116\u2192    const bool disabled_by_default = proto_config.disabled();\n   117\u2192\n   118\u2192    // Ensure the terminal filter will not be disabled by default. Because the terminal filter must\n   119\u2192    // be the last filter in the chain (ensured by 'validateTerminalFilters') and terminal flag of\n   120\u2192    // dynamic filter could not determined and may changed at runtime, so we check the last filter\n   121\u2192    // flag here as alternative.\n   122\u2192    if (last_filter_in_current_config && disabled_by_default) {\n   123\u2192      return absl::InvalidArgumentError(fmt::format(\n   124\u2192          \"Error: the last (terminal) filter ({}) in the chain cannot be disabled by default.\",\n   125\u2192          proto_config.name()));\n   126\u2192    }\n   127\u2192\n   128\u2192    if (proto_config.config_type_case() ==\n   129\u2192        envoy::extensions::filters::network::http_connection_manager::v3::HttpFilter::\n   130\u2192            ConfigTypeCase::kConfigDiscovery) {\n   131\u2192      return processDynamicFilterConfig(proto_config.name(), proto_config.config_discovery(),\n   132\u2192                                        filter_factories, filter_chain_type,\n   133\u2192                                        last_filter_in_current_config, disabled_by_default);\n   134\u2192    }\n   135\u2192\n   136\u2192    // Now see if there is a factory that will accept the config.\n   137\u2192    auto* factory = Config::Utility::getAndCheckFactory<NeutralNamedHttpFilterFactory>(\n   138\u2192        proto_config, proto_config.is_optional());\n   139\u2192    // null pointer returned only when the filter is optional, then skip all the processes.\n   140\u2192    if (factory == nullptr) {\n   141\u2192      ENVOY_LOG(warn, \"Didn't find a registered factory for the optional http filter {}\",\n   142\u2192                proto_config.name());\n   143\u2192      return absl::OkStatus();\n   144\u2192    }\n   145\u2192    ProtobufTypes::MessagePtr message = Config::Utility::translateToFactoryConfig(\n   146\u2192        proto_config, server_context_.messageValidationVisitor(), *factory);\n   147\u2192    absl::StatusOr<Http::FilterFactoryCb> callback_or_error =\n   148\u2192        factory->createFilterFactoryFromProto(*message, stats_prefix_, factory_context_);\n   149\u2192    if (!callback_or_error.status().ok()) {\n   150\u2192      return callback_or_error.status();\n   151\u2192    }\n   152\u2192    dependency_manager.registerFilter(factory->name(), *factory->dependencies());\n   153\u2192    const bool is_terminal = factory->isTerminalFilterByProto(*message, server_context_);\n   154\u2192    RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(Config::Utility::validateTerminalFilters(proto_config.name(), factory->name(),\n   155\u2192                                                              filter_chain_type, is_terminal,\n   156\u2192                                                              last_filter_in_current_config));\n   157\u2192    auto filter_config_provider = filter_config_provider_manager_.createStaticFilterConfigProvider(\n   158\u2192        callback_or_error.value(), proto_config.name());\n   159\u2192#ifdef ENVOY_ENABLE_YAML\n   160\u2192    ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"      name: {}\", filter_config_provider->name());\n   161\u2192    ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"    config: {}\",\n   162\u2192              MessageUtil::getJsonStringFromMessageOrError(\n   163\u2192                  static_cast<const Protobuf::Message&>(proto_config.typed_config())));\n   164\u2192#endif\n   165\u2192    filter_factories.push_back({std::move(filter_config_provider), disabled_by_default});\n   166\u2192    return absl::OkStatus();\n   167\u2192  }\n   168\u2192\n   169\u2192  absl::Status\n   170\u2192  processDynamicFilterConfig(const std::string& name,\n   171\u2192                             const envoy::config::core::v3::ExtensionConfigSource& config_discovery,\n   172\u2192                             FilterFactoriesList& filter_factories,\n   173\u2192                             const std::string& filter_chain_type,\n   174\u2192                             bool last_filter_in_current_config, bool disabled_by_default) {\n   175\u2192    ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"      dynamic filter name: {}\", name);\n   176\u2192    if (config_discovery.apply_default_config_without_warming() &&\n   177\u2192        !config_discovery.has_default_config()) {\n   178\u2192      return absl::InvalidArgumentError(fmt::format(\n   179\u2192          \"Error: filter config {} applied without warming but has no default config.\", name));\n   180\u2192    }\n   181\u2192    for (const auto& type_url : config_discovery.type_urls()) {\n   182\u2192      auto factory_type_url = TypeUtil::typeUrlToDescriptorFullName(type_url);\n   183\u2192      auto* factory = Registry::FactoryRegistry<NeutralNamedHttpFilterFactory>::getFactoryByType(\n   184\u2192          factory_type_url);\n   185\u2192      if (factory == nullptr) {\n   186\u2192        return absl::InvalidArgumentError(\n   187\u2192            fmt::format(\"Error: no factory found for a required type URL {}.\", factory_type_url));\n   188\u2192      }\n   189\u2192    }\n   190\u2192\n   191\u2192    auto filter_config_provider = filter_config_provider_manager_.createDynamicFilterConfigProvider(\n   192\u2192        config_discovery, name, server_context_, factory_context_, cluster_manager_,\n   193\u2192        last_filter_in_current_config, filter_chain_type, nullptr);\n   194\u2192    filter_factories.push_back({std::move(filter_config_provider), disabled_by_default});\n   195\u2192    return absl::OkStatus();\n   196\u2192  }\n   197\u2192\n   198\u2192  FilterConfigProviderManager& filter_config_provider_manager_;\n   199\u2192  Server::Configuration::ServerFactoryContext& server_context_;\n   200\u2192  Upstream::ClusterManager& cluster_manager_;\n   201\u2192  FilterCtx& factory_context_;\n   202\u2192  const std::string& stats_prefix_;\n   203\u2192};\n   204\u2192\n   205\u2192} // namespace Http\n   206\u2192} // namespace Envoy\n   207\u2192"
65. Task @ -
Input
{
  "description": "Explore router and upstream flow",
  "max_turns": 30,
  "prompt": "I need to understand how Envoy's router filter (terminal HTTP filter) forwards requests to upstream servers and how responses flow back. Explore thoroughly:\n\n1. In `source/common/router/`, find:\n   - The Router::Filter class - how it obtains the target cluster (look for route(), cluster(), getRouteFromEntries(), etc.)\n   - How a specific upstream host is selected (host selection, load balancing)\n   - The `UpstreamRequest` class - what it does, how it manages the upstream connection\n   - How the upstream filter chain works\n\n2. Specifically look for:\n   - `router.cc` / `router.h` - the main router filter implementation\n   - `upstream_request.cc` / `upstream_request.h` - the UpstreamRequest class\n   - How `decodeHeaders()` in the router triggers upstream connection setup\n   - How `onUpstreamHeaders()` or similar callbacks receive the response\n   - How the response flows back (encode path) through the filter chain to downstream\n\n3. Look for the upstream codec filter or upstream filter chain in `source/common/router/` or `source/extensions/upstreams/http/`\n\nProvide specific file paths, class names, function names, and line numbers for all findings.",
  "subagent_type": "Explore"
}
Output
{
  "agentId": "aa7f4a0",
  "content": [
    {
      "text": "Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me create a detailed report with all the key findings:\n\n## Comprehensive Analysis of Envoy Router Filter (Terminal HTTP Filter)\n\nBased on my thorough exploration of the Envoy router codebase, here's a detailed breakdown of how requests are forwarded to upstream servers and how responses flow back:\n\n---\n\n### 1. **ROUTER FILTER CLASS STRUCTURE**\n\n**File Path:** `/workspace/source/common/router/router.h` (Lines 308-662)\n\n**Main Class:** `Filter : public Http::StreamDecoderFilter, public Upstream::LoadBalancerContextBase, public RouterFilterInterface`\n\n**Key Components:**\n- **Line 607:** `Http::StreamDecoderFilterCallbacks* callbacks_` - Connection to downstream filter chain\n- **Line 608:** `RouteConstSharedPtr route_` - Matched route for this request\n- **Line 609:** `const RouteEntry* route_entry_` - Route entry with cluster information\n- **Line 610:** `Upstream::ClusterInfoConstSharedPtr cluster_` - Target upstream cluster info\n- **Line 618:** `std::list<UpstreamRequestPtr> upstream_requests_` - List of active upstream requests (supports hedging)\n- **Line 622:** `UpstreamRequest* final_upstream_request_` - The \"winning\" upstream request whose response is forwarded\n\n---\n\n### 2. **REQUEST DECODING PATH - ROUTE & CLUSTER OBTAINING**\n\n**Entry Point:** `Filter::decodeHeaders()` \n- **File:** `/workspace/source/common/router/router.cc`\n- **Line:** 445-704\n\n**Step 1: Route Matching (Lines 468-517)**\n```\nLine 468:  route_ = callbacks_->route();  // Match route from route config\nLine 506:  route_entry_ = route_->routeEntry();  // Get RouteEntry\nLine 519:  cluster = config_->cm_.getThreadLocalCluster(route_entry_->clusterName());\nLine 530:  cluster_ = cluster->info();  // Get ClusterInfoConstSharedPtr\n```\n\n**Step 2: Host Selection via Load Balancing (Lines 664-676)**\n```\nLine 664:  auto host_selection_response = cluster->chooseHost(this);\n           // This calls the load balancer with:\n           // - computeHashKey() - for hash-based routing\n           // - metadataMatchCriteria() - for subset load balancing\n           // - shouldSelectAnotherHost() - for retry logic\n           // - determinePriorityLoad() - for priority-based selection\n           // - hostSelectionRetryCount() - for host selection retries\n```\n\nThe `Filter` class is also a `Upstream::LoadBalancerContextBase` (Line 310), providing callbacks needed for load balancing decisions through methods like:\n- **Line 363-378:** `computeHashKey()` - Computes hash for consistent hashing\n- **Line 379-403:** `metadataMatchCriteria()` - Returns metadata for subset matching\n- **Line 410-419:** `shouldSelectAnotherHost()` - Consulted during retries\n- **Line 421-431:** `determinePriorityLoad()` - Modifies priority load on retries\n- **Line 433-438:** `hostSelectionRetryCount()` - Max host selection attempts\n\n**Step 3: Connection Pool Creation (Lines 724-847)**\n```\nLine 724:  std::unique_ptr<GenericConnPool> generic_conn_pool = createConnPool(*cluster, selected_host);\n           (Function at Line 905-948)\nLine 845-847: Creates UpstreamRequest with the connection pool\nLine 849:  upstream_requests_.front()->acceptHeadersFromRouter(end_stream);\n```\n\n---\n\n### 3. **UPSTREAM REQUEST CLASS - REQUEST FORWARDING**\n\n**File Path:** `/workspace/source/common/router/upstream_request.h` (Lines 66-263)\n\n**Class:** `UpstreamRequest : public UpstreamToDownstream, public GenericConnectionPoolCallbacks`\n\n**Key Members:**\n- **Line 195:** `std::unique_ptr<GenericConnPool> conn_pool_` - Connection pool for upstream\n- **Line 198:** `std::unique_ptr<GenericUpstream> upstream_` - Actual upstream stream (set by onPoolReady)\n- **Line 200:** `Upstream::HostDescriptionConstSharedPtr upstream_host_` - Selected upstream host\n- **Line 222:** `std::unique_ptr<UpstreamRequestFilterManagerCallbacks> filter_manager_callbacks_`\n- **Line 223:** `std::unique_ptr<Http::FilterManager> filter_manager_` - Upstream filter chain\n\n**Request Flow Methods:**\n\n1. **`acceptHeadersFromRouter()` (Line 80, implemented at Line 380)**\n   - Called by router filter with request headers\n   - **Line 404:** `conn_pool_->newStream(this)` - Initiates connection pool request\n   - **Line 433:** `filter_manager_->decodeHeaders(*parent_.downstreamHeaders(), end_stream)` - Passes through upstream filter chain\n\n2. **`acceptDataFromRouter()` (Line 81, implemented at Line 436)**\n   - Forwards request body to upstream filter chain\n   - **Line 440:** `filter_manager_->decodeData(data, end_stream)`\n\n3. **`acceptTrailersFromRouter()` (Line 82, implemented at Line 443)**\n   - Forwards request trailers\n   - **Line 448:** `filter_manager_->decodeTrailers(trailers)`\n\n---\n\n### 4. **CONNECTION POOL CALLBACK - UPSTREAM CONNECTION ESTABLISHMENT**\n\n**File Path:** `/workspace/source/common/router/upstream_request.cc`\n\n**Method:** `UpstreamRequest::onPoolReady()` (Line 584-696)\n\nWhen connection pool has a ready stream:\n- **Line 593:** `upstream_ = std::move(upstream)` - Store the upstream stream handle\n- **Line 633:** `onUpstreamHostSelected(host, true)` - Callback to router with selected host\n- **Line 681:** `stream_info_.setRequestHeaders(*parent_.downstreamHeaders())` - Log request headers\n- **Line 694-695:** Notifies upstream callbacks that connection is established\n\n---\n\n### 5. **UPSTREAM FILTER CHAIN**\n\n**File Path:** `/workspace/source/common/router/upstream_codec_filter.h` (Lines 28-98)\n\n**Purpose:** Terminal filter in upstream HTTP filter chain that:\n- Takes request headers/data/trailers from filter manager\n- Encodes them to the upstream codec\n- Registers CodecBridge to receive upstream responses\n- Forwards upstream responses back through filter manager\n\n**Key Method:** `UpstreamCodecFilter::decodeHeaders()` (Lines 52-94)\n- **Line 64:** `callbacks_->upstreamCallbacks()->upstream()->encodeHeaders(headers, end_stream)` - Sends headers to codec\n\n**CodecBridge (Lines 54-85):**\n- Receives decoded response from upstream codec\n- Acts as `UpstreamToDownstream` interface\n- **Line 189-190:** `filter_.callbacks_->encodeHeaders(std::move(headers), end_stream)` - Sends response through filter manager\n\n---\n\n### 6. **RESPONSE FLOW BACK - UPSTREAM CODEC FILTER BRIDGE**\n\n**File Path:** `/workspace/source/common/router/upstream_codec_filter.cc`\n\n**CodecBridge Methods** (receiving from upstream codec):\n\n1. **`CodecBridge::decodeHeaders()` (Line 147-191)**\n   - **Line 151-152:** Records timing: `onFirstUpstreamRxByteReceived()`\n   - **Line 189-190:** Passes response headers through filter manager: `encodeHeaders()`\n\n2. **`CodecBridge::decodeData()` (Line 194-197)**\n   - Passes response body through filter manager: `encodeData()`\n\n3. **`CodecBridge::decodeTrailers()` (Line 200-203)**\n   - **Line 201:** `maybeEndDecode(true)` - Record final timing\n   - **Line 202:** `encodeTrailers()` - Pass to filter manager\n\n---\n\n### 7. **UPSTREAM REQUEST - RESPONSE RECEPTION**\n\n**File Path:** `/workspace/source/common/router/upstream_request.cc`\n\n**Response Methods** (from upstream filter manager):\n\n1. **`UpstreamRequest::decodeHeaders()` (Line 267-307)**\n   - Called by filter manager after response headers pass through upstream filters\n   - **Line 303:** `maybeHandleDeferredReadDisable()` - Apply deferred read disabling\n   - **Line 306:** `parent_.onUpstreamHeaders(response_code, std::move(headers), *this, end_stream)` - Send to router\n\n2. **`UpstreamRequest::decodeData()` (Line 321-327)**\n   - Called with response body data\n   - **Line 326:** `parent_.onUpstreamData(data, *this, end_stream)` - Send to router\n\n3. **`UpstreamRequest::decodeTrailers()` (Line 329-340)**\n   - Called with response trailers\n   - **Line 339:** `parent_.onUpstreamTrailers(std::move(trailers), *this)` - Send to router\n\n---\n\n### 8. **ROUTER FILTER - RESPONSE FORWARDING TO DOWNSTREAM**\n\n**File Path:** `/workspace/source/common/router/router.cc`\n\n**Response Callbacks from UpstreamRequest:**\n\n1. **`Filter::onUpstreamHeaders()` (Line 1652-1806)**\n   - **Line 1658:** `modify_headers_(*headers)` - Apply configured header modifications\n   - **Line 1688-1728:** Check retry policy and decide whether to retry\n   - **Line 1796:** `final_upstream_request_ = &upstream_request` - Mark as final response\n   - **Line 1799:** `resetOtherUpstreams(upstream_request)` - Cancel other hedged requests\n   - **Line 1804-1805:** `callbacks_->encodeHeaders(std::move(headers), end_stream)` - Forward to downstream\n\n2. **`Filter::onUpstreamData()` (Line 1808-1823)**\n   - **Line 1822:** `callbacks_->encodeData(data, end_stream)` - Forward response body downstream\n\n3. **`Filter::onUpstreamTrailers()` (Line 1825-1847)**\n   - **Line 1846:** `callbacks_->encodeTrailers(std::move(trailers))` - Forward trailers downstream\n\n4. **`Filter::onUpstreamComplete()` (Line 1853-1910)**\n   - Records timing and stats after response complete\n   - **Line 1862:** Records response time for stats\n   - **Line 1873:** `upstream_request.upstreamHost()->outlierDetector().putResponseTime(response_time)` - Outlier detection\n\n---\n\n### 9. **KEY DATA STRUCTURES & INTERFACES**\n\n**UpstreamToDownstream Interface** (codec response receiver):\n- Defined in envoy/http/conn_pool.h\n- Implemented by: `UpstreamCodecFilter::CodecBridge`\n- Methods: `decode1xxHeaders()`, `decodeHeaders()`, `decodeData()`, `decodeTrailers()`, `onResetStream()`\n\n**GenericConnectionPoolCallbacks** (pool state callbacks):\n- Implemented by: `UpstreamRequest`\n- **Line 116-123:** `onPoolFailure()` and `onPoolReady()` - Pool lifecycle\n\n**RouterFilterInterface** (upstream callbacks):\n- Implemented by: `Filter`\n- Methods at Lines 485-501: `onUpstream1xxHeaders()`, `onUpstreamHeaders()`, `onUpstreamData()`, `onUpstreamTrailers()`, `onUpstreamMetadata()`, `onUpstreamReset()`, etc.\n\n---\n\n### 10. **REQUEST/RESPONSE FLOW DIAGRAM**\n\n```\nDOWNSTREAM REQUEST\n        \u2193\nFilter::decodeHeaders() [Line 445]\n    \u251c\u2500 Match route [Line 468]\n    \u251c\u2500 Get cluster [Line 519]\n    \u251c\u2500 Load balancer chooses host [Line 664]\n    \u2514\u2500 continueDecodeHeaders() [Line 714]\n        \u251c\u2500 Create connection pool [Line 724]\n        \u2514\u2500 Create UpstreamRequest [Line 845]\n            \u2514\u2500 acceptHeadersFromRouter() [Line 380]\n                \u251c\u2500 newStream() on conn_pool [Line 404]\n                \u2514\u2500 filter_manager_->decodeHeaders() [Line 433]\n                    \u2514\u2500 UpstreamCodecFilter::decodeHeaders() [Line 52]\n                        \u2514\u2500 upstream_->encodeHeaders() [Line 64]\n                            \u2193\n                        UPSTREAM SERVER\n                            \u2193\nUPSTREAM RESPONSE\n        \u2193\nCodec \u2192 CodecBridge::decodeHeaders() [Line 147]\n    \u2514\u2500 filter_manager_->encodeHeaders() [Line 189]\n        \u2514\u2500 UpstreamRequest::decodeHeaders() [Line 267]\n            \u2514\u2500 parent_.onUpstreamHeaders() [Line 306]\n                \u2514\u2500 Filter::onUpstreamHeaders() [Line 1652]\n                    \u2514\u2500 callbacks_->encodeHeaders() [Line 1804]\n                        \u2193\n                    DOWNSTREAM RESPONSE\n```\n\n---\n\n### 11. **IMPORTANT FILE PATHS SUMMARY**\n\n| Component | File | Key Lines |\n|-----------|------|-----------|\n| Router Filter Main | `/workspace/source/common/router/router.h` | 308-662 |\n| Router Implementation | `/workspace/source/common/router/router.cc` | 445-1910 |\n| decodeHeaders() | `/workspace/source/common/router/router.cc` | 445-704 |\n| continueDecodeHeaders() | `/workspace/source/common/router/router.cc` | 714-903 |\n| createConnPool() | `/workspace/source/common/router/router.cc` | 905-948 |\n| onUpstreamHeaders() | `/workspace/source/common/router/router.cc` | 1652-1806 |\n| onUpstreamData() | `/workspace/source/common/router/router.cc` | 1808-1823 |\n| UpstreamRequest Header | `/workspace/source/common/router/upstream_request.h` | 66-263 |\n| UpstreamRequest Implementation | `/workspace/source/common/router/upstream_request.cc` | 1-900 |\n| acceptHeadersFromRouter() | `/workspace/source/common/router/upstream_request.cc` | 380-434 |\n| onPoolReady() | `/workspace/source/common/router/upstream_request.cc` | 584-696 |\n| decodeHeaders() | `/workspace/source/common/router/upstream_request.cc` | 267-307 |\n| UpstreamCodecFilter Header | `/workspace/source/common/router/upstream_codec_filter.h` | 28-98 |\n| UpstreamCodecFilter Implementation | `/workspace/source/common/router/upstream_codec_filter.cc` | 1-279 |\n| CodecFilter::decodeHeaders() | `/workspace/source/common/router/upstream_codec_filter.cc` | 52-94 |\n| CodecBridge::decodeHeaders() | `/workspace/source/common/router/upstream_codec_filter.cc` | 147-191 |\n\n---\n\n### 12. **CRITICAL FLOW SUMMARY**\n\n**Upstream Selection:**\n- Route matching \u2192 Cluster lookup \u2192 Host selection via load balancer (with hash, subset matching, retry logic)\n\n**Request Forwarding:**\n- Router decodeHeaders \u2192 Create UpstreamRequest \u2192 Pass through upstream filter chain \u2192 UpstreamCodecFilter encodes to codec\n\n**Response Path:**\n- Codec decodes response \u2192 CodecBridge passes through upstream filter chain \u2192 UpstreamRequest callbacks \u2192 Router encodes downstream \u2192 Downstream filter chain\n\nThis architecture supports advanced features like request hedging (multiple upstream_requests_), retry logic, internal redirects, and customizable upstream HTTP filters.",
      "type": "text"
    }

    const Http::HeaderEntry* entry_;\n    71\u2192    };\n    72\u2192\n    73\u2192    /**\n    74\u2192     * Determine whether a given header's value passes the strict validation\n    75\u2192     * defined for that header.\n    76\u2192     * @param headers supplies the headers from which to get the target header.\n    77\u2192     * @param target_header is the header to be validated.\n    78\u2192     * @return HeaderCheckResult containing the entry for @param target_header\n    79\u2192     *         and valid_ set to FALSE if @param target_header is set to an\n    80\u2192     *         invalid value. If @param target_header doesn't appear in\n    81\u2192     *         @param headers, return a result with valid_ set to TRUE.\n    82\u2192     */\n    83\u2192    static const HeaderCheckResult checkHeader(Http::RequestHeaderMap& headers,\n    84\u2192                                               const Http::LowerCaseString& target_header);\n    85\u2192\n    86\u2192    using ParseRetryFlagsFunc = std::function<std::pair<uint32_t, bool>(absl::string_view)>;\n    87\u2192\n    88\u2192  private:\n    89\u2192    static HeaderCheckResult hasValidRetryFields(const Http::HeaderEntry* header_entry,\n    90\u2192                                                 const ParseRetryFlagsFunc& parse_fn) {\n    91\u2192      HeaderCheckResult r;\n    92\u2192      if (header_entry) {\n    93\u2192        const auto flags_and_validity = parse_fn(header_entry->value().getStringView());\n    94\u2192        r.valid_ = flags_and_validity.second;\n    95\u2192        r.entry_ = header_entry;\n    96\u2192      }\n    97\u2192      return r;\n    98\u2192    }\n    99\u2192\n   100\u2192    static HeaderCheckResult isInteger(const Http::HeaderEntry* header_entry) {\n   101\u2192      HeaderCheckResult r;\n   102\u2192      if (header_entry) {\n   103\u2192        uint64_t out;\n   104\u2192        r.valid_ = absl::SimpleAtoi(header_entry->value().getStringView(), &out);\n   105\u2192        r.entry_ = header_entry;\n   106\u2192      }\n   107\u2192      return r;\n   108\u2192    }\n   109\u2192  };\n   110\u2192\n   111\u2192  /**\n   112\u2192   * Returns response_time / timeout, as a  percentage as [0, 100]. Returns 0\n   113\u2192   * if there is no timeout.\n   114\u2192   * @param response_time supplies the response time thus far.\n   115\u2192   * @param timeout supplies  the timeout to get the percentage of.\n   116\u2192   * @return the percentage of timeout [0, 100] for stats use.\n   117\u2192   */\n   118\u2192  static uint64_t percentageOfTimeout(const std::chrono::milliseconds response_time,\n   119\u2192                                      const std::chrono::milliseconds timeout);\n   120\u2192\n   121\u2192  /**\n   122\u2192   * Set the :scheme header using the best information available. In order this is\n   123\u2192   * - whether the upstream connection is using TLS if use_upstream is true\n   124\u2192   * - existing scheme header if valid\n   125\u2192   * - x-forwarded-proto header if valid\n   126\u2192   * - whether the downstream connection is using TLS\n   127\u2192   */\n   128\u2192  static void setUpstreamScheme(Http::RequestHeaderMap& headers, bool downstream_ssl,\n   129\u2192                                bool upstream_ssl, bool use_upstream);\n   130\u2192\n   131\u2192  /**\n   132\u2192   * Determine whether a request should be shadowed.\n   133\u2192   * @param policy supplies the route's shadow policy.\n   134\u2192   * @param runtime supplies the runtime to lookup the shadow key in.\n   135\u2192   * @param stable_random supplies the random number to use when determining whether shadowing\n   136\u2192   *        should take place.\n   137\u2192   * @return TRUE if shadowing should take place.\n   138\u2192   */\n   139\u2192  static bool shouldShadow(const ShadowPolicy& policy, Runtime::Loader& runtime,\n   140\u2192                           uint64_t stable_random);\n   141\u2192\n   142\u2192  /**\n   143\u2192   * Determine the final timeout to use based on the route as well as the request headers.\n   144\u2192   * @param route supplies the request route.\n   145\u2192   * @param request_headers supplies the request headers.\n   146\u2192   * @param insert_envoy_expected_request_timeout_ms insert\n   147\u2192   *        x-envoy-expected-request-timeout-ms?\n   148\u2192   * @param grpc_request tells if the request is a gRPC request.\n   149\u2192   * @return TimeoutData for both the global and per try timeouts.\n   150\u2192   */\n   151\u2192  static TimeoutData finalTimeout(const RouteEntry& route, Http::RequestHeaderMap& request_headers,\n   152\u2192                                  bool insert_envoy_expected_request_timeout_ms, bool grpc_request,\n   153\u2192                                  bool per_try_timeout_hedging_enabled,\n   154\u2192                                  bool respect_expected_rq_timeout);\n   155\u2192\n   156\u2192  /**\n   157\u2192   * Set the x-envoy-expected-request-timeout-ms and grpc-timeout headers if needed.\n   158\u2192   * @param elapsed_time time elapsed since completion of the downstream request\n   159\u2192   * @param timeout final TimeoutData to use for the request\n   160\u2192   * @param request_headers the request headers to modify\n   161\u2192   * @param insert_envoy_expected_request_timeout_ms insert\n   162\u2192   *        x-envoy-expected-request-timeout-ms?\n   163\u2192   * @param grpc_request tells if the request is a gRPC request.\n   164\u2192   * @param per_try_timeout_headging_enabled is request hedging enabled?\n   165\u2192   */\n   166\u2192  static void setTimeoutHeaders(uint64_t elapsed_time, const TimeoutData& timeout,\n   167\u2192                                const RouteEntry& route, Http::RequestHeaderMap& request_headers,\n   168\u2192                                bool insert_envoy_expected_request_timeout_ms, bool grpc_request,\n   169\u2192                                bool per_try_timeout_hedging_enabled);\n   170\u2192\n   171\u2192  /**\n   172\u2192   * Try to parse a header entry that may have a timeout field\n   173\u2192   *\n   174\u2192   * @param header_timeout_entry header entry which may contain a timeout value.\n   175\u2192   * @return result timeout value from header. It will return nullopt if parse failed.\n   176\u2192   */\n   177\u2192  static absl::optional<std::chrono::milliseconds>\n   178\u2192  tryParseHeaderTimeout(const Http::HeaderEntry& header_timeout_entry);\n   179\u2192\n   180\u2192  /**\n   181\u2192   * Try to set global timeout.\n   182\u2192   *\n   183\u2192   * @param header_timeout_entry header entry which may contain a timeout value.\n   184\u2192   * @param timeout timeout data to set from header timeout entry.\n   185\u2192   */\n   186\u2192  static void trySetGlobalTimeout(const Http::HeaderEntry& header_timeout_entry,\n   187\u2192                                  TimeoutData& timeout);\n   188\u2192\n   189\u2192  /**\n   190\u2192   * Determine the final hedging settings after applying randomized behavior.\n   191\u2192   * @param route supplies the request route.\n   192\u2192   * @param request_headers supplies the request headers.\n   193\u2192   * @return HedgingParams the final parameters to use for request hedging.\n   194\u2192   */\n   195\u2192  static HedgingParams finalHedgingParams(const RouteEntry& route,\n   196\u2192                                          Http::RequestHeaderMap& request_headers);\n   197\u2192};\n   198\u2192\n   199\u2192/**\n   200\u2192 * Configuration for the router filter.\n   201\u2192 */\n   202\u2192class FilterConfig : public Http::FilterChainFactory {\n   203\u2192public:\n   204\u2192  FilterConfig(Server::Configuration::CommonFactoryContext& factory_context,\n   205\u2192               Stats::StatName stat_prefix, const LocalInfo::LocalInfo& local_info,\n   206\u2192               Stats::Scope& scope, Upstream::ClusterManager& cm, Runtime::Loader& runtime,\n   207\u2192               Random::RandomGenerator& random, ShadowWriterPtr&& shadow_writer,\n   208\u2192               bool emit_dynamic_stats, bool start_child_span, bool suppress_envoy_headers,\n   209\u2192               bool respect_expected_rq_timeout, bool suppress_grpc_request_failure_code_stats,\n   210\u2192               bool flush_upstream_log_on_upstream_stream,\n   211\u2192               const Protobuf::RepeatedPtrField<std::string>& strict_check_headers,\n   212\u2192               TimeSource& time_source, Http::Context& http_context,\n   213\u2192               Router::Context& router_context)\n   214\u2192      : factory_context_(factory_context), router_context_(router_context), scope_(scope),\n   215\u2192        local_info_(local_info), cm_(cm), runtime_(runtime),\n   216\u2192        default_stats_(router_context_.statNames(), scope_, stat_prefix),\n   217\u2192        async_stats_(router_context_.statNames(), scope, http_context.asyncClientStatPrefix()),\n   218\u2192        random_(random), emit_dynamic_stats_(emit_dynamic_stats),\n   219\u2192        start_child_span_(start_child_span), suppress_envoy_headers_(suppress_envoy_headers),\n   220\u2192        respect_expected_rq_timeout_(respect_expected_rq_timeout),\n   221\u2192        suppress_grpc_request_failure_code_stats_(suppress_grpc_request_failure_code_stats),\n   222\u2192        flush_upstream_log_on_upstream_stream_(flush_upstream_log_on_upstream_stream),\n   223\u2192        http_context_(http_context), zone_name_(local_info_.zoneStatName()),\n   224\u2192        shadow_writer_(std::move(shadow_writer)), time_source_(time_source) {\n   225\u2192    if (!strict_check_headers.empty()) {\n   226\u2192      strict_check_headers_ = std::make_unique<HeaderVector>();\n   227\u2192      for (const auto& header : strict_check_headers) {\n   228\u2192        strict_check_headers_->emplace_back(Http::LowerCaseString(header));\n   229\u2192      }\n   230\u2192    }\n   231\u2192  }\n   232\u2192\n   233\u2192  static absl::StatusOr<std::unique_ptr<FilterConfig>>\n   234\u2192  create(Stats::StatName stat_prefix, Server::Configuration::FactoryContext& context,\n   235\u2192         ShadowWriterPtr&& shadow_writer,\n   236\u2192         const envoy::extensions::filters::http::router::v3::Router& config);\n   237\u2192\n   238\u2192protected:\n   239\u2192  FilterConfig(Stats::StatName stat_prefix, Server::Configuration::FactoryContext& context,\n   240\u2192               ShadowWriterPtr&& shadow_writer,\n   241\u2192               const envoy::extensions::filters::http::router::v3::Router& config,\n   242\u2192               absl::Status& creation_status);\n   243\u2192\n   244\u2192public:\n   245\u2192  bool createFilterChain(\n   246\u2192      Http::FilterChainManager& manager,\n   247\u2192      const Http::FilterChainOptions& options = Http::EmptyFilterChainOptions{}) const override {\n   248\u2192    // Currently there is no default filter chain, so only_create_if_configured true doesn't make\n   249\u2192    // sense.\n   250\u2192    if (upstream_http_filter_factories_.empty()) {\n   251\u2192      return false;\n   252\u2192    }\n   253\u2192    Http::FilterChainUtility::createFilterChainForFactories(manager, options,\n   254\u2192                                                            upstream_http_filter_factories_);\n   255\u2192    return true;\n   256\u2192  }\n   257\u2192\n   258\u2192  bool createUpgradeFilterChain(absl::string_view, const UpgradeMap*, Http::FilterChainManager&,\n   259\u2192                                const Http::FilterChainOptions&) const override {\n   260\u2192    // Upgrade filter chains not yet supported for upstream HTTP filters.\n   261\u2192    return false;\n   262\u2192  }\n   263\u2192\n   264\u2192  using HeaderVector = std::vector<Http::LowerCaseString>;\n   265\u2192  using HeaderVectorPtr = std::unique_ptr<HeaderVector>;\n   266\u2192\n   267\u2192  ShadowWriter& shadowWriter() { return *shadow_writer_; }\n   268\u2192  TimeSource& timeSource() { return time_source_; }\n   269\u2192\n   270\u2192  Server::Configuration::CommonFactoryContext& factory_context_;\n   271\u2192  Router::Context& router_context_;\n   272\u2192  Stats::Scope& scope_;\n   273\u2192  const LocalInfo::LocalInfo& local_info_;\n   274\u2192  Upstream::ClusterManager& cm_;\n   275\u2192  Runtime::Loader& runtime_;\n   276\u2192  FilterStats default_stats_;\n   277\u2192  FilterStats async_stats_;\n   278\u2192  Random::RandomGenerator& random_;\n   279\u2192  const bool emit_dynamic_stats_;\n   280\u2192  const bool start_child_span_;\n   281\u2192  const bool suppress_envoy_headers_;\n   282\u2192  const bool respect_expected_rq_timeout_;\n   283\u2192  const bool suppress_grpc_request_failure_code_stats_;\n   284\u2192  // TODO(xyu-stripe): Make this a bitset to keep cluster memory footprint down.\n   285\u2192  HeaderVectorPtr strict_check_headers_;\n   286\u2192  const bool flush_upstream_log_on_upstream_stream_;\n   287\u2192  absl::optional<std::chrono::milliseconds> upstream_log_flush_interval_;\n   288\u2192  std::list<AccessLog::InstanceSharedPtr> upstream_logs_;\n   289\u2192  Http::Context& http_context_;\n   290\u2192  Stats::StatName zone_name_;\n   291\u2192  Stats::StatName empty_stat_name_;\n   292\u2192  std::unique_ptr<Server::Configuration::UpstreamFactoryContext> upstream_ctx_;\n   293\u2192  Http::FilterChainUtility::FilterFactoriesList upstream_http_filter_factories_;\n   294\u2192\n   295\u2192private:\n   296\u2192  ShadowWriterPtr shadow_writer_;\n   297\u2192  TimeSource& time_source_;\n   298\u2192};\n   299\u2192\n   300\u2192using FilterConfigSharedPtr = std::shared_ptr<FilterConfig>;\n   301\u2192\n   302\u2192class UpstreamRequest;\n   303\u2192using UpstreamRequestPtr = std::unique_ptr<UpstreamRequest>;\n   304\u2192\n   305\u2192/**\n   306\u2192 * Service routing filter.\n   307\u2192 */\n   308\u2192class Filter : Logger::Loggable<Logger::Id::router>,\n   309\u2192               public Http::StreamDecoderFilter,\n   310\u2192               public Upstream::LoadBalancerContextBase,\n   311\u2192               public RouterFilterInterface {\n   312\u2192public:\n   313\u2192  Filter(const FilterConfigSharedPtr& config, FilterStats& stats)\n   314\u2192      : config_(config), stats_(stats), grpc_request_(false), exclude_http_code_stats_(false),\n   315\u2192        downstream_response_started_(false), downstream_end_stream_(false), is_retry_(false),\n   316\u2192        request_buffer_overflowed_(false), streaming_shadows_(Runtime::runtimeFeatureEnabled(\n   317\u2192                                               \"envoy.reloadable_features.streaming_shadow\")),\n   318\u2192        allow_multiplexed_upstream_half_close_(Runtime::runtimeFeatureEnabled(\n   319\u2192            \"envoy.reloadable_features.allow_multiplexed_upstream_half_close\")),\n   320\u2192        upstream_request_started_(false), orca_load_report_received_(false) {}\n   321\u2192\n   322\u2192  ~Filter() override;\n   323\u2192\n   324\u2192  static StreamInfo::CoreResponseFlag\n   325\u2192  streamResetReasonToResponseFlag(Http::StreamResetReason reset_reason);\n   326\u2192\n   327\u2192  // Http::StreamFilterBase\n   328\u2192  void onDestroy() override;\n   329\u2192\n   330\u2192  Http::LocalErrorStatus onLocalReply(const LocalReplyData&) override {\n   331\u2192    // If there's a local reply (e.g. timeout) during host selection, cancel host\n   332\u2192    // selection.\n   333\u2192    if (host_selection_cancelable_) {\n   334\u2192      host_selection_cancelable_->cancel();\n   335\u2192      host_selection_cancelable_.reset();\n   336\u2192    }\n   337\u2192\n   338\u2192    // Clean up the upstream_requests_.\n   339\u2192    if (Runtime::runtimeFeatureEnabled(\n   340\u2192            \"envoy.reloadable_features.router_filter_resetall_on_local_reply\")) {\n   341\u2192      resetAll();\n   342\u2192    }\n   343\u2192    return Http::LocalErrorStatus::Continue;\n   344\u2192  }\n   345\u2192\n   346\u2192  // Http::StreamDecoderFilter\n   347\u2192  Http::FilterHeadersStatus decodeHeaders(Http::RequestHeaderMap& headers,\n   348\u2192                                          bool end_stream) override;\n   349\u2192\n   350\u2192  Http::FilterHeadersStatus\n   351\u2192  continueDecodeHeaders(Upstream::ThreadLocalCluster* cluster, Http::RequestHeaderMap& headers,\n   352\u2192                        bool end_stream,\n   353\u2192                        std::function<void(Http::ResponseHeaderMap&)> modify_headers,\n   354\u2192                        bool* should_continue_decoding, Upstream::HostConstSharedPtr&& host,\n   355\u2192                        absl::optional<std::string> host_selection_detailsi = {});\n   356\u2192\n   357\u2192  Http::FilterDataStatus decodeData(Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream) override;\n   358\u2192  Http::FilterTrailersStatus decodeTrailers(Http::RequestTrailerMap& trailers) override;\n   359\u2192  Http::FilterMetadataStatus decodeMetadata(Http::MetadataMap& metadata_map) override;\n   360\u2192  void setDecoderFilterCallbacks(Http::StreamDecoderFilterCallbacks& callbacks) override;\n   361\u2192\n   362\u2192  // Upstream::LoadBalancerContext\n   363\u2192  absl::optional<uint64_t> computeHashKey() override {\n   364\u2192    if (route_entry_ && downstream_headers_) {\n   365\u2192      auto hash_policy = route_entry_->hashPolicy();\n   366\u2192      if (hash_policy) {\n   367\u2192        return hash_policy->generateHash(\n   368\u2192            callbacks_->streamInfo().downstreamAddressProvider().remoteAddress().get(),\n   369\u2192            *downstream_headers_,\n   370\u2192            [this](const std::string& key, const std::string& path, std::chrono::seconds max_age,\n   371\u2192                   Http::CookieAttributeRefVector attributes) {\n   372\u2192              return addDownstreamSetCookie(key, path, max_age, attributes);\n   373\u2192            },\n   374\u2192            callbacks_->streamInfo().filterState());\n   375\u2192      }\n   376\u2192    }\n   377\u2192    return {};\n   378\u2192  }\n   379\u2192  const Router::MetadataMatchCriteria* metadataMatchCriteria() override {\n   380\u2192    if (route_entry_) {\n   381\u2192      // Have we been called before? If so, there's no need to recompute because\n   382\u2192      // by the time this method is called for the first time, route_entry_ should\n   383\u2192      // not change anymore.\n   384\u2192      if (metadata_match_ != nullptr) {\n   385\u2192        return metadata_match_.get();\n   386\u2192      }\n   387\u2192\n   388\u2192      // The request's metadata, if present, takes precedence over the route's.\n   389\u2192      const auto& request_metadata = callbacks_->streamInfo().dynamicMetadata().filter_metadata();\n   390\u2192      const auto filter_it = request_metadata.find(Envoy::Config::MetadataFilters::get().ENVOY_LB);\n   391\u2192      if (filter_it != request_metadata.end()) {\n   392\u2192        if (route_entry_->metadataMatchCriteria() != nullptr) {\n   393\u2192          metadata_match_ =\n   394\u2192              route_entry_->metadataMatchCriteria()->mergeMatchCriteria(filter_it->second);\n   395\u2192        } else {\n   396\u2192          metadata_match_ = std::make_unique<Router::MetadataMatchCriteriaImpl>(filter_it->second);\n   397\u2192        }\n   398\u2192        return metadata_match_.get();\n   399\u2192      }\n   400\u2192      return route_entry_->metadataMatchCriteria();\n   401\u2192    }\n   402\u2192    return nullptr;\n   403\u2192  }\n   404\u2192  const Network::Connection* downstreamConnection() const override {\n   405\u2192    return callbacks_->connection().ptr();\n   406\u2192  }\n   407\u2192  StreamInfo::StreamInfo* requestStreamInfo() const override { return &callbacks_->streamInfo(); }\n   408\u2192  const Http::RequestHeaderMap* downstreamHeaders() const override { return downstream_headers_; }\n   409\u2192\n   410\u2192  bool shouldSelectAnotherHost(const Upstream::Host& host) override {\n   411\u2192    // We only care about host selection when performing a retry, at which point we consult the\n   412\u2192    // RetryState to see if we're configured to avoid certain hosts during retries.\n   413\u2192    if (!is_retry_) {\n   414\u2192      return false;\n   415\u2192    }\n   416\u2192\n   417\u2192    ASSERT(retry_state_);\n   418\u2192    return retry_state_->shouldSelectAnotherHost(host);\n   419\u2192  }\n   420\u2192\n   421\u2192  const Upstream::HealthyAndDegradedLoad& determinePriorityLoad(\n   422\u2192      const Upstream::PrioritySet& priority_set,\n   423\u2192      const Upstream::HealthyAndDegradedLoad& original_priority_load,\n   424\u2192      const Upstream::RetryPriority::PriorityMappingFunc& priority_mapping_func) override {\n   425\u2192    // We only modify the priority load on retries.\n   426\u2192    if (!is_retry_) {\n   427\u2192      return original_priority_load;\n   428\u2192    }\n   429\u2192    return retry_state_->priorityLoadForRetry(priority_set, original_priority_load,\n   430\u2192                                              priority_mapping_func);\n   431\u2192  }\n   432\u2192\n   433\u2192  uint32_t hostSelectionRetryCount() const override {\n   434\u2192    if (!is_retry_) {\n   435\u2192      return 1;\n   436\u2192    }\n   437\u2192    return retry_state_->hostSelectionMaxAttempts();\n   438\u2192  }\n   439\u2192\n   440\u2192  Network::Socket::OptionsSharedPtr upstreamSocketOptions() const override {\n   441\u2192    return (upstream_options_ != nullptr) ? upstream_options_\n   442\u2192                                          : callbacks_->getUpstreamSocketOptions();\n   443\u2192  }\n   444\u2192\n   445\u2192  Network::TransportSocketOptionsConstSharedPtr upstreamTransportSocketOptions() const override {\n   446\u2192    return transport_socket_options_;\n   447\u2192  }\n   448\u2192\n   449\u2192  absl::optional<OverrideHost> overrideHostToSelect() const override {\n   450\u2192    if (is_retry_) {\n   451\u2192      return {};\n   452\u2192    }\n   453\u2192    return callbacks_->upstreamOverrideHost();\n   454\u2192  }\n   455\u2192\n   456\u2192  void onAsyncHostSelection(Upstream::HostConstSharedPtr&& host, std::string&& details) override;\n   457\u2192\n   458\u2192  /**\n   459\u2192   * Set a computed cookie to be sent with the downstream headers.\n   460\u2192   * @param key supplies the size of the cookie\n   461\u2192   * @param max_age the lifetime of the cookie\n   462\u2192   * @param  path the path of the cookie, or \"\"\n   463\u2192   * @return std::string the value of the new cookie\n   464\u2192   */\n   465\u2192  std::string addDownstreamSetCookie(const std::string& key, const std::string& path,\n   466\u2192                                     std::chrono::seconds max_age,\n   467\u2192                                     Http::CookieAttributeRefVector attributes) {\n   468\u2192    // The cookie value should be the same per connection so that if multiple\n   469\u2192    // streams race on the same path, they all receive the same cookie.\n   470\u2192    // Since the downstream port is part of the hashed value, multiple HTTP1\n   471\u2192    // connections can receive different cookies if they race on requests.\n   472\u2192    std::string value;\n   473\u2192    const Network::Connection* conn = downstreamConnection();\n   474\u2192    // Need to check for null conn if this is ever used by Http::AsyncClient in the future.\n   475\u2192    value = conn->connectionInfoProvider().remoteAddress()->asString() +\n   476\u2192            conn->connectionInfoProvider().localAddress()->asString();\n   477\u2192\n   478\u2192    const std::string cookie_value = Hex::uint64ToHex(HashUtil::xxHash64(value));\n   479\u2192    downstream_set_cookies_.emplace_back(\n   480\u2192        Http::Utility::makeSetCookieValue(key, cookie_value, path, max_age, true, attributes));\n   481\u2192    return cookie_value;\n   482\u2192  }\n   483\u2192\n   484\u2192  // RouterFilterInterface\n   485\u2192  void onUpstream1xxHeaders(Http::ResponseHeaderMapPtr&& headers,\n   486\u2192                            UpstreamRequest& upstream_request) override;\n   487\u2192  void onUpstreamHeaders(uint64_t response_code, Http::ResponseHeaderMapPtr&& headers,\n   488\u2192                         UpstreamRequest& upstream_request, bool end_stream) override;\n   489\u2192  void onUpstreamData(Buffer::Instance& data, UpstreamRequest& upstream_request,\n   490\u2192                      bool end_stream) override;\n   491\u2192  void onUpstreamTrailers(Http::ResponseTrailerMapPtr&& trailers,\n   492\u2192                          UpstreamRequest& upstream_request) override;\n   493\u2192  void onUpstreamMetadata(Http::MetadataMapPtr&& metadata_map) override;\n   494\u2192  void onUpstreamReset(Http::StreamResetReason reset_reason, absl::string_view transport_failure,\n   495\u2192                       UpstreamRequest& upstream_request) override;\n   496\u2192  void onUpstreamHostSelected(Upstream::HostDescriptionConstSharedPtr host,\n   497\u2192                              bool pool_success) override;\n   498\u2192  void onPerTryTimeout(UpstreamRequest& upstream_request) override;\n   499\u2192  void onPerTryIdleTimeout(UpstreamRequest& upstream_request) override;\n   500\u2192  void onStreamMaxDurationReached(UpstreamRequest& upstream_request) override;\n   501\u2192  Http::StreamDecoderFilterCallbacks* callbacks() override { return callbacks_; }\n   502\u2192 \"source/common/tracing/null_span_impl.h\"\n    28\u2192#include \"source/extensions/filters/http/common/factory_base.h\"\n    29\u2192\n    30\u2192namespace Envoy {\n    31\u2192namespace Router {\n    32\u2192\n    33\u2192class GenericUpstream;\n    34\u2192class GenericConnectionPoolCallbacks;\n    35\u2192class RouterFilterInterface;\n    36\u2192class UpstreamRequest;\n    37\u2192class UpstreamRequestFilterManagerCallbacks;\n    38\u2192class UpstreamFilterManager;\n    39\u2192class UpstreamCodecFilter;\n    40\u2192\n    41\u2192/* The Upstream request is the base class for forwarding HTTP upstream.\n    42\u2192 *\n    43\u2192 * On the new request path, payload (headers/body/metadata/data) still arrives via\n    44\u2192 * the accept[X]fromRouter functions. Said data is immediately passed off to the\n    45\u2192 * UpstreamFilterManager, which passes each item through the filter chain until\n    46\u2192 * it arrives at the last filter in the chain, the UpstreamCodecFilter. If the upstream\n    47\u2192 * stream is not established, the UpstreamCodecFilter returns StopAllIteration, and the\n    48\u2192 * FilterManager will buffer data, using watermarks to push back to the router\n    49\u2192 * filter if buffers become overrun. When an upstream connection is established,\n    50\u2192 * the UpstreamCodecFilter will send data upstream.\n    51\u2192 *\n    52\u2192 * On the new response path, payload arrives from upstream via the UpstreamCodecFilter's\n    53\u2192 * CodecBridge. It is passed off directly to the FilterManager, traverses the\n    54\u2192 * filter chain, and completion is signaled via the\n    55\u2192 * UpstreamRequestFilterManagerCallbacks's encode[X] functions. These somewhat\n    56\u2192 * confusingly pass through the UpstreamRequest's legacy decode[X] functions\n    57\u2192 * (required due to the UpstreamToDownstream interface, but will be renamed once\n    58\u2192 * the classic mode is deprecated), and are finally passed to the router via the\n    59\u2192 * RouterFilterInterface onUpstream[X] functions.\n    60\u2192 *\n    61\u2192 * There is some required communication between the UpstreamRequest and\n    62\u2192 * UpstreamCodecFilter. This is accomplished via the UpstreamStreamFilterCallbacks\n    63\u2192 * interface, with the UpstreamFilterManager acting as intermediary.\n    64\u2192 *\n    65\u2192 */\n    66\u2192class UpstreamRequest : public Logger::Loggable<Logger::Id::router>,\n    67\u2192                        public UpstreamToDownstream,\n    68\u2192                        public LinkedObject<UpstreamRequest>,\n    69\u2192                        public GenericConnectionPoolCallbacks,\n    70\u2192                        public Event::DeferredDeletable {\n    71\u2192public:\n    72\u2192  UpstreamRequest(RouterFilterInterface& parent, std::unique_ptr<GenericConnPool>&& conn_pool,\n    73\u2192                  bool can_send_early_data, bool can_use_http3, bool enable_half_close);\n    74\u2192  ~UpstreamRequest() override;\n    75\u2192  void deleteIsPending() override { cleanUp(); }\n    76\u2192\n    77\u2192  // To be called from the destructor, or prior to deferred delete.\n    78\u2192  void cleanUp();\n    79\u2192\n    80\u2192  virtual void acceptHeadersFromRouter(bool end_stream);\n    81\u2192  virtual void acceptDataFromRouter(Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream);\n    82\u2192  void acceptTrailersFromRouter(Http::RequestTrailerMap& trailers);\n    83\u2192  void acceptMetadataFromRouter(Http::MetadataMapPtr&& metadata_map_ptr);\n    84\u2192\n    85\u2192  virtual void resetStream();\n    86\u2192  void setupPerTryTimeout();\n    87\u2192  void maybeEndDecode(bool end_stream);\n    88\u2192  void onUpstreamHostSelected(Upstream::HostDescriptionConstSharedPtr host, bool pool_success);\n    89\u2192\n    90\u2192  // Http::StreamDecoder\n    91\u2192  void decodeData(Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream) override;\n    92\u2192  void decodeMetadata(Http::MetadataMapPtr&& metadata_map) override;\n    93\u2192\n    94\u2192  // UpstreamToDownstream (Http::ResponseDecoder)\n    95\u2192  void decode1xxHeaders(Http::ResponseHeaderMapPtr&& headers) override;\n    96\u2192  void decodeHeaders(Http::ResponseHeaderMapPtr&& headers, bool end_stream) override;\n    97\u2192  void decodeTrailers(Http::ResponseTrailerMapPtr&& trailers) override;\n    98\u2192  void dumpState(std::ostream& os, int indent_level) const override;\n    99\u2192\n   100\u2192  // UpstreamToDownstream (Http::StreamCallbacks)\n   101\u2192  void onResetStream(Http::StreamResetReason reason,\n   102\u2192                     absl::string_view transport_failure_reason) override;\n   103\u2192  void onAboveWriteBufferHighWatermark() override { disableDataFromDownstreamForFlowControl(); }\n   104\u2192  void onBelowWriteBufferLowWatermark() override { enableDataFromDownstreamForFlowControl(); }\n   105\u2192  // UpstreamToDownstream\n   106\u2192  const Route& route() const override;\n   107\u2192  OptRef<const Network::Connection> connection() const override;\n   108\u2192  const Http::ConnectionPool::Instance::StreamOptions& upstreamStreamOptions() const override {\n   109\u2192    return stream_options_;\n   110\u2192  }\n   111\u2192\n   112\u2192  void disableDataFromDownstreamForFlowControl();\n   113\u2192  void enableDataFromDownstreamForFlowControl();\n   114\u2192\n   115\u2192  // GenericConnPool\n   116\u2192  void onPoolFailure(ConnectionPool::PoolFailureReason reason,\n   117\u2192                     absl::string_view transport_failure_reason,\n   118\u2192                     Upstream::HostDescriptionConstSharedPtr host) override;\n   119\u2192  void onPoolReady(std::unique_ptr<GenericUpstream>&& upstream,\n   120\u2192                   Upstream::HostDescriptionConstSharedPtr host,\n   121\u2192                   const Network::ConnectionInfoProvider& address_provider,\n   122\u2192                   StreamInfo::StreamInfo& info, absl::optional<Http::Protocol> protocol) override;\n   123\u2192  UpstreamToDownstream& upstreamToDownstream() override;\n   124\u2192\n   125\u2192  void clearRequestEncoder();\n   126\u2192  void onStreamMaxDurationReached();\n   127\u2192\n   128\u2192  // Either disable upstream reading immediately or defer it and keep tracking\n   129\u2192  // of how many read disabling has happened.\n   130\u2192  void readDisableOrDefer(bool disable);\n   131\u2192  // Called upon receiving the first response headers from the upstream. And\n   132\u2192  // applies read disabling to it if there is any pending read disabling.\n   133\u2192  void maybeHandleDeferredReadDisable();\n   134\u2192\n   135\u2192  struct DownstreamWatermarkManager : public Http::DownstreamWatermarkCallbacks {\n   136\u2192    DownstreamWatermarkManager(UpstreamRequest& parent) : parent_(parent) {}\n   137\u2192\n   138\u2192    // Http::DownstreamWatermarkCallbacks\n   139\u2192    void onBelowWriteBufferLowWatermark() override;\n   140\u2192    void onAboveWriteBufferHighWatermark() override;\n   141\u2192\n   142\u2192    UpstreamRequest& parent_;\n   143\u2192  };\n   144\u2192\n   145\u2192  void readEnable();\n   146\u2192  void encodeBodyAndTrailers();\n   147\u2192\n   148\u2192  // Getters and setters\n   149\u2192  Upstream::HostDescriptionOptConstRef upstreamHost() {\n   150\u2192    return makeOptRefFromPtr(upstream_host_.get());\n   151\u2192  }\n   152\u2192  void outlierDetectionTimeoutRecorded(bool recorded) {\n   153\u2192    outlier_detection_timeout_recorded_ = recorded;\n   154\u2192  }\n   155\u2192  bool outlierDetectionTimeoutRecorded() { return outlier_detection_timeout_recorded_; }\n   156\u2192  void retried(bool value) { retried_ = value; }\n   157\u2192  bool retried() { return retried_; }\n   158\u2192  bool grpcRqSuccessDeferred() { return grpc_rq_success_deferred_; }\n   159\u2192  void grpcRqSuccessDeferred(bool deferred) { grpc_rq_success_deferred_ = deferred; }\n   160\u2192  void upstreamCanary(bool value) { upstream_canary_ = value; }\n   161\u2192  bool upstreamCanary() { return upstream_canary_; }\n   162\u2192  bool awaitingHeaders() { return awaiting_headers_; }\n   163\u2192  void recordTimeoutBudget(bool value) { record_timeout_budget_ = value; }\n   164\u2192  bool createPerTryTimeoutOnRequestComplete() {\n   165\u2192    return create_per_try_timeout_on_request_complete_;\n   166\u2192  }\n   167\u2192  bool encodeComplete() const { return router_sent_end_stream_; }\n   168\u2192  // Exposes streamInfo for the upstream stream.\n   169\u2192  StreamInfo::StreamInfo& streamInfo() { return stream_info_; }\n   170\u2192  bool hadUpstream() const { return had_upstream_; }\n   171\u2192\n   172\u2192private:\n   173\u2192  friend class UpstreamFilterManager;\n   174\u2192  friend class UpstreamCodecFilter;\n   175\u2192  friend class UpstreamRequestFilterManagerCallbacks;\n   176\u2192  StreamInfo::UpstreamTiming& upstreamTiming() {\n   177\u2192    return stream_info_.upstreamInfo()->upstreamTiming();\n   178\u2192  }\n   179\u2192  // Records the latency from when the upstream request was first created to\n   180\u2192  // when the pool callback fires. This latency can be useful to track excessive\n   181\u2192  // queuing.\n   182\u2192  void recordConnectionPoolCallbackLatency();\n   183\u2192\n   184\u2192  void addResponseHeadersStat(uint64_t size, size_t count) {\n   185\u2192    response_headers_size_ = response_headers_size_.value_or(0) + size;\n   186\u2192    response_headers_count_ = response_headers_count_.value_or(0) + count;\n   187\u2192  }\n   188\u2192  void resetPerTryIdleTimer();\n   189\u2192  void onPerTryTimeout();\n   190\u2192  void onPerTryIdleTimeout();\n   191\u2192  void upstreamLog(AccessLog::AccessLogType access_log_type);\n   192\u2192  void resetUpstreamLogFlushTimer();\n   193\u2192\n   194\u2192  RouterFilterInterface& parent_;\n   195\u2192  std::unique_ptr<GenericConnPool> conn_pool_;\n   196\u2192  Event::TimerPtr per_try_timeout_;\n   197\u2192  Event::TimerPtr per_try_idle_timeout_;\n   198\u2192  std::unique_ptr<GenericUpstream> upstream_;\n   199\u2192  absl::optional<Http::StreamResetReason> deferred_reset_reason_;\n   200\u2192  Upstream::HostDescriptionConstSharedPtr upstream_host_;\n   201\u2192  DownstreamWatermarkManager downstream_watermark_manager_{*this};\n   202\u2192  Tracing::SpanPtr span_;\n   203\u2192  StreamInfo::StreamInfoImpl stream_info_;\n   204\u2192  const MonotonicTime start_time_;\n   205\u2192  // This is wrapped in an optional, since we want to avoid computing zero size headers when in\n   206\u2192  // reality we just didn't get a response back.\n   207\u2192  absl::optional<uint64_t> response_headers_size_;\n   208\u2192  absl::optional<size_t> response_headers_count_;\n   209\u2192  // Copies of upstream headers/trailers. These are only set if upstream\n   210\u2192  // access logging is configured.\n   211\u2192  Http::ResponseHeaderMapPtr upstream_headers_;\n   212\u2192  Http::ResponseTrailerMapPtr upstream_trailers_;\n   213\u2192  OptRef<UpstreamToDownstream> upstream_interface_;\n   214\u2192  std::list<Http::UpstreamCallbacks*> upstream_callbacks_;\n   215\u2192\n   216\u2192  Event::TimerPtr max_stream_duration_timer_;\n   217\u2192\n   218\u2192  // Per-stream access log flush duration. This timer is enabled once when the stream is created\n   219\u2192  // and will log to all access logs once per trigger.\n   220\u2192  Event::TimerPtr upstream_log_flush_timer_;\n   221\u2192\n   222\u2192  std::unique_ptr<UpstreamRequestFilterManagerCallbacks> filter_manager_callbacks_;\n   223\u2192  std::unique_ptr<Http::FilterManager> filter_manager_;\n   224\u2192\n   225\u2192  // The number of outstanding readDisable to be called with parameter value true.\n   226\u2192  // When downstream send buffers get above high watermark before response headers arrive, we\n   227\u2192  // increment this counter instead of immediately calling readDisable on upstream stream. This is\n   228\u2192  // to avoid the upstream request from being spuriously retried or reset because of upstream\n   229\u2192  // timeouts while upstream stream is readDisabled by downstream but the response has actually\n   230\u2192  // arrived from upstream. See https://github.com/envoyproxy/envoy/issues/25901. During the\n   231\u2192  // deferring period, if the downstream buffer gets below low watermark, this counter gets\n   232\u2192  // decremented. Once the response headers arrive, call readDisable the number of times as the\n   233\u2192  // remaining value of this counter.\n   234\u2192  size_t deferred_read_disabling_count_{0};\n   235\u2192\n   236\u2192  // Keep small members (bools and enums) at the end of class, to reduce alignment overhead.\n   237\u2192  // Tracks the number of times the flow of data from downstream has been disabled.\n   238\u2192  uint32_t downstream_data_disabled_{};\n   239\u2192  bool upstream_canary_ : 1;\n   240\u2192  bool router_sent_end_stream_ : 1;\n   241\u2192  bool encode_trailers_ : 1;\n   242\u2192  bool retried_ : 1;\n   243\u2192  bool awaiting_headers_ : 1;\n   244\u2192  bool outlier_detection_timeout_recorded_ : 1;\n   245\u2192  // Tracks whether we deferred a per try timeout because the downstream request\n   246\u2192  // had not been completed yet.\n   247\u2192  bool create_per_try_timeout_on_request_complete_ : 1;\n   248\u2192  // True if the CONNECT headers have been sent but proxying payload is paused\n   249\u2192  // waiting for response headers.\n   250\u2192  bool paused_for_connect_ : 1;\n   251\u2192  bool paused_for_websocket_ : 1;\n   252\u2192  bool reset_stream_ : 1;\n   253\u2192\n   254\u2192  // Sentinel to indicate if timeout budget tracking is configured for the cluster,\n   255\u2192  // and if so, if the per-try histogram should record a value.\n   256\u2192  bool record_timeout_budget_ : 1;\n   257\u2192  // Track if one time clean up has been performed.\n   258\u2192  bool cleaned_up_ : 1;\n   259\u2192  bool had_upstream_ : 1;\n   260\u2192  Http::ConnectionPool::Instance::StreamOptions stream_options_;\n   261\u2192  bool grpc_rq_success_deferred_ : 1;\n   262\u2192  bool enable_half_close_ : 1;\n   263\u2192};\n                         creation_status);\n   127\u2192  }\n   128\u2192}\n   129\u2192\n   130\u2192// Express percentage as [0, TimeoutPrecisionFactor] because stats do not accept floating point\n   131\u2192// values, and getting multiple significant figures on the histogram would be nice.\n   132\u2192uint64_t FilterUtility::percentageOfTimeout(const std::chrono::milliseconds response_time,\n   133\u2192                                            const std::chrono::milliseconds timeout) {\n   134\u2192  // Timeouts of 0 are considered infinite. Any portion of an infinite timeout used is still\n   135\u2192  // none of it.\n   136\u2192  if (timeout.count() == 0) {\n   137\u2192    return 0;\n   138\u2192  }\n   139\u2192\n   140\u2192  return static_cast<uint64_t>(response_time.count() * TimeoutPrecisionFactor / timeout.count());\n   141\u2192}\n   142\u2192\n   143\u2192void FilterUtility::setUpstreamScheme(Http::RequestHeaderMap& headers, bool downstream_ssl,\n   144\u2192                                      bool upstream_ssl, bool use_upstream) {\n   145\u2192  if (use_upstream) {\n   146\u2192    if (upstream_ssl) {\n   147\u2192      headers.setReferenceScheme(Http::Headers::get().SchemeValues.Https);\n   148\u2192    } else {\n   149\u2192      headers.setReferenceScheme(Http::Headers::get().SchemeValues.Http);\n   150\u2192    }\n   151\u2192    return;\n   152\u2192  }\n   153\u2192\n   154\u2192  if (Http::Utility::schemeIsValid(headers.getSchemeValue())) {\n   155\u2192    return;\n   156\u2192  }\n   157\u2192  // After all the changes in https://github.com/envoyproxy/envoy/issues/14587\n   158\u2192  // this path should only occur if a buggy filter has removed the :scheme\n   159\u2192  // header. In that case best-effort set from X-Forwarded-Proto.\n   160\u2192  absl::string_view xfp = headers.getForwardedProtoValue();\n   161\u2192  if (Http::Utility::schemeIsValid(xfp)) {\n   162\u2192    headers.setScheme(xfp);\n   163\u2192    return;\n   164\u2192  }\n   165\u2192\n   166\u2192  if (downstream_ssl) {\n   167\u2192    headers.setReferenceScheme(Http::Headers::get().SchemeValues.Https);\n   168\u2192  } else {\n   169\u2192    headers.setReferenceScheme(Http::Headers::get().SchemeValues.Http);\n   170\u2192  }\n   171\u2192}\n   172\u2192\n   173\u2192bool FilterUtility::shouldShadow(const ShadowPolicy& policy, Runtime::Loader& runtime,\n   174\u2192                                 uint64_t stable_random) {\n   175\u2192\n   176\u2192  // The policy's default value is set correctly regardless of whether there is a runtime key\n   177\u2192  // or not, thus this call is sufficient for all cases (100% if no runtime set, otherwise\n   178\u2192  // using the default value within the runtime fractional percent setting).\n   179\u2192  return runtime.snapshot().featureEnabled(policy.runtimeKey(), policy.defaultValue(),\n   180\u2192                                           stable_random);\n   181\u2192}\n   182\u2192\n   183\u2192TimeoutData FilterUtility::finalTimeout(const RouteEntry& route,\n   184\u2192                                        Http::RequestHeaderMap& request_headers,\n   185\u2192                                        bool insert_envoy_expected_request_timeout_ms,\n   186\u2192                                        bool grpc_request, bool per_try_timeout_hedging_enabled,\n   187\u2192                                        bool respect_expected_rq_timeout) {\n   188\u2192  // See if there is a user supplied timeout in a request header. }\n   699\u2192\n   700\u2192void UpstreamRequest::onStreamMaxDurationReached() {\n   701\u2192  upstream_host_->cluster().trafficStats()->upstream_rq_max_duration_reached_.inc();\n   702\u2192\n   703\u2192  // The upstream had closed then try to retry along with retry policy.\n   704\u2192  parent_.onStreamMaxDurationReached(*this);\n   705\u2192}\n   706\u2192\n   707\u2192void UpstreamRequest::clearRequestEncoder() {\n   708\u2192  // Before clearing the encoder, unsubscribe from callbacks.\n   709\u2192  if (upstream_) {\n   710\u2192    parent_.callbacks()->removeDownstreamWatermarkCallbacks(downstream_watermark_manager_);\n   711\u2192  }\n   712\u2192  upstream_.reset();\n   713\u2192}\n   714\u2192\n   715\u2192void UpstreamRequest::readDisableOrDefer(bool disable) {\n   716\u2192  if (disable) {\n   717\u2192    // See comments on deferred_read_disabling_count_ for when we do and don't defer.\n   718\u2192    if (parent_.downstreamResponseStarted()) {\n   719\u2192      // The downstream connection is overrun. counting.\n   722\u2192      parent_.cluster()->trafficStats()->upstream_flow_control_paused_reading_total_.inc();\n   723\u2192      upstream_->readDisable(disable);\n   724\u2192    } else {\n   725\u2192      ++deferred_read_disabling_count_;\n   726\u2192    }\n   727\u2192    return;\n   728\u2192  }\n   729\u2192\n   730\u2192  // One source of connection blockage has buffer available.\n   731\u2192  if (deferred_read_disabling_count_ > 0) {\n   732\u2192    ASSERT(!parent_.downstreamResponseStarted());\n   733\u2192    // Cancel out an existing deferred read disabling.\n   734\u2192 739\u2192  // will resume reads if this was the last remaining high watermark.\n   740\u2192  parent_.cluster()->trafficStats()->upstream_flow_control_resumed_reading_total_.inc();\n   741\u2192  upstream_->readDisable(disable);\n   742\u2192}\n   743\u2192\n   744\u2192void UpstreamRequest::DownstreamWatermarkManager::onAboveWriteBufferHighWatermark() {\n   745\u2192 {\n   750\u2192  ASSERT(parent_.upstream_);\n   751\u2192  parent_.readDisableOrDefer(false);\n   752\u2192}\n   753\u2192\n   754\u2192void UpstreamRequest::disableDataFromDownstreamForFlowControl() {\n   755\u2192  parent_.cluster()->trafficStats()->upstream_flow_control_backed_up_total_.inc();\n   756\u2192  parent_.callbacks()->onDecoderFilterAboveWriteBufferHighWatermark();\n   757\u2192 parent_.cluster()->trafficStats()->upstream_flow_control_drained_total_.inc();\n   762\u2192  parent_.callbacks()->onDecoderFilterBelowWriteBufferLowWatermark();\n   763\u2192  ASSERT(downstream_data_disabled_ != 0);\n   764\u2192  if (downstream_data_disabled_ > 0) {\n   765\u2192    --downstream_data_disabled_;\n   766\u2192  }\n   767\u2192}\n   768\u2192\n
