773\u2192Http::RequestTrailerMapOptRef UpstreamRequestFilterManagerCallbacks::requestTrailers() {\n   774\u2192  if (upstream_request_.parent_.downstreamTrailers()) {\n   775\u2192    return {*upstream_request_.parent_.downstreamTrailers()};\n   776\u2192  }\n   777\u2192  if (trailers_) {\n   778\u2192    return {*trailers_};\n   779\u2192  }\n   780\u2192  return {};\n   781\u2192}\n Envoy::Config::Utility::translateToFactoryConfig(\n   920\u2192          *cluster_->upstreamConfig(), config_->factory_context_.messageValidationVisitor(),\n   921\u2192          *factory);\n (!message) {\n   929\u2192    message = factory->createEmptyConfigProto();\n   930\u2192  }\n   931\u2192\n   932\u2192  using UpstreamProtocol = Envoy::Router::GenericConnPoolFactory::UpstreamProtocol;\n   933\u2192  UpstreamProtocol upstream_protocol = UpstreamProtocol::HTTP;\n   934\u2192  if (route_entry_->connectConfig().has_value()) {\n   935\u2192    auto method = downstream_headers_->getMethodValue();\n   936\u2192    if (Http::HeaderUtility::isConnectUdpRequest(*downstream_headers_)) {\n   937\u2192      upstream_protocol = UpstreamProtocol::UDP;\n   938\u2192    } else if (method == Http::Headers::get().MethodValues.Connect ||\n   939\u2192               (route_entry_->connectConfig()->allow_post() &&\n   940\u2192 UpstreamProtocol::TCP;\n   943\u2192    }\n   944\u2192  }\n   945\u2192\n   946\u2192  return factory->createGenericConnPool(host, cluster, upstream_protocol, route_entry_->priority(),\n   947\u2192                                        callbacks_->streamInfo().protocol(), this, *message);\n   948\u2192}\n   949\u2192\n   950\u2192void Filter::sendNoHealthyUpstreamResponse(absl::optional<std::string> optional_details) {\n   951\u2192  callbacks_->streamInfo().setResponseFlag(StreamInfo::CoreResponseFlag::NoHealthyUpstream);\n   952\u2192  chargeUpstreamCode(Http::Code::ServiceUnavailable, {}, false);\n   953\u2192  absl::string_view details = (optional_details.has_value() && !optional_details->empty())\n   954\u2192                                  ? absl::string_view(*optional_details)\n   955\u2192                                  : StreamInfo::ResponseCodeDetails::get().NoHealthyUpstream;\n   956\u2192  callbacks_->sendLocalReply(Http::Code::ServiceUnavailable, \"no healthy upstream\", modify_headers_,\n   957\u2192                             absl::nullopt, details);\n   958\u2192}\n   959\u2192\n   960\u2192Http::FilterDataStatus Filter::decodeData(Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream) {\n   961\u2192  // upstream_requests_.size() cannot be > 1 because that only happens when a per\n   962\u2192  // try timeout occurs with hedge_on_per_try_timeout enabled but the per\n   963\u2192  // try timeout timer is not started until onRequestComplete(). It could be zero\n   964\u2192  // if the first request attempt has already failed and a retry is waiting for\n   965\u2192  // a backoff timer.\n   966\u2192  ASSERT(upstream_requests_.size() <= 1);\n   967\u2192\n   968\u2192  bool buffering = (retry_state_ && retry_state_->enabled()) ||\n   969\u2192                   (!active_shadow_policies_.empty() && !streaming_shadows_) ||\n   970\u2192                   (route_entry_ && route_entry_->internalRedirectPolicy().enabled());\n   971\u2192  if (buffering &&\n   972\u2192      getLength(callbacks_->decodingBuffer()) + data.length() > retry_shadow_buffer_limit_) {\n   973\u2192    ENVOY_LOG(debug,\n   974\u2192              \"The request payload has at least {} bytes data which exceeds buffer limit {}. Give \"\n   975\u2192              \"up on the retry/shadow.\",\n   976\u2192              getLength(callbacks_->decodingBuffer()) + data.length(), retry_shadow_buffer_limit_);\n   977\u2192    cluster_->trafficStats()->retry_or_shadow_abandoned_.inc();\n   978\u2192    retry_state_.reset();\n   979\u2192    buffering = false;\n   980\u2192    active_shadow_policies_.clear();\n   981\u2192    request_buffer_overflowed_ = true;\n   982\u2192\n   983\u2192    // If we had to abandon buffering and there's no request in progress, abort the request and\n   984\u2192    // clean up. This happens if the initial upstream request failed, and we are currently waiting\n   985\u2192    // for a backoff timer before starting the next upstream attempt.\n   986\u2192    if (upstream_requests_.empty()) {\n   987\u2192      cleanup();\n   988\u2192      callbacks_->sendLocalReply(\n   989\u2192          Http::Code::InsufficientStorage, \"exceeded request buffer limit while retrying upstream\",\n   990\u2192          modify_headers_, absl::nullopt,\n   991\u2192          StreamInfo::ResponseCodeDetails::get().RequestPayloadExceededRetryBufferLimit);\n   992\u2192      return Http::FilterDataStatus::StopIterationNoBuffer;\n   993\u2192    }\n   994\u2192  }\n   995\u2192\n   996\u2192  for (auto* shadow_stream : shadow_streams_) {\n   997\u2192    if (end_stream) {\n   998\u2192      shadow_stream->removeDestructorCallback();\n   999\u2192      shadow_stream->removeWatermarkCallbacks();\n  1000\u2192    }\n  1001\u2192    Buffer::OwnedImpl copy(data);\n  1002\u2192    shadow_stream->sendData(copy, end_stream);\n  1003\u2192  }\n  1004\u2192  if (end_stream) {\n  1005\u2192    shadow_streams_.clear();\n  1006\u2192  }\n  1007\u2192  if (buffering) {\n  1008\u2192    if (!upstream_requests_.empty()) {\n  1009\u2192      Buffer::OwnedImpl copy(data);\n  1010\u2192      upstream_requests_.front()->acceptDataFromRouter(copy, end_stream);\n  1011\u2192    }\n  1012\u2192\n  1013\u2192    // If we are potentially going to retry or buffer shadow this request we need to buffer.\n  1014\u2192    // This will not cause the connection manager to 413 because before we hit the\n  1015\u2192    // buffer limit we give up on retries and buffering. We must buffer using addDecodedData()\n  1016\u2192    // so that all buffered data is available by the time we do request complete processing and\n  1017\u2192    // potentially shadow. Additionally, we can't do a copy here because there's a check down\n  1018\u2192    // this stack for whether `data` is the same buffer as already buffered data.\n  1019\u2192    callbacks_->addDecodedData(data, true);\n  1020\u2192  } else {\n  1021\u2192    if (!upstream_requests_.empty()) {\n  1022\u2192      upstream_requests_.front()->acceptDataFromRouter(data, end_stream);\n  1023\u2192    } else {\n  1024\u2192      // not buffering any data for retry, shadow, and internal redirect, and there will be\n  1025\u2192      // no more upstream request, abort the request and clean up.\n  1026\u2192      cleanup();\n  1027\u2192      callbacks_->sendLocalReply(\n  1028\u2192          Http::Code::ServiceUnavailable,\n  1029\u2192          \"upstream is closed prematurely during decoding data from downstream\", modify_headers_,\n  1030\u2192          absl::nullopt, StreamInfo::ResponseCodeDetails::get().EarlyUpstreamReset);\n  1031\u2192      return Http::FilterDataStatus::StopIterationNoBuffer;\n  1032\u2192    }\n  1033\u2192  }\n  1034\u2192\n  1035\u2192  if (end_stream) {\n  1036\u2192    onRequestComplete();\n  1037\u2192  }\n  1038\u2192\n  1039\u2192  return Http::FilterDataStatus::StopIterationNoBuffer;\n  1040\u2192}\n  1041\u2192\n  1042\u2192Http::FilterTrailersStatus Filter::decodeTrailers(Http::RequestTrailerMap& trailers) {\n  1043\u2192  ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(debug, \"router decoding trailers:\\n{}\", *callbacks_, trailers);\n  1044\u2192\n  1045\u2192  if (shadow_headers_) {\n  1046\u2192    shadow_trailers_ = Http::createHeaderMap<Http::RequestTrailerMapImpl>(trailers);\n  1047\u2192  }\n  1048\u2192\n  1049\u2192  // upstream_requests_.size() cannot be > 1 because that only happens when a per\n  1050\u2192  // try timeout occurs with hedge_on_per_try_timeout enabled but the per\n  1051\u2192  // try timeout timer is not started until onRequestComplete(). It could be zero\n  1052\u2192  // if the first request attempt has already failed and a retry is waiting for\n  1053\u2192  // a backoff timer.\n  1054\u2192  ASSERT(upstream_requests_.size() <= 1);"

"  1652\u2192void Filter::onUpstreamHeaders(uint64_t response_code, Http::ResponseHeaderMapPtr&& headers,\n  1653\u2192                               UpstreamRequest& upstream_request, bool end_stream) {\n  1654\u2192  ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(debug, \"upstream headers complete: end_stream={}\", *callbacks_, end_stream);\n  1655\u2192\n  1656\u2192  ASSERT(!host_selection_cancelable_);\n  1657\u2192\n  1658\u2192  modify_headers_(*headers);\n  1659\u2192  // When grpc-status appears in response headers, convert grpc-status to HTTP status code\n  1660\u2192  // for outlier detection. This does not currently change any stats or logging and does not\n  1661\u2192  // handle the case when an error grpc-status is sent as a trailer.\n  1662\u2192  absl::optional<Grpc::Status::GrpcStatus> grpc_status;\n  1663\u2192  uint64_t grpc_to_http_status = 0;\n  1664\u2192  if (grpc_request_) {\n  1665\u2192    grpc_status = Grpc::Common::getGrpcStatus(*headers);\n  1666\u2192    if (grpc_status.has_value()) {\n  1667\u2192      grpc_to_http_status = Grpc::Utility::grpcToHttpStatus(grpc_status.value());\n  1668\u2192    }\n  1669\u2192  }\n  1670\u2192\n  1671\u2192  maybeProcessOrcaLoadReport(*headers, upstream_request);\n  1672\u2192\n  1673\u2192  if (grpc_status.has_value()) {\n  1674\u2192    upstream_request.upstreamHost()->outlierDetector().putHttpResponseCode(grpc_to_http_status);\n  1675\u2192  } else {\n  1676\u2192    upstream_request.upstreamHost()->outlierDetector().putHttpResponseCode(response_code);\n  1677\u2192  }\n  1678\u2192\n  1679\u2192  if (headers->EnvoyImmediateHealthCheckFail() != nullptr) {\n  1680\u2192    upstream_request.upstreamHost()->healthChecker().setUnhealthy(\n  1681\u2192        Upstream::HealthCheckHostMonitor::UnhealthyType::ImmediateHealthCheckFail);\n  1682\u2192  }\n  1683\u2192\n  1684\u2192  bool could_not_retry = false;\n  1685\u2192\n  1686\u2192  // Check if this upstream request was already retried, for instance after\n  1687\u2192  // hitting a per try timeout. Don't retry it if we already have.\n  1688\u2192  if (retry_state_) {\n  1689\u2192    if (upstream_request.retried()) {\n  1690\u2192      // We already retried this request (presumably for a per try timeout) so\n  1691\u2192      // we definitely won't retry it again. Check if we would have retried it\n  1692\u2192      // if we could.\n  1693\u2192      bool retry_as_early_data; // Not going to be used as we are not retrying.\n  1694\u2192      could_not_retry = retry_state_->wouldRetryFromHeaders(*headers, *downstream_headers_,\n  1695\u2192                                                            retry_as_early_data) !=\n  1696\u2192 *downstream_headers_,\n  1700\u2192          [this, can_use_http3 = upstream_request.upstreamStreamOptions().can_use_http3_,\n  1701\u2192           had_early_data = upstream_request.upstreamStreamOptions().can_send_early_data_](\n  1702\u2192              bool disable_early_data) -> void {\n  1703\u2192            doRetry((disable_early_data ? false : had_early_data), can_use_http3, TimeoutRetry::No);\n  1704\u2192          });\n  1705\u2192      if (retry_status == RetryStatus::Yes) {\n  1706\u2192        runRetryOptionsPredicates(upstream_request);\n  1707\u2192        pending_retries_++;\n  1708\u2192        upstream_request.upstreamHost()->stats().rq_error_.inc();\n  1709\u2192        Http::CodeStats& code_stats = httpContext().codeStats();\n  1710\u2192        code_stats.chargeBasicResponseStat(cluster_->statsScope(), stats_.stat_names_.retry_,\n  1711\u2192                                           static_cast<Http::Code>(response_code),\n  1712\u2192                                           exclude_http_code_stats_);\n  1713\u2192\n  1714\u2192        if (!end_stream || !upstream_request.encodeComplete()) {\n  1715\u2192          upstream_request.resetStream();\n  1716\u2192        }\n  1717\u2192        auto request_ptr = upstream_request.removeFromList(upstream_requests_);\n  1718\u2192        callbacks_->dispatcher().deferredDelete(std::move(request_ptr));\n  1719\u2192        return;\n  1720\u2192      } else if (retry_status == RetryStatus::NoOverflow) {\n  1721\u2192        callbacks_->streamInfo().setResponseFlag(StreamInfo::CoreResponseFlag::UpstreamOverflow);\n  1722\u2192        could_not_retry = true;\n  1723\u2192      } else if (retry_status == RetryStatus::NoRetryLimitExceeded) {\n  1724\u2192        callbacks_->streamInfo().setResponseFlag(\n  1725\u2192            StreamInfo::CoreResponseFlag::UpstreamRetryLimitExceeded);\n  1726\u2192        could_not_retry = true;\n  1727\u2192      }\n  1728\u2192    }\n  1729\u2192  }\n  1730\u2192\n  1731\u2192  if (route_entry_->internalRedirectPolicy().enabled() &&\n  1732\u2192      route_entry_->internalRedirectPolicy().shouldRedirectForResponseCode(\n  1733\u2192          static_cast<Http::Code>(response_code)) &&\n  1734\u2192      setupRedirect(*headers)) {\n  1735\u2192    return;\n  1736\u2192    // If the redirect could not be handled, fail open and let it pass to the\n  1737\u2192    // next downstream.\n  1738\u2192  }\n  1739\u2192\n  1740\u2192  // Check if we got a \"bad\" response, but there are still upstream requests in\n  1741\u2192  // flight awaiting headers or scheduled retries. If so, exit to give them a\n  1742\u2192  // chance to return before returning a response downstream.\n  1743\u2192  if (could_not_retry && (numRequestsAwaitingHeaders() > 0 || pending_retries_ > 0)) {\n  1744\u2192    upstream_request.upstreamHost()->stats().rq_error_.inc();\n  1745\u2192\n  1746\u2192    // Reset the stream because there are other in-flight requests that we'll\n  1747\u2192    // wait around for and we're not interested in consuming any body/trailers.\n  1748\u2192    auto request_ptr = upstream_request.removeFromList(upstream_requests_);\n  1749\u2192    request_ptr->resetStream();\n  1750\u2192    callbacks_->dispatcher().deferredDelete(std::move(request_ptr));\n  1751\u2192    return;\n  1752\u2192  }\n  1753\u2192\n  1754\u2192  // Make sure any retry timers are destroyed since we may not call cleanup() if end_stream is\n  1755\u2192  // false.\n  1756\u2192  if (retry_state_) {\n  1757\u2192    retry_state_.reset();\n  1758\u2192  }\n  1759\u2192\n  1760\u2192  // Only send upstream service time if we received the complete request and this is not a\n  1761\u2192  // premature response.\n  1762\u2192  if (DateUtil::timePointValid(downstream_request_complete_time_)) {\n  1763\u2192    Event::Dispatcher& dispatcher = callbacks_->dispatcher();\n  1764\u2192    MonotonicTime response_received_time = dispatcher.timeSource().monotonicTime();\n  1765\u2192    std::chrono::milliseconds ms = std::chrono::duration_cast<std::chrono::milliseconds>(\n  1766\u2192        response_received_time - downstream_request_complete_time_);\n  1767\u2192    if (!config_->suppress_envoy_headers_) {\n  1768\u2192      headers->setEnvoyUpstreamServiceTime(ms.count());\n  1769\u2192    }\n  1770\u2192  }\n  1771\u2192\n  1772\u2192  upstream_request.upstreamCanary(\n  1773\u2192      (headers->EnvoyUpstreamCanary() && headers->EnvoyUpstreamCanary()->value() == \"true\") ||\n  1774\u2192      upstream_request.upstreamHost()->canary());\n  1775\u2192  chargeUpstreamCode(response_code, *headers, upstream_request.upstreamHost(), false);\n  1776\u2192  if (!Http::CodeUtility::is5xx(response_code)) {\n  1777\u2192    handleNon5xxResponseHeaders(grpc_status, upstream_request, end_stream, grpc_to_http_status);\n  1778\u2192  }\n  1779\u2192\n  1780\u2192  // Append routing cookies\n  1781\u2192  for (const auto& header_value : downstream_set_cookies_) {\n  1782\u2192    headers->addReferenceKey(Http::Headers::get().SetCookie, header_value);\n  1783\u2192  }\n  1784\u2192\n  1785\u2192  callbacks_->streamInfo().setResponseCodeDetails(\n  1786\u2192      StreamInfo::ResponseCodeDetails::get().ViaUpstream);\n  1787\u2192\n  1788\u2192  callbacks_->streamInfo().setResponseCode(response_code);\n  1789\u2192\n  1790\u2192  // TODO(zuercher): If access to response_headers_to_add (at any level) is ever needed outside\n  1791\u2192  // Router::Filter we'll need to find a better location for this work. One possibility is to\n  1792\u2192  // provide finalizeResponseHeaders functions on the Router::Config and VirtualHost interfaces.\n  1793\u2192  route_entry_->finalizeResponseHeaders(*headers, callbacks_->streamInfo());\n  1794\u2192\n  1795\u2192  downstream_response_started_ = true;\n  1796\u2192  final_upstream_request_ = &upstream_request;\n  1797\u2192  // Make sure that for request hedging, we end up with the correct final upstream info.\n  1798\u2192  callbacks_->streamInfo().setUpstreamInfo(final_upstream_request_->streamInfo().upstreamInfo());\n  1799\u2192  resetOtherUpstreams(upstream_request);\n  1800\u2192  if (end_stream) {\n  1801\u2192    onUpstreamComplete(upstream_request);\n  1802\u2192  }\n  1803\u2192\n  1804\u2192  callbacks_->encodeHeaders(std::move(headers), end_stream,\n  1805\u2192                            StreamInfo::ResponseCodeDetails::get().ViaUpstream);\n  1806\u2192}\n  1807\u2192\n  1808\u2192void Filter::onUpstreamData(Buffer::Instance& data, UpstreamRequest& upstream_request,\n  1809\u2192                            bool end_stream) {\n  1810\u2192  // This should be true because when we saw headers we either reset the stream\n  1811\u2192  // (hence wouldn't have made it to onUpstreamData) or all other in-flight\n  1812\u2192  // streams.\n  1813\u2192  ASSERT(upstream_requests_.size() == 1);\n  1814\u2192  if (end_stream) {\n  1815\u2192    // gRPC request termination without trailers is an error.\n  1816\u2192    if (upstream_request.grpcRqSuccessDeferred()) {\n  1817\u2192      upstream_request.upstreamHost()->stats().rq_error_.inc();\n  1818\u2192    }\n  1819\u2192    onUpstreamComplete(upstream_request);\n  1820\u2192  }\n  1821\u2192\n  1822\u2192  callbacks_->encodeData(data, end_stream);\n  1823\u2192}\n  1824\u2192\n  1825\u2192void Filter::onUpstreamTrailers(Http::ResponseTrailerMapPtr&& trailers,\n  1826\u2192                                UpstreamRequest& upstream_request) {\n  1827\u2192  // This should be true because when we saw headers we either reset the stream\n  1828\u2192  // (hence wouldn't have made it to onUpstreamTrailers) or all other in-flight\n  1829\u2192  // streams.\n  1830\u2192  ASSERT(upstream_requests_.size() == 1);\n  1831\u2192\n  1832\u2192  if (upstream_request.grpcRqSuccessDeferred()) {\n  1833\u2192    absl::optional<Grpc::Status::GrpcStatus> grpc_status = Grpc::Common::getGrpcStatus(*trailers);\n  1834\u2192    if (grpc_status &&\n  1835\u2192        !Http::CodeUtility::is5xx(Grpc::Utility::grpcToHttpStatus(grpc_status.value()))) {\n  1836\u2192      upstream_request.upstreamHost()->stats().rq_success_.inc();\n  1837\u2192    } else {\n  1838\u2192      upstream_request.upstreamHost()->stats().rq_error_.inc();\n  1839\u2192    }\n  1840\u2192  }\n  1841\u2192\n  1842\u2192  maybeProcessOrcaLoadReport(*trailers, upstream_request);\n  1843\u2192\n  1844\u2192  onUpstreamComplete(upstream_request);\n  1845\u2192\n  1846\u2192  callbacks_->encodeTrailers(std::move(trailers));\n  1847\u2192}\n  1848\u2192\n  1849\u2192void Filter::onUpstreamMetadata(Http::MetadataMapPtr&& metadata_map) {\n  1850\u2192  callbacks_->encodeMetadata(std::move(metadata_map));\n  1851\u2192}"

"   250\u2192    upstream_log->log(log_context, stream_info_);\n   251\u2192  }\n   252\u2192}\n   253\u2192\n   254\u2192// This is called by the FilterManager when all filters have processed 1xx headers. Forward them\n   255\u2192// on to the router.\n   256\u2192void UpstreamRequest::decode1xxHeaders(Http::ResponseHeaderMapPtr&& headers) {\n   257\u2192  ScopeTrackerScopeState scope(&parent_.callbacks()->scope(), parent_.callbacks()->dispatcher());\n   258\u2192\n   259\u2192  ASSERT(Http::HeaderUtility::isSpecial1xx(*headers));\n   260\u2192  addResponseHeadersStat(headers->byteSize(), headers->size());\n   261\u2192  maybeHandleDeferredReadDisable();\n   262\u2192  parent_.onUpstream1xxHeaders(std::move(headers), *this);\n   263\u2192}\n   264\u2192\n   265\u2192// This is called by the FilterManager when all filters have processed headers. Forward them\n   266\u2192// on to the router.\n   267\u2192void UpstreamRequest::decodeHeaders(Http::ResponseHeaderMapPtr&& headers, bool end_stream) {\n   268\u2192  ASSERT(headers.get());\n   269\u2192  ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"end_stream: {}, upstream response headers:\\n{}\", *parent_.callbacks(),\n   270\u2192                   end_stream, *headers);\n   271\u2192  ScopeTrackerScopeState scope(&parent_.callbacks()->scope(), parent_.callbacks()->dispatcher());\n   272\u2192\n   273\u2192  resetPerTryIdleTimer();\n   274\u2192\n   275\u2192  addResponseHeadersStat(headers->byteSize(), headers->size());\n   276\u2192\n   277\u2192  // We drop unsupported 1xx on the floor here. 101 upgrade headers need to be passed to the client\n   278\u2192  // as part of the final response. Most 1xx headers are handled in onUpstream1xxHeaders.\n   279\u2192  //\n   280\u2192  // We could in principle handle other headers here, but this might result in the double invocation\n   281\u2192  // of decodeHeaders() (once for informational, again for non-informational), which is likely an\n   282\u2192  // easy to miss corner case in the filter and HCM contract.\n   283\u2192  //\n   284\u2192  // This filtering is done early in upstream request, unlike 100 coalescing which is performed in\n   285\u2192  // the router filter, since the filtering only depends on the state of a single upstream, and we\n   286\u2192  // don't want to confuse accounting such as onFirstUpstreamRxByteReceived() with informational\n   287\u2192  // headers.\n   288\u2192  const uint64_t response_code = Http::Utility::getResponseStatus(*headers);\n   289\u2192  if (Http::CodeUtility::is1xx(response_code) &&\n   290\u2192      response_code != enumToInt(Http::Code::SwitchingProtocols)) {\n   291\u2192    return;\n   292\u2192  }\n   293\u2192\n   294\u2192  awaiting_headers_ = false;\n   295\u2192  if (span_ != nullptr) {\n   296\u2192    Tracing::HttpTracerUtility::onUpstreamResponseHeaders(*span_, headers.get());\n   297\u2192  }\n   298\u2192  if (!parent_.config().upstream_logs_.empty()) {\n   299\u2192    upstream_headers_ = Http::createHeaderMap<Http::ResponseHeaderMapImpl>(*headers);\n   300\u2192  }\n   301\u2192  stream_info_.setResponseCode(static_cast<uint32_t>(response_code));\n   302\u2192\n   303\u2192  maybeHandleDeferredReadDisable();\n   304\u2192  ASSERT(headers.get());\n   305\u2192\n   306\u2192  parent_.onUpstreamHeaders(response_code, std::move(headers), *this, end_stream);\n   307\u2192}\n   308\u2192\n   309\u2192void UpstreamRequest::maybeHandleDeferredReadDisable() {\n   310\u2192  for (; deferred_read_disabling_count_ > 0; --deferred_read_disabling_count_) {\n   311\u2192    // If the deferred read disabling count hasn't been cancelled out by read\n   312\u2192    // enabling count so far, stop the upstream from reading the rest response.\n   313\u2192    // Because readDisable keeps track of how many time it is called with\n   314\u2192    // \"true\" or \"false\", here it has to be called with \"true\" the same number\n   315\u2192    // of times as it would be called with \"false\" in the future.\n   316\u2192    parent_.cluster()->trafficStats()->upstream_flow_control_paused_reading_total_.inc();\n   317\u2192    upstream_->readDisable(true);\n   318\u2192  }\n   319\u2192}\n   320\u2192\n   321\u2192void UpstreamRequest::decodeData(Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream) {\n   322\u2192  ScopeTrackerScopeState scope(&parent_.callbacks()->scope(), parent_.callbacks()->dispatcher());\n   323\u2192\n   324\u2192  resetPerTryIdleTimer();\n   325\u2192  stream_info_.addBytesReceived(data.length());\n   326\u2192  parent_.onUpstreamData(data, *this, end_stream);\n   327\u2192}\n   328\u2192\n   329\u2192void UpstreamRequest::decodeTrailers(Http::ResponseTrailerMapPtr&& trailers) {\n   330\u2192  ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"upstream response trailers:\\n{}\", *parent_.callbacks(), *trailers);\n   331\u2192  ScopeTrackerScopeState scope(&parent_.callbacks()->scope(), parent_.callbacks()->dispatcher());\n   332\u2192\n   333\u2192  if (span_ != nullptr) {\n   334\u2192    Tracing::HttpTracerUtility::onUpstreamResponseTrailers(*span_, trailers.get());\n   335\u2192  }\n   336\u2192  if (!parent_.config().upstream_logs_.empty()) {\n   337\u2192    upstream_trailers_ = Http::createHeaderMap<Http::ResponseTrailerMapImpl>(*trailers);\n   338\u2192  }\n   339\u2192  parent_.onUpstreamTrailers(std::move(trailers), *this);\n   340\u2192}\n   341\u2192\n   342\u2192void UpstreamRequest::dumpState(std::ostream& os, int indent_level) const {\n   343\u2192  const char* spaces = spacesForLevel(indent_level);\n   344\u2192  os << spaces << \"UpstreamRequest \" << this << \"\\n\";\n   345\u2192  if (connection()) {\n   346\u2192    const auto addressProvider = connection()->connectionInfoProviderSharedPtr();\n   347\u2192    DUMP_DETAILS(addressProvider);\n   348\u2192  }\n   349\u2192  const Http::RequestHeaderMap* request_headers = parent_.downstreamHeaders();"

(callbacks_->upstreamCallbacks()->pausedForConnect()) {\n    89\u2192    return Http::FilterHeadersStatus::StopAllIterationAndWatermark;\n    90\u2192  } else if Http::FilterHeadersStatus::Continue;\n    94\u2192}\n    95\u2192\n    96\u2192// This is the last stop in the filter chain: take the data and ship it to the codec.\n    97\u2192Http::FilterDataStatus UpstreamCodecFilter::decodeData(Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream) {\n    98\u2192  ASSERT(!callbacks_->upstreamCallbacks()->pausedForConnect());\n    99\u2192  ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, 119\u2192Http::FilterMetadataStatus UpstreamCodecFilter::decodeMetadata(Http::MetadataMap& metadata_map) {\n   120\u2192  ASSERT(!callbacks_->upstreamCallbacks()->pausedForConnect());\n   121\u2192 return Http::FilterMetadataStatus::Continue;\n   126\u2192}\n   127\u2192\n   128\u2192// Store the callbacks from the UpstreamFilterManager, for sending the response to.\n   129\u2192void UpstreamCodecFilter::setDecoderFilterCallbacks(Http::StreamDecoderFilterCallbacks& callbacks) {\n   130\u2192  callbacks_ = &callbacks;\n   131\u2192 callbacks_->upstreamCallbacks()->setUpstreamToDownstream(bridge_);\n   134\u2192}\n   135\u2192\n   136\u2192// This is the response 1xx headers arriving from the codec. Send them through the filter manager.\n   137\u2192void UpstreamCodecFilter::CodecBridge::decode1xxHeaders(Http::ResponseHeaderMapPtr&& headers) {\n   138\u2192  // The filter manager can not handle more than 1 1xx header, so only forward\n   139\u2192  // the first one.\n   140\u2192  if (!seen_1xx_headers_) {\n   141\u2192    seen_1xx_headers_ = true;\n   142\u2192    filter_.callbacks_->encode1xxHeaders(std::move(headers));\n   143\u2192  }\n   144\u2192}\n   145\u2192\n   146\u2192// This is the response headers arriving from the codec. Send them through the filter manager.\n   147\u2192void UpstreamCodecFilter::CodecBridge::decodeHeaders(Http::ResponseHeaderMapPtr&& headers,\n   148\u2192                                                     bool end_stream) {\n   149\u2192  // TODO(rodaine): This is actually measuring after the headers are parsed and not the first\n   150\u2192  // byte.\n   151\u2192  filter_.upstreamTiming().onFirstUpstreamRxByteReceived(\n   152\u2192      filter_.callbacks_->dispatcher().timeSource());\n   153\u2192\n   154\u2192  if (filter_.callbacks_->upstreamCallbacks()->pausedForConnect() &&\n   155\u2192      ((Http::CodeUtility::is2xx(Http::Utility::getResponseStatus(*headers))))) {\n   156\u2192    filter_.callbacks_->upstreamCallbacks()->setPausedForConnect(false);\n   157\u2192    filter_.callbacks_->continueDecoding();\n   158\u2192  }\n   159\u2192\n   160\u2192  if (filter_.callbacks_->upstreamCallbacks()->pausedForWebsocketUpgrade()) {\n   161\u2192    const uint64_t status = Http::Utility::getResponseStatus(*headers);\n   162\u2192    const auto protocol = filter_.callbacks_->upstreamCallbacks()->upstreamStreamInfo().protocol();\n   163\u2192    if (status == static_cast<uint64_t>(Http::Code::SwitchingProtocols) ||\n   164\u2192        (protocol.has_value() && protocol.value() != Envoy::Http::Protocol::Http11)) {\n   165\u2192      // handshake is finished and continue the data processing.\n   166\u2192      filter_.callbacks_->upstreamCallbacks()->setPausedForWebsocketUpgrade(false);\n   167\u2192      filter_.callbacks_->continueDecoding();\n   168\u2192    } else {\n   169\u2192      // Other status, e.g., 426 or 200, indicate a failed handshake, Envoy as a proxy will proxy\n   170\u2192      // back the response header to downstream and then close the request, since WebSocket\n   171\u2192      // just needs headers for handshake per RFC-6455. Note: HTTP/2 200 will be normalized to\n   172\u2192      // 101 before this point in codec and this patch will skip this scenario from the above\n   173\u2192      // proto check.\n   174\u2192      filter_.callbacks_->sendLocalReply(\n   175\u2192          static_cast<Envoy::Http::Code>(status), \"\",\n   176\u2192          [&headers](Http::ResponseHeaderMap& local_headers) {\n   177\u2192            headers->iterate([&local_headers](const Envoy::Http::HeaderEntry& header) {\n   178\u2192              local_headers.addCopy(Http::LowerCaseString(header.key().getStringView()),\n   179\u2192                                    header.value().getStringView());\n   180\u2192              return Envoy::Http::HeaderMap::Iterate::Continue;\n   181\u2192            });\n   182\u2192          },\n   183\u2192          std::nullopt, StreamInfo::ResponseCodeDetails::get().WebsocketHandshakeUnsuccessful);\n   184\u2192      return;\n   185\u2192    }\n   186\u2192  }\n   187\u2192\n   188\u2192  maybeEndDecode(end_stream);\n   189\u2192  filter_.callbacks_->encodeHeaders(std::move(headers), end_stream,\n   190\u2192                                    StreamInfo::ResponseCodeDetails::get().ViaUpstream);\n   191\u2192}\n   192\u2192\n   193\u2192// This is response data arriving from the codec. Send it through the filter manager.\n   194\u2192void UpstreamCodecFilter::CodecBridge::decodeData(Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream) {\n   195\u2192  maybeEndDecode(end_stream);\n   196\u2192  filter_.callbacks_->encodeData(data, end_stream);\n   197\u2192}\n   198\u2192\n   199\u2192// This is response trailers arriving from the codec. Send them through the filter manager.\n   200\u2192void UpstreamCodecFilter::CodecBridge::decodeTrailers(Http::ResponseTrailerMapPtr&& trailers) {\n   201\u2192  maybeEndDecode(true);\n   202\u2192  filter_.callbacks_->encodeTrailers(std::move(trailers));\n   203\u2192}\n   204\u2192\n   205\u2192// This is response metadata arriving from the codec. Send it through the filter manager.\n   206\u2192void UpstreamCodecFilter::CodecBridge::decodeMetadata(Http::MetadataMapPtr&& metadata_map) {\n   207\u2192  filter_.callbacks_->encodeMetadata(std::move(metadata_map));\n   208\u2192}\n   209\u2192\n   210\u2192void UpstreamCodecFilter::CodecBridge::dumpState(std::ostream& os, int indent_level) const {\n   211\u2192  filter_.callbacks_->upstreamCallbacks()->dumpState(os, indent_level);\n   212\u2192}\n   213\u2192\n   214\u2192void UpstreamCodecFilter::CodecBridge::maybeEndDecode(bool end_stream) {\n   215\u2192  if (end_stream) {\n   216\u2192    filter_.upstreamTiming().onLastUpstreamRxByteReceived(\n   217\u2192        filter_.callbacks_->dispatcher().timeSource());\n   218\u2192  }\n   219\u2192}\n   220\u2192\n   221\u2192void UpstreamCodecFilter::CodecBridge::onResetStream(Http::StreamResetReason reason,\n   222\u2192                                                     absl::string_view transport_failure_reason) {\n   223\u2192  if (filter_.calling_encode_headers_) {\n   224\u2192    // If called while still encoding errors, the reset reason won't be appended to the details\n   225\u2192    // string through the reset stream call, so append it here.\n   226\u2192    std::string failure_reason(Http::Utility::resetReasonToString(reason));\n   227\u2192    if (!transport_failure_reason.empty()) {\n   228\u2192      absl::StrAppend(&failure_reason, \"|\", transport_failure_reason);\n   229\u2192    }\n   230\u2192    filter_.deferred_reset_status_ = absl::InternalError(failure_reason);\n   231\u2192    return;\n   232\u2192  }\n   233\u2192\n   234\u2192  std::string failure_reason(transport_failure_reason);\n   235\u2192  if (reason == Http::StreamResetReason::LocalReset) {\n   236\u2192    if (!Runtime::runtimeFeatureEnabled(\n   237\u2192            \"envoy.reloadable_features.report_stream_reset_error_code\")) {\n   238\u2192      ASSERT(transport_failure_reason.empty());\n   239\u2192      // Use this to communicate to the upstream request to not force-terminate.\n   240\u2192      failure_reason = \"codec_error\";\n   241\u2192    } else {\n   242\u2192      failure_reason = absl::StrCat(transport_failure_reason, \"|codec_error\");\n   243\u2192    }\n   244\u2192  }\n   245\u2192  filter_.callbacks_->resetStream(reason, failure_reason);\n   246\u2192}\n   247\u2192\n   248\u2192REGISTER_FACTORY(UpstreamCodecFilterFactory,\n   249\u2192                 Server::Configuration::UpstreamHttpFilterConfigFactory);\n   250\u2192\n   251\u2192class DefaultUpstreamHttpFilterChainFactory : public Http::FilterChainFactory {\n   252\u2192public:\n   253\u2192  DefaultUpstreamHttpFilterChainFactory()\n   254\u2192      : factory_([](Http::FilterChainFactoryCallbacks& callbacks) -> void {\n   255\u2192          callbacks.addStreamDecoderFilter(std::make_shared<UpstreamCodecFilter>());\n   256\u2192        }) {}\n   257\u2192\n   258\u2192  bool createFilterChain(\n   259\u2192      Http::FilterChainManager& manager,\n   260\u2192      const Http::FilterChainOptions& = Http::EmptyFilterChainOptions{}) const override {\n   261\u2192    manager.applyFilterFactoryCb({\"envoy.filters.http.upstream_codec\"}, factory_);\n   262\u2192    return true;\n   263\u2192  }\n   264\u2192  bool createUpgradeFilterChain(absl::string_view, const UpgradeMap*, Http::FilterChainManager&,\n   265\u2192                                const Http::FilterChainOptions&) const override {\n   266\u2192    // Upgrade filter chains not yet supported for upstream HTTP filters.\n   267\u2192    return false;\n   268\u2192  }\n   269\u2192\n   270\u2192private:\n   271\u2192  mutable Http::FilterFactoryCb factory_;\n   272\u2192};\n   273\u2192\n   274\u2192const Http::FilterChainFactory& defaultUpstreamHttpFilterChainFactory() {\n   275\u2192  CONSTRUCT_ON_FIRST_USE(DefaultUpstreamHttpFilterChainFactory);\n   276\u2192}\n   277\u2192\n   278\u2192} // namespace Router\n   279\u2192} // namespace Envoy\n   280\u2192"

1569\u2192  case Http::StreamResetReason::Overflow:\n  1570\u2192    return StreamInfo::CoreResponseFlag::UpstreamOverflow;\n  1571\u2192  case Http::StreamResetReason::RemoteReset:\n  1572\u2192 Filter::handleNon5xxResponseHeaders(absl::optional<Grpc::Status::GrpcStatus> grpc_status,\n  1585\u2192                                         UpstreamRequest& upstream_request, bool end_stream,\n  1586\u2192                                         uint64_t grpc_to_http_status) {\n  1587\u2192  // We need to defer gRPC success until after we have processed grpc-status in\n  1588\u2192  // the trailers.\n  1589\u2192  if (grpc_request_) {\n  1590\u2192    if (end_stream) {\n  1591\u2192      if (grpc_status && !Http::CodeUtility::is5xx(grpc_to_http_status)) {\n  1592\u2192        upstream_request.upstreamHost()->stats().rq_success_.inc();\n  1593\u2192      } else {\n  1594\u2192        upstream_request.upstreamHost()->stats().rq_error_.inc();\n  1595\u2192      }\n  1596\u2192    } else {\n  1597\u2192      upstream_request.grpcRqSuccessDeferred(true);\n  1598\u2192    }\n  1599\u2192  } else {\n  1600\u2192    upstream_request.upstreamHost()->stats().rq_success_.inc();\n  1601\u2192  }\n  1602\u2192}\n  1603\u2192\n  1604\u2192void Filter::onUpstream1xxHeaders(Http::ResponseHeaderMapPtr&& headers,\n  1605\u2192                                  UpstreamRequest& upstream_request) {\n  1606\u2192  const uint64_t response_code = Http::Utility::getResponseStatus(*headers);\n  1607\u2192  chargeUpstreamCode(response_code, *headers, upstream_request.upstreamHost(), false);\n  1608\u2192  ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(debug, \"upstream 1xx ({}).\", *callbacks_, response_code);\n  1609\u2192\n  1610\u2192  downstream_response_started_ = true;\n  1611\u2192  final_upstream_request_ = &upstream_request;"

"   380\u2192void UpstreamRequest::acceptHeadersFromRouter(bool end_stream) {\n   381\u2192  ASSERT(!router_sent_end_stream_);\n   382\u2192  router_sent_end_stream_ = end_stream;\n   383\u2192\n   384\u2192  // Make sure that when we are forwarding CONNECT payload we do not do so until\n   385\u2192  // the upstream has accepted the CONNECT request.\n   386\u2192  // This must be done before conn_pool->newStream, as onPoolReady un-pauses for CONNECT\n   387\u2192  // termination.\n   388\u2192  auto* headers = parent_.downstreamHeaders();\n   389\u2192  if (headers->getMethodValue() == Http::Headers::get().MethodValues.Connect) {\n   390\u2192    paused_for_connect_ = true;\n   391\u2192    // If this is a websocket upgrade request, pause the request until the upstream sends\n   392\u2192    // the 101 Switching Protocols response code. \"envoy.reloadable_features.check_switch_protocol_websocket_handshake\")) &&\n   396\u2192             Http::Utility::isWebSocketUpgradeRequest(*headers)) {\n   397\u2192    paused_for_websocket_ = true;\n   398\u2192  }\n   399\u2192\n   400\u2192  // Kick off creation of the upstream connection immediately upon receiving headers.\n   401\u2192  // In future it may be possible for upstream HTTP filters to delay this, or influence connection\n   402\u2192  // creation but for now optimize for minimal latency and fetch the connection\n   403\u2192  // as soon as possible.\n   404\u2192  conn_pool_->newStream(this);\n   405\u2192\n   406\u2192  if (parent_.config().upstream_log_flush_interval_.has_value()) {\n   407\u2192    upstream_log_flush_timer_ = do the periodic log.\n   410\u2192      if (!streamInfo().requestComplete().has_value()) {\n   411\u2192        upstreamLog(AccessLog::AccessLogType::UpstreamPeriodic);\n   412\u2192        resetUpstreamLogFlushTimer();\n   413\u2192      }\n   414\u2192      // Both downstream and upstream bytes meters may not be initialized when\n   415\u2192      // the timer goes off, e.g. if it takes longer than the interval for a\n   416\u2192      // connection to be initialized; check for nullptr.\n   417\u2192      auto& downstream_bytes_meter = stream_info_.getDownstreamBytesMeter();\n 424\u2192        upstream_bytes_meter->takeUpstreamPeriodicLoggingSnapshot(now);\n   425\u2192      }\n   426\u2192    });\n   427\u2192\n   428\u2192    resetUpstreamLogFlushTimer();\n   429\u2192 filter_manager_->decodeHeaders(*parent_.downstreamHeaders(), end_stream);\n   434\u2192}\n   435\u2192\n   436\u2192void UpstreamRequest::acceptDataFromRouter(Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream) {\n   437\u2192  ASSERT(!router_sent_end_stream_);\n   438\u2192  router_sent_end_stream_ = end_stream;\n   439\u2192\n   440\u2192  filter_manager_->decodeData(data, end_stream);\n   441\u2192}\n scope(&parent_.callbacks()->scope(), parent_.callbacks()->dispatcher());\n   458\u2192\n   459\u2192  if (span_ != nullptr) {\n   460\u2192    // Add tags about reset.\n   461\u2192 667\u2192    if (host_selection_response.cancelable) {\n   668\u2192      host_selection_response.cancelable->cancel();\n   669\u2192    }\n   670\u2192    // This branch handles the common case of ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(debug, \"Doing asynchronous host selection\\n\", *callbacks_);\n   679\u2192  // Latch the cancel handle and call it in Filter::onDestroy to avoid any use-after-frees for cases\n   680\u2192  // like stream timeout.\n   681\u2192  host_selection_cancelable_ = std::move(host_selection_response.cancelable);\n   682\u2192  // Configure a callback to be called on asynchronous host selection.\n   683\u2192  on_host_selected_ = ([this, cluster, end_stream,\n   684\u2192                        modify_headers](Upstream::HostConstSharedPtr&& host,\n   685\u2192                                        std::string host_selection_details) -> void {\n   686\u2192    // It should always be safe to call continueDecodeHeaders. In the case the\n   687\u2192    // stream had a local reply before host selection completed,\n   688\u2192    // the lookup should be canceled.\n   689\u2192    bool should_continue_decoding = false;\n   690\u2192    continueDecodeHeaders(cluster, *downstream_headers_, end_stream, modify_headers,\n   691\u2192                          &should_continue_decoding, std::move(host), host_selection_details);\n   692\u2192    // continueDecodeHeaders can itself send a local reply, in which case should_continue_decoding\n   693\u2192    // should be false. If this is not the case, we can continue the filter chain due to successful\n   694\u2192    // asynchronous host selection.\n   695\u2192    if (should_continue_decoding) {\n   696\u2192      ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(debug, \"Continuing stream now that host resolution is complete\\n\",\n   697\u2192                       *callbacks_);\n   698\u2192      callbacks_->continueDecoding();\n   699\u2192    } else {\n   700\u2192      ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(debug, \"Aborting stream after host resolution is complete\\n\", *callbacks_);\n   701\u2192    }\n   702\u2192  });\n   703\u2192  return Http::FilterHeadersStatus::StopAllIterationAndWatermark;\n   704\u2192}\n   705\u2192\n   706\u2192// When asynchronous host selection is complete, call the pre-configured on_host_selected_function.\n   707\u2192void Filter::onAsyncHostSelection(Upstream::HostConstSharedPtr&& host, std::string&& details) {\n   708\u2192  ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(debug, \"Completing asynchronous host selection [{}]\\n\", on_host_selected_(std::move(host), details);\n   712\u2192}\n   713\u2192\n   714\u2192Http::FilterHeadersStatus Filter::continueDecodeHeaders(\n   715\u2192    Upstream::ThreadLocalCluster* cluster, callbacks_->streamInfo().filterState();\n   720\u2192  const DebugConfig* debug_config = filter_state->getDataReadOnly<DebugConfig>(DebugConfig::key());\n   721\u2192 98. Task @ -
Input
{
  "description": "Explore filter chain boundaries",
  "max_turns": 30,
  "prompt": "I need to understand the architectural distinction between the two \"filter chain\" concepts in Envoy:\n\n1. The **network-level filter chain** managed by `FilterChainManager` (in listener_manager):\n   - Look at `source/common/listener_manager/filter_chain_manager_impl.h` for FilterChainManagerImpl\n   - Look at `source/common/network/filter_manager_impl.h` for the Network::FilterManagerImpl\n   - Find the interfaces in `envoy/network/filter.h` - ReadFilter, WriteFilter, Filter\n   - How network filters are chained (addReadFilter, addWriteFilter)\n\n2. The **HTTP-level filter chain** managed by `Http::FilterManager`:\n   - Look at `source/common/http/filter_manager.h` for Http::FilterManager\n   - Find the interfaces in `envoy/http/filter.h` - StreamDecoderFilter, StreamEncoderFilter, StreamFilter\n   - How HTTP filters are chained\n\n3. Understand WHY they are separate:\n   - What does the network filter chain handle vs the HTTP filter chain?\n   - How do they relate to each other? (HTTP Connection Manager is a network filter that contains the HTTP filter chain)\n   - What other network filters exist besides HCM?\n\n4. Look at the connection object and how it dispatches to network filters in `source/common/network/connection_impl.h` or similar\n\nProvide specific file paths, class names, function names, and line numbers for all findings.",
  "subagent_type": "Explore"
}
Output
{
  "agentId": "a9c79ea",
  "content": [
    {
      "text": "Excellent! Now I have enough information. Let me compile a comprehensive architectural analysis document.\n\n## Comprehensive Analysis: Network-Level vs HTTP-Level Filter Chains in Envoy\n\nBased on my exploration of the Envoy codebase, here's a detailed architectural distinction between the two filter chain concepts:\n\n---\n\n## 1. NETWORK-LEVEL FILTER CHAIN\n\n### Architecture Overview\nThe network-level filter chain is managed by the **Network::FilterManager** (implemented as **Network::FilterManagerImpl**) and operates at Layer 4 (TCP/UDP).\n\n#### Key Components:\n\n**File Location:** `/workspace/source/common/network/filter_manager_impl.h` (lines 108-204)\n\n**Class Hierarchy:**\n```\nNetwork::FilterManagerImpl (lines 108-123)\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 Manages Network::ReadFilter and Network::WriteFilter\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 Operates on raw bytes/Buffer::Instance\n\u2514\u2500\u2500 Manages connection-level data flow\n```\n\n**Filter Interface:** `/workspace/envoy/network/filter.h`\n\nThe network filter interfaces are:\n\n1. **ReadFilter** (lines 240-280)\n   - `FilterStatus onData(Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream)`\n   - `FilterStatus onNewConnection()`\n   - `void initializeReadFilterCallbacks(ReadFilterCallbacks& callbacks)`\n\n2. **WriteFilter** (lines 122-146)\n   - `FilterStatus onWrite(Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream)`\n   - `void initializeWriteFilterCallbacks(WriteFilterCallbacks&)`\n\n3. **Filter** (line 288)\n   - Combination of ReadFilter + WriteFilter\n\n#### Internal Data Structures:\n\n**Filter Storage in FilterManagerImpl** (lines 196-199):\n```cpp\nstd::list<ActiveReadFilterPtr> upstream_filters_;     // FIFO order for reads\nstd::list<ActiveWriteFilterPtr> downstream_filters_;  // LIFO order for writes\n```\n\n**Read Filter Execution Order:**\n- FIFO (First-In-First-Out): filters added first are executed first\n- Location: `/workspace/source/common/network/filter_manager_impl.h` line 314 comment\n\n**Write Filter Execution Order:**\n- LIFO (Last-In-First-Out): filters added last are executed first\n- Location: `/workspace/source/common/network/filter_manager_impl.h` line 299 comment\n\n#### Filter Chain Iteration:\n\n**Read Path:**\n```\nonContinueReading() [lines 62-98, filter_manager_impl.cc]\n  \u2193\nLoop through upstream_filters_ (FIFO order)\n  \u2193\nFor each filter:\n  - Call onNewConnection() if not initialized (line 84)\n  - Call onData() with buffer (line 92)\n```\n\n**Write Path:**\n```\nonWrite() \n  \u2193\nLoop through downstream_filters_ (LIFO order)\n  \u2193\nFor each filter:\n  - Call onWrite() with buffer\n```\n\n#### Example Network Filter: TCP Proxy\n\n**File:** `/workspace/source/common/tcp_proxy/tcp_proxy.h` (lines 414-426)\n\n```cpp\nclass Filter : public Network::ReadFilter,\n               public Upstream::LoadBalancerContextBase,\n               protected Logger::Loggable<Logger::Id::filter>,\n               public GenericConnectionPoolCallbacks {\npublic:\n  // Network::ReadFilter implementation\n  Network::FilterStatus onData(Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream) override;\n  Network::FilterStatus onNewConnection() override;\n  void initializeReadFilterCallbacks(Network::ReadFilterCallbacks& callbacks) override;\n  bool startUpstreamSecureTransport() override;\n```\n\nThe TCP Proxy filter:\n- Implements `Network::ReadFilter` to intercept incoming data\n- Operates on raw bytes before any HTTP processing\n- Can establish upstream connections\n- Passes data through to next filters or to upstream\n\n---\n\n## 2. HTTP-LEVEL FILTER CHAIN\n\n### Architecture Overview\nThe HTTP-level filter chain is managed by **Http::FilterManager** (implemented as **Http::DownstreamFilterManager**) and operates at Layer 7 (HTTP).\n\n#### Key Components:\n\n**File Location:** `/workspace/source/common/http/filter_manager.h` (lines 681-1164)\n\n**Class Definition** (lines 681-683):\n```cpp\nclass FilterManager : public ScopeTrackedObject,\n                      public FilterChainManager,\n                      Logger::Loggable<Logger::Id::http>\n```\n\n**Filter Interfaces:** `/workspace/envoy/http/filter.h`\n\nThe HTTP filter interfaces are:\n\n1. **StreamDecoderFilter** (lines 902-953)\n   - `FilterHeadersStatus decodeHeaders(RequestHeaderMap& headers, bool end_stream)`\n   - `FilterDataStatus decodeData(Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream)`\n   - `FilterTrailersStatus decodeTrailers(RequestTrailerMap& trailers)`\n   - `FilterMetadataStatus decodeMetadata(MetadataMap&)`\n   - `void setDecoderFilterCallbacks(StreamDecoderFilterCallbacks& callbacks)`\n\n2. **StreamEncoderFilter** (lines 1118-1181)\n   - `Filter1xxHeadersStatus encode1xxHeaders(ResponseHeaderMap& headers)`\n   - `FilterHeadersStatus encodeHeaders(ResponseHeaderMap& headers, bool end_stream)`\n   - `FilterDataStatus encodeData(Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream)`\n   - `FilterTrailersStatus encodeTrailers(ResponseTrailerMap& trailers)`\n   - `FilterMetadataStatus encodeMetadata(MetadataMap&)`\n   - `void setEncoderFilterCallbacks(StreamEncoderFilterCallbacks& callbacks)`\n\n3. **StreamFilter** (line 1188)\n   - Combination of StreamDecoderFilter + StreamEncoderFilter\n\n#### Internal Data Structures:\n\n**Filter Storage in FilterManager** (lines 1109-1111):\n```cpp\nStreamDecoderFilters decoder_filters_;    // Stores decoder filters\nStreamEncoderFilters encoder_filters_;    // Stores encoder filters\nstd::vector<StreamFilterBase*> filters_;  // All filters for lifecycle management\n```\n\n**StreamDecoderFilters** (`/workspace/source/common/http/filter_manager.h`, lines 73-81):\n```cpp\nstruct StreamDecoderFilters {\n  using Element = ActiveStreamDecoderFilter;\n  using Iterator = std::vector<ActiveStreamDecoderFilterPtr>::iterator;\n  \n  Iterator begin() { return entries_.begin(); }\n  Iterator end() { return entries_.end(); }\n  \n  std::vector<ActiveStreamDecoderFilterPtr> entries_;\n};\n```\n\n**StreamEncoderFilters** (lines 92-100):\n```cpp\nstruct StreamEncoderFilters {\n  using Element = ActiveStreamEncoderFilter;\n  using Iterator = std::vector<ActiveStreamEncoderFilterPtr>::reverse_iterator;\n  \n  Iterator begin() { return entries_.rbegin(); }    // REVERSE iteration!\n  Iterator end() { return entries_.rend(); }\n  \n  std::vector<ActiveStreamEncoderFilterPtr> entries_;\n};\n```\n\n#### Filter Chain Iteration:\n\n**Decoder Path (Request Incoming):**\n```\ndecodeHeaders() [line 745]\n  \u2193\ndecodeHeaders(nullptr, headers, end_stream) [line 747]\n  \u2193\nLoop through decoder_filters_ (FIFO order)\n  \u2193\nFor each filter:\n  - Call decodeHeaders() on filter\n  - Process status (Continue, StopIteration, etc.)\n```\n\n**Encoder Path (Response Outgoing):**\n```\nencodeHeaders() [line 1068]\n  \u2193\nLoop through encoder_filters_ in REVERSE order (lines 92-100)\n  \u2193\nFor each filter (C, B, A if configured as A, B, C):\n  - Call encodeHeaders() on filter\n  - Process status\n```\n\n**Design Note:** Encoder filters iterate in reverse (LIFO-like) compared to decoder filters. See lines 83-100 for detailed comments.\n\n---\n\n## 3. THE RELATIONSHIP: HTTP Connection Manager as a Network Filter\n\n### The Bridge Between Two Levels\n\n**File:** `/workspace/source/common/http/conn_manager_impl.h` (lines 60-64)\n\n```cpp\nclass ConnectionManagerImpl : Logger::Loggable<Logger::Id::http>,\n                             public Network::ReadFilter,  // <-- It's a NETWORK filter!\n                             public ServerConnectionCallbacks,\n                             public Network::ConnectionCallbacks,\n                             public Http::ApiListener\n```\n\n### How It Works:\n\n1. **HTTP Connection Manager is a Network-Level Filter**\n   - Location: `/workspace/source/common/http/conn_manager_impl.h`, lines 60-64\n   - Implements `Network::ReadFilter` interface\n   - Registered as a network filter in the listener's filter chain\n\n2. **It Contains the HTTP Filter Chain**\n   - It instantiates an `Http::FilterManager` (line 1109 in filter_manager.h)\n   - Receives raw TCP data via `onData()` (line 94)\n   - Parses HTTP using a codec\n   - Creates HTTP streams and manages the HTTP filter chain\n\n3. **Network Filter Execution Flow:**\n   ```\n   Network Layer (FilterManagerImpl)\n        \u2193\n   TCP Data arrives \u2192 onData()\n        \u2193\n   HttpConnectionManager (Network::ReadFilter)\n        \u2193\n   Parse HTTP from bytes\n        \u2193\n   Create HTTP Stream\n        \u2193\n   HTTP Layer (FilterManager)\n        \u2193\n   Decoder Filters \u2192 Router \u2192 Encoder Filters\n   ```\n\n**Code Evidence:**\n- HCM implements `Network::ReadFilter::onData()` at line 94 of conn_manager_impl.h\n- HCM calls `FilterManager::decodeHeaders()` internally to process HTTP requests\n\n---\n\n## 4. OTHER NETWORK FILTERS (Besides HCM)\n\nNetwork filters that exist at the same level as HTTP Connection Manager:\n\n| Filter | File | Purpose |\n|--------|------|---------|\n| **TCP Proxy** | `/workspace/source/common/tcp_proxy/tcp_proxy.h:414` | Layer 4 TCP proxying, establishes upstream connections |\n| **SNI Cluster** | `/workspace/source/extensions/filters/network/sni_cluster/` | Extracts SNI and selects upstream cluster |\n| **Local RateLimit** | `/workspace/source/extensions/filters/network/local_ratelimit/` | Rate limiting at connection level |\n| **Redis Proxy** | `/workspace/source/extensions/filters/network/redis_proxy/` | Redis protocol handling |\n| **Mongo Proxy** | `/workspace/source/extensions/filters/network/mongo_proxy/` | MongoDB protocol handling |\n\nAll of these implement `Network::ReadFilter` or `Network::WriteFilter` and operate on raw bytes before/instead of HTTP processing.\n\n---\n\n## 5. WHY THEY ARE SEPARATE: Architectural Rationale\n\n### Network Filter Chain Benefits:\n- **Protocol Agnostic:** Can handle TCP, UDP, TLS, HTTP/1.1, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, etc.\n- **Connection-Level Operations:** Rate limiting, monitoring, routing decisions before protocol parsing\n- **Performance:** Can stop iteration without parsing full protocol\n- **Extensibility:** Any protocol-level filter can be plugged in\n\n### HTTP Filter Chain Benefits:\n- **HTTP-Specific:** Headers, trailers, metadata, body streaming\n- **Request/Response Semantics:** Decoder/Encoder split reflecting request/response lifecycle\n- **Advanced Features:** Routing, load balancing, authentication per-request\n- **Multiple Streams:** One TCP connection can carry multiple HTTP streams\n\n### Connection Flow:\n```\nListener\n  \u2193\nNetwork::FilterChainManager (findFilterChain)\n  \u2193\nNetwork::FilterManagerImpl instantiated\n  \u2193\nNetwork Filters (TCP Proxy, etc.)\n  \u2193\nHTTP Connection Manager (Network::ReadFilter)\n  \u2193\nHttp::FilterManager instantiated\n  \u2193\nHTTP Filters (Router, Authentication, etc.)\n```\n\n---\n\n## 6. KEY DIFFERENCES SUMMARY\n\n| Aspect | Network Level | HTTP Level |\n|--------|---------------|-----------|\n| **Location** | `/workspace/source/common/network/filter_manager_impl.h` | `/workspace/source/common/http/filter_manager.h` |\n| **Interface** | `Network::ReadFilter`, `Network::WriteFilter` | `StreamDecoderFilter`, `StreamEncoderFilter` |\n| **Data Type** | `Buffer::Instance` (raw bytes) | `RequestHeaderMap`, `ResponseHeaderMap`, etc. |\n| **Scope** | Connection (all data on connection) | Stream (single request/response pair) |\n| **Order** | Read: FIFO, Write: LIFO | Decoder: FIFO, Encoder: Reverse |\n| **Parser** | None (raw bytes) | HTTP codec (HTTP/1.1, HTTP/2, HTTP/3) |\n| **Initialization** | `onNewConnection()` | Per-stream in decoder |\n| **Termination** | Connection close | Stream complete |\n| **Key Methods** | `onData()`, `onNewConnection()` | `decodeHeaders()`, `encodeHeaders()`, etc. |\n| **Callbacks** | `ReadFilterCallbacks`, `WriteFilterCallbacks` | `StreamDecoderFilterCallbacks`, `StreamEncoderFilterCallbacks` |\n\n---\n\n## 7. CONCRETE CODE REFERENCES\n\n**Network Filter Chain Manager:**\n- Declaration: `/workspace/source/common/listener_manager/filter_chain_manager_impl.h:127`\n- Finding filter chain: `/workspace/source/common/listener_manager/filter_chain_manager_impl.h:148`\n\n**Network Filter Iteration:**\n- Read filter initialization: `/workspace/source/common/network/filter_manager_impl.cc:41-60`\n- Read filter continuation: `/workspace/source/common/network/filter_manager_impl.cc:62-98`\n\n**HTTP Filter Manager:**\n- Main class: `/workspace/source/common/http/filter_manager.h:681-1164`\n- Decoder filter iteration: `/workspace/source/common/http/filter_manager.h:745-767`\n- Encoder filter iteration: `/workspace/source/common/http/filter_manager.h:92-100` (reverse order)\n\n**HTTP Connection Manager (the bridge):**\n- Implements Network::ReadFilter: `/workspace/source/common/http/conn_manager_impl.h:60-64`\n- onData method: `/workspace/source/common/http/conn_manager_impl.h:94`\n- Creates HTTP streams: `/workspace/source/common/http/conn_manager_impl.h:102-106`\n\nThis two-level architecture allows Envoy to:\n1. Process non-HTTP protocols at the network layer\n2. Efficiently handle multiple HTTP streams on a single connection\n3. Share network-level functionality (rate limiting, TLS) across all protocols\n4. Keep HTTP-specific logic isolated and composable",
      "type": "text"
    }
  ],
  "prompt": "I need to understand the architectural distinction between the two \"filter chain\" concepts in Envoy:\n\n1. The **network-level filter chain** managed by `FilterChainManager` (in listener_manager):\n   - Look at `source/common/listener_manager/filter_chain_manager_impl.h` for FilterChainManagerImpl\n   - Look at `source/common/network/filter_manager_impl.h` for the Network::FilterManagerImpl\n   - Find the interfaces in `envoy/network/filter.h` - ReadFilter, WriteFilter, Filter\n   - How network filters are chained (addReadFilter, addWriteFilter)\n\n2. The **HTTP-level filter chain** managed by `Http::FilterManager`:\n   - Look at `source/common/http/filter_manager.h` for Http::FilterManager\n   - Find the interfaces in `envoy/http/filter.h` - StreamDecoderFilter, StreamEncoderFilter, StreamFilter\n   - How HTTP filters are chained\n\n3. Understand WHY they are separate:\n   - What does the network filter chain handle vs the HTTP filter chain?\n   - How do they relate to each other? (HTTP Connection Manager is a network filter that contains the HTTP filter chain)\n   - What other network filters exist besides HCM?\n\n4. Look at the connection object and how it dispatches to network filters in `source/common/network/connection_impl.h` or similar\n\nProvide specific file paths, class names, function names, and line numbers for all findings.",

During the lifetime of FilterChainManagerImpl this\n   152\u2192  // should be called at most once.\n   153\u2192  absl::Status addFilterChains(\n   154\u2192      const xds::type::matcher::v3::Matcher* filter_chain_matcher,\n   155\u2192      absl::Span<const envoy::config::listener::v3::FilterChain* const> filter_chain_span,\n   156\u2192      const envoy::config::listener::v3::FilterChain* default_filter_chain,\n   157\u2192      FilterChainFactoryBuilder& filter_chain_factory_builder,\n   158\u2192      FilterChainFactoryContextCreator& context_creator);\n   159\u2192\n   160\u2192  static bool isWildcardServerName(const std::string& name);\n   161\u2192\n   162\u2192  // Return the current view of filter chains, keyed by filter chain message. Used by the owning\n   163\u2192  // listener to calculate the intersection of filter chains with another listener.\n   164\u2192  const FcContextMap& filterChainsByMessage() const { return fc_contexts_; }\n   165\u2192  const absl::optional<envoy::config::listener::v3::FilterChain>&\n   166\u2192  defaultFilterChainMessage() const {\n   167\u2192    return default_filter_chain_message_;\n   168\u2192  }\n   169\u2192  const Network::DrainableFilterChainSharedPtr& defaultFilterChain() const {\n   170\u2192    return default_filter_chain_;\n   171\u2192  }\n   172\u2192\n   173\u2192private:\n   174\u2192  absl::Status convertIPsToTries();\n   175\u2192  const Network::FilterChain* findFilterChainUsingMatcher(const Network::ConnectionSocket& socket,\n   176\u2192                                                          const StreamInfo::StreamInfo& info) const;\n   177\u2192\n   178\u2192  // Build default filter chain from filter chain message. Skip the build but copy from original\n   179\u2192  // filter chain manager if the default filter chain message duplicates the message in origin\n   180\u2192  // filter chain manager. Called by addFilterChains().\n   181\u2192  absl::Status copyOrRebuildDefaultFilterChain(\n   182\u2192      const envoy::config::listener::v3::FilterChain* default_filter_chain,\n   183\u2192      FilterChainFactoryBuilder& filter_chain_factory_builder,\n   184\u2192      FilterChainFactoryContextCreator& context_creator);\n   185\u2192\n   186\u2192  using SourcePortsMap = absl::flat_hash_map<uint16_t, Network::FilterChainSharedPtr>;\n   187\u2192  using SourcePortsMapSharedPtr = std::shared_ptr<SourcePortsMap>;\n   188\u2192  using SourceIPsMap = absl::flat_hash_map<std::string, SourcePortsMapSharedPtr>;\n   189\u2192  using SourceIPsTrie = Network::LcTrie::LcTrie<SourcePortsMapSharedPtr>;\n   190\u2192  using SourceIPsTriePtr = std::unique_ptr<SourceIPsTrie>;\n   191\u2192  using SourceTypesArray = std::array<std::pair<SourceIPsMap, SourceIPsTriePtr>, 3>;\n   192\u2192  using SourceTypesArraySharedPtr = std::shared_ptr<SourceTypesArray>;\n   193\u2192  using DirectSourceIPsMap = absl::flat_hash_map<std::string, SourceTypesArraySharedPtr>;\n   194\u2192  using DirectSourceIPsTrie = Network::LcTrie::LcTrie<SourceTypesArraySharedPtr>;\n   195\u2192  using DirectSourceIPsTriePtr = std::unique_ptr<DirectSourceIPsTrie>;\n   196\u2192\n   197\u2192  // This would nominally be a `std::pair`, but that version crashes the Windows clang_cl compiler\n   198\u2192  // for unknown reasons. This variation, which is equivalent, does not crash the compiler.\n   199\u2192  // The `std::pair` version was confirmed to crash both clang 11 and clang 12.\n   200\u2192  struct DirectSourceIPsPair {\n   201\u2192    DirectSourceIPsMap first;\n   202\u2192    DirectSourceIPsTriePtr second;\n   203\u2192  };\n   204\u2192\n   205\u2192  using ApplicationProtocolsMap = absl::flat_hash_map<std::string, DirectSourceIPsPair>;\n   206\u2192  using TransportProtocolsMap = absl::flat_hash_map<std::string, ApplicationProtocolsMap>;\n   207\u2192  // Both exact server names and wildcard domains are part of the same map, in which wildcard\n   208\u2192  // domains are prefixed with \".\" (i.e. \".example.com\" for \"*.example.com\") to differentiate\n   209\u2192  // between exact and wildcard entries.\n   210\u2192  using ServerNamesMap = absl::flat_hash_map<std::string, TransportProtocolsMap>;\n   211\u2192  using ServerNamesMapSharedPtr = std::shared_ptr<ServerNamesMap>;\n   212\u2192  using DestinationIPsMap = absl::flat_hash_map<std::string, ServerNamesMapSharedPtr>;\n   213\u2192  using DestinationIPsTrie = Network::LcTrie::LcTrie<ServerNamesMapSharedPtr>;\n   214\u2192  using DestinationIPsTriePtr = std::unique_ptr<DestinationIPsTrie>;\n   215\u2192  using DestinationPortsMap =\n   216\u2192      absl::flat_hash_map<uint16_t, std::pair<DestinationIPsMap, DestinationIPsTriePtr>>;\n   217\u2192\n   218\u2192  absl::Status addFilterChainForDestinationPorts(\n   219\u2192      DestinationPortsMap& destination_ports_map, uint16_t destination_port,\n   220\u2192      const std::vector<std::string>& destination_ips,\n   221\u2192      const absl::Span<const std::string> server_names, const std::string& transport_protocol,\n   222\u2192      const absl::Span<const std::string* const> application_protocols,\n   223\u2192      const std::vector<std::string>& direct_source_ips,\n const;\n   296\u2192\n   297\u2192  const FilterChainManagerImpl* getOriginFilterChainManager() { return origin_.value(); }\n   298\u2192  // Duplicate the inherent factory context if any.\n   299\u2192  Network::DrainableFilterChainSharedPtr\n   300\u2192  findExistingFilterChain(const envoy::config::listener::v3::FilterChain& filter_chain_message);\n   301\u2192\n   302\u2192  // Mapping from filter chain message to filter chain. This is used by LDS response handler to\n   303\u2192  // detect the filter chains in the intersection of existing listener and new listener.\n   304\u2192  FcContextMap fc_contexts_;\n   305\u2192\n   306\u2192  absl::optional<envoy::config::listener::v3::FilterChain> default_filter_chain_message_;\n   307\u2192  // The optional fallback filter chain if destination_ports_map_ does not find a matched filter\n   308\u2192  // chain.\n   309\u2192  Network::DrainableFilterChainSharedPtr default_filter_chain_;\n   310\u2192\n   311\u2192  // Mapping of FilterChain's configured destination ports, IPs, server names, transport protocols\n   312\u2192  // and application protocols, using structures defined above.\n   313\u2192  DestinationPortsMap destination_ports_map_;\n   314\u2192\n   315\u2192  const std::vector<Network::Address::InstanceConstSharedPtr>& addresses_;\n   316\u2192  // This is the reference to a factory context which all the generations of listener share.\n   317\u2192  Configuration::FactoryContext& parent_context_;\n   318\u2192  std::list<std::shared_ptr<Configuration::FilterChainFactoryContext>> factory_contexts_;\n   319\u2192\n   320\u2192  // Reference to the previous generation of filter chain manager to share the filter chains.\n   321\u2192  // Caution: only during warm up could the optional have value.\n   322\u2192  absl::optional<const FilterChainManagerImpl*> origin_{nullptr};\n   323\u2192\n   324\u2192  // For FilterChainFactoryContextCreator\n   325\u2192  // init manager owned by the corresponding listener. The reference is valid when building the\n   326\u2192  // filter chain.\n   327\u2192  Init::Manager& init_manager_;\n   328\u2192\n   329\u2192  // Matcher selecting the filter chain name.\n   330\u2192  Matcher::MatchTreePtr<Network::MatchingData> matcher_;\n   331\u2192\n   332\u2192  // Index filter chains by name, used by the matcher actions.\n   333\u2192  FilterChainsByName filter_chains_by_name_;\n   334\u2192};\n   335\u2192\n   336\u2192namespace FilterChain {\n   337\u2192DECLARE_FACTORY(FilterChainNameActionFactory);\n   338\u2192}\n   339\u2192\n   340\u2192} // namespace Server\n   341\u2192} // namespace Envoy\n   342\u2192"

"     1\u2192#pragma once\n     2\u2192\n     3\u2192#include <list>\n     4\u2192#include <memory>\n     5\u2192\n     6\u2192#include \"envoy/network/connection.h\"\n     7\u2192#include \"envoy/network/filter.h\"\n     8\u2192#include \"envoy/network/listen_socket.h\"\n     9\u2192\n    10\u2192#include \"source/common/common/linked_object.h\"\n    11\u2192\n    12\u2192namespace Envoy {\n    13\u2192namespace Network {\n    14\u2192\n    15\u2192struct StreamBuffer {\n    16\u2192  Buffer::Instance& buffer;\n    17\u2192  const bool end_stream;\n    18\u2192};\n    19\u2192\n    20\u2192/**\n    21\u2192 * Interface used to obtain read buffers.\n    22\u2192 */\n    23\u2192class ReadBufferSource {\n    24\u2192public:\n    25\u2192  virtual ~ReadBufferSource() = default;\n    26\u2192\n    27\u2192  /**\n    28\u2192   * Fetch the read buffer for the source.\n    29\u2192   */\n    30\u2192  virtual StreamBuffer getReadBuffer() PURE;\n    31\u2192};\n    32\u2192\n    33\u2192/**\n    34\u2192 * Interface used to obtain write buffers.\n    35\u2192 */\n    36\u2192class WriteBufferSource {\n    37\u2192public:\n    38\u2192  virtual ~WriteBufferSource() = default;\n    39\u2192\n    40\u2192  /**\n    41\u2192   * Fetch the write buffer for the source.\n    42\u2192   */\n    43\u2192  virtual StreamBuffer getWriteBuffer() PURE;\n    44\u2192};\n    45\u2192\n    46\u2192/**\n    47\u2192 * Adapter that masquerades a given buffer instance as a ReadBufferSource.\n    48\u2192 */\n    49\u2192class FixedReadBufferSource : public ReadBufferSource {\n    50\u2192public:\n    51\u2192  FixedReadBufferSource(Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream)\n    52\u2192      : data_(data), end_stream_(end_stream) {}\n    53\u2192\n    54\u2192  StreamBuffer getReadBuffer() override { return {data_, end_stream_}; }\n    55\u2192\n    56\u2192private:\n    57\u2192  Buffer::Instance& data_;\n    58\u2192  const bool end_stream_;\n    59\u2192};\n    60\u2192\n    61\u2192/**\n    62\u2192 * Adapter that masquerades a given buffer instance as a WriteBufferSource.\n    63\u2192 */\n    64\u2192class FixedWriteBufferSource : public WriteBufferSource {\n    65\u2192public:\n    66\u2192  FixedWriteBufferSource(Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream)\n    67\u2192      : data_(data), end_stream_(end_stream) {}\n    68\u2192\n    69\u2192  StreamBuffer getWriteBuffer() override { return {data_, end_stream_}; }\n    70\u2192\n    71\u2192private:\n    72\u2192  Buffer::Instance& data_;\n    73\u2192  const bool end_stream_;\n    74\u2192};\n    75\u2192\n    76\u2192/**\n    77\u2192 * Connection enriched with methods for advanced cases, i.e. write data bypassing filter chain.\n    78\u2192 *\n    79\u2192 * Since FilterManager is only user of those methods for now, the class is named after it.\n    80\u2192 */\n    81\u2192class FilterManagerConnection : public virtual Connection,\n    82\u2192                                public ReadBufferSource,\n    83\u2192                                public WriteBufferSource {\n    84\u2192public:\n    85\u2192  ~FilterManagerConnection() override = default;\n    86\u2192\n    87\u2192  /**\n    88\u2192   * Write data to the connection bypassing filter chain.\n    89\u2192   *\n    90\u2192   * I.e., consider a scenario where iteration over the filter chain is stopped at some point\n    91\u2192   * and later is resumed via a call to WriteFilterCallbacks::injectWriteDataToFilterChain().\n    92\u2192   *\n    93\u2192   * @param data supplies the data to write to the connection.\n    94\u2192   * @param end_stream supplies whether this is the last byte to write on the connection.\n    95\u2192   */\n    96\u2192  virtual void rawWrite(Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream) PURE;\n    97\u2192\n    98\u2192  /**\n    99\u2192   * Close the connection based on the ConnectionCLoseAction.\n   100\u2192   * @param action for how the connection will be closed.\n   101\u2192   */\n   102\u2192  virtual void closeConnection(ConnectionCloseAction action) PURE;\n   103\u2192};\n   104\u2192\n   105\u2192/**\n   106\u2192 * This is a filter manager for TCP (L4) filters. It is split out for ease of testing.\n   107\u2192 */\n   108\u2192class FilterManagerImpl : protected Logger::Loggable<Logger::Id::connection> {\n   109\u2192public:\n   110\u2192  FilterManagerImpl(FilterManagerConnection& connection, const ConnectionSocket& socket)\n   111\u2192      : connection_(connection), socket_(socket) {}\n   112\u2192\n   113\u2192  void addWriteFilter(WriteFilterSharedPtr filter);\n   114\u2192  void addFilter(FilterSharedPtr filter);\n   115\u2192  void addReadFilter(ReadFilterSharedPtr filter);\n   116\u2192  void removeReadFilter(ReadFilterSharedPtr filter);\n   117\u2192  bool initializeReadFilters();\n   118\u2192  void onRead();\n   119\u2192  FilterStatus onWrite();\n   120\u2192  bool startUpstreamSecureTransport();\n   121\u2192  void maybeClose();\n   122\u2192  void onConnectionClose(ConnectionCloseAction close_action);\n   123\u2192  bool pendingClose() { return state_.local_close_pending_ || state_.remote_close_pending_; }\n   124\u2192\n   125\u2192protected:\n   126\u2192  struct State {\n   127\u2192    // Number of pending filters awaiting closure.\n   128\u2192    uint32_t filter_pending_close_count_{0};\n   129\u2192\n   130\u2192    // True if a RemoteClose is currently pending.\n   131\u2192    bool remote_close_pending_{false};\n   132\u2192\n   133\u2192    // True if a LocalClose is currently pending.\n   134\u2192    bool local_close_pending_{false};\n   135\u2192  };\n   136\u2192\n   137\u2192private:\n   138\u2192  struct ActiveReadFilter : public ReadFilterCallbacks, LinkedObject<ActiveReadFilter> {\n   139\u2192    ActiveReadFilter(FilterManagerImpl& parent, ReadFilterSharedPtr filter)\n   140\u2192        : parent_(parent), filter_(filter) {}\n   141\u2192\n   142\u2192    Connection& connection() override { return parent_.connection_; }\n   143\u2192    const ConnectionSocket& socket() override { return parent_.socket_; }\n   144\u2192    void continueReading() override { parent_.onContinueReading(this, parent_.connection_); }\n   145\u2192    void injectReadDataToFilterChain(Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream) override {\n   146\u2192      FixedReadBufferSource buffer_source{data, end_stream};\n   147\u2192      parent_.onContinueReading(this, buffer_source);\n   148\u2192    }\n   149\u2192\n   150\u2192    void disableClose(bool disable) override;\n   151\u2192\n   152\u2192    Upstream::HostDescriptionConstSharedPtr upstreamHost() override {\n   153\u2192      return parent_.host_description_;\n   154\u2192    }\n   155\u2192    void upstreamHost(Upstream::HostDescriptionConstSharedPtr host) override {\n   156\u2192      parent_.host_description_ = host;\n   157\u2192    }\n   158\u2192    bool startUpstreamSecureTransport() override { return parent_.startUpstreamSecureTransport(); }\n   159\u2192\n   160\u2192    FilterManagerImpl& parent_;\n   161\u2192    ReadFilterSharedPtr filter_;\n   162\u2192    bool initialized_{};\n   163\u2192    bool pending_close_{false};\n   164\u2192  };\n   165\u2192\n   166\u2192  using ActiveReadFilterPtr = std::unique_ptr<ActiveReadFilter>;\n   167\u2192\n   168\u2192  struct ActiveWriteFilter : public WriteFilterCallbacks, LinkedObject<ActiveWriteFilter> {\n   169\u2192    ActiveWriteFilter(FilterManagerImpl& parent, WriteFilterSharedPtr filter)\n   170\u2192        : parent_(parent), filter_(std::move(filter)) {}\n   171\u2192\n   172\u2192    Connection& connection() override { return parent_.connection_; }\n   173\u2192    const ConnectionSocket& socket() override { return parent_.socket_; }\n   174\u2192    void injectWriteDataToFilterChain(Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream) override {\n   175\u2192      FixedWriteBufferSource buffer_source{data, end_stream};\n   176\u2192      parent_.onResumeWriting(this, buffer_source);\n   177\u2192    }\n   178\u2192\n   179\u2192    void disableClose(bool disable) override;\n   180\u2192\n   181\u2192    FilterManagerImpl& parent_;\n   182\u2192    WriteFilterSharedPtr filter_;\n   183\u2192    bool pending_close_{false};\n   184\u2192  };\n   185\u2192\n   186\u2192  using ActiveWriteFilterPtr = std::unique_ptr<ActiveWriteFilter>;\n   187\u2192\n   188\u2192  void onContinueReading(ActiveReadFilter* filter, ReadBufferSource& buffer_source);\n   189\u2192\n   190\u2192  FilterStatus onWrite(ActiveWriteFilter* filter, WriteBufferSource& buffer_source);\n   191\u2192  void onResumeWriting(ActiveWriteFilter* filter, WriteBufferSource& buffer_source);\n   192\u2192\n   193\u2192  FilterManagerConnection& connection_;\n   194\u2192  const ConnectionSocket& socket_;\n   195\u2192  Upstream::HostDescriptionConstSharedPtr host_description_;\n   196\u2192  std::list<ActiveReadFilterPtr> upstream_filters_;\n   197\u2192  std::list<ActiveWriteFilterPtr> downstream_filters_;\n   198\u2192  State state_;\n   199\u2192  absl::optional<ConnectionCloseAction> latched_close_action_;\n   200\u2192};\n   201\u2192\n   202\u2192} // namespace Network\n   203\u2192} // namespace Envoy\n   204\u2192"
