"     1\u2192#pragma once\n     2\u2192\n     3\u2192#include <memory>\n     4\u2192\n     5\u2192#include \"envoy/buffer/buffer.h\"\n     6\u2192#include \"envoy/config/extension_config_provider.h\"\n     7\u2192#include \"envoy/network/listen_socket.h\"\n     8\u2192#include \"envoy/network/listener_filter_buffer.h\"\n     9\u2192#include \"envoy/network/transport_socket.h\"\n    10\u2192#include \"envoy/stream_info/stream_info.h\"\n    11\u2192#include \"envoy/upstream/host_description.h\"\n    12\u2192\n    13\u2192#include \"source/common/protobuf/protobuf.h\"\n    14\u2192\n    15\u2192namespace quiche {\n    16\u2192class QuicheSocketAddress;\n    17\u2192} // namespace quiche\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192namespace quic {\n    20\u2192class QuicReceivedPacket;\n    21\u2192} // namespace quic\n    22\u2192\n    23\u2192namespace Envoy {\n    24\u2192\n    25\u2192namespace Event {\n    26\u2192class Dispatcher;\n    27\u2192}\n    28\u2192\n    29\u2192namespace Network {\n    30\u2192\n    31\u2192class Connection;\n    32\u2192class ConnectionSocket;\n    33\u2192class Socket;\n    34\u2192class UdpListener;\n    35\u2192struct UdpRecvData;\n    36\u2192\n    37\u2192/**\n    38\u2192 * Status codes returned by filters that can cause future filters to not get iterated to.\n    39\u2192 */\n    40\u2192enum class FilterStatus {\n    41\u2192  // Continue to further filters.\n    42\u2192  Continue,\n    43\u2192  // Stop executing further filters.\n    44\u2192  StopIteration\n    45\u2192};\n    46\u2192\n    47\u2192/**\n    48\u2192 * Callbacks used by individual filter instances to communicate with the filter manager.\n    49\u2192 */\n    50\u2192class NetworkFilterCallbacks {\n    51\u2192public:\n    52\u2192  virtual ~NetworkFilterCallbacks() = default;\n    53\u2192\n    54\u2192  /**\n    55\u2192   * @return the connection that owns this filter.\n    56\u2192   */\n    57\u2192  virtual Connection& connection() PURE;\n    58\u2192\n    59\u2192  /**\n    60\u2192   * @return Socket the socket the filter is operating on.\n    61\u2192   */\n    62\u2192  virtual const ConnectionSocket& socket() PURE;\n    63\u2192};\n    64\u2192\n    65\u2192/**\n    66\u2192 * Callbacks used by individual write filter instances to communicate with the filter manager.\n    67\u2192 */\n    68\u2192class WriteFilterCallbacks : public virtual NetworkFilterCallbacks {\n    69\u2192public:\n    70\u2192  ~WriteFilterCallbacks() override = default;\n    71\u2192\n    72\u2192  /**\n    73\u2192   * Pass data directly to subsequent filters in the filter chain. This method is used in\n    74\u2192   * advanced cases in which a filter needs full control over how subsequent filters view data.\n    75\u2192   * Using this method allows a filter to buffer data (or not) and then periodically inject data\n    76\u2192   * to subsequent filters, indicating end_stream at an appropriate time.\n    77\u2192   * This can be used to implement rate limiting, periodic data emission, etc.\n    78\u2192   *\n    79\u2192   * When using this callback, filters should generally move passed in buffer and return\n    80\u2192   * FilterStatus::StopIteration from their onWrite() call, since use of this method\n    81\u2192   * indicates that a filter does not wish to participate in a standard write flow\n    82\u2192   * and will perform any necessary buffering and continuation on its own.\n    83\u2192   *\n    84\u2192   * @param data supplies the write data to be propagated directly to further filters in the filter\n    85\u2192   *             chain.\n    86\u2192   * @param end_stream supplies the end_stream status to be propagated directly to further filters\n    87\u2192   *                   in the filter chain.\n    88\u2192   */\n    89\u2192  virtual void injectWriteDataToFilterChain(Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream) PURE;\n    90\u2192\n    91\u2192  /**\n    92\u2192   * Control the filter close status for write filters.\n    93\u2192   *\n    94\u2192   * When `disabled` is `true`, the connection closure process is paused or delayed by marking the\n    95\u2192   * closure as pending. When `disabled` is `false`, the connection closure process is resumed if it\n    96\u2192   * was previously delayed.\n    97\u2192   *\n    98\u2192   * This method affects the filter's \"close status\" within the context of the connection closure\n    99\u2192   * process managed by the filter manager:\n   100\u2192   *    - `disableClose(true)` marks the filter as unable to close by delaying the closure process.\n   101\u2192   *    - Calling `disableClose(true)` again has no additional effect, as the closure is already\n   102\u2192   *      marked as pending.\n   103\u2192   *    - `disableClose(false)` mark the filter is ready to be closed. If no further pending\n   104\u2192   * closures exist and there is a latched close action, it will close the connection with the\n   105\u2192   * latched close action.\n   106\u2192   *\n   107\u2192   * Note that this method only takes effect when the connection closure is being managed through\n   108\u2192   * the filter manager.\n   109\u2192   *\n   110\u2192   * @param disabled A boolean flag:\n   111\u2192   *   - `true`: Delays the connection closure process if there is any,\n   112\u2192   *             marking the filter as unable to close.\n   113\u2192   *   - `false`: Resumes the connection closure process if there is any,\n   114\u2192   *              marking the filter as close ready.\n   115\u2192   */\n   116\u2192  virtual void disableClose(bool disabled) PURE;\n   117\u2192};\n   118\u2192\n   119\u2192/**\n   120\u2192 * A write path binary connection filter.\n   121\u2192 */\n   122\u2192class WriteFilter {\n   123\u2192public:\n   124\u2192  virtual ~WriteFilter() = default;\n   125\u2192\n   126\u2192  /**\n   127\u2192   * Called when data is to be written on the connection.\n   128\u2192   * @param data supplies the buffer to be written which may be modified.\n   129\u2192   * @param end_stream supplies whether this is the last byte to write on the connection.\n   130\u2192   * @return status used by the filter manager to manage further filter iteration.\n   131\u2192   */\n   132\u2192  virtual FilterStatus onWrite(Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream) PURE;\n   133\u2192\n   134\u2192  /**\n   135\u2192   * Initializes the write filter callbacks used to interact with the filter manager. It will be\n   136\u2192   * called by the filter manager a single time when the filter is first registered. Thus, any\n   137\u2192   * construction that requires the backing connection should take place in the context of this\n   138\u2192   * function.\n   139\u2192   *\n   140\u2192   * IMPORTANT: No outbound networking or complex processing should be done in this function.\n   141\u2192   *            That should be done in the context of ReadFilter::onNewConnection() if needed.\n   142\u2192   *\n   143\u2192   * @param callbacks supplies the callbacks.\n   144\u2192   */\n   145\u2192  virtual void initializeWriteFilterCallbacks(WriteFilterCallbacks&) {}\n   146\u2192};\n   147\u2192\n   148\u2192using WriteFilterSharedPtr = std::shared_ptr<WriteFilter>;\n   149\u2192\n   150\u2192/**\n   151\u2192 * Callbacks used by individual read filter instances to communicate with the filter manager.\n   152\u2192 */\n   153\u2192class ReadFilterCallbacks : public virtual NetworkFilterCallbacks {\n   154\u2192public:\n   155\u2192  ~ReadFilterCallbacks() override = default;\n   156\u2192\n   157\u2192  /**\n   158\u2192   * If a read filter stopped filter iteration, continueReading() can be called to continue the\n   159\u2192   * filter chain. The next filter will be called with all currently available data in the read\n   160\u2192   * buffer (it will also have onNewConnection() called on it if it was not previously called).\n   161\u2192   */\n   162\u2192  virtual void continueReading() PURE;\n   163\u2192\n   164\u2192  /**\n   165\u2192   * Pass data directly to subsequent filters in the filter chain. This method is used in\n   166\u2192   * advanced cases in which a filter needs full control over how subsequent filters view data,\n   167\u2192   * and does not want to make use of connection-level buffering. Using this method allows\n   168\u2192   * a filter to buffer data (or not) and then periodically inject data to subsequent filters,\n   169\u2192   * indicating end_stream at an appropriate time. This can be used to implement rate limiting,\n   170\u2192   * periodic data emission, etc.\n   171\u2192   *\n   172\u2192   * When using this callback, filters should generally move passed in buffer and return\n   173\u2192   * FilterStatus::StopIteration from their onData() call, since use of this method\n   174\u2192   * indicates that a filter does not wish to participate in standard connection-level\n   175\u2192   * buffering and continuation and will perform any necessary buffering and continuation on its\n   176\u2192   * own.\n   177\u2192   *\n   178\u2192   * This callback is different from continueReading() in that the specified data and end_stream\n   179\u2192   * status will be propagated verbatim to further filters in the filter chain\n   180\u2192   * (while continueReading() propagates connection-level read buffer and end_stream status).\n   181\u2192   *\n   182\u2192   * @param data supplies the read data to be propagated directly to further filters in the filter\n   183\u2192   *             chain.\n   184\u2192   * @param end_stream supplies the end_stream status to be propagated directly to further filters\n   185\u2192   *                   in the filter chain.\n   186\u2192   */\n   187\u2192  virtual void injectReadDataToFilterChain(Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream) PURE;\n   188\u2192\n   189\u2192  /**\n   190\u2192   * Return the currently selected upstream host, if any. This can be used for communication\n   191\u2192   * between multiple network level filters, for example the TCP proxy filter communicating its\n   192\u2192   * selection to another filter for logging.\n   193\u2192   */\n   194\u2192  virtual Upstream::HostDescriptionConstSharedPtr upstreamHost() PURE;\n   195\u2192\n   196\u2192  /**\n   197\u2192   * Set the currently selected upstream host for the connection.\n   198\u2192   */\n   199\u2192  virtual void upstreamHost(Upstream::HostDescriptionConstSharedPtr host) PURE;\n   200\u2192\n   201\u2192  /**\n   202\u2192   * Signal to the filter manager to enable secure transport mode in upstream connection.\n   203\u2192   * This is done when upstream connection's transport socket is of startTLS type. At the moment\n   204\u2192   * it is the only transport socket type which can be programmatically converted from non-secure\n   205\u2192   * mode to secure mode.\n   206\u2192   */\n   207\u2192  virtual bool startUpstreamSecureTransport() PURE;\n   208\u2192\n   209\u2192  /**\n   210\u2192   * Control the filter close status for read filters.\n   211\u2192   *\n   212\u2192   * When `disabled` is `true`, the connection closure process is paused or delayed by marking the\n   213\u2192   * closure as pending. When `disabled` is `false`, the connection closure process is resumed if it\n   214\u2192   * was previously delayed.\n   215\u2192   *\n   216\u2192   * This method affects the filter's \"close status\" within the context of the connection closure\n   217\u2192   * process managed by the filter manager:\n   218\u2192   *    - `disableClose(true)` marks the filter as unable to close by delaying the closure process.\n   219\u2192   *    - Calling `disableClose(true)` again has no additional effect, as the closure is already\n   220\u2192   *      marked as pending.\n   221\u2192   *    - `disableClose(false)` mark the filter is ready to be closed. If no further pending\n   222\u2192   * closures exist and there is a latched close action, it will close the connection with the\n   223\u2192   * latched close action.\n   224\u2192   *\n   225\u2192   * Note that this method only takes effect when the connection closure is being managed through\n   226\u2192   * the filter manager.\n   227\u2192   *\n   228\u2192   * @param disabled A boolean flag:\n   229\u2192   *   - `true`: Delays the connection closure process if there is any,\n   230\u2192   *             marking the filter as unable to close.\n   231\u2192   *   - `false`: Resumes the connection closure process if there is any,\n   232\u2192   *              marking the filter as close ready.\n   233\u2192   */\n   234\u2192  virtual void disableClose(bool disable) PURE;\n   235\u2192};\n   236\u2192\n   237\u2192/**\n   238\u2192 * A read path binary connection filter.\n   239\u2192 */\n   240\u2192class ReadFilter {\n   241\u2192public:\n   242\u2192  virtual ~ReadFilter() = default;\n   243\u2192\n   244\u2192  /**\n   245\u2192   * Called when data is read on the connection.\n   246\u2192   * @param data supplies the read data which may be modified.\n   247\u2192   * @param end_stream supplies whether this is the last byte on the connection. This will only\n   248\u2192   *        be set if the connection has half-close semantics enabled.\n   249\u2192   * @return status used by the filter manager to manage further filter iteration.\n   250\u2192   */\n   251\u2192  virtual FilterStatus onData(Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream) PURE;\n   252\u2192\n   253\u2192  /**\n   254\u2192   * Called when a connection is first established. Filters should do one time long term processing\n   255\u2192   * that needs to be done when a connection is established. Filter chain iteration can be stopped\n   256\u2192   * if needed.\n   257\u2192   * @return status used by the filter manager to manage further filter iteration.\n   258\u2192   */\n   259\u2192  virtual FilterStatus onNewConnection() PURE;\n   260\u2192\n   261\u2192  /**\n   262\u2192   * Initializes the read filter callbacks used to interact with the filter manager. It will be\n   263\u2192   * called by the filter manager a single time when the filter is first registered. Thus, any\n   264\u2192   * construction that requires the backing connection should take place in the context of this\n   265\u2192   * function.\n   266\u2192   *\n   267\u2192   * IMPORTANT: No outbound networking or complex processing should be done in this function.\n   268\u2192   *            That should be done in the context of onNewConnection() if needed.\n   269\u2192   *\n   270\u2192   * @param callbacks supplies the callbacks.\n   271\u2192   */\n   272\u2192  virtual void initializeReadFilterCallbacks(ReadFilterCallbacks& callbacks) PURE;\n   273\u2192\n   274\u2192  /**\n   275\u2192   * Method is called by the filter manager to convert upstream's connection transport socket\n   276\u2192   * from non-secure mode to secure mode. Only terminal filters are aware of upstream connection and\n   277\u2192   * non-terminal filters should not implement startUpstreamSecureTransport.\n   278\u2192   */\n   279\u2192  virtual bool startUpstreamSecureTransport() { return false; }\n   280\u2192};\n   281\u2192\n   282\u2192using ReadFilterSharedPtr = std::shared_ptr<ReadFilter>;\n   283\u2192\n   284\u2192/**\n   285\u2192 * A combination read and write filter. This allows a single filter instance to cover\n   286\u2192 * both the read and write paths.\n   287\u2192 */\n   288\u2192class Filter : public WriteFilter, public ReadFilter {};\n   289\u2192using FilterSharedPtr = std::shared_ptr<Filter>;\n   290\u2192\n   291\u2192/**\n   292\u2192 * Interface for adding individual network filters to a manager.\n   293\u2192 */\n   294\u2192class FilterManager {\n   295\u2192public:\n   296\u2192  virtual ~FilterManager() = default;\n   297\u2192\n   298\u2192  /**\n   299\u2192   * Add a write filter to the connection. Filters are invoked in LIFO order (the last added\n   300\u2192   * filter is called first).\n   301\u2192   */\n   302\u2192  virtual void addWriteFilter(WriteFilterSharedPtr filter) PURE;\n   303\u2192\n   304\u2192  /**\n   305\u2192   * Add a combination filter to the connection. Equivalent to calling both addWriteFilter()\n   306\u2192   * and addReadFilter() with the same filter instance.\n   307\u2192   */\n   308\u2192  virtual void addFilter(FilterSharedPtr filter) PURE;\n   309\u2192\n   310\u2192  /**\n   311\u2192   * Add a read filter to the connection. Filters are invoked in FIFO order (the filter added\n   312\u2192   * first is called first).\n   313\u2192   */\n   314\u2192  virtual void addReadFilter(ReadFilterSharedPtr filter) PURE;\n   315\u2192\n   316\u2192  /**\n   317\u2192   * Remove a read filter from the connection.\n   318\u2192   */\n   319\u2192  virtual void removeReadFilter(ReadFilterSharedPtr filter) PURE;\n   320\u2192\n   321\u2192  /**\n   322\u2192   * Initialize all of the installed read filters. This effectively calls onNewConnection() on\n   323\u2192   * each of them.\n   324\u2192   * @return true if read filters were initialized successfully, otherwise false.\n   325\u2192   */\n   326\u2192  virtual bool initializeReadFilters() PURE;\n   327\u2192};\n   328\u2192\n   329\u2192/**\n   330\u2192 * This function is used to wrap the creation of a network filter chain for new connections as\n   331\u2192 * they come in. Filter factories create the lambda at configuration initialization time, and then\n   332\u2192 * they are used at runtime.\n   333\u2192 * @param filter_manager supplies the filter manager for the connection to install filters\n   334\u2192 * to. Typically the function will install a single filter, but it's technically possibly to\n   335\u2192 * install more than one if desired.\n   336\u2192 */\n   337\u2192using FilterFactoryCb = std::function<void(FilterManager& filter_manager)>;\n   338\u2192\n   339\u2192/**\n   340\u2192 * Callbacks used by individual listener filter instances to communicate with the listener filter\n   341\u2192 * manager.\n   342\u2192 */\n   343\u2192class ListenerFilterCallbacks {\n   344\u2192public:\n   345\u2192  virtual ~ListenerFilterCallbacks() = default;\n   346\u2192\n   347\u2192  /**\n   348\u2192   * @return ConnectionSocket the socket the filter is operating on.\n   349\u2192   */\n   350\u2192  virtual ConnectionSocket& socket() PURE;\n   351\u2192\n   352\u2192  /**\n   353\u2192   * @param name the namespace used in the metadata in reverse DNS format, for example:\n   354\u2192   * envoy.test.my_filter.\n   355\u2192   * @param value the struct to set on the namespace. A merge will be performed with new values for\n   356\u2192   * the same key overriding existing.\n   357\u2192   */\n   358\u2192  virtual void setDynamicMetadata(const std::string& name, const ProtobufWkt::Struct& value) PURE;\n   359\u2192\n   360\u2192  /**\n   361\u2192   * @param name the namespace used in the metadata in reverse DNS format, for example:\n   362\u2192   * envoy.test.my_filter.\n   363\u2192   * @param value of type protobuf any to set on the namespace. A merge will be performed with new\n   364\u2192   * values for the same key overriding existing.\n   365\u2192   */\n   366\u2192  virtual void setDynamicTypedMetadata(const std::string& name, const ProtobufWkt::Any& value) PURE;\n   367\u2192\n   368\u2192  /**\n   369\u2192   * @return const envoy::config::core::v3::Metadata& the dynamic metadata associated with this\n   370\u2192   * connection.\n   371\u2192   */\n   372\u2192  virtual envoy::config::core::v3::Metadata& dynamicMetadata() PURE;\n   373\u2192  virtual const envoy::config::core::v3::Metadata& dynamicMetadata() const PURE;\n   374\u2192\n   375\u2192  /**\n   376\u2192   * @return Object on which filters can share data on a per-request basis.\n   377\u2192   */\n   378\u2192  virtual StreamInfo::FilterState& filterState() PURE;\n   379\u2192\n   380\u2192  /**\n   381\u2192   * @return the Dispatcher for issuing events.\n   382\u2192   */\n   383\u2192  virtual Event::Dispatcher& dispatcher() PURE;\n   384\u2192\n   385\u2192  /**\n   386\u2192   * If a filter returned `FilterStatus::ContinueIteration`, `continueFilterChain(true)`\n   387\u2192   * should be called to continue the filter chain iteration. Or `continueFilterChain(false)`\n   388\u2192   * should be called if the filter returned `FilterStatus::StopIteration` and closed\n   389\u2192   * the socket.\n   390\u2192   * @param success boolean telling whether the filter execution was successful or not.\n   391\u2192   */\n   392\u2192  virtual void continueFilterChain(bool success) PURE;\n   393\u2192\n   394\u2192  /**\n   395\u2192   * Overwrite the default use original dst setting for current connection. This allows the\n   396\u2192   * listener filter to control whether the connection should be forwarded to other listeners\n   397\u2192   * based on the original destination address or not.\n   398\u2192   * @param use_original_dst whether to use original destination address or not.\n   399\u2192   */\n   400\u2192  virtual void useOriginalDst(bool use_original_dst) PURE;\n   401\u2192};\n   402\u2192\n   403\u2192/**\n   404\u2192 *  Interface for a listener filter matching with incoming traffic.\n   405\u2192 */\n   406\u2192class ListenerFilterMatcher {\n   407\u2192public:\n   408\u2192  virtual ~ListenerFilterMatcher() = default;\n   409\u2192  virtual bool matches(Network::ListenerFilterCallbacks& cb) const PURE;\n   410\u2192};\n   411\u2192using ListenerFilterMatcherPtr = std::unique_ptr<ListenerFilterMatcher>;\n   412\u2192using ListenerFilterMatcherSharedPtr = std::shared_ptr<ListenerFilterMatcher>;\n   413\u2192\n   414\u2192/**\n   415\u2192 * TCP Listener Filter\n   416\u2192 */\n   417\u2192class ListenerFilter {\n   418\u2192public:\n   419\u2192  virtual ~ListenerFilter() = default;\n   420\u2192\n   421\u2192  /**\n   422\u2192   * Called when a new connection is accepted, but before a Connection is created.\n   423\u2192   * Filter chain iteration can be stopped if need more data from the connection\n   424\u2192   * by returning `FilterStatus::StopIteration`, or continue the filter chain iteration\n   425\u2192   * by returning `FilterStatus::ContinueIteration`. Reject the connection by closing\n   426\u2192   * the socket and returning `FilterStatus::StopIteration`.\n   427\u2192   * @param cb the callbacks the filter instance can use to communicate with the filter chain.\n   428\u2192   * @return status used by the filter manager to manage further filter iteration.\n   429\u2192   */\n   430\u2192  virtual FilterStatus onAccept(ListenerFilterCallbacks& cb) PURE;\n   431\u2192\n   432\u2192  /**\n   433\u2192   * Called when data is read from the connection. If the filter doesn't get\n   434\u2192   * enough data, filter chain iteration can be stopped if needed by returning\n   435\u2192   * `FilterStatus::StopIteration`. Or continue the filter chain iteration by returning\n   436\u2192   * `FilterStatus::ContinueIteration` if the filter get enough data. Reject the connection\n   437\u2192   * by closing the socket and returning `FilterStatus::StopIteration`.\n   438\u2192   * @param buffer the buffer of data.\n   439\u2192   * @return status used by the filter manager to manage further filter iteration.\n   440\u2192   */\n   441\u2192  virtual FilterStatus onData(Network::ListenerFilterBuffer& buffer) PURE;\n   442\u2192\n   443\u2192  /**\n   444\u2192   * Return the size of data the filter want to inspect from the connection.\n   445\u2192   * The size can be increased after filter need to inspect more data.\n   446\u2192   * @return maximum number of bytes of the data consumed by the filter. 0 means filter does not\n   447\u2192   * need any data.\n   448\u2192   */\n   449\u2192  virtual size_t maxReadBytes() const PURE;\n   450\u2192};\n   451\u2192\n   452\u2192using ListenerFilterPtr = std::unique_ptr<ListenerFilter>;\n   453\u2192\n   454\u2192/**\n   455\u2192 * Interface for filter callbacks and adding listener filters to a manager.\n   456\u2192 */\n   457\u2192class ListenerFilterManager {\n   458\u2192public:\n   459\u2192  virtual ~ListenerFilterManager() = default;\n   460\u2192\n   461\u2192  /**\n   462\u2192   * Add a filter to the listener. Filters are invoked in FIFO order (the filter added\n   463\u2192   * first is called first).\n   464\u2192   * @param listener_filter_matcher supplies the matcher to decide when filter is enabled.\n   465\u2192   * @param filter supplies the filter being added.\n   466\u2192   */\n   467\u2192  virtual void addAcceptFilter(const ListenerFilterMatcherSharedPtr& listener_filter_matcher,\n   468\u2192                               ListenerFilterPtr&& filter) PURE;\n   469\u2192};\n   470\u2192\n   471\u2192/**\n   472\u2192 * This function is used to wrap the creation of a listener filter chain for new sockets as they are\n   473\u2192 * created. Filter factories create the lambda at configuration initialization time, and then they\n   474\u2192 * are used at runtime.\n   475\u2192 * @param filter_manager supplies the filter manager for the listener to install filters to.\n   476\u2192 * Typically the function will install a single filter, but it's technically possibly to install\n   477\u2192 * more than one if desired.\n   478\u2192 */\n   479\u2192using ListenerFilterFactoryCb = std::function<void(ListenerFilterManager& filter_manager)>;\n   480\u2192\n   481\u2192/**\n   482\u2192 * QUIC Listener Filter\n   483\u2192 */\n   484\u2192class QuicListenerFilter {\n   485\u2192public:\n   486\u2192  virtual ~QuicListenerFilter() = default;\n   487\u2192\n   488\u2192  /**\n   489\u2192   * Called when a new connection is accepted, but before a Connection is created.\n   490\u2192   * Filter chain iteration can be terminated if the cb shouldn't be accessed any more\n   491\u2192   * by returning `FilterStatus::StopIteration`, or continue the filter chain iteration\n   492\u2192   * by returning `FilterStatus::ContinueIteration`. Reject the connection by closing\n   493\u2192   * the socket and returning `FilterStatus::StopIteration`. If `FilterStatus::StopIteration` is\n   494\u2192   * returned, following filters' onAccept() will be skipped, but the connection creation is not\n   495\u2192   * going to be paused. If the connection socket is closed upon connection creation, the connection\n   496\u2192   * will be closed immediately.\n   497\u2192   * @param cb the callbacks the filter instance can use to communicate with the filter chain.\n   498\u2192   * @param server_preferred_addresses the server's preferred addresses to be advertised.\n   499\u2192   * @return status used by the filter manager to manage further filter iteration.\n   500\u2192   */\n   501\u2192  virtual FilterStatus onAccept(ListenerFilterCallbacks& cb) PURE;\n   502\u2192\n   503\u2192  /**\n   504\u2192   * Called before connection creation.\n   505\u2192   * @return false if the given preferred address is incomplatible with this filter and the listener\n   506\u2192   * shouldn't advertise the given preferred address. I.e. onAccept() would have behaved differently\n   507\u2192   * if the connection socket's destination address were the preferred address.\n   508\u2192   */\n   509\u2192  virtual bool isCompatibleWithServerPreferredAddress(\n   510\u2192      const quiche::QuicheSocketAddress& server_preferred_address) const PURE;\n   511\u2192\n   512\u2192  /**\n   513\u2192   * Called after the peer has migrated to a different address. Check if the connection\n   514\u2192   * migration is compatible with this listener filter. If not, close the connection and return\n   515\u2192   * `FilterStatus::StopIteration`. An alternative approach is to disable active migration on the\n   516\u2192   * given connection during connection creation. But peer address change is inevitable given NAT\n   517\u2192   * rebinding is more frequent than TCP, and such passive address change is indistinguishable from\n   518\u2192   * active migration. So there is no way to completely disable connection migration. disable client\n   519\u2192   * connection migration.\n   520\u2192   * @param new_address the address the peer has migrated to.\n   521\u2192   * @param connection the connection just migrated.\n   522\u2192   * @return status used by the filter manager to manage further filter iteration.\n   523\u2192   */\n   524\u2192  virtual FilterStatus onPeerAddressChanged(const quiche::QuicheSocketAddress& new_address,\n   525\u2192                                            Connection& connection) PURE;\n   526\u2192\n   527\u2192  /**\n   528\u2192   * Called when the QUIC server session processes its first packet.\n   529\u2192   * @param packet the received packet.\n   530\u2192   * @return status used by the filter manager to manage further filter iteration.\n   531\u2192   */\n   532\u2192  virtual FilterStatus onFirstPacketReceived(const quic::QuicReceivedPacket&) PURE;\n   533\u2192};\n   534\u2192\n   535\u2192using QuicListenerFilterPtr = std::unique_ptr<QuicListenerFilter>;\n   536\u2192\n   537\u2192/**\n   538\u2192 * Interface for filter callbacks and adding listener filters to a manager.\n   539\u2192 */\n   540\u2192class QuicListenerFilterManager {\n   541\u2192public:\n   542\u2192  virtual ~QuicListenerFilterManager() = default;\n   543\u2192\n   544\u2192  /**\n   545\u2192   * Add a filter to the listener. Filters are invoked in FIFO order (the filter added\n   546\u2192   * first is called first).\n   547\u2192   * @param listener_filter_matcher supplies the matcher to decide when filter is enabled.\n   548\u2192   * @param filter supplies the filter being added.\n   549\u2192   */\n   550\u2192  virtual void addFilter(const ListenerFilterMatcherSharedPtr& listener_filter_matcher,\n   551\u2192                         QuicListenerFilterPtr&& filter) PURE;\n   552\u2192\n   553\u2192  virtual bool shouldAdvertiseServerPreferredAddress(\n   554\u2192      const quiche::QuicheSocketAddress& server_preferred_address) const PURE;\n   555\u2192\n   556\u2192  virtual void onPeerAddressChanged(const quiche::QuicheSocketAddress& new_address,\n   557\u2192                                    Connection& connection) PURE;\n   558\u2192  virtual void onFirstPacketReceived(const quic::QuicReceivedPacket&) PURE;\n   559\u2192};\n   560\u2192\n   561\u2192/**\n   562\u2192 * This function is used to wrap the creation of a QUIC listener filter chain for new connections.\n   563\u2192 * Filter factories create the lambda at configuration initialization time, and then they are used\n   564\u2192 * at runtime.\n   565\u2192 * @param filter_manager supplies the filter manager for the listener to install filters to.\n   566\u2192 * Typically the function will install a single filter, but it's technically possibly to install\n   567\u2192 * more than one if desired.\n   568\u2192 */\n   569\u2192using QuicListenerFilterFactoryCb = std::function<void(QuicListenerFilterManager& filter_manager)>;\n   570\u2192\n   571\u2192template <class FactoryCb>\n   572\u2192using FilterConfigProvider = Envoy::Config::ExtensionConfigProvider<FactoryCb>;\n   573\u2192\n   574\u2192template <class FactoryCb>\n   575\u2192using FilterConfigProviderPtr = std::unique_ptr<FilterConfigProvider<FactoryCb>>;\n   576\u2192\n   577\u2192using NetworkFilterFactoriesList = std::vector<FilterConfigProviderPtr<FilterFactoryCb>>;\n   578\u2192\n   579\u2192/**\n   580\u2192 * Interface representing a single filter chain.\n   581\u2192 */\n   582\u2192class FilterChain {\n   583\u2192public:\n   584\u2192  virtual ~FilterChain() = default;\n   585\u2192\n   586\u2192  /**\n   587\u2192   * @return const TransportSocketFactory& a transport socket factory to be used by the new\n   588\u2192   * connection.\n   589\u2192   */\n   590\u2192  virtual const DownstreamTransportSocketFactory& transportSocketFactory() const PURE;\n   591\u2192\n   592\u2192  /**\n   593\u2192   * @return std::chrono::milliseconds the amount of time to wait for the transport socket to report\n   594\u2192   * that a connection has been established. If the timeout is reached, the connection is closed. 0\n   595\u2192   * specifies a disabled timeout.\n   596\u2192   */\n   597\u2192  virtual std::chrono::milliseconds transportSocketConnectTimeout() const PURE;\n   598\u2192\n   599\u2192  /**\n   600\u2192   * @return const Filter::NetworkFilterFactoriesList& a list of filter factory providers to be\n   601\u2192   * used by the new connection.\n   602\u2192   */\n   603\u2192  virtual const NetworkFilterFactoriesList& networkFilterFactories() const PURE;\n   604\u2192\n   605\u2192  /**\n   606\u2192   * @return the name of this filter chain.\n   607\u2192   */\n   608\u2192  virtual absl::string_view name() const PURE;\n   609\u2192};\n   610\u2192\n   611\u2192using FilterChainSharedPtr = std::shared_ptr<FilterChain>;\n   612\u2192\n   613\u2192/**\n   614\u2192 * A filter chain that can be drained.\n   615\u2192 */\n   616\u2192class DrainableFilterChain : public FilterChain {\n   617\u2192public:\n   618\u2192  virtual void startDraining() PURE;\n   619\u2192};\n   620\u2192\n   621\u2192using DrainableFilterChainSharedPtr = std::shared_ptr<DrainableFilterChain>;\n   622\u2192\n   623\u2192/**\n   624\u2192 * Interface for searching through configured filter chains.\n   625\u2192 */\n   626\u2192class FilterChainManager {\n   627\u2192public:\n   628\u2192  virtual ~FilterChainManager() = default;\n   629\u2192\n   630\u2192  /**\n   631\u2192   * Find filter chain that's matching metadata from the new connection.\n   632\u2192   * @param socket supplies connection metadata that's going to be used for the filter chain lookup.\n   633\u2192   * @param info supplies the dynamic metadata and the filter state populated by the listener\n   634\u2192   * filters.\n   635\u2192   * @return const FilterChain* filter chain to be used by the new connection,\n   636\u2192   *         nullptr if no matching filter chain was found.\n   637\u2192   */\n   638\u2192  virtual const FilterChain* findFilterChain(const ConnectionSocket& socket,\n   639\u2192                                             const StreamInfo::StreamInfo& info) const PURE;\n   640\u2192};\n   641\u2192\n   642\u2192/**\n   643\u2192 * Callbacks used by individual UDP listener read filter instances to communicate with the filter\n   644\u2192 * manager.\n   645\u2192 */\n   646\u2192class UdpReadFilterCallbacks {\n   647\u2192public:\n   648\u2192  virtual ~UdpReadFilterCallbacks() = default;\n   649\u2192\n   650\u2192  /**\n   651\u2192   * @return the udp listener that owns this read filter.\n   652\u2192   */\n   653\u2192  virtual UdpListener& udpListener() PURE;\n   654\u2192};\n   655\u2192\n   656\u2192/**\n   657\u2192 * UDP Listener Read Filter\n   658\u2192 */\n   659\u2192class UdpListenerReadFilter {\n   660\u2192public:\n   661\u2192  virtual ~UdpListenerReadFilter() = default;\n   662\u2192\n   663\u2192  /**\n   664\u2192   * Called when a new data packet is received on a UDP listener.\n   665\u2192   * @param data supplies the read data which may be modified.\n   666\u2192   * @return status used by the filter manager to manage further filter iteration.\n   667\u2192   */\n   668\u2192  virtual FilterStatus onData(UdpRecvData& data) PURE;\n   669\u2192\n   670\u2192  /**\n   671\u2192   * Called when there is an error event in the receive data path.\n   672\u2192   *\n   673\u2192   * @param error_code supplies the received error on the listener.\n   674\u2192   * @return status used by the filter manager to manage further filter iteration.\n   675\u2192   */\n   676\u2192  virtual FilterStatus onReceiveError(Api::IoError::IoErrorCode error_code) PURE;\n   677\u2192\n   678\u2192protected:\n   679\u2192  /**\n   680\u2192   * @param callbacks supplies the read filter callbacks used to interact with the filter manager.\n   681\u2192   */\n   682\u2192  UdpListenerReadFilter(UdpReadFilterCallbacks& callbacks) : read_callbacks_(&callbacks) {}\n   683\u2192\n   684\u2192  UdpReadFilterCallbacks* read_callbacks_{};\n   685\u2192};\n   686\u2192\n   687\u2192using UdpListenerReadFilterPtr = std::unique_ptr<UdpListenerReadFilter>;\n   688\u2192\n   689\u2192/**\n   690\u2192 * Interface for adding UDP listener filters to a manager.\n   691\u2192 */\n   692\u2192class UdpListenerFilterManager {\n   693\u2192public:\n   694\u2192  virtual ~UdpListenerFilterManager() = default;\n   695\u2192\n   696\u2192  /**\n   697\u2192   * Add a read filter to the udp listener. Filters are invoked in FIFO order (the\n   698\u2192   * filter added first is called first).\n   699\u2192   * @param filter supplies the filter being added.\n   700\u2192   */\n   701\u2192  virtual void addReadFilter(UdpListenerReadFilterPtr&& filter) PURE;\n   702\u2192};\n   703\u2192\n   704\u2192using UdpListenerFilterFactoryCb = std::function<void(\n   705\u2192    UdpListenerFilterManager& udp_listener_filter_manager, UdpReadFilterCallbacks& callbacks)>;\n   706\u2192\n   707\u2192/**\n   708\u2192 * Common interface for UdpSessionReadFilterCallbacks and UdpSessionWriteFilterCallbacks.\n   709\u2192 */\n   710\u2192class UdpSessionFilterCallbacks {\n   711\u2192public:\n   712\u2192  virtual ~UdpSessionFilterCallbacks() = default;\n   713\u2192\n   714\u2192  /**\n   715\u2192   * @return uint64_t the ID of the originating UDP session.\n   716\u2192   */\n   717\u2192  virtual uint64_t sessionId() const PURE;\n   718\u2192\n   719\u2192  /**\n   720\u2192   * @return StreamInfo for logging purposes.\n   721\u2192   */\n   722\u2192  virtual StreamInfo::StreamInfo& streamInfo() PURE;\n   723\u2192\n   724\u2192  /**\n   725\u2192   * Allows a filter to inject a datagram to successive filters in the session filter chain.\n   726\u2192   * The injected datagram will be iterated as a regular received datagram, and may also be\n   727\u2192   * stopped by further filters. This can be used, for example, to continue processing previously\n   728\u2192   * buffered datagrams by a filter after an asynchronous operation ended.\n   729\u2192   */\n   730\u2192  virtual void injectDatagramToFilterChain(Network::UdpRecvData& data) PURE;\n   731\u2192};\n   732\u2192\n   733\u2192class UdpSessionReadFilterCallbacks : public UdpSessionFilterCallbacks {\n   734\u2192public:\n   735\u2192  ~UdpSessionReadFilterCallbacks() override = default;\n   736\u2192\n   737\u2192  /**\n   738\u2192   * If a read filter stopped filter iteration, continueFilterChain() can be called to continue the\n   739\u2192   * filter chain. It will have onNewSession() called if it was not previously called.\n   740\u2192   * @return false if the session is removed and no longer valid, otherwise returns true.\n   741\u2192   */\n   742\u2192  virtual bool continueFilterChain() PURE;\n   743\u2192};\n   744\u2192\n   745\u2192class UdpSessionWriteFilterCallbacks : public UdpSessionFilterCallbacks {};\n   746\u2192\n   747\u2192class UdpSessionFilterBase {\n   748\u2192public:\n   749\u2192  virtual ~UdpSessionFilterBase() = default;\n   750\u2192\n   751\u2192  /**\n   752\u2192   * This routine is called before the access log handlers' final log() is called. Filters can use\n   753\u2192   * this callback to enrich the data passed in to the log handlers.\n   754\u2192   */\n   755\u2192  void onSessionComplete() {\n   756\u2192    if (!on_session_complete_already_called_) {\n   757\u2192      onSessionCompleteInternal();\n   758\u2192      on_session_complete_already_called_ = true;\n   759\u2192    }\n   760\u2192  }\n   761\u2192\n   762\u2192protected:\n   763\u2192  /**\n   764\u2192   * This routine is called by onSessionComplete to enrich the data passed in to the log handlers.\n   765\u2192   */\n   766\u2192  virtual void onSessionCompleteInternal() { ASSERT(!on_session_complete_already_called_); }\n   767\u2192\n   768\u2192private:\n   769\u2192  bool on_session_complete_already_called_{false};\n   770\u2192};\n   771\u2192\n   772\u2192/**\n   773\u2192 * Return codes for read filter invocations.\n   774\u2192 */\n   775\u2192enum class UdpSessionReadFilterStatus {\n   776\u2192  // Continue to further session filters.\n   777\u2192  Continue,\n   778\u2192  // Stop executing further session filters.\n   779\u2192  StopIteration,\n   780\u2192};\n   781\u2192\n   782\u2192/**\n   783\u2192 * Session read filter interface.\n   784\u2192 */\n   785\u2192class UdpSessionReadFilter : public virtual UdpSessionFilterBase {\n   786\u2192public:\n   787\u2192  ~UdpSessionReadFilter() override = default;\n   788\u2192\n   789\u2192  /**\n   790\u2192   * Called when a new UDP session is first established. Filters should do one time long term\n   791\u2192   * processing that needs to be done when a session is established. Filter chain iteration\n   792\u2192   * can be stopped if needed.\n   793\u2192   * @return status used by the filter manager to manage further filter iteration.\n   794\u2192   */\n   795\u2192  virtual UdpSessionReadFilterStatus onNewSession() PURE;\n   796\u2192\n   797\u2192  /**\n   798\u2192   * Called when UDP datagram is read and matches the session that manages the filter.\n   799\u2192   * @param data supplies the read data which may be modified.\n   800\u2192   * @return status used by the filter manager to manage further filter iteration.\n   801\u2192   */\n   802\u2192  virtual UdpSessionReadFilterStatus onData(Network::UdpRecvData& data) PURE;\n   803\u2192\n   804\u2192  /**\n   805\u2192   * Initializes the read filter callbacks used to interact with the filter manager. It will be\n   806\u2192   * called by the filter manager a single time when the filter is first registered.\n   807\u2192   *\n   808\u2192   * IMPORTANT: No outbound networking or complex processing should be done in this function.\n   809\u2192   *            That should be done in the context of onNewSession() if needed.\n   810\u2192   *\n   811\u2192   * @param callbacks supplies the callbacks.\n   812\u2192   */\n   813\u2192  virtual void initializeReadFilterCallbacks(UdpSessionReadFilterCallbacks& callbacks) PURE;\n   814\u2192};\n   815\u2192\n   816\u2192using UdpSessionReadFilterSharedPtr = std::shared_ptr<UdpSessionReadFilter>;\n   817\u2192\n   818\u2192/**\n   819\u2192 * Return codes for write filter invocations.\n   820\u2192 */\n   821\u2192enum class UdpSessionWriteFilterStatus {\n   822\u2192  // Continue to further session filters.\n   823\u2192  Continue,\n   824\u2192  // Stop executing further session filters.\n   825\u2192  StopIteration,\n   826\u2192};\n   827\u2192\n   828\u2192/**\n   829\u2192 * Session write filter interface.\n   830\u2192 */\n   831\u2192class UdpSessionWriteFilter : public virtual UdpSessionFilterBase {\n   832\u2192public:\n   833\u2192  ~UdpSessionWriteFilter() override = default;\n   834\u2192\n   835\u2192  /**\n   836\u2192   * Called when data is to be written on the UDP session.\n   837\u2192   * @param data supplies the buffer to be written which may be modified.\n   838\u2192   * @return status used by the filter manager to manage further filter iteration.\n   839\u2192   */\n   840\u2192  virtual UdpSessionWriteFilterStatus onWrite(Network::UdpRecvData& data) PURE;\n   841\u2192\n   842\u2192  /**\n   843\u2192   * Initializes the write filter callbacks used to interact with the filter manager. It will be\n   844\u2192   * called by the filter manager a single time when the filter is first registered.\n   845\u2192   *\n   846\u2192   * IMPORTANT: No outbound networking or complex processing should be done in this function.\n   847\u2192   *            That should be done in the context of ReadFilter::onNewSession() if needed.\n   848\u2192   *\n   849\u2192   * @param callbacks supplies the callbacks.\n   850\u2192   */\n   851\u2192  virtual void initializeWriteFilterCallbacks(UdpSessionWriteFilterCallbacks& callbacks) PURE;\n   852\u2192};\n   853\u2192\n   854\u2192using UdpSessionWriteFilterSharedPtr = std::shared_ptr<UdpSessionWriteFilter>;\n   855\u2192\n   856\u2192/**\n   857\u2192 * A combination read and write filter. This allows a single filter instance to cover\n   858\u2192 * both the read and write paths.\n   859\u2192 */\n   860\u2192class UdpSessionFilter : public virtual UdpSessionReadFilter,\n   861\u2192                         public virtual UdpSessionWriteFilter {};\n   862\u2192using UdpSessionFilterSharedPtr = std::shared_ptr<UdpSessionFilter>;\n   863\u2192\n   864\u2192/**\n   865\u2192 * These callbacks are provided by the UDP session manager to the factory so that the factory\n   866\u2192 * can * build the filter chain in an application specific way.\n   867\u2192 */\n   868\u2192class UdpSessionFilterChainFactoryCallbacks {\n   869\u2192public:\n   870\u2192  virtual ~UdpSessionFilterChainFactoryCallbacks() = default;\n   871\u2192\n   872\u2192  /**\n   873\u2192   * Add a read filter that is used when reading UDP session data.\n   874\u2192   * @param filter supplies the filter to add.\n   875\u2192   */\n   876\u2192  virtual void addReadFilter(UdpSessionReadFilterSharedPtr filter) PURE;\n   877\u2192\n   878\u2192  /**\n   879\u2192   * Add a write filter that is used when writing UDP session data.\n   880\u2192   * @param filter supplies the filter to add.\n   881\u2192   */\n   882\u2192  virtual void addWriteFilter(UdpSessionWriteFilterSharedPtr filter) PURE;\n   883\u2192\n   884\u2192  /**\n   885\u2192   * Add a bidirectional filter that is used when reading and writing UDP session data.\n   886\u2192   * @param filter supplies the filter to add.\n   887\u2192   */\n   888\u2192  virtual void addFilter(UdpSessionFilterSharedPtr filter) PURE;\n   889\u2192};\n   890\u2192\n   891\u2192/**\n   892\u2192 * This function is used to wrap the creation of a UDP session filter chain for new sessions as they\n   893\u2192 * come in. Filter factories create the function at configuration initialization time, and then\n   894\u2192 * they are used at runtime.\n   895\u2192 * @param callbacks supplies the callbacks for the stream to install filters to. Typically the\n   896\u2192 * function will install a single filter, but it's technically possibly to install more than one\n   897\u2192 * if desired.\n   898\u2192 */\n   899\u2192using UdpSessionFilterFactoryCb =\n   900\u2192    std::function<void(UdpSessionFilterChainFactoryCallbacks& callbacks)>;\n   901\u2192\n   902\u2192/**\n   903\u2192 * A UdpSessionFilterChainFactory is used by a UDP session manager to create a UDP session filter\n   904\u2192 * chain when a new session is created.\n   905\u2192 */\n   906\u2192class UdpSessionFilterChainFactory {\n   907\u2192public:\n   908\u2192  virtual ~UdpSessionFilterChainFactory() = default;\n   909\u2192\n   910\u2192  /**\n   911\u2192   * Called when a new UDP session is created.\n   912\u2192   * @param callbacks supplies the \"sink\" that is used for actually creating the filter chain. @see\n   913\u2192   *                  UdpSessionFilterChainFactoryCallbacks.\n   914\u2192   * @return true if filter chain was created successfully. Otherwise false.\n   915\u2192   */\n   916\u2192  virtual bool createFilterChain(UdpSessionFilterChainFactoryCallbacks& callbacks) const PURE;\n   917\u2192};\n   918\u2192\n   919\u2192/**\n   920\u2192 * Creates a chain of network filters for a new connection.\n   921\u2192 */\n   922\u2192class FilterChainFactory {\n   923\u2192public:\n   924\u2192  virtual ~FilterChainFactory() = default;\n   925\u2192\n   926\u2192  /**\n   927\u2192   * Called to create the network filter chain.\n   928\u2192   * @param connection supplies the connection to create the chain on.\n   929\u2192   * @param filter_factories supplies a list of filter factories to create the chain from.\n   930\u2192   * @return true if filter chain was created successfully. Otherwise\n   931\u2192   *   false, e.g. filter chain is empty.\n   932\u2192   */\n   933\u2192  virtual bool createNetworkFilterChain(Connection& connection,\n   934\u2192                                        const NetworkFilterFactoriesList& filter_factories) PURE;\n   935\u2192\n   936\u2192  /**\n   937\u2192   * Called to create the listener filter chain.\n   938\u2192   * @param listener supplies the listener to create the chain on.\n   939\u2192   * @return true if filter chain was created successfully. Otherwise false.\n   940\u2192   */\n   941\u2192  virtual bool createListenerFilterChain(ListenerFilterManager& listener) PURE;\n   942\u2192\n   943\u2192  /**\n   944\u2192   * Called to create a Udp Listener Filter Chain object\n   945\u2192   *\n   946\u2192   * @param udp_listener supplies the listener to create the chain on.\n   947\u2192   * @param callbacks supplies the callbacks needed to create a filter.\n   948\u2192   */\n   949\u2192  virtual void createUdpListenerFilterChain(UdpListenerFilterManager& udp_listener,\n   950\u2192                                            UdpReadFilterCallbacks& callbacks) PURE;\n   951\u2192\n   952\u2192  /**\n   953\u2192   * Called to create the QUIC listener filter chain.\n   954\u2192   * @param manager supplies the filter manager to create the chain on.\n   955\u2192   * @return true if filter chain was created successfully. Otherwise false.\n   956\u2192   */\n   957\u2192  virtual bool createQuicListenerFilterChain(QuicListenerFilterManager& manager) PURE;\n   958\u2192};\n   959\u2192\n   960\u2192/**\n   961\u2192 * Network filter matching context data for unified matchers.\n   962\u2192 */\n   963\u2192class MatchingData {\n   964\u2192public:\n   965\u2192  static absl::string_view name() { return \"network\"; }\n   966\u2192\n   967\u2192  virtual ~MatchingData() = default;\n   968\u2192\n   969\u2192  virtual const ConnectionSocket& socket() const PURE;\n   970\u2192  virtual const StreamInfo::FilterState& filterState() const PURE;\n   971\u2192  virtual const envoy::config::core::v3::Metadata& dynamicMetadata() const PURE;\n   972\u2192\n   973\u2192  const ConnectionInfoProvider& connectionInfoProvider() const {\n   974\u2192    return socket().connectionInfoProvider();\n   975\u2192  }\n   976\u2192\n   977\u2192  const Address::Instance& localAddress() const { return *connectionInfoProvider().localAddress(); }\n   978\u2192\n   979\u2192  const Address::Instance& remoteAddress() const {\n   980\u2192    return *connectionInfoProvider().remoteAddress();\n   981\u2192  }\n   982\u2192\n   983\u2192  Ssl::ConnectionInfoConstSharedPtr ssl() const { return connectionInfoProvider().sslConnection(); }\n   984\u2192};\n   985\u2192\n   986\u2192/**\n   987\u2192 * UDP listener filter matching context data for unified matchers.\n   988\u2192 */\n   989\u2192class UdpMatchingData {\n   990\u2192public:\n   991\u2192  static absl::string_view name() { return \"network\"; }\n   992\u2192\n   993\u2192  virtual ~UdpMatchingData() = default;\n   994\u2192\n   995\u2192  virtual const Address::Instance& localAddress() const PURE;\n   996\u2192  virtual const Address::Instance& remoteAddress() const PURE;\n   997\u2192};\n   998\u2192\n   999\u2192} // namespace Network\n  1000\u2192} // namespace Envoy\n  1001\u2192"
102. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/source/common/http/filter_manager.h"
}
Output
