Output
"     1\u2192#pragma once\n     2\u2192\n     3\u2192#include <cstdint>\n     4\u2192#include <functional>\n     5\u2192#include <memory>\n     6\u2192#include <string>\n     7\u2192\n     8\u2192#include \"envoy/access_log/access_log.h\"\n     9\u2192#include \"envoy/buffer/buffer.h\"\n    10\u2192#include \"envoy/common/scope_tracker.h\"\n    11\u2192#include \"envoy/event/dispatcher.h\"\n    12\u2192#include \"envoy/grpc/status.h\"\n    13\u2192#include \"envoy/http/codec.h\"\n    14\u2192#include \"envoy/http/filter_factory.h\"\n    15\u2192#include \"envoy/http/header_map.h\"\n    16\u2192#include \"envoy/matcher/matcher.h\"\n    17\u2192#include \"envoy/router/router.h\"\n    18\u2192#include \"envoy/router/scopes.h\"\n    19\u2192#include \"envoy/ssl/connection.h\"\n    20\u2192#include \"envoy/tracing/tracer.h\"\n    21\u2192#include \"envoy/upstream/load_balancer.h\"\n    22\u2192#include \"envoy/upstream/upstream.h\"\n    23\u2192\n    24\u2192#include \"source/common/common/scope_tracked_object_stack.h\"\n    25\u2192\n    26\u2192#include \"absl/types/optional.h\"\n    27\u2192\n    28\u2192namespace Envoy {\n    29\u2192namespace Router {\n    30\u2192class RouteConfigProvider;\n    31\u2192}\n    32\u2192namespace Http {\n    33\u2192\n    34\u2192/**\n    35\u2192 * Return codes for encode/decode headers filter invocations. The connection manager bases further\n    36\u2192 * filter invocations on the return code of the previous filter.\n    37\u2192 */\n    38\u2192enum class FilterHeadersStatus {\n    39\u2192  // Continue filter chain iteration.\n    40\u2192  Continue,\n    41\u2192  // Do not iterate for headers on any of the remaining filters in the chain.\n    42\u2192  //\n    43\u2192  // Returning FilterDataStatus::Continue from decodeData()/encodeData() or calling\n    44\u2192  // continueDecoding()/continueEncoding() MUST be called if continued filter iteration is desired.\n    45\u2192  //\n    46\u2192  // Note that if a local reply was sent, no further iteration for headers as well as data and\n    47\u2192  // trailers for the current filter and the filters following will happen. A local reply can be\n    48\u2192  // triggered via sendLocalReply() or encodeHeaders().\n    49\u2192  StopIteration,\n    50\u2192  // Continue headers iteration to remaining filters, but delay ending the stream. This status MUST\n    51\u2192  // NOT be returned when end_stream is already set to false.\n    52\u2192  //\n    53\u2192  // Used when a filter wants to add a body to a headers-only request/response, but this body is not\n    54\u2192  // readily available. Delaying end_stream allows the filter to add the body once it's available\n    55\u2192  // without stopping headers iteration.\n    56\u2192  //\n    57\u2192  // The filter is responsible to continue the stream by providing a body through calling\n    58\u2192  // injectDecodedDataToFilterChain()/injectEncodedDataToFilterChain(), possibly multiple times\n    59\u2192  // if the body needs to be divided into several chunks. The filter may need to handle\n    60\u2192  // watermark events when injecting a body, see:\n    61\u2192  // https://github.com/envoyproxy/envoy/blob/main/source/docs/flow_control.md.\n    62\u2192  //\n    63\u2192  // The last call to inject data MUST have end_stream set to true to conclude the stream.\n    64\u2192  // If the filter cannot provide a body the stream should be reset.\n    65\u2192  //\n    66\u2192  // Adding a body through calling addDecodedData()/addEncodedData() then\n    67\u2192  // continueDecoding()/continueEncoding() is currently NOT supported and causes an assert failure.\n    68\u2192  //\n    69\u2192  // Adding trailers in this scenario is currently NOT supported.\n    70\u2192  //\n    71\u2192  // The filter MUST NOT attempt to continue the stream without providing a body using\n    72\u2192  // continueDecoding()/continueEncoding().\n    73\u2192  //\n    74\u2192  // TODO(yosrym93): Support adding a body in this case by calling addDecodedData()/addEncodedData()\n    75\u2192  // then continueDecoding()/continueEncoding(). To support this a new FilterManager::IterationState\n    76\u2192  // needs to be added and set when a filter returns this status in\n    77\u2192  // FilterManager::decodeHeaders/FilterManager::encodeHeaders()\n    78\u2192  // Currently, when a filter returns this, the IterationState is Continue. This causes ASSERTs in\n    79\u2192  // FilterManager::commonContinue() to fail when continueDecoding()/continueEncoding() is called;\n    80\u2192  // due to trying to continue iteration when the IterationState is already Continue.\n    81\u2192  // In this case, a different ASSERT will be needed to make sure the filter does not try to\n    82\u2192  // continue without adding a body first.\n    83\u2192  //\n    84\u2192  // TODO(yosrym93): Support adding trailers in this case by implementing new functions to inject\n    85\u2192  // trailers, similar to the inject data functions.\n    86\u2192  ContinueAndDontEndStream,\n    87\u2192  // Do not iterate for headers as well as data and trailers for the current filter and the filters\n    88\u2192  // following, and buffer body data for later dispatching. ContinueDecoding() MUST\n    89\u2192  // be called if continued filter iteration is desired.\n    90\u2192  //\n    91\u2192  // Used when a filter wants to stop iteration on data and trailers while waiting for headers'\n    92\u2192  // iteration to resume.\n    93\u2192  //\n    94\u2192  // If buffering the request causes buffered data to exceed the configured buffer limit, a 413 will\n    95\u2192  // be sent to the user. On the response path exceeding buffer limits will result in a 500.\n    96\u2192  //\n    97\u2192  // TODO(soya3129): stop metadata parsing when StopAllIterationAndBuffer is set.\n    98\u2192  StopAllIterationAndBuffer,\n    99\u2192  // Do not iterate for headers as well as data and trailers for the current filter and the filters\n   100\u2192  // following, and buffer body data for later dispatching. continueDecoding() MUST\n   101\u2192  // be called if continued filter iteration is desired.\n   102\u2192  //\n   103\u2192  // Used when a filter wants to stop iteration on data and trailers while waiting for headers'\n   104\u2192  // iteration to resume.\n   105\u2192  //\n   106\u2192  // This will cause the flow of incoming data to cease until continueDecoding() function is called.\n   107\u2192  //\n   108\u2192  // TODO(soya3129): stop metadata parsing when StopAllIterationAndWatermark is set.\n   109\u2192  StopAllIterationAndWatermark,\n   110\u2192};\n   111\u2192\n   112\u2192/**\n   113\u2192 * Return codes for encode on informative headers filter invocations.\n   114\u2192 */\n   115\u2192enum class Filter1xxHeadersStatus {\n   116\u2192  // Continue filter chain iteration.\n   117\u2192  Continue,\n   118\u2192  // Do not iterate for informative headers on any of the remaining filters in the chain.\n   119\u2192  //\n   120\u2192  // Returning FilterDataStatus::Continue from encodeData() or calling\n   121\u2192  // continueEncoding() MUST be called if continued filter iteration is desired.\n   122\u2192  StopIteration\n   123\u2192};\n   124\u2192\n   125\u2192/**\n   126\u2192 * Return codes for encode/decode data filter invocations. The connection manager bases further\n   127\u2192 * filter invocations on the return code of the previous filter.\n   128\u2192 */\n   129\u2192enum class FilterDataStatus {\n   130\u2192  // Continue filter chain iteration. If headers have not yet been sent to the next filter, they\n   131\u2192  // will be sent first via decodeHeaders()/encodeHeaders(). If data has previously been buffered,\n   132\u2192  // the data in this callback will be added to the buffer before the entirety is sent to the next\n   133\u2192  // filter.\n   134\u2192  Continue,\n   135\u2192  // Do not iterate to any of the remaining filters in the chain, and buffer body data for later\n   136\u2192  // dispatching. Returning FilterDataStatus::Continue from decodeData()/encodeData() or calling\n   137\u2192  // continueDecoding()/continueEncoding() MUST be called if continued filter iteration is desired.\n   138\u2192  //\n   139\u2192  // This should be called by filters which must parse a larger block of the incoming data before\n   140\u2192  // continuing processing and so can not push back on streaming data via watermarks.\n   141\u2192  //\n   142\u2192  // If buffering the request causes buffered data to exceed the configured buffer limit, a 413 will\n   143\u2192  // be sent to the user. On the response path exceeding buffer limits will result in a 500.\n   144\u2192  StopIterationAndBuffer,\n   145\u2192  // Do not iterate to any of the remaining filters in the chain, and buffer body data for later\n   146\u2192  // dispatching. Returning FilterDataStatus::Continue from decodeData()/encodeData() or calling\n   147\u2192  // continueDecoding()/continueEncoding() MUST be called if continued filter iteration is desired.\n   148\u2192  //\n   149\u2192  // This will cause the flow of incoming data to cease until one of the continue.*() functions is\n   150\u2192  // called.\n   151\u2192  //\n   152\u2192  // This should be returned by filters which can nominally stream data but have a transient back-up\n   153\u2192  // such as the configured delay of the fault filter, or if the router filter is still fetching an\n   154\u2192  // upstream connection.\n   155\u2192  StopIterationAndWatermark,\n   156\u2192  // Do not iterate to any of the remaining filters in the chain, but do not buffer any of the\n   157\u2192  // body data for later dispatching. Returning FilterDataStatus::Continue from\n   158\u2192  // decodeData()/encodeData() or calling continueDecoding()/continueEncoding() MUST be called if\n   159\u2192  // continued filter iteration is desired.\n   160\u2192  //\n   161\u2192  // Note that if a local reply was sent, no further iteration for either data or trailers\n   162\u2192  // for the current filter and the filters following will happen. A local reply can be\n   163\u2192  // triggered via sendLocalReply() or encodeHeaders().\n   164\u2192  StopIterationNoBuffer\n   165\u2192};\n   166\u2192\n   167\u2192/**\n   168\u2192 * Return codes for encode/decode trailers filter invocations. The connection manager bases further\n   169\u2192 * filter invocations on the return code of the previous filter.\n   170\u2192 */\n   171\u2192enum class FilterTrailersStatus {\n   172\u2192  // Continue filter chain iteration.\n   173\u2192  Continue,\n   174\u2192  // Do not iterate to any of the remaining filters in the chain. Calling\n   175\u2192  // continueDecoding()/continueEncoding() MUST be called if continued filter iteration is desired.\n   176\u2192  StopIteration\n   177\u2192};\n   178\u2192\n   179\u2192/**\n   180\u2192 * Return codes for encode metadata filter invocations. Metadata currently can not stop filter\n   181\u2192 * iteration except in the case of local replies.\n   182\u2192 */\n   183\u2192enum class FilterMetadataStatus {\n   184\u2192  // Continue filter chain iteration for metadata only. Does not unblock returns of StopIteration*\n   185\u2192  // from (decode|encode)(Headers|Data).\n   186\u2192  Continue,\n   187\u2192\n   188\u2192  // Continue filter chain iteration. If headers have not yet been sent to the next filter, they\n   189\u2192  // will be sent first via (decode|encode)Headers().\n   190\u2192  ContinueAll,\n   191\u2192\n   192\u2192  // Stops iteration of the entire filter chain. Only to be used in the case of sending a local\n   193\u2192  // reply from (decode|encode)Metadata.\n   194\u2192  StopIterationForLocalReply,\n   195\u2192};\n   196\u2192\n   197\u2192/**\n   198\u2192 * Return codes for onLocalReply filter invocations.\n   199\u2192 */\n   200\u2192enum class LocalErrorStatus {\n   201\u2192  // Continue sending the local reply after onLocalReply has been sent to all filters.\n   202\u2192  Continue,\n   203\u2192\n   204\u2192  // Continue sending onLocalReply to all filters, but reset the stream once all filters have been\n   205\u2192  // informed rather than sending the local reply.\n   206\u2192  ContinueAndResetStream,\n   207\u2192};\n   208\u2192\n   209\u2192// These are events that upstream HTTP filters can register for, via the addUpstreamCallbacks\n   210\u2192// function.\n   211\u2192class UpstreamCallbacks {\n   212\u2192public:\n   213\u2192  virtual ~UpstreamCallbacks() = default;\n   214\u2192\n   215\u2192  // Called when the upstream connection is established and\n   216\u2192  // UpstreamStreamFilterCallbacks::upstream should be available.\n   217\u2192  //\n   218\u2192  // This indicates that data may begin flowing upstream.\n   219\u2192  virtual void onUpstreamConnectionEstablished() PURE;\n   220\u2192};\n   221\u2192\n   222\u2192// These are filter callbacks specific to upstream HTTP filters, accessible via\n   223\u2192// StreamFilterCallbacks::upstreamCallbacks()\n   224\u2192class UpstreamStreamFilterCallbacks {\n   225\u2192public:\n   226\u2192  virtual ~UpstreamStreamFilterCallbacks() = default;\n   227\u2192\n   228\u2192  // Returns a handle to the upstream stream's stream info.\n   229\u2192  virtual StreamInfo::StreamInfo& upstreamStreamInfo() PURE;\n   230\u2192\n   231\u2192  // Returns a handle to the generic upstream.\n   232\u2192  virtual OptRef<Router::GenericUpstream> upstream() PURE;\n   233\u2192\n   234\u2192  // Dumps state for the upstream request.\n   235\u2192  virtual void dumpState(std::ostream& os, int indent_level = 0) const PURE;\n   236\u2192\n   237\u2192  // Setters and getters to determine if sending body payload is paused on\n   238\u2192  // confirmation of a CONNECT upgrade. These should only be used by the upstream codec filter.\n   239\u2192  // TODO(alyssawilk) after deprecating the classic path, move this logic to the\n   240\u2192  // upstream codec filter and remove these APIs\n   241\u2192  virtual bool pausedForConnect() const PURE;\n   242\u2192  virtual void setPausedForConnect(bool value) PURE;\n   243\u2192\n   244\u2192  // Setters and getters to determine if sending body payload is paused on\n   245\u2192  // confirmation of a WebSocket upgrade. These should only be used by the upstream codec filter.\n   246\u2192  virtual bool pausedForWebsocketUpgrade() const PURE;\n   247\u2192  virtual void setPausedForWebsocketUpgrade(bool value) PURE;\n   248\u2192\n   249\u2192  // Return the upstreamStreamOptions for this stream.\n   250\u2192  virtual const Http::ConnectionPool::Instance::StreamOptions& upstreamStreamOptions() const PURE;\n   251\u2192\n   252\u2192  // Adds the supplied UpstreamCallbacks to the list of callbacks to be called\n   253\u2192  // as various upstream events occur. Callbacks should persist for the lifetime\n   254\u2192  // of the upstream stream.\n   255\u2192  virtual void addUpstreamCallbacks(UpstreamCallbacks& callbacks) PURE;\n   256\u2192\n   257\u2192  // This should only be called by the UpstreamCodecFilter, and is used to let the\n   258\u2192  // UpstreamCodecFilter supply the interface used by the GenericUpstream to receive\n   259\u2192  // response data from the upstream stream once it is established.\n   260\u2192  virtual void\n   261\u2192  setUpstreamToDownstream(Router::UpstreamToDownstream& upstream_to_downstream_interface) PURE;\n   262\u2192};\n   263\u2192\n   264\u2192/**\n   265\u2192 * RouteConfigUpdatedCallback is used to notify an OnDemandRouteUpdate filter about completion of a\n   266\u2192 * RouteConfig update. The filter (and the associated ActiveStream) where the original on-demand\n   267\u2192 * request was originated can be destroyed before a response to an on-demand update request is\n   268\u2192 * received and updates are propagated. To handle this:\n   269\u2192 *\n   270\u2192 * OnDemandRouteUpdate filter instance holds a RouteConfigUpdatedCallbackSharedPtr to a callback.\n   271\u2192 * Envoy::Router::RdsRouteConfigProviderImpl holds a weak pointer to the RouteConfigUpdatedCallback\n   272\u2192 * above in an Envoy::Router::UpdateOnDemandCallback struct\n   273\u2192 *\n   274\u2192 * In RdsRouteConfigProviderImpl::onConfigUpdate(), before invoking the callback, a check is made to\n   275\u2192 * verify if the callback is still available.\n   276\u2192 */\n   277\u2192using RouteConfigUpdatedCallback = std::function<void(bool)>;\n   278\u2192using RouteConfigUpdatedCallbackSharedPtr = std::shared_ptr<RouteConfigUpdatedCallback>;\n   279\u2192\n   280\u2192// This class allows entities caching a route (e.g.) ActiveStream to delegate route clearing to xDS\n   281\u2192// components.\n   282\u2192class RouteCache {\n   283\u2192public:\n   284\u2192  virtual ~RouteCache() = default;\n   285\u2192\n   286\u2192  // Returns true if the route cache currently has a cached route.\n   287\u2192  virtual bool hasCachedRoute() const PURE;\n   288\u2192  // Forces the route cache to refresh the cached route.\n   289\u2192  virtual void refreshCachedRoute() PURE;\n   290\u2192};\n   291\u2192\n   292\u2192class RouteConfigUpdateRequester {\n   293\u2192public:\n   294\u2192  virtual ~RouteConfigUpdateRequester() = default;\n   295\u2192\n   296\u2192  virtual void\n   297\u2192  requestRouteConfigUpdate(RouteCache& route_cache,\n   298\u2192                           Http::RouteConfigUpdatedCallbackSharedPtr route_config_updated_cb,\n   299\u2192                           absl::optional<Router::ConfigConstSharedPtr> route_config,\n   300\u2192                           Event::Dispatcher& dispatcher, RequestHeaderMap& request_headers) PURE;\n   301\u2192  virtual void\n   302\u2192  requestVhdsUpdate(const std::string& host_header, Event::Dispatcher& thread_local_dispatcher,\n   303\u2192                    Http::RouteConfigUpdatedCallbackSharedPtr route_config_updated_cb) PURE;\n   304\u2192  virtual void\n   305\u2192  requestSrdsUpdate(RouteCache& route_cache, Router::ScopeKeyPtr scope_key,\n   306\u2192                    Event::Dispatcher& thread_local_dispatcher,\n   307\u2192                    Http::RouteConfigUpdatedCallbackSharedPtr route_config_updated_cb) PURE;\n   308\u2192};\n   309\u2192\n   310\u2192class RouteConfigUpdateRequesterFactory : public Config::UntypedFactory {\n   311\u2192public:\n   312\u2192  // UntypedFactory\n   313\u2192  std::string category() const override { return \"envoy.route_config_update_requester\"; }\n   314\u2192\n   315\u2192  virtual std::unique_ptr<RouteConfigUpdateRequester>\n   316\u2192  createRouteConfigUpdateRequester(Router::RouteConfigProvider* route_config_provider) PURE;\n   317\u2192  virtual std::unique_ptr<RouteConfigUpdateRequester>\n   318\u2192  createRouteConfigUpdateRequester(Config::ConfigProvider* scoped_route_config_provider,\n   319\u2192                                   OptRef<const Router::ScopeKeyBuilder> scope_key_builder) PURE;\n   320\u2192};\n   321\u2192\n   322\u2192class DownstreamStreamFilterCallbacks {\n   323\u2192public:\n   324\u2192  virtual ~DownstreamStreamFilterCallbacks() = default;\n   325\u2192\n   326\u2192  /**\n   327\u2192   * Sets the cached route for the current request to the passed-in RouteConstSharedPtr parameter.\n   328\u2192   *\n   329\u2192   * Similar to route(const Router::RouteCallback& cb), this route that is set will be\n   330\u2192   * overridden by clearRouteCache() in subsequent filters. Usage is intended for filters at the end\n   331\u2192   * of the filter chain.\n   332\u2192   *\n   333\u2192   * NOTE: Passing nullptr in as the route parameter is equivalent to route resolution being\n   334\u2192   * attempted and failing to find a route. An example of when this happens is when\n   335\u2192   * RouteConstSharedPtr route(const RouteCallback& cb, const Http::RequestHeaderMap& headers, const\n   336\u2192   * StreamInfo::StreamInfo& stream_info, uint64_t random_value) returns nullptr during a\n   337\u2192   * refreshCachedRoute. It is important to note that setRoute(nullptr) is different from a\n   338\u2192   * clearRouteCache(), because clearRouteCache() wants route resolution to be attempted again.\n   339\u2192   * clearRouteCache() achieves this by setting cached_route_ and cached_cluster_info_ to\n   340\u2192   * absl::optional ptrs instead of null ptrs.\n   341\u2192   */\n   342\u2192  virtual void setRoute(Router::RouteConstSharedPtr route) PURE;\n   343\u2192\n   344\u2192  /**\n   345\u2192   * Invokes callback with a matched route, callback can choose to accept this route by returning\n   346\u2192   * Router::RouteMatchStatus::Accept or continue route match from last matched route by returning\n   347\u2192   * Router::RouteMatchStatus::Continue, if there are more routes available.\n   348\u2192   *\n   349\u2192   * Returns route accepted by the callback or nullptr if no match found or none of route is\n   350\u2192   * accepted by the callback.\n   351\u2192   *\n   352\u2192   * NOTE: clearRouteCache() must be called before invoking this method otherwise cached route will\n   353\u2192   * be returned directly to the caller and the callback will not be invoked.\n   354\u2192   *\n   355\u2192   * Currently a route callback's decision is overridden by clearRouteCache() / route() call in the\n   356\u2192   * subsequent filters. We may want to persist callbacks so they always participate in later route\n   357\u2192   * resolution or make it an independent entity like filters that gets called on route resolution.\n   358\u2192   */\n   359\u2192  virtual Router::RouteConstSharedPtr route(const Router::RouteCallback& cb) PURE;\n   360\u2192\n   361\u2192  /**\n   362\u2192   * Clears the route cache for the current request. This must be called when a filter has modified\n   363\u2192   * the headers in a way that would affect routing.\n   364\u2192   */\n   365\u2192  virtual void clearRouteCache() PURE;\n   366\u2192\n   367\u2192  /**\n   368\u2192   * Schedules a request for a RouteConfiguration update from the management server.\n   369\u2192   * @param route_config_updated_cb callback to be called when the configuration update has been\n   370\u2192   * propagated to the worker thread.\n   371\u2192   */\n   372\u2192  virtual void\n   373\u2192  requestRouteConfigUpdate(RouteConfigUpdatedCallbackSharedPtr route_config_updated_cb) PURE;\n   374\u2192};\n   375\u2192\n   376\u2192/**\n   377\u2192 * The stream filter callbacks are passed to all filters to use for writing response data and\n   378\u2192 * interacting with the underlying stream in general.\n   379\u2192 */\n   380\u2192class StreamFilterCallbacks {\n   381\u2192public:\n   382\u2192  virtual ~StreamFilterCallbacks() = default;\n   383\u2192\n   384\u2192  /**\n   385\u2192   * @return OptRef<const Network::Connection> the downstream connection, or nullptr if there is\n   386\u2192   * none.\n   387\u2192   */\n   388\u2192  virtual OptRef<const Network::Connection> connection() PURE;\n   389\u2192\n   390\u2192  /**\n   391\u2192   * @return Event::Dispatcher& the thread local dispatcher for allocating timers, etc.\n   392\u2192   */\n   393\u2192  virtual Event::Dispatcher& dispatcher() PURE;\n   394\u2192\n   395\u2192  /**\n   396\u2192   * Reset the underlying stream.\n   397\u2192   */\n   398\u2192  virtual void\n   399\u2192  resetStream(Http::StreamResetReason reset_reason = Http::StreamResetReason::LocalReset,\n   400\u2192              absl::string_view transport_failure_reason = \"\") PURE;\n   401\u2192\n   402\u2192  /**\n   403\u2192   * Returns the route for the current request. The assumption is that the implementation can do\n   404\u2192   * caching where applicable to avoid multiple lookups. If a filter has modified the headers in\n   405\u2192   * a way that affects routing, clearRouteCache() must be called to clear the cache.\n   406\u2192   */\n   407\u2192  virtual Router::RouteConstSharedPtr route() PURE;\n   408\u2192\n   409\u2192  /**\n   410\u2192   * Returns the clusterInfo for the cached route.\n   411\u2192   * This method is to avoid multiple look ups in the filter chain, it also provides a consistent\n   412\u2192   * view of clusterInfo after a route is picked/repicked.\n   413\u2192   * NOTE: Cached clusterInfo and route will be updated the same time.\n   414\u2192   */\n   415\u2192  virtual Upstream::ClusterInfoConstSharedPtr clusterInfo() PURE;\n   416\u2192\n   417\u2192  /**\n   418\u2192   * @return uint64_t the ID of the originating stream for logging purposes.\n   419\u2192   */\n   420\u2192  virtual uint64_t streamId() const PURE;\n   421\u2192\n   422\u2192  /**\n   423\u2192   * @return streamInfo for logging purposes. Individual filter may add specific information to be\n   424\u2192   * put into the access log.\n   425\u2192   */\n   426\u2192  virtual StreamInfo::StreamInfo& streamInfo() PURE;\n   427\u2192\n   428\u2192  /**\n   429\u2192   * @return span context used for tracing purposes. Individual filters may add or modify\n   430\u2192   *              information in the span context.\n   431\u2192   */\n   432\u2192  virtual Tracing::Span& activeSpan() PURE;\n   433\u2192\n   434\u2192  /**\n   435\u2192   * @return tracing configuration if present.\n   436\u2192   */\n   437\u2192  virtual OptRef<const Tracing::Config> tracingConfig() const PURE;\n   438\u2192\n   439\u2192  /**\n   440\u2192   * @return the ScopeTrackedObject for this stream.\n   441\u2192   */\n   442\u2192  virtual const ScopeTrackedObject& scope() PURE;\n   443\u2192\n   444\u2192  /**\n   445\u2192   * Should be used when we continue processing a request or response by invoking a filter directly\n   446\u2192   * from an asynchronous callback to restore crash context. If not explicitly used by the filter\n   447\u2192   * itself, this gets invoked in ActiveStreamFilterBase::commonContinue().\n   448\u2192   *\n   449\u2192   * @param tracked_object_stack ScopeTrackedObjectStack where relevant ScopeTrackedObjects will be\n   450\u2192   * added to.\n   451\u2192   */\n   452\u2192  virtual void restoreContextOnContinue(ScopeTrackedObjectStack& tracked_object_stack) PURE;\n   453\u2192\n   454\u2192  /**\n   455\u2192   * Called when filter activity indicates that the stream idle timeout should be reset.\n   456\u2192   */\n   457\u2192  virtual void resetIdleTimer() PURE;\n   458\u2192\n   459\u2192  /**\n   460\u2192   * This is a helper to get the route's per-filter config if it exists, otherwise the virtual\n   461\u2192   * host's. Or nullptr if none of them exist.\n   462\u2192   */\n   463\u2192  virtual const Router::RouteSpecificFilterConfig* mostSpecificPerFilterConfig() const PURE;\n   464\u2192\n   465\u2192  /**\n   466\u2192   * Return all the available per route filter configs. The configs is in order of specificity.\n   467\u2192   * That means that the config from a route configuration will be first, then the config from a\n   468\u2192   * virtual host, then the config from a route.\n   469\u2192   */\n   470\u2192  virtual Router::RouteSpecificFilterConfigs perFilterConfigs() const PURE;\n   471\u2192\n   472\u2192  /**\n   473\u2192   * Return the HTTP/1 stream encoder options if applicable. If the stream is not HTTP/1 returns\n   474\u2192   * absl::nullopt.\n   475\u2192   */\n   476\u2192  virtual Http1StreamEncoderOptionsOptRef http1StreamEncoderOptions() PURE;\n   477\u2192\n   478\u2192  /**\n   479\u2192   * Return a handle to the upstream callbacks. This is valid for upstream HTTP filters, and nullopt\n   480\u2192   * for downstream HTTP filters.\n   481\u2192   */\n   482\u2192  virtual OptRef<UpstreamStreamFilterCallbacks> upstreamCallbacks() PURE;\n   483\u2192\n   484\u2192  /**\n   485\u2192   * Return a handle to the downstream callbacks. This is valid for downstream HTTP filters, and\n   486\u2192   * nullopt for upstream HTTP filters.\n   487\u2192   */\n   488\u2192  virtual OptRef<DownstreamStreamFilterCallbacks> downstreamCallbacks() PURE;\n   489\u2192\n   490\u2192  /**\n   491\u2192   * @return absl::string_view the name of the filter as configured in the filter chain.\n   492\u2192   */\n   493\u2192  virtual absl::string_view filterConfigName() const PURE;\n   494\u2192\n   495\u2192  /**\n   496\u2192   * The downstream request headers if present.\n   497\u2192   */\n   498\u2192  virtual RequestHeaderMapOptRef requestHeaders() PURE;\n   499\u2192\n   500\u2192  /**\n   501\u2192   * The downstream request trailers if present.\n   502\u2192   */\n   503\u2192  virtual RequestTrailerMapOptRef requestTrailers() PURE;\n   504\u2192\n   505\u2192  /**\n   506\u2192   * Retrieves a pointer to the continue headers if present.\n   507\u2192   */\n   508\u2192  virtual ResponseHeaderMapOptRef informationalHeaders() PURE;\n   509\u2192\n   510\u2192  /**\n   511\u2192   * Retrieves a pointer to the response headers if present.\n   512\u2192   * Note that response headers might be set multiple times (e.g. if a local reply is issued after\n   513\u2192   * headers have been received but before headers have been encoded), so it is not safe in general\n   514\u2192   * to assume that any set of headers will be valid for the duration of the stream.\n   515\u2192   */\n   516\u2192  virtual ResponseHeaderMapOptRef responseHeaders() PURE;\n   517\u2192\n   518\u2192  /**\n   519\u2192   * Retrieves a pointer to the last response trailers if present.\n   520\u2192   * Note that response headers might be set multiple times (e.g. if a local reply is issued after\n   521\u2192   * headers have been received but before headers have been encoded), so it is not safe in general\n   522\u2192   * to assume that any set of headers will be valid for the duration of the stream.\n   523\u2192   */\n   524\u2192  virtual ResponseTrailerMapOptRef responseTrailers() PURE;\n   525\u2192};\n   526\u2192\n   527\u2192/**\n   528\u2192 * Stream decoder filter callbacks add additional callbacks that allow a\n   529\u2192 * decoding filter to restart decoding if they decide to hold data (e.g. for\n   530\u2192 * buffering or rate limiting).\n   531\u2192 */\n   532\u2192class StreamDecoderFilterCallbacks : public virtual StreamFilterCallbacks {\n   533\u2192public:\n   534\u2192  /**\n   535\u2192   * Continue iterating through the filter chain with buffered headers and body data. This routine\n   536\u2192   * can only be called if the filter has previously returned StopIteration from decodeHeaders()\n   537\u2192   * AND one of StopIterationAndBuffer, StopIterationAndWatermark, or StopIterationNoBuffer\n   538\u2192   * from each previous call to decodeData().\n   539\u2192   *\n   540\u2192   * The connection manager will dispatch headers and any buffered body data to the\n   541\u2192   * next filter in the chain. Further note that if the request is not complete, this filter will\n   542\u2192   * still receive decodeData() calls and must return an appropriate status code depending on what\n   543\u2192   * the filter needs to do.\n   544\u2192   */\n   545\u2192  virtual void continueDecoding() PURE;\n   546\u2192\n   547\u2192  /**\n   548\u2192   * @return const Buffer::Instance* the currently buffered data as buffered by this filter or\n   549\u2192   *         previous ones in the filter chain. May be nullptr if nothing has been buffered yet.\n   550\u2192   */\n   551\u2192  virtual const Buffer::Instance* decodingBuffer() PURE;\n   552\u2192\n   553\u2192  /**\n   554\u2192   * Allows modifying the decoding buffer. May only be called before any data has been continued\n   555\u2192   * past the calling filter.\n   556\u2192   */\n   557\u2192  virtual void modifyDecodingBuffer(std::function<void(Buffer::Instance&)> callback) PURE;\n   558\u2192\n   559\u2192  /**\n   560\u2192   * Add buffered body data. This method is used in advanced cases where returning\n   561\u2192   * StopIterationAndBuffer from decodeData() is not sufficient.\n   562\u2192   *\n   563\u2192   * 1) If a headers only request needs to be turned into a request with a body, this method can\n   564\u2192   * be called to add body in the decodeHeaders() callback. Subsequent filters will receive\n   565\u2192   * decodeHeaders(..., false) followed by decodeData(..., true). This works both in the direct\n   566\u2192   * iteration as well as the continuation case.\n   567\u2192   *\n   568\u2192   * 2) If a filter is going to look at all buffered data from within a data callback with end\n   569\u2192   * stream set, this method can be called to immediately buffer the data. This avoids having\n   570\u2192   * to deal with the existing buffered data and the data from the current callback.\n   571\u2192   *\n   572\u2192   * 3) If additional buffered body data needs to be added by a filter before continuation of data\n   573\u2192   * to further filters (outside of callback context).\n   574\u2192   *\n   575\u2192   * 4) If additional data needs to be added in the decodeTrailers() callback, this method can be\n   576\u2192   * called in the context of the callback. All further filters will receive decodeData(..., false)\n   577\u2192   * followed by decodeTrailers(). However if the iteration is stopped, the added data will\n   578\u2192   * buffered, so that the further filters will not receive decodeData() before decodeHeaders().\n   579\u2192   *\n   580\u2192   * It is an error to call this method in any other case.\n   581\u2192   *\n   582\u2192   * See also injectDecodedDataToFilterChain() for a different way of passing data to further\n   583\u2192   * filters and also how the two methods are different.\n   584\u2192   *\n   585\u2192   * @param data Buffer::Instance supplies the data to be decoded.\n   586\u2192   * @param streaming_filter boolean supplies if this filter streams data or buffers the full body.\n   587\u2192   */\n   588\u2192  virtual void addDecodedData(Buffer::Instance& data, bool streaming_filter) PURE;\n   589\u2192\n   590\u2192  /**\n   591\u2192   * Decode data directly to subsequent filters in the filter chain. This method is used in\n   592\u2192   * advanced cases in which a filter needs full control over how subsequent filters view data,\n   593\u2192   * and does not want to make use of HTTP connection manager buffering. Using this method allows\n   594\u2192   * a filter to buffer data (or not) and then periodically inject data to subsequent filters,\n   595\u2192   * indicating end_stream at an appropriate time. This can be used to implement rate limiting,\n   596\u2192   * periodic data emission, etc.\n   597\u2192   *\n   598\u2192   * This method should only be called outside of callback context. I.e., do not call this method\n   599\u2192   * from within a filter's decodeData() call.\n   600\u2192   *\n   601\u2192   * When using this callback, filters should generally only return\n   602\u2192   * FilterDataStatus::StopIterationNoBuffer from their decodeData() call, since use of this method\n   603\u2192   * indicates that a filter does not wish to participate in standard HTTP connection manager\n   604\u2192   * buffering and continuation and will perform any necessary buffering and continuation on its\n   605\u2192   * own.\n   606\u2192   *\n   607\u2192   * This callback is different from addDecodedData() in that the specified data and end_stream\n   608\u2192   * status will be propagated directly to further filters in the filter chain. This is different\n   609\u2192   * from addDecodedData() where data is added to the HTTP connection manager's buffered data with\n   610\u2192   * the assumption that standard HTTP connection manager buffering and continuation are being used.\n   611\u2192   */\n   612\u2192  virtual void injectDecodedDataToFilterChain(Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream) PURE;\n   613\u2192\n   614\u2192  /**\n   615\u2192   * Adds decoded trailers. May only be called in decodeData when end_stream is set to true.\n   616\u2192   * If called in any other context, an assertion will be triggered.\n   617\u2192   *\n   618\u2192   * When called in decodeData, the trailers map will be initialized to an empty map and returned by\n   619\u2192   * reference. Calling this function more than once is invalid.\n   620\u2192   *\n   621\u2192   * @return a reference to the newly created trailers map.\n   622\u2192   */\n   623\u2192  virtual RequestTrailerMap& addDecodedTrailers() PURE;\n   624\u2192\n   625\u2192  /**\n   626\u2192   * Attempts to create a locally generated response using the provided response_code and body_text\n   627\u2192   * parameters. If the request was a gRPC request the local reply will be encoded as a gRPC\n   628\u2192   * response with a 200 HTTP response code and grpc-status and grpc-message headers mapped from the\n   629\u2192   * provided parameters.\n   630\u2192   *\n   631\u2192   * If a response has already started (e.g. if the router calls sendLocalReply after encoding\n   632\u2192   * headers) this will either ship the reply directly to the downstream codec, or reset the stream.\n   633\u2192   *\n   634\u2192   * @param response_code supplies the HTTP response code.\n   635\u2192   * @param body_text supplies the optional body text which is sent using the text/plain content\n   636\u2192   *                  type, or encoded in the grpc-message header.\n   637\u2192   * @param modify_headers supplies an optional callback function that can modify the\n   638\u2192   *                       response headers.\n   639\u2192   * @param grpc_status the gRPC status code to override the httpToGrpcStatus mapping with.\n   640\u2192   * @param details a string detailing why this local reply was sent.\n   641\u2192   */\n   642\u2192  virtual void sendLocalReply(Code response_code, absl::string_view body_text,\n   643\u2192                              std::function<void(ResponseHeaderMap& headers)> modify_headers,\n   644\u2192                              const absl::optional<Grpc::Status::GrpcStatus> grpc_status,\n   645\u2192                              absl::string_view details) PURE;\n   646\u2192\n   647\u2192  /**\n   648\u2192   * Attempt to send GOAWAY and close the connection, and no filter chain will move forward.\n   649\u2192   */\n   650\u2192  virtual void sendGoAwayAndClose() PURE;\n   651\u2192\n   652\u2192  /**\n   653\u2192   * Adds decoded metadata. This function can only be called in\n   654\u2192   * StreamDecoderFilter::decodeHeaders/Data/Trailers(). Do not call in\n   655\u2192   * StreamDecoderFilter::decodeMetadata().\n   656\u2192   *\n   657\u2192   * @return a reference to metadata map vector, where new metadata map can be added.\n   658\u2192   */\n   659\u2192  virtual MetadataMapVector& addDecodedMetadata() PURE;\n   660\u2192\n   661\u2192  /**\n   662\u2192   * Called with 1xx headers to be encoded.\n   663\u2192   *\n   664\u2192   * Currently supported codes for this function include 100.\n   665\u2192   *\n   666\u2192   * This is not folded into encodeHeaders because most Envoy users and filters will not be proxying\n   667\u2192   * 1xx headers and with it split out, can ignore the complexity of multiple encodeHeaders calls.\n   668\u2192   *\n   669\u2192   * This is currently only called once per request but implementations should\n   670\u2192   * handle multiple calls as multiple 1xx headers are legal.\n   671\u2192   *\n   672\u2192   * @param headers supplies the headers to be encoded.\n   673\u2192   */\n   674\u2192  virtual void encode1xxHeaders(ResponseHeaderMapPtr&& headers) PURE;\n   675\u2192\n   676\u2192  /**\n   677\u2192   * Called with headers to be encoded, optionally indicating end of stream.\n   678\u2192   *\n   679\u2192   * The connection manager inspects certain pseudo headers that are not actually sent downstream.\n   680\u2192   * - See source/common/http/headers.h\n   681\u2192   *\n   682\u2192   * The only 1xx that may be provided to encodeHeaders() is a 101 upgrade, which will be the final\n   683\u2192   * encodeHeaders() for a response.\n   684\u2192   *\n   685\u2192   * @param headers supplies the headers to be encoded.\n   686\u2192   * @param end_stream supplies whether this is a header only request/response.\n   687\u2192   * @param details supplies the details of why this response was sent.\n   688\u2192   */\n   689\u2192  virtual void encodeHeaders(ResponseHeaderMapPtr&& headers, bool end_stream,\n   690\u2192                             absl::string_view details) PURE;\n   691\u2192\n   692\u2192  /**\n   693\u2192   * Called with data to be encoded, optionally indicating end of stream.\n   694\u2192   * @param data supplies the data to be encoded.\n   695\u2192   * @param end_stream supplies whether this is the last data frame.\n   696\u2192   */\n   697\u2192  virtual void encodeData(Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream) PURE;\n   698\u2192\n   699\u2192  /**\n   700\u2192   * Called with trailers to be encoded. This implicitly ends the stream.\n   701\u2192   * @param trailers supplies the trailers to encode.\n   702\u2192   */\n   703\u2192  virtual void encodeTrailers(ResponseTrailerMapPtr&& trailers) PURE;\n   704\u2192\n   705\u2192  /**\n   706\u2192   * Called with metadata to be encoded.\n   707\u2192   *\n   708\u2192   * @param metadata_map supplies the unique_ptr of the metadata to be encoded.\n   709\u2192   */\n   710\u2192  virtual void encodeMetadata(MetadataMapPtr&& metadata_map) PURE;\n   711\u2192\n   712\u2192  /**\n   713\u2192   * Called when the buffer for a decoder filter or any buffers the filter sends data to go over\n   714\u2192   * their high watermark.\n   715\u2192   *\n   716\u2192   * In the case of a filter such as the router filter, which spills into multiple buffers (codec,\n   717\u2192   * connection etc.) this may be called multiple times. Any such filter is responsible for calling\n   718\u2192   * the low watermark callbacks an equal number of times as the respective buffers are drained.\n   719\u2192   */\n   720\u2192  virtual void onDecoderFilterAboveWriteBufferHighWatermark() PURE;\n   721\u2192\n   722\u2192  /**\n   723\u2192   * Called when a decoder filter or any buffers the filter sends data to go from over its high\n   724\u2192   * watermark to under its low watermark.\n   725\u2192   */\n   726\u2192  virtual void onDecoderFilterBelowWriteBufferLowWatermark() PURE;\n   727\u2192\n   728\u2192  /**\n   729\u2192   * This routine can be called by a filter to subscribe to watermark events on the downstream\n   730\u2192   * stream and downstream connection.\n   731\u2192   *\n   732\u2192   * Immediately after subscribing, the filter will get a high watermark callback for each\n   733\u2192   * outstanding backed up buffer.\n   734\u2192   */\n   735\u2192  virtual void addDownstreamWatermarkCallbacks(DownstreamWatermarkCallbacks& callbacks) PURE;\n   736\u2192\n   737\u2192  /**\n   738\u2192   * This routine can be called by a filter to stop subscribing to watermark events on the\n   739\u2192   * downstream stream and downstream connection.\n   740\u2192   *\n   741\u2192   * It is not safe to call this from under the stack of a DownstreamWatermarkCallbacks callback.\n   742\u2192   */\n   743\u2192  virtual void removeDownstreamWatermarkCallbacks(DownstreamWatermarkCallbacks& callbacks) PURE;\n   744\u2192\n   745\u2192  /**\n   746\u2192   * This routine may be called to change the buffer limit for decoder filters.\n   747\u2192   *\n   748\u2192   * It is recommended (but not required) that filters calling this function should\n   749\u2192   * generally only perform increases to the buffer limit, to avoid potentially\n   750\u2192   * conflicting with the buffer requirements of other filters in the chain, i.e.\n   751\u2192   *\n   752\u2192   * if (desired_limit > decoderBufferLimit()) {setDecoderBufferLimit(desired_limit);}\n   753\u2192   *\n   754\u2192   * @param limit supplies the desired buffer limit.\n   755\u2192   */\n   756\u2192  virtual void setDecoderBufferLimit(uint32_t limit) PURE;\n   757\u2192\n   758\u2192  /**\n   759\u2192   * This routine returns the current buffer limit for decoder filters. Filters should abide by\n   760\u2192   * this limit or change it via setDecoderBufferLimit.\n   761\u2192   * A buffer limit of 0 bytes indicates no limits are applied.\n   762\u2192   *\n   763\u2192   * @return the buffer limit the filter should apply.\n   764\u2192   */\n   765\u2192  virtual uint32_t decoderBufferLimit() PURE;\n   766\u2192\n   767\u2192  /**\n   768\u2192   * @return the account, if any, used by this stream.\n   769\u2192   */\n   770\u2192  virtual Buffer::BufferMemoryAccountSharedPtr account() const PURE;\n   771\u2192\n   772\u2192  /**\n   773\u2192   * Takes a stream, and acts as if the headers are newly arrived.\n   774\u2192   * On success, this will result in a creating a new filter chain and likely\n   775\u2192   * upstream request associated with the original downstream stream. On\n   776\u2192   * failure, if the preconditions outlined below are not met, the caller is\n   777\u2192   * responsible for handling or terminating the original stream.\n   778\u2192   *\n   779\u2192   * This is currently limited to\n   780\u2192   *   - streams which are completely read\n   781\u2192   *   - streams which do not have a request body.\n   782\u2192   *\n   783\u2192   * Note that HttpConnectionManager sanitization will *not* be performed on the\n   784\u2192   * recreated stream, as it is assumed that sanitization has already been done.\n   785\u2192   *\n   786\u2192   * @param original_response_headers Headers used for logging in the access logs and for charging\n   787\u2192   * stats. Ignored if null.\n   788\u2192   */\n   789\u2192  virtual bool recreateStream(const ResponseHeaderMap* original_response_headers) PURE;\n   790\u2192\n   791\u2192  /**\n   792\u2192   * Adds socket options to be applied to any connections used for upstream requests. Note that\n   793\u2192   * unique values for the options will likely lead to many connection pools being created. The\n   794\u2192   * added options are appended to any previously added.\n   795\u2192   *\n   796\u2192   * @param options The options to be added.\n   797\u2192   */\n   798\u2192  virtual void addUpstreamSocketOptions(const Network::Socket::OptionsSharedPtr& options) PURE;\n   799\u2192\n   800\u2192  /**\n   801\u2192   * @return The socket options to be applied to the upstream request.\n   802\u2192   */\n   803\u2192  virtual Network::Socket::OptionsSharedPtr getUpstreamSocketOptions() const PURE;\n   804\u2192\n   805\u2192  /**\n   806\u2192   * Set override host to be used by the upstream load balancing. If the target host exists in the\n   807\u2192   * host list of the routed cluster, the host should be selected first.\n   808\u2192   * @param host The override host address.\n   809\u2192   */\n   810\u2192  virtual void setUpstreamOverrideHost(Upstream::LoadBalancerContext::OverrideHost) PURE;\n   811\u2192\n   812\u2192  /**\n   813\u2192   * @return absl::optional<absl::string_view> optional override host for the upstream\n   814\u2192   * load balancing.\n   815\u2192   */\n   816\u2192  virtual absl::optional<Upstream::LoadBalancerContext::OverrideHost>\n   817\u2192  upstreamOverrideHost() const PURE;\n   818\u2192\n   819\u2192  /**\n   820\u2192   * @return true if the filter should shed load based on the system pressure, typically memory.\n   821\u2192   */\n   822\u2192  virtual bool shouldLoadShed() const PURE;\n   823\u2192};\n   824\u2192\n   825\u2192/**\n   826\u2192 * Common base class for both decoder and encoder filters. Functions here are related to the\n   827\u2192 * lifecycle of a filter. Currently the life cycle is as follows:\n   828\u2192 * - All filters receive onStreamComplete()\n   829\u2192 * - All log handlers receive final log()\n   830\u2192 * - All filters receive onDestroy()\n   831\u2192 *\n   832\u2192 * This means:\n   833\u2192 * - onStreamComplete can be used to make state changes that are intended to appear in the final\n   834\u2192 * access logs (like streamInfo().dynamicMetadata() or streamInfo().filterState()).\n   835\u2192 * - onDestroy is used to cleanup all pending filter resources like pending http requests and\n   836\u2192 * timers.\n   837\u2192 */\n   838\u2192class StreamFilterBase {\n   839\u2192public:\n   840\u2192  virtual ~StreamFilterBase() = default;\n   841\u2192\n   842\u2192  /**\n   843\u2192   * This routine is called before the access log handlers' final log() is called. Filters can use\n   844\u2192   * this callback to enrich the data passed in to the log handlers.\n   845\u2192   */\n   846\u2192  virtual void onStreamComplete() {}\n   847\u2192\n   848\u2192  /**\n   849\u2192   * This routine is called prior to a filter being destroyed. This may happen after normal stream\n   850\u2192   * finish (both downstream and upstream) or due to reset. Every filter is responsible for making\n   851\u2192   * sure that any async events are cleaned up in the context of this routine. This includes timers,\n   852\u2192   * network calls, etc. The reason there is an onDestroy() method vs. doing this type of cleanup\n   853\u2192   * in the destructor is due to the deferred deletion model that Envoy uses to avoid stack unwind\n   854\u2192   * complications. Filters must not invoke either encoder or decoder filter callbacks after having\n   855\u2192   * onDestroy() invoked. Filters that cross-register as access log handlers receive log() before\n   856\u2192   * onDestroy().\n   857\u2192   */\n   858\u2192  virtual void onDestroy() PURE;\n   859\u2192\n   860\u2192  /**\n   861\u2192   * Called when a match result occurs that isn't handled by the filter manager.\n   862\u2192   * @param action the resulting match action\n   863\u2192   */\n   864\u2192  virtual void onMatchCallback(const Matcher::Action&) {}\n   865\u2192\n   866\u2192  struct LocalReplyData {\n   867\u2192    // The error code which (barring reset) will be sent to the client.\n   868\u2192    Http::Code code_;\n   869\u2192    // The gRPC status set in local reply.\n   870\u2192    absl::optional<Grpc::Status::GrpcStatus> grpc_status_;\n   871\u2192    // The details of why a local reply is being sent.\n   872\u2192    absl::string_view details_;\n   873\u2192    // True if a reset will occur rather than the local reply (some prior filter\n   874\u2192    // has returned ContinueAndResetStream)\n   875\u2192    bool reset_imminent_;\n   876\u2192  };\n   877\u2192\n   878\u2192  /**\n   879\u2192   * Called after sendLocalReply is called, and before any local reply is\n   880\u2192   * serialized either to filters, or downstream.\n   881\u2192   * This will be called on both encoder and decoder filters starting at the\n   882\u2192   * terminal filter (generally the router filter) and working towards the first filter configured.\n   883\u2192   *\n   884\u2192   * Note that in some circumstances, onLocalReply may be called more than once\n   885\u2192   * for a given stream, because it is possible that a filter call\n   886\u2192   * sendLocalReply while processing the original local reply response.\n   887\u2192   *\n   888\u2192   * Filters implementing onLocalReply are responsible for never calling sendLocalReply\n   889\u2192   * from onLocalReply, as that has the potential for looping.\n   890\u2192   *\n   891\u2192   * @param data data associated with the sendLocalReply call.\n   892\u2192   * @param LocalErrorStatus the action to take after onLocalReply completes.\n   893\u2192   */\n   894\u2192  virtual LocalErrorStatus onLocalReply(const LocalReplyData&) {\n   895\u2192    return LocalErrorStatus::Continue;\n   896\u2192  }\n   897\u2192};\n   898\u2192\n   899\u2192/**\n   900\u2192 * Stream decoder filter interface.\n   901\u2192 */\n   902\u2192class StreamDecoderFilter : public virtual StreamFilterBase {\n   903\u2192public:\n   904\u2192  /**\n   905\u2192   * Called with decoded headers, optionally indicating end of stream.\n   906\u2192   * @param headers supplies the decoded headers map.\n   907\u2192   * @param end_stream supplies whether this is a header only request/response.\n   908\u2192   * @return FilterHeadersStatus determines how filter chain iteration proceeds.\n   909\u2192   */\n   910\u2192  virtual FilterHeadersStatus decodeHeaders(RequestHeaderMap& headers, bool end_stream) PURE;\n   911\u2192\n   912\u2192  /**\n   913\u2192   * Called with a decoded data frame.\n   914\u2192   * @param data supplies the decoded data.\n   915\u2192   * @param end_stream supplies whether this is the last data frame.\n   916\u2192   * Further note that end_stream is only true if there are no trailers.\n   917\u2192   * @return FilterDataStatus determines how filter chain iteration proceeds.\n   918\u2192   */\n   919\u2192  virtual FilterDataStatus decodeData(Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream) PURE;\n   920\u2192\n   921\u2192  /**\n   922\u2192   * Called with decoded trailers, implicitly ending the stream.\n   923\u2192   * @param trailers supplies the decoded trailers.\n   924\u2192   */\n   925\u2192  virtual FilterTrailersStatus decodeTrailers(RequestTrailerMap& trailers) PURE;\n   926\u2192\n   927\u2192  /**\n   928\u2192   * Called with decoded metadata. Add new metadata to metadata_map directly. Do not call\n   929\u2192   * StreamDecoderFilterCallbacks::addDecodedMetadata() to add new metadata.\n   930\u2192   *\n   931\u2192   * Note: decodeMetadata() currently cannot stop the filter iteration, and always returns Continue.\n   932\u2192   * That means metadata will go through the complete filter chain at once, even if the other frame\n   933\u2192   * types return StopIteration. If metadata should not pass through all filters at once, users\n   934\u2192   * should consider using StopAllIterationAndBuffer or StopAllIterationAndWatermark in\n   935\u2192   * decodeHeaders() to prevent metadata passing to the following filters.\n   936\u2192   *\n   937\u2192   * @param metadata_map supplies the decoded metadata.\n   938\u2192   */\n   939\u2192  virtual FilterMetadataStatus decodeMetadata(MetadataMap& /* metadata_map */) {\n   940\u2192    return Http::FilterMetadataStatus::Continue;\n   941\u2192  }\n   942\u2192\n   943\u2192  /**\n   944\u2192   * Called by the filter manager once to initialize the filter decoder callbacks that the\n   945\u2192   * filter should use. Callbacks will not be invoked by the filter after onDestroy() is called.\n   946\u2192   */\n   947\u2192  virtual void setDecoderFilterCallbacks(StreamDecoderFilterCallbacks& callbacks) PURE;\n   948\u2192\n   949\u2192  /**\n   950\u2192   * Called at the end of the stream, when all data has been decoded.\n   951\u2192   */\n   952\u2192  virtual void decodeComplete() {}\n   953\u2192};\n   954\u2192\n   955\u2192using StreamDecoderFilterSharedPtr = std::shared_ptr<StreamDecoderFilter>;\n   956\u2192\n   957\u2192/**\n   958\u2192 * Stream encoder filter callbacks add additional callbacks that allow a encoding filter to restart\n   959\u2192 * encoding if they decide to hold data (e.g. for buffering or rate limiting).\n   960\u2192 */\n   961\u2192class StreamEncoderFilterCallbacks : public virtual StreamFilterCallbacks {\n   962\u2192public:\n   963\u2192  /**\n   964\u2192   * Continue iterating through the filter chain with buffered headers and body data. This routine\n   965\u2192   * can only be called if the filter has previously returned StopIteration from encodeHeaders() AND\n   966\u2192   * one of StopIterationAndBuffer, StopIterationAndWatermark, or StopIterationNoBuffer\n   967\u2192   * from each previous call to encodeData().\n   968\u2192   *\n   969\u2192   * The connection manager will dispatch headers and any buffered body data to the next filter in\n   970\u2192   * the chain. Further note that if the response is not complete, this filter will still receive\n   971\u2192   * encodeData() calls and must return an appropriate status code depending on what the filter\n   972\u2192   * needs to do.\n   973\u2192   */\n   974\u2192  virtual void continueEncoding() PURE;\n   975\u2192\n   976\u2192  /**\n   977\u2192   * @return const Buffer::Instance* the currently buffered data as buffered by this filter or\n   978\u2192   *         previous ones in the filter chain. May be nullptr if nothing has been buffered yet.\n   979\u2192   */\n   980\u2192  virtual const Buffer::Instance* encodingBuffer() PURE;\n   981\u2192\n   982\u2192  /**\n   983\u2192   * Allows modifying the encoding buffer. May only be called before any data has been continued\n   984\u2192   * past the calling filter.\n   985\u2192   */\n   986\u2192  virtual void modifyEncodingBuffer(std::function<void(Buffer::Instance&)> callback) PURE;\n   987\u2192\n   988\u2192  /**\n   989\u2192   * Add buffered body data. This method is used in advanced cases where returning\n   990\u2192   * StopIterationAndBuffer from encodeData() is not sufficient.\n   991\u2192   *\n   992\u2192   * 1) If a headers only response needs to be turned into a response with a body, this method can\n   993\u2192   * be called to add body in the encodeHeaders() callback. Subsequent filters will receive\n   994\u2192   * encodeHeaders(..., false) followed by encodeData(..., true). This works both in the direct\n   995\u2192   * iteration as well as the continuation case.\n   996\u2192   *\n   997\u2192   * 2) If a filter is going to look at all buffered data from within a data callback with end\n   998\u2192   * stream set, this method can be called to immediately buffer the data. This avoids having\n   999\u2192   * to deal with the existing buffered data and the data from the current callback.\n  1000\u2192   *\n  1001\u2192   * 3) If additional buffered body data needs to be added by a filter before continuation of data\n  1002\u2192   * to further filters (outside of callback context).\n  1003\u2192   *\n  1004\u2192   * 4) If additional data needs to be added in the encodeTrailers() callback, this method can be\n  1005\u2192   * called in the context of the callback. All further filters will receive encodeData(..., false)\n  1006\u2192   * followed by encodeTrailers(). However if the iteration is stopped, the added data will\n  1007\u2192   * buffered, so that the further filters will not receive encodeData() before encodeHeaders().\n  1008\u2192   *\n  1009\u2192   * It is an error to call this method in any other case.\n  1010\u2192   *\n  1011\u2192   * See also injectEncodedDataToFilterChain() for a different way of passing data to further\n  1012\u2192   * filters and also how the two methods are different.\n  1013\u2192   *\n  1014\u2192   * @param data Buffer::Instance supplies the data to be encoded.\n  1015\u2192   * @param streaming_filter boolean supplies if this filter streams data or buffers the full body.\n  1016\u2192   */\n  1017\u2192  virtual void addEncodedData(Buffer::Instance& data, bool streaming_filter) PURE;\n  1018\u2192\n  1019\u2192  /**\n  1020\u2192   * Encode data directly to subsequent filters in the filter chain. This method is used in\n  1021\u2192   * advanced cases in which a filter needs full control over how subsequent filters view data,\n  1022\u2192   * and does not want to make use of HTTP connection manager buffering. Using this method allows\n  1023\u2192   * a filter to buffer data (or not) and then periodically inject data to subsequent filters,\n  1024\u2192   * indicating end_stream at an appropriate time. This can be used to implement rate limiting,\n  1025\u2192   * periodic data emission, etc.\n  1026\u2192   *\n  1027\u2192   * This method should only be called outside of callback context. I.e., do not call this method\n  1028\u2192   * from within a filter's encodeData() call.\n  1029\u2192   *\n  1030\u2192   * When using this callback, filters should generally only return\n  1031\u2192   * FilterDataStatus::StopIterationNoBuffer from their encodeData() call, since use of this method\n  1032\u2192   * indicates that a filter does not wish to participate in standard HTTP connection manager\n  1033\u2192   * buffering and continuation and will perform any necessary buffering and continuation on its\n  1034\u2192   * own.\n  1035\u2192   *\n  1036\u2192   * This callback is different from addEncodedData() in that the specified data and end_stream\n  1037\u2192   * status will be propagated directly to further filters in the filter chain. This is different\n  1038\u2192   * from addEncodedData() where data is added to the HTTP connection manager's buffered data with\n  1039\u2192   * the assumption that standard HTTP connection manager buffering and continuation are being used.\n  1040\u2192   */\n  1041\u2192  virtual void injectEncodedDataToFilterChain(Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream) PURE;\n  1042\u2192\n  1043\u2192  /**\n  1044\u2192   * Adds encoded trailers. May only be called in encodeData when end_stream is set to true.\n  1045\u2192   * If called in any other context, an assertion will be triggered.\n  1046\u2192   *\n  1047\u2192   * When called in encodeData, the trailers map will be initialized to an empty map and returned by\n  1048\u2192   * reference. Calling this function more than once is invalid.\n  1049\u2192   *\n  1050\u2192   * @return a reference to the newly created trailers map.\n  1051\u2192   */\n  1052\u2192  virtual ResponseTrailerMap& addEncodedTrailers() PURE;\n  1053\u2192\n  1054\u2192  /**\n  1055\u2192   * Attempts to create a locally generated response using the provided response_code and body_text\n  1056\u2192   * parameters. If the request was a gRPC request the local reply will be encoded as a gRPC\n  1057\u2192   * response with a 200 HTTP response code and grpc-status and grpc-message headers mapped from the\n  1058\u2192   * provided parameters.\n  1059\u2192   *\n  1060\u2192   * If a response has already started (e.g. if the router calls sendLocalReply after encoding\n  1061\u2192   * headers) this will either ship the reply directly to the downstream codec, or reset the stream.\n  1062\u2192   *\n  1063\u2192   * @param response_code supplies the HTTP response code.\n  1064\u2192   * @param body_text supplies the optional body text which is sent using the text/plain content\n  1065\u2192   *                  type, or encoded in the grpc-message header.\n  1066\u2192   * @param modify_headers supplies an optional callback function that can modify the\n  1067\u2192   *                       response headers.\n  1068\u2192   * @param grpc_status the gRPC status code to override the httpToGrpcStatus mapping with.\n  1069\u2192   * @param details a string detailing why this local reply was sent.\n  1070\u2192   */\n  1071\u2192  virtual void sendLocalReply(Code response_code, absl::string_view body_text,\n  1072\u2192                              std::function<void(ResponseHeaderMap& headers)> modify_headers,\n  1073\u2192                              const absl::optional<Grpc::Status::GrpcStatus> grpc_status,\n  1074\u2192                              absl::string_view details) PURE;\n  1075\u2192  /**\n  1076\u2192   * Adds new metadata to be encoded.\n  1077\u2192   *\n  1078\u2192   * @param metadata_map supplies the unique_ptr of the metadata to be encoded.\n  1079\u2192   */\n  1080\u2192  virtual void addEncodedMetadata(MetadataMapPtr&& metadata_map) PURE;\n  1081\u2192\n  1082\u2192  /**\n  1083\u2192   * Called when an encoder filter goes over its high watermark.\n  1084\u2192   */\n  1085\u2192  virtual void onEncoderFilterAboveWriteBufferHighWatermark() PURE;\n  1086\u2192\n  1087\u2192  /**\n  1088\u2192   * Called when a encoder filter goes from over its high watermark to under its low watermark.\n  1089\u2192   */\n  1090\u2192  virtual void onEncoderFilterBelowWriteBufferLowWatermark() PURE;\n  1091\u2192\n  1092\u2192  /**\n  1093\u2192   * This routine may be called to change the buffer limit for encoder filters.\n  1094\u2192   *\n  1095\u2192   * It is recommended (but not required) that filters calling this function should\n  1096\u2192   * generally only perform increases to the buffer limit, to avoid potentially\n  1097\u2192   * conflicting with the buffer requirements of other filters in the chain, i.e.\n  1098\u2192   *\n  1099\u2192   * if (desired_limit > encoderBufferLimit()) {setEncoderBufferLimit(desired_limit);}\n  1100\u2192   *\n  1101\u2192   * @param limit supplies the desired buffer limit.\n  1102\u2192   */\n  1103\u2192  virtual void setEncoderBufferLimit(uint32_t limit) PURE;\n  1104\u2192\n  1105\u2192  /**\n  1106\u2192   * This routine returns the current buffer limit for encoder filters. Filters should abide by\n  1107\u2192   * this limit or change it via setEncoderBufferLimit.\n  1108\u2192   * A buffer limit of 0 bytes indicates no limits are applied.\n  1109\u2192   *\n  1110\u2192   * @return the buffer limit the filter should apply.\n  1111\u2192   */\n  1112\u2192  virtual uint32_t encoderBufferLimit() PURE;\n  1113\u2192};\n  1114\u2192\n  1115\u2192/**\n  1116\u2192 * Stream encoder filter interface.\n  1117\u2192 */\n  1118\u2192class StreamEncoderFilter : public virtual StreamFilterBase {\n  1119\u2192public:\n  1120\u2192  /**\n  1121\u2192   * Called with supported 1xx headers.\n  1122\u2192   *\n  1123\u2192   * This is not folded into encodeHeaders because most Envoy users and filters\n  1124\u2192   * will not be proxying 1xxs and with it split out, can ignore the\n  1125\u2192   * complexity of multiple encodeHeaders calls.\n  1126\u2192   *\n  1127\u2192   * This will only be invoked once per request.\n  1128\u2192   *\n  1129\u2192   * @param headers supplies the 1xx response headers to be encoded.\n  1130\u2192   * @return Filter1xxHeadersStatus determines how filter chain iteration proceeds.\n  1131\u2192   *\n  1132\u2192   */\n  1133\u2192  virtual Filter1xxHeadersStatus encode1xxHeaders(ResponseHeaderMap& headers) PURE;\n  1134\u2192\n  1135\u2192  /**\n  1136\u2192   * Called with headers to be encoded, optionally indicating end of stream.\n  1137\u2192   *\n  1138\u2192   * The only 1xx that may be provided to encodeHeaders() is a 101 upgrade, which will be the final\n  1139\u2192   * encodeHeaders() for a response.\n  1140\u2192   *\n  1141\u2192   * @param headers supplies the headers to be encoded.\n  1142\u2192   * @param end_stream supplies whether this is a header only request/response.\n  1143\u2192   * @return FilterHeadersStatus determines how filter chain iteration proceeds.\n  1144\u2192   */\n  1145\u2192  virtual FilterHeadersStatus encodeHeaders(ResponseHeaderMap& headers, bool end_stream) PURE;\n  1146\u2192\n  1147\u2192  /**\n  1148\u2192   * Called with data to be encoded, optionally indicating end of stream.\n  1149\u2192   * @param data supplies the data to be encoded.\n  1150\u2192   * @param end_stream supplies whether this is the last data frame.\n  1151\u2192   * Further note that end_stream is only true if there are no trailers.\n  1152\u2192   * @return FilterDataStatus determines how filter chain iteration proceeds.\n  1153\u2192   */\n  1154\u2192  virtual FilterDataStatus encodeData(Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream) PURE;\n  1155\u2192\n  1156\u2192  /**\n  1157\u2192   * Called with trailers to be encoded, implicitly ending the stream.\n  1158\u2192   * @param trailers supplies the trailers to be encoded.\n  1159\u2192   */\n  1160\u2192  virtual FilterTrailersStatus encodeTrailers(ResponseTrailerMap& trailers) PURE;\n  1161\u2192\n  1162\u2192  /**\n  1163\u2192   * Called with metadata to be encoded. New metadata should be added directly to metadata_map. DO\n  1164\u2192   * NOT call StreamDecoderFilterCallbacks::encodeMetadata() interface to add new metadata.\n  1165\u2192   *\n  1166\u2192   * @param metadata_map supplies the metadata to be encoded.\n  1167\u2192   * @return FilterMetadataStatus, which currently is always FilterMetadataStatus::Continue;\n  1168\u2192   */\n  1169\u2192  virtual FilterMetadataStatus encodeMetadata(MetadataMap& metadata_map) PURE;\n  1170\u2192\n  1171\u2192  /**\n  1172\u2192   * Called by the filter manager once to initialize the filter callbacks that the filter should\n  1173\u2192   * use. Callbacks will not be invoked by the filter after onDestroy() is called.\n  1174\u2192   */\n  1175\u2192  virtual void setEncoderFilterCallbacks(StreamEncoderFilterCallbacks& callbacks) PURE;\n  1176\u2192\n  1177\u2192  /**\n  1178\u2192   * Called at the end of the stream, when all data has been encoded.\n  1179\u2192   */\n  1180\u2192  virtual void encodeComplete() {}\n  1181\u2192};\n  1182\u2192\n  1183\u2192using StreamEncoderFilterSharedPtr = std::shared_ptr<StreamEncoderFilter>;\n  1184\u2192\n  1185\u2192/**\n  1186\u2192 * A filter that handles both encoding and decoding.\n  1187\u2192 */\n  1188\u2192class StreamFilter : public virtual StreamDecoderFilter, public virtual StreamEncoderFilter {};\n  1189\u2192\n  1190\u2192using StreamFilterSharedPtr = std::shared_ptr<StreamFilter>;\n  1191\u2192\n  1192\u2192class HttpMatchingData {\n  1193\u2192public:\n  1194\u2192  static absl::string_view name() { return \"http\"; }\n  1195\u2192\n  1196\u2192  virtual ~HttpMatchingData() = default;\n  1197\u2192\n  1198\u2192  virtual RequestHeaderMapOptConstRef requestHeaders() const PURE;\n  1199\u2192  virtual RequestTrailerMapOptConstRef requestTrailers() const PURE;\n  1200\u2192  virtual ResponseHeaderMapOptConstRef responseHeaders() const PURE;\n  1201\u2192  virtual ResponseTrailerMapOptConstRef responseTrailers() const PURE;\n  1202\u2192  virtual const StreamInfo::StreamInfo& streamInfo() const PURE;\n  1203\u2192  virtual const Network::ConnectionInfoProvider& connectionInfoProvider() const PURE;\n  1204\u2192\n  1205\u2192  const StreamInfo::FilterState& filterState() const { return streamInfo().filterState(); }\n  1206\u2192  const envoy::config::core::v3::Metadata& metadata() const {\n  1207\u2192    return streamInfo().dynamicMetadata();\n  1208\u2192  }\n  1209\u2192\n  1210\u2192  const Network::Address::Instance& localAddress() const {\n  1211\u2192    return *connectionInfoProvider().localAddress();\n  1212\u2192  }\n  1213\u2192\n  1214\u2192  const Network::Address::Instance& remoteAddress() const {\n  1215\u2192    return *connectionInfoProvider().remoteAddress();\n  1216\u2192  }\n  1217\u2192\n  1218\u2192  Ssl::ConnectionInfoConstSharedPtr ssl() const { return connectionInfoProvider().sslConnection(); }\n  1219\u2192};\n  1220\u2192\n  1221\u2192/**\n  1222\u2192 * These callbacks are provided by the connection manager to the factory so that the factory can\n  1223\u2192 * build the filter chain in an application specific way.\n  1224\u2192 */\n  1225\u2192class FilterChainFactoryCallbacks {\n  1226\u2192public:\n  1227\u2192  virtual ~FilterChainFactoryCallbacks() = default;\n  1228\u2192\n  1229\u2192  /**\n  1230\u2192   * Add a decoder filter that is used when reading stream data.\n  1231\u2192   * @param filter supplies the filter to add.\n  1232\u2192   */\n  1233\u2192  virtual void addStreamDecoderFilter(Http::StreamDecoderFilterSharedPtr filter) PURE;\n  1234\u2192\n  1235\u2192  /**\n  1236\u2192   * Add an encoder filter that is used when writing stream data.\n  1237\u2192   * @param filter supplies the filter to add.\n  1238\u2192   */\n  1239\u2192  virtual void addStreamEncoderFilter(Http::StreamEncoderFilterSharedPtr filter) PURE;\n  1240\u2192\n  1241\u2192  /**\n  1242\u2192   * Add a decoder/encoder filter that is used both when reading and writing stream data.\n  1243\u2192   * @param filter supplies the filter to add.\n  1244\u2192   */\n  1245\u2192  virtual void addStreamFilter(Http::StreamFilterSharedPtr filter) PURE;\n  1246\u2192\n  1247\u2192  /**\n  1248\u2192   * Add an access log handler that is called when the stream is destroyed.\n  1249\u2192   * @param handler supplies the handler to add.\n  1250\u2192   */\n  1251\u2192  virtual void addAccessLogHandler(AccessLog::InstanceSharedPtr handler) PURE;\n  1252\u2192\n  1253\u2192  /**\n  1254\u2192   * Allows filters to access the thread local dispatcher.\n  1255\u2192   * @param return the worker thread's dispatcher.\n  1256\u2192   */\n  1257\u2192  virtual Event::Dispatcher& dispatcher() PURE;\n  1258\u2192};\n  1259\u2192} // namespace Http\n  1260\u2192} // namespace Envoy\n  1261\u2192"

for the connection implementation.\n    29\u2192 */\n    30\u2192class ConnectionImplUtility {\n    31\u2192public:\n    32\u2192  /**\n    33\u2192   * Update the buffer stats for a connection.\n total buffer size. previous_total will be\n    37\u2192   *        updated to new_total when the call is complete.\n    38\u2192   * @param stat_total supplies the counter to increment with the delta.\n    39\u2192   * @param stat_current supplies the gauge that should be updated with the delta of previous_total\n    40\u2192   *        and new_total.\n    41\u2192   */\n    42\u2192  static 50\u2192class ConnectionImpl : public ConnectionImplBase, public TransportSocketCallbacks {\n    51\u2192public:\n    52\u2192  ConnectionImpl(Event::Dispatcher& dispatcher, ConnectionSocketPtr&& socket,\n    53\u2192                 TransportSocketPtr&& transport_socket, StreamInfo::StreamInfo& stream_info,\n    54\u2192                 bool connected);\n    55\u2192\n    56\u2192  ~ConnectionImpl() override;\n    57\u2192\n    58\u2192  // Network::FilterManager\n    59\u2192  void addWriteFilter(WriteFilterSharedPtr filter) override;\n    60\u2192  void addFilter(FilterSharedPtr filter) override;\n    61\u2192  void addReadFilter(ReadFilterSharedPtr filter) override;\n    62\u2192  void removeReadFilter(ReadFilterSharedPtr filter) override;\n    63\u2192  bool initializeReadFilters() override;\n    64\u2192\n    65\u2192  // Network::Connection\n    66\u2192  void addBytesSentCallback(BytesSentCb cb) override;\n    67\u2192  void enableHalfClose(bool enabled) override;\n    68\u2192  bool isHalfCloseEnabled() const override { return enable_half_close_; }\n    69\u2192  void close(ConnectionCloseType type) final;\n    70\u2192  void close(ConnectionCloseType type, absl::string_view details) override {\n    71\u2192    if (!details.empty()) {\n    72\u2192      setLocalCloseReason(details);\n    73\u2192    }\n    74\u2192    close(type);\n    75\u2192  }\n    76\u2192\n    77\u2192  std::string nextProtocol() const override { return transport_socket_->protocol(); }\n    78\u2192  void noDelay(bool enable) override;\n    79\u2192  ReadDisableStatus readDisable(bool disable) override;\n    80\u2192  void detectEarlyCloseWhenReadDisabled(bool value) override { detect_early_close_ = value; }\n    81\u2192  bool readEnabled() const override;\n    82\u2192  ConnectionInfoSetter& connectionInfoSetter() override {\n    83\u2192    return socket_->connectionInfoProvider();\n    84\u2192  }\n    85\u2192  const ConnectionInfoProvider& connectionInfoProvider() const override {\n    86\u2192    return socket_->connectionInfoProvider();\n    87\u2192  }\n    88\u2192  ConnectionInfoProviderSharedPtr connectionInfoProviderSharedPtr() const override {\n    89\u2192    return socket_->connectionInfoProviderSharedPtr();\n    90\u2192  }\n    91\u2192  absl::optional<UnixDomainSocketPeerCredentials> unixSocketPeerCredentials() const override;\n    92\u2192  Ssl::ConnectionInfoConstSharedPtr ssl() const override {\n    93\u2192    // SSL info may be overwritten by a filter in the provider.\n    94\u2192    return socket_->connectionInfoProvider().sslConnection();\n    95\u2192  }\n    96\u2192  State state() const override;\n    97\u2192  bool connecting() const override {\n    98\u2192    ENVOY_CONN_LOG_EVENT(debug, \"connection_connecting_state\", \"current connecting state: {}\",\n    99\u2192                         *this, connecting_);\n   100\u2192    return connecting_;"

generateStats(const std::string& prefix, Stats::Scope& scope);\n    76\u2192  static ConnectionManagerTracingStats generateTracingStats(const std::string& prefix,\n    77\u2192
