when either:\n    85\u2192  //   a) onConnection for H3.\n    86\u2192  //   b) onData for H1 and H2.\n    87\u2192  // With the introduction of ApiListeners, neither event occurs. This function allows consumer code\n    88\u2192  // to manually create a codec.\n    89\u2192  // TODO(junr03): consider passing a synthetic codec instead of creating once. The codec in the\n    90\u2192  // ApiListener case is solely used to determine the protocol version.\n    91\u2192  void createCodec(Buffer::Instance& data);\n    92\u2192\n    93\u2192  // Network::ReadFilter\n    94\u2192  Network::FilterStatus onData(Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream) override;\n    95\u2192  Network::FilterStatus onNewConnection() override;\n    96\u2192  void initializeReadFilterCallbacks(Network::ReadFilterCallbacks& callbacks) override;\n    97\u2192\n    98\u2192  // Http::ConnectionCallbacks\n    99\u2192  void onGoAway(GoAwayErrorCode error_code) override;\n   100\u2192\n   101\u2192  // Http::ServerConnectionCallbacks\n   102\u2192  RequestDecoder& newStream(ResponseEncoder& response_encoder,\n   103\u2192                            bool is_internally_created = false) override;\n   104\u2192\n   105\u2192  RequestDecoderHandlePtr newStreamHandle(ResponseEncoder& response_encoder,\n   106\u2192                                          bool is_internally_created = false) override;\n   107\u2192\n   108\u2192  // Network::ConnectionCallbacks\n   109\u2192  void onEvent(Network::ConnectionEvent event) override;\n   110\u2192  // Pass connection watermark events on to all the streams associated with that connection.\n   111\u2192  void onAboveWriteBufferHighWatermark() override {\n   112\u2192    codec_->onUnderlyingConnectionAboveWriteBufferHighWatermark();\n   113\u2192  }\n   114\u2192  void onBelowWriteBufferLowWatermark() override {\n   115\u2192    codec_->onUnderlyingConnectionBelowWriteBufferLowWatermark();\n   116\u2192  }\n   117\u2192\n   118\u2192  TimeSource& timeSource() { return time_source_; }\n   119\u2192\n   120\u2192  void setClearHopByHopResponseHeaders(bool value) { clear_hop_by_hop_response_headers_ = value; }\n   121\u2192  bool clearHopByHopResponseHeaders() const { return clear_hop_by_hop_response_headers_; }\n   122\u2192\n   123\u2192  // This runtime key configures the number of streams which must be closed on a connection before\n   124\u2192  // envoy will potentially drain a connection due to excessive prematurely reset streams.\n   125\u2192  static const absl::string_view PrematureResetTotalStreamCountKey;\n   126\u2192\n   127\u2192  // The minimum lifetime of a stream, in seconds, in order not to be considered\n   128\u2192  // prematurely closed.\n   129\u2192  static const absl::string_view PrematureResetMinStreamLifetimeSecondsKey;\n   130\u2192  static const absl::string_view MaxRequestsPerIoCycle;\n   131\u2192  static const absl::string_view OptionallyDelayClose;\n   132\u2192\n   133\u2192private:\n   134\u2192  struct ActiveStream;\n   135\u2192  class MobileConnectionManagerImpl;\n   136\u2192\n   137\u2192  /**\n   138\u2192   * Wraps a single active stream on the connection. These are either full request/response pairs\n   139\u2192   * or pushes.\n   140\u2192   */\n   141\u2192  struct ActiveStream final : LinkedObject<ActiveStream>,\n   142\u2192                              public Event::DeferredDeletable,\n   143\u2192                              public StreamCallbacks,\n   144\u2192                              public CodecEventCallbacks,\n   145\u2192                              public RequestDecoder,\n   146\u2192                              public Tracing::Config,\n   147\u2192                              public ScopeTrackedObject,\n   148\u2192                              public FilterManagerCallbacks,\n   149\u2192                              public DownstreamStreamFilterCallbacks,\n   150\u2192                              public RouteCache {\n   151\u2192    ActiveStream(ConnectionManagerImpl& connection_manager, uint32_t buffer_limit,\n   152\u2192                 Buffer::BufferMemoryAccountSharedPtr account);\n   153\u2192\n   154\u2192    // Event::DeferredDeletable\n   155\u2192    void deleteIsPending() override {\n   156\u2192      // The stream should not be accessed once deferred delete has been called.\n   157\u2192      still_alive_.reset();\n   158\u2192    }\n   159\u2192\n   160\u2192    void log(AccessLog::AccessLogType type);\n   161\u2192    void completeRequest();\n   162\u2192\n   163\u2192    const Network::Connection* connection();\n   164\u2192    uint64_t streamId() { return stream_id_; }\n   165\u2192\n   166\u2192    // Http::StreamCallbacks\n   167\u2192    void onResetStream(StreamResetReason reason,\n   168\u2192                       absl::string_view transport_failure_reason) override;\n   169\u2192    void onAboveWriteBufferHighWatermark() override;\n   170\u2192    void onBelowWriteBufferLowWatermark() override;\n   171\u2192\n   172\u2192    // Http::CodecEventCallbacks\n   173\u2192    void onCodecEncodeComplete() override;\n   174\u2192    void onCodecLowLevelReset() override;\n   175\u2192\n   176\u2192    // Http::StreamDecoder\n   177\u2192    void decodeData(Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream) override;\n   178\u2192    void decodeMetadata(MetadataMapPtr&&) override;\n   179\u2192\n   180\u2192    // Record the timestamp of last downstream byte is received.\n   181\u2192    void maybeRecordLastByteReceived(bool end_stream);\n   182\u2192\n   183\u2192    // Http::RequestDecoder\n   184\u2192    void decodeHeaders(RequestHeaderMapSharedPtr&& headers, bool end_stream) override;\n   185\u2192    void decodeTrailers(RequestTrailerMapPtr&& trailers) override;\n   186\u2192    StreamInfo::StreamInfo& streamInfo() override { return filter_manager_.streamInfo(); }\n   187\u2192    void sendLocalReply(Code code, absl::string_view body,\n   188\u2192                        const std::function<void(ResponseHeaderMap& headers)>& modify_headers,\n   189\u2192                        const absl::optional<Grpc::Status::GrpcStatus> grpc_status,\n   190\u2192                        absl::string_view details) override {\n   191\u2192      return filter_manager_.sendLocalReply(code, body, modify_headers, grpc_status, details);\n   192\u2192    }\n   193\u2192\n   194\u2192    void sendGoAwayAndClose() override { return connection_manager_.sendGoAwayAndClose(); }\n   195\u2192\n   196\u2192    AccessLog::InstanceSharedPtrVector accessLogHandlers() override {\n   197\u2192      const AccessLog::InstanceSharedPtrVector& config_log_handlers =\n   198\u2192          connection_manager_.config_->accessLogs();\n   199\u2192      const AccessLog::InstanceSharedPtrVector& filter_log_handlers =\n   200\u2192          filter_manager_.accessLogHandlers();"

"     1\u2192#include \"source/common/network/filter_manager_impl.h\"\n     2\u2192\n     3\u2192#include <list>\n     4\u2192\n     5\u2192#include \"envoy/network/connection.h\"\n     6\u2192\n     7\u2192#include \"source/common/common/assert.h\"\n     8\u2192#include \"source/common/runtime/runtime_features.h\"\n     9\u2192\n    10\u2192namespace Envoy {\n    11\u2192namespace Network {\n    12\u2192\n    13\u2192void FilterManagerImpl::addWriteFilter(WriteFilterSharedPtr filter) {\n    14\u2192  ASSERT(connection_.state() == Connection::State::Open);\n    15\u2192  ActiveWriteFilterPtr new_filter = std::make_unique<ActiveWriteFilter>(*this, filter);\n    16\u2192  filter->initializeWriteFilterCallbacks(*new_filter);\n    17\u2192  LinkedList::moveIntoList(std::move(new_filter), downstream_filters_);\n    18\u2192}\n    19\u2192\n    20\u2192void FilterManagerImpl::addFilter(FilterSharedPtr filter) {\n    21\u2192  addReadFilter(filter);\n    22\u2192  addWriteFilter(filter);\n    23\u2192}\n    24\u2192\n    25\u2192void FilterManagerImpl::addReadFilter(ReadFilterSharedPtr filter) {\n    26\u2192  ASSERT(connection_.state() == Connection::State::Open);\n    27\u2192  ActiveReadFilterPtr new_filter = std::make_unique<ActiveReadFilter>(*this, filter);\n    28\u2192  filter->initializeReadFilterCallbacks(*new_filter);\n    29\u2192  LinkedList::moveIntoListBack(std::move(new_filter), upstream_filters_);\n    30\u2192}\n    31\u2192\n    32\u2192void FilterManagerImpl::removeReadFilter(ReadFilterSharedPtr filter_to_remove) {\n    33\u2192  // For perf/safety reasons, null this out rather than removing.\n    34\u2192  for (auto& filter : upstream_filters_) {\n    35\u2192    if (filter->filter_ == filter_to_remove) {\n    36\u2192      filter->filter_ = nullptr;\n    37\u2192    }\n    38\u2192  }\n    39\u2192}\n    40\u2192\n    41\u2192bool FilterManagerImpl::initializeReadFilters() {\n    42\u2192  if (upstream_filters_.empty()) {\n    43\u2192    return false;\n    44\u2192  }\n    45\u2192\n    46\u2192  // Initialize read filters without calling onData() afterwards.\n    47\u2192  // This is called just after an connection has been established and nothing may have been read\n    48\u2192  // yet. onData() will be called separately as data is read from the connection.\n    49\u2192  for (auto& entry : upstream_filters_) {\n    50\u2192    if (entry->filter_ && !entry->initialized_) {\n    51\u2192      entry->initialized_ = true;\n    52\u2192      FilterStatus status = entry->filter_->onNewConnection();\n    53\u2192      if (status == FilterStatus::StopIteration || connection_.state() != Connection::State::Open) {\n    54\u2192        break;\n    55\u2192      }\n    56\u2192    }\n    57\u2192  }\n    58\u2192\n    59\u2192  return true;\n    60\u2192}\n    61\u2192\n    62\u2192void FilterManagerImpl::onContinueReading(ActiveReadFilter* filter,\n    63\u2192                                          ReadBufferSource& buffer_source) {\n    64\u2192  // Filter could return status == FilterStatus::StopIteration immediately, close the connection and\n    65\u2192  // use callback to call this function.\n    66\u2192  if (connection_.state() != Connection::State::Open) {\n    67\u2192    return;\n    68\u2192  }\n    69\u2192\n    70\u2192  std::list<ActiveReadFilterPtr>::iterator entry;\n    71\u2192  if (!filter) {\n    72\u2192    connection_.streamInfo().addBytesReceived(buffer_source.getReadBuffer().buffer.length());\n    73\u2192    entry = upstream_filters_.begin();\n    74\u2192  } else {\n    75\u2192    entry = std::next(filter->entry());\n    76\u2192  }\n    77\u2192\n    78\u2192  for (; entry != upstream_filters_.end(); entry++) {\n    79\u2192    if (!(*entry)->filter_) {\n    80\u2192      continue;\n    81\u2192    }\n    82\u2192    if (!(*entry)->initialized_) {\n    83\u2192      (*entry)->initialized_ = true;\n    84\u2192      FilterStatus status = (*entry)->filter_->onNewConnection();\n    85\u2192      if (status == FilterStatus::StopIteration || connection_.state() != Connection::State::Open) {\n    86\u2192        return;\n    87\u2192      }\n    88\u2192    }\n    89\u2192\n    90\u2192    StreamBuffer read_buffer = buffer_source.getReadBuffer();\n    91\u2192    if (read_buffer.buffer.length() > 0 || read_buffer.end_stream) {\n    92\u2192      FilterStatus status = (*entry)->filter_->onData(read_buffer.buffer, read_buffer.end_stream);\n    93\u2192      if (status == FilterStatus::StopIteration || connection_.state() != Connection::State::Open) {\n    94\u2192        return;\n    95\u2192      }\n    96\u2192    }\n    97\u2192  }\n    98\u2192}\n    99\u2192\n   100\u2192void FilterManagerImpl::onRead() {"

TcpProxy {\n    44\u2192\n    45\u2192constexpr absl::string_view PerConnectionIdleTimeoutMs =\n    46\u2192    \"envoy.tcp_proxy.per_connection_idle_timeout_ms\";\n    47\u2192/**\n    48\u2192 * ReceiveBeforeConnectKey is the key for the receive_before_connect filter state. The\n    49\u2192 * filter state value is a ``StreamInfo::BoolAccessor`` indicating whether the\n    50\u2192 * receive_before_connect functionality should be enabled. Network filters setting this filter\n    51\u2192 * state should return `StopIteration` in their `onNewConnection` and `onData` methods until they\n    52\u2192 * have read the data they need before the upstream connection establishment, and only then allow\n    53\u2192 * the filter chain to proceed to the TCP_PROXY filter.\n    54\u2192 */\n    55\u2192constexpr absl::string_view ReceiveBeforeConnectKey = \"envoy.tcp_proxy.receive_before_connect\";\n    56\u2192\n    57\u2192/**\n    58\u2192 * All tcp proxy stats. @see stats_macros.h\n    59\u2192 */\n    60\u2192#define ALL_TCP_PROXY_STATS(COUNTER, GAUGE)                                                        \\\n    61\u2192  COUNTER(downstream_cx_no_route)                                                                  \\\n    62\u2192  COUNTER(downstream_cx_rx_bytes_total)                                                            \\\n    63\u2192  COUNTER(downstream_cx_total)                                                                     \\\n    64\u2192  COUNTER(downstream_cx_tx_bytes_total)                                                            \\\n    65\u2192  COUNTER(downstream_flow_control_paused_reading_total)                                            \\\n    66\u2192  COUNTER(downstream_flow_control_resumed_reading_total)                                           \\\n    67\u2192  COUNTER(early_data_received_count_total)                                                         \\\n    68\u2192  COUNTER(idle_timeout)                                                                            \\\n    69\u2192  COUNTER(max_downstream_connection_duration)                                                      \\\n    70\u2192  COUNTER(upstream_flush_total)                                                                    \\\n    71\u2192  GAUGE(downstream_cx_rx_bytes_buffered, Accumulate)                                               \\\n    72\u2192  GAUGE(downstream_cx_tx_bytes_buffered, Accumulate)                                               \\\n    73\u2192  GAUGE(upstream_flush_active, Accumulate)\n    74\u2192\n    75\u2192/**\n    76\u2192 * Tcp proxy stats for on-demand. These stats are generated only if the tcp proxy enables on demand.\n    77\u2192 */\n    78\u2192#define ON_DEMAND_TCP_PROXY_STATS(COUNTER)                                                         \\\n    79\u2192  COUNTER(on_demand_cluster_attempt)                                                               \\\n    80\u2192  COUNTER(on_demand_cluster_missing)                                                               \\\n    81\u2192  COUNTER(on_demand_cluster_timeout)                                                               \\\n    82\u2192  COUNTER(on_demand_cluster_success)\n    83\u2192\n    84\u2192/**\n    85\u2192 * Struct definition for all tcp proxy stats. @see stats_macros.h\n    86\u2192 */\n    87\u2192struct TcpProxyStats {\n    88\u2192  ALL_TCP_PROXY_STATS(GENERATE_COUNTER_STRUCT, GENERATE_GAUGE_STRUCT)\n    89\u2192};\n    90\u2192\n    91\u2192/**\n    92\u2192 * Struct definition for on-demand related tcp proxy stats. @see stats_macros.h\n    93\u2192 * These stats are available if and only if the tcp proxy enables on-demand.\n    94\u2192 * Note that these stats has the same prefix as `TcpProxyStats`.\n    95\u2192 */\n    96\u2192struct OnDemandStats {\n    97\u2192  ON_DEMAND_TCP_PROXY_STATS(GENERATE_COUNTER_STRUCT)\n    98\u2192};\n    99\u2192\n   100\u2192class Drainer;"

126. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/source/common/http/conn_manager_impl.h",
  "limit": 80,
  "offset": 55
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "/**\n * Implementation of both ConnectionManager and ServerConnectionCallbacks. This is a\n * Network::Filter that can be installed on a connection that will perform HTTP protocol agnostic\n * handling of a connection and all requests/pushes that occur on a connection.\n */\nclass ConnectionManagerImpl : Logger::Loggable<Logger::Id::http>,\n                              public Network::ReadFilter,\n                              public ServerConnectionCallbacks,\n                              public Network::ConnectionCallbacks,\n                              public Http::ApiListener {\npublic:\n  ConnectionManagerImpl(ConnectionManagerConfigSharedPtr config,\n                        const Network::DrainDecision& drain_close,\n                        Random::RandomGenerator& random_generator, Http::Context& http_context,\n                        Runtime::Loader& runtime, const LocalInfo::LocalInfo& local_info,\n                        Upstream::ClusterManager& cluster_manager,\n                        Server::OverloadManager& overload_manager, TimeSource& time_system,\n                        envoy::config::core::v3::TrafficDirection direction);\n  ~ConnectionManagerImpl() override;\n\n  static ConnectionManagerStats generateStats(const std::string& prefix, Stats::Scope& scope);\n  static ConnectionManagerTracingStats generateTracingStats(const std::string& prefix,\n                                                            Stats::Scope& scope);\n  static void chargeTracingStats(const Tracing::Reason& tracing_reason,\n                                 ConnectionManagerTracingStats& tracing_stats);\n  static ConnectionManagerListenerStats generateListenerStats(const std::string& prefix,\n                                                              Stats::Scope& scope);\n  static const ResponseHeaderMap& continueHeader();\n\n  // Currently the ConnectionManager creates a codec lazily when either:\n  //   a) onConnection for H3.\n  //   b) onData for H1 and H2.\n  // With the introduction of ApiListeners, neither event occurs. This function allows consumer code\n  // to manually create a codec.\n  // TODO(junr03): consider passing a synthetic codec instead of creating once. The codec in the\n  // ApiListener case is solely used to determine the protocol version.\n  void createCodec(Buffer::Instance& data);\n\n  // Network::ReadFilter\n  Network::FilterStatus onData(Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream) override;\n  Network::FilterStatus onNewConnection() override;\n  void initializeReadFilterCallbacks(Network::ReadFilterCallbacks& callbacks) override;\n\n  // Http::ConnectionCallbacks\n  void onGoAway(GoAwayErrorCode error_code) override;\n\n  // Http::ServerConnectionCallbacks\n  RequestDecoder& newStream(ResponseEncoder& response_encoder,\n                            bool is_internally_created = false) override;\n\n  RequestDecoderHandlePtr newStreamHandle(ResponseEncoder& response_encoder,\n                                          bool is_internally_created = false) override;\n\n  // Network::ConnectionCallbacks\n  void onEvent(Network::ConnectionEvent event) override;\n  // Pass connection watermark events on to all the streams associated with that connection.\n  void onAboveWriteBufferHighWatermark() override {\n    codec_->onUnderlyingConnectionAboveWriteBufferHighWatermark();\n  }\n  void onBelowWriteBufferLowWatermark() override {\n    codec_->onUnderlyingConnectionBelowWriteBufferLowWatermark();\n  }\n\n  TimeSource& timeSource() { return time_source_; }\n\n  void setClearHopByHopResponseHeaders(bool value) { clear_hop_by_hop_response_headers_ = value; }\n  bool clearHopByHopResponseHeaders() const { return clear_hop_by_hop_response_headers_; }\n\n  // This runtime key configures the number of streams which must be closed on a connection before\n  // envoy will potentially drain a connection due to excessive prematurely reset streams.\n  static const absl::string_view PrematureResetTotalStreamCountKey;\n\n  // The minimum lifetime of a stream, in seconds, in order not to be considered\n  // prematurely closed.\n  static const absl::string_view PrematureResetMinStreamLifetimeSecondsKey;\n  static const absl::string_view MaxRequestsPerIoCycle;\n  static const absl::string_view OptionallyDelayClose;\n\nprivate:\n  struct ActiveStream;",

  "file": {
    "content": "\nRequestDecoderHandlePtr ConnectionManagerImpl::newStreamHandle(ResponseEncoder& response_encoder,\n                                                               bool is_internally_created) {\n  RequestDecoder& decoder = newStream(response_encoder, is_internally_created);\n  return std::make_unique<ActiveStreamHandle>(static_cast<ActiveStream&>(decoder));\n}\n\nRequestDecoder& ConnectionManagerImpl::newStream(ResponseEncoder& response_encoder,\n                                                 bool is_internally_created) {\n  TRACE_EVENT(\"core\", \"ConnectionManagerImpl::newStream\");\n  if (connection_idle_timer_) {\n    connection_idle_timer_->disableTimer();\n  }\n\n  ENVOY_CONN_LOG(debug, \"new stream\", read_callbacks_->connection());\n\n  Buffer::BufferMemoryAccountSharedPtr downstream_stream_account =\n      response_encoder.getStream().account();\n\n  if (downstream_stream_account == nullptr) {\n    // Create account, wiring the stream to use it for tracking bytes.\n    // If tracking is disabled, the wiring becomes a NOP.\n    auto& buffer_factory = dispatcher_->getWatermarkFactory();\n    downstream_stream_account = buffer_factory.createAccount(response_encoder.getStream());\n    response_encoder.getStream().setAccount(downstream_stream_account);\n  }\n\n  auto new_stream = std::make_unique<ActiveStream>(\n      *this, response_encoder.getStream().bufferLimit(), std::move(downstream_stream_account));\n\n  accumulated_requests_++;\n  if (config_->maxRequestsPerConnection() > 0 &&\n      accumulated_requests_ >= config_->maxRequestsPerConnection()) {\n    if (codec_->protocol() < Protocol::Http2) {\n      new_stream->filter_manager_.streamInfo().setShouldDrainConnectionUponCompletion(true);\n      // Prevent erroneous debug log of closing due to incoming connection close header.\n      drain_state_ = DrainState::Closing;\n    } else if (drain_state_ == DrainState::NotDraining) {\n      startDrainSequence();\n    }\n    ENVOY_CONN_LOG(debug, \"max requests per connection reached\", read_callbacks_->connection());\n    stats_.named_.downstream_cx_max_requests_reached_.inc();\n  }\n\n  if (soft_drain_http1_) {\n    new_stream->filter_manager_.streamInfo().setShouldDrainConnectionUponCompletion(true);\n    // Prevent erroneous debug log of closing due to incoming connection close header.\n    drain_state_ = DrainState::Closing;\n  }\n\n  new_stream->state_.is_internally_created_ = is_internally_created;\n  new_stream->response_encoder_ = &response_encoder;\n  new_stream->response_encoder_->getStream().addCallbacks(*new_stream);\n  new_stream->response_encoder_->getStream().registerCodecEventCallbacks(new_stream.get());\n  new_stream->response_encoder_->getStream().setFlushTimeout(new_stream->idle_timeout_ms_);\n  new_stream->streamInfo().setDownstreamBytesMeter(response_encoder.getStream().bytesMeter());\n  // If the network connection is backed up, the stream should be made aware of it on creation.\n  // Both HTTP/1.x and HTTP/2 codecs handle this in StreamCallbackHelper::addCallbacksHelper.\n  ASSERT(read_callbacks_->connection().aboveHighWatermark() == false ||\n         new_stream->filter_manager_.aboveHighWatermark());",

    "numLines": 60,
    "startLine": 380,

    "content": "  handleCodecErrorImpl(error,\n                       absl::StrCat(\"overload_error:\", StringUtil::replaceAllEmptySpace(error)),\n                       StreamInfo::CoreResponseFlag::OverloadManager);\n}\n\nvoid ConnectionManagerImpl::createCodec(Buffer::Instance& data) {\n  ASSERT(!codec_);\n  codec_ = config_->createCodec(read_callbacks_->connection(), data, *this, overload_manager_);\n\n  switch (codec_->protocol()) {\n  case Protocol::Http3:\n    stats_.named_.downstream_cx_http3_total_.inc();\n    stats_.named_.downstream_cx_http3_active_.inc();\n    break;\n  case Protocol::Http2:\n    stats_.named_.downstream_cx_http2_total_.inc();\n    stats_.named_.downstream_cx_http2_active_.inc();\n    break;\n  case Protocol::Http11:\n  case Protocol::Http10:\n    stats_.named_.downstream_cx_http1_total_.inc();\n    stats_.named_.downstream_cx_http1_active_.inc();\n    break;\n  }\n}\n\nNetwork::FilterStatus ConnectionManagerImpl::onData(Buffer::Instance& data, bool) {\n  requests_during_dispatch_count_ = 0;\n  if (!codec_) {\n    // Close connections if Envoy is under pressure, typically memory, before creating codec.\n    if (hcm_ondata_creating_codec_ != nullptr && hcm_ondata_creating_codec_->shouldShedLoad()) {\n      stats_.named_.downstream_rq_overload_close_.inc();\n      handleCodecOverloadError(\"onData codec creation overload\");\n      return Network::FilterStatus::StopIteration;\n    }\n    // Http3 codec should have been instantiated by now.\n    createCodec(data);\n  }\n\n  bool redispatch;\n  do {\n    redispatch = false;\n\n    const Status status = codec_->dispatch(data);\n\n    if (isBufferFloodError(status) || isInboundFramesWithEmptyPayloadError(status)) {\n      handleCodecError(status.message());\n      return Network::FilterStatus::StopIteration;\n    } else if (isCodecProtocolError(status)) {\n      stats_.named_.downstream_cx_protocol_error_.inc();\n      handleCodecError(status.message());\n      return Network::FilterStatus::StopIteration;\n    } else if (isEnvoyOverloadError(status)) {\n      // The other codecs aren't wired to send this status.\n      ASSERT(codec_->protocol() < Protocol::Http2,\n             \"Expected only HTTP1.1 and below to send overload error.\");\n      stats_.named_.downstream_rq_overload_close_.inc();\n      handleCodecOverloadError(status.message());\n      return Network::FilterStatus::StopIteration;\n    }\n    ASSERT(status.ok());\n\n    // Processing incoming data may release outbound data so check for closure here as well.\n    checkForDeferredClose(false);\n",
    "filePath": "/workspace/source/common/http/conn_manager_impl.cc",
    "numLines": 65,

  "file": {
    "content": "//   http_filters:\n//     - A\n//     - B\n//     - C\n// The decoder filter chain will iterate through filters A, B, C.\nstruct StreamDecoderFilters {\n  using Element = ActiveStreamDecoderFilter;\n  using Iterator = std::vector<ActiveStreamDecoderFilterPtr>::iterator;\n\n  Iterator begin() { return entries_.begin(); }\n  Iterator end() { return entries_.end(); }\n\n  std::vector<ActiveStreamDecoderFilterPtr> entries_;\n};\n\n// HTTP encoder filters. If filters are configured in the following order (assume all three\n// filters are both decoder/encoder filters):\n//   http_filters:\n//     - A\n//     - B\n//     - C\n// Unlike the decoder filter, the encoder filter chain will iterate with the\n// reverse order of the configured filters, i.e., C, B, A. This is why we use reverse_iterator\n// here.\nstruct StreamEncoderFilters {\n  using Element = ActiveStreamEncoderFilter;\n  using Iterator = std::vector<ActiveStreamEncoderFilterPtr>::reverse_iterator;\n\n  Iterator begin() { return entries_.rbegin(); }\n  Iterator end() { return entries_.rend(); }\n\n  std::vector<ActiveStreamEncoderFilterPtr> entries_;\n};\n\n/**\n * Base class wrapper for both stream encoder and decoder filters.\n *\n * This class is responsible for performing matching and updating match data when a match tree is\n * configured for the associated filter. When not using a match tree, only minimal overhead (i.e.\n * memory overhead of unused fields) should apply.",
    "filePath": "/workspace/source/common/http/filter_manager.h",
    "numLines": 40,
    "startLine": 68,
    "totalLines": 1297
  },
  "type": "text"
}

  "limit": 50,

Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "\n}; // namespace\n\nconst Network::FilterChain*\nFilterChainManagerImpl::findFilterChain(const Network::ConnectionSocket& socket,\n                                        const StreamInfo::StreamInfo& info) const {\n  if (matcher_) {\n    return findFilterChainUsingMatcher(socket, info);\n  }\n\n  const auto& address = socket.connectionInfoProvider().localAddress();\n\n  const Network::FilterChain* best_match_filter_chain = nullptr;\n  // Match on destination port (only for IP addresses).\n  if (address->type() == Network::Address::Type::Ip) {\n    const auto port_match = destination_ports_map_.find(address->ip()->port());\n    if (port_match != destination_ports_map_.end()) {\n      best_match_filter_chain = findFilterChainForDestinationIP(*port_match->second.second, socket);\n      if (best_match_filter_chain != nullptr) {\n        return best_match_filter_chain;\n      } else {\n        // There is entry for specific port but none of the filter chain matches. Instead of\n        // matching catch-all port 0, the fallback filter chain is returned.\n        return default_filter_chain_.get();\n      }\n    }\n  }\n  // Match on catch-all port 0 if there is no specific port sub tree.\n  const auto port_match = destination_ports_map_.find(0);\n  if (port_match != destination_ports_map_.end()) {\n    best_match_filter_chain = findFilterChainForDestinationIP(*port_match->second.second, socket);\n  }\n  return best_match_filter_chain != nullptr\n             ? best_match_filter_chain\n             // Neither exact port nor catch-all port matches. Use fallback filter chain.\n             : default_filter_chain_.get();\n}\n\nconst Network::FilterChain*\nFilterChainManagerImpl::findFilterChainUsingMatcher(const Network::ConnectionSocket& socket,\n                                                    const StreamInfo::StreamInfo& info) const {\n  Network::Matching::MatchingDataImpl data(socket, info.filterState(), info.dynamicMetadata());\n  const auto& match_result = Matcher::evaluateMatch<Network::MatchingData>(*matcher_, data);\n  ASSERT(match_result.match_state_ == Matcher::MatchState::MatchComplete,\n         \"Matching must complete for network streams.\");\n  if (match_result.result_) {\n    const auto result = match_result.result_();\n    return result->getTyped<Configuration::FilterChainBaseAction>().get(filter_chains_by_name_,\n                                                                        info);\n  }",
    "filePath": "/workspace/source/common/listener_manager/filter_chain_manager_impl.cc",
    "numLines": 50,
    "startLine": 510,
    "totalLines": 824
  },
  "type": "text"
}

  "file": {
    "content": "  }\n}\n\nvoid ActiveStreamListenerBase::newConnection(Network::ConnectionSocketPtr&& socket,\n                                             std::unique_ptr<StreamInfo::StreamInfo> stream_info) {\n  // Find matching filter chain.\n  const auto filter_chain = config_->filterChainManager().findFilterChain(*socket, *stream_info);\n  if (filter_chain == nullptr) {\n    RELEASE_ASSERT(socket->connectionInfoProvider().remoteAddress() != nullptr, \"\");\n    ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"closing connection from {}: no matching filter chain found\",\n              socket->connectionInfoProvider().remoteAddress()->asString());\n    stats_.no_filter_chain_match_.inc();\n    stream_info->setResponseFlag(StreamInfo::CoreResponseFlag::NoRouteFound);\n    stream_info->setResponseCodeDetails(StreamInfo::ResponseCodeDetails::get().FilterChainNotFound);\n    emitLogs(*config_, *stream_info);\n    socket->close();\n    return;\n  }\n\n  socket->connectionInfoProvider().setListenerInfo(config_->listenerInfo());\n  socket->connectionInfoProvider().setFilterChainInfo(\n      std::make_shared<FilterChainInfoImpl>(filter_chain->name()));\n\n  auto transport_socket = filter_chain->transportSocketFactory().createDownstreamTransportSocket();\n  auto server_conn_ptr = dispatcher().createServerConnection(\n      std::move(socket), std::move(transport_socket), *stream_info);\n  if (const auto timeout = filter_chain->transportSocketConnectTimeout();\n      timeout != std::chrono::milliseconds::zero()) {\n    server_conn_ptr->setTransportSocketConnectTimeout(\n        timeout, stats_.downstream_cx_transport_socket_connect_timeout_);\n  }\n  server_conn_ptr->setBufferLimits(config_->perConnectionBufferLimitBytes());\n  RELEASE_ASSERT(server_conn_ptr->connectionInfoProvider().remoteAddress() != nullptr, \"\");\n  const bool empty_filter_chain = !config_->filterChainFactory().createNetworkFilterChain(\n      *server_conn_ptr, filter_chain->networkFilterFactories());\n  if (empty_filter_chain) {\n    ENVOY_CONN_LOG(debug, \"closing connection from {}: no filters\", *server_conn_ptr,\n                   server_conn_ptr->connectionInfoProvider().remoteAddress()->asString());\n    server_conn_ptr->close(Network::ConnectionCloseType::NoFlush, \"no_filters\");\n  }\n  newActiveConnection(*filter_chain, std::move(server_conn_ptr), std::move(stream_info));\n}\n\nActiveConnections::ActiveConnections(OwnedActiveStreamListenerBase& listener,\n                                     const Network::FilterChain& filter_chain)\n    : listener_(listener), filter_chain_(filter_chain) {}\n\nActiveConnections::~ActiveConnections() {\n  // connections should be defer deleted already.\n  ASSERT(connections_.empty());",
    "filePath": "/workspace/source/common/listener_manager/active_stream_listener_base.cc",

  "file": {
    "content": "   * @param action for how the connection will be closed.\n   */\n  virtual void closeConnection(ConnectionCloseAction action) PURE;\n};\n\n/**\n * This is a filter manager for TCP (L4) filters. It is split out for ease of testing.\n */\nclass FilterManagerImpl : protected Logger::Loggable<Logger::Id::connection> {\npublic:\n  FilterManagerImpl(FilterManagerConnection& connection, const ConnectionSocket& socket)\n      : connection_(connection), socket_(socket) {}\n\n  void addWriteFilter(WriteFilterSharedPtr filter);\n  void addFilter(FilterSharedPtr filter);\n  void addReadFilter(ReadFilterSharedPtr filter);\n  void removeReadFilter(ReadFilterSharedPtr filter);\n  bool initializeReadFilters();\n  void onRead();\n  FilterStatus onWrite();\n  bool startUpstreamSecureTransport();\n  void maybeClose();\n  void onConnectionClose(ConnectionCloseAction close_action);\n  bool pendingClose() { return state_.local_close_pending_ || state_.remote_close_pending_; }\n\nprotected:\n  struct State {\n    // Number of pending filters awaiting closure.\n    uint32_t filter_pending_close_count_{0};\n\n    // True if a RemoteClose is currently pending.\n    bool remote_close_pending_{false};\n\n    // True if a LocalClose is currently pending.\n    bool local_close_pending_{false};\n  };\n\nprivate:\n  struct ActiveReadFilter : public ReadFilterCallbacks, LinkedObject<ActiveReadFilter> {\n    ActiveReadFilter(FilterManagerImpl& parent, ReadFilterSharedPtr filter)\n        : parent_(parent), filter_(filter) {}\n\n    Connection& connection() override { return parent_.connection_; }\n    const ConnectionSocket& socket() override { return parent_.socket_; }\n    void continueReading() override { parent_.onContinueReading(this, parent_.connection_); }\n    void injectReadDataToFilterChain(Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream) override {\n      FixedReadBufferSource buffer_source{data, end_stream};\n      parent_.onContinueReading(this, buffer_source);\n    }\n\n    void disableClose(bool disable) override;\n\n    Upstream::HostDescriptionConstSharedPtr upstreamHost() override {\n      return parent_.host_description_;\n    }\n    void upstreamHost(Upstream::HostDescriptionConstSharedPtr host) override {\n      parent_.host_description_ = host;\n    }\n    bool startUpstreamSecureTransport() override { return parent_.startUpstreamSecureTransport(); }\n",
    "filePath": "/workspace/source/common/network/filter_manager_impl.h",
    "numLines": 60,
    "startLine": 100,
    "totalLines": 204

Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "class RouteConfigProvider;\n}\nnamespace Http {\n\n/**\n * Return codes for encode/decode headers filter invocations. The connection manager bases further\n * filter invocations on the return code of the previous filter.\n */\nenum class FilterHeadersStatus {\n  // Continue filter chain iteration.\n  Continue,\n  // Do not iterate for headers on any of the remaining filters in the chain.\n  //\n  // Returning FilterDataStatus::Continue from decodeData()/encodeData() or calling\n  // continueDecoding()/continueEncoding() MUST be called if continued filter iteration is desired.\n  //\n  // Note that if a local reply was sent, no further iteration for headers as well as data and\n  // trailers for the current filter and the filters following will happen. A local reply can be\n  // triggered via sendLocalReply() or encodeHeaders().\n  StopIteration,\n  // Continue headers iteration to remaining filters, but delay ending the stream. This status MUST\n  // NOT be returned when end_stream is already set to false.\n  //\n  // Used when a filter wants to add a body to a headers-only request/response, but this body is not\n  // readily available. Delaying end_stream allows the filter to add the body once it's available\n  // without stopping headers iteration.\n  //\n  // The filter is responsible to continue the stream by providing a body through calling\n  // injectDecodedDataToFilterChain()/injectEncodedDataToFilterChain(), possibly multiple times\n  // if the body needs to be divided into several chunks. The filter may need to handle\n  // watermark events when injecting a body, see:\n  // https://github.com/envoyproxy/envoy/blob/main/source/docs/flow_control.md.\n  //\n  // The last call to inject data MUST have end_stream set to true to conclude the stream.\n  // If the filter cannot provide a body the stream should be reset.\n  //\n  // Adding a body through calling addDecodedData()/addEncodedData() then\n  // continueDecoding()/continueEncoding() is currently NOT supported and causes an assert failure.\n  //\n  // Adding trailers in this scenario is currently NOT supported.\n  //\n  // The filter MUST NOT attempt to continue the stream without providing a body using\n  // continueDecoding()/continueEncoding().\n  //\n  // TODO(yosrym93): Support adding a body in this case by calling addDecodedData()/addEncodedData()\n  // then continueDecoding()/continueEncoding(). To support this a new FilterManager::IterationState\n  // needs to be added and set when a filter returns this status in\n  // FilterManager::decodeHeaders/FilterManager::encodeHeaders()\n  // Currently, when a filter returns this, the IterationState is Continue. This causes ASSERTs in\n  // FilterManager::commonContinue() to fail when continueDecoding()/continueEncoding() is called;\n  // due to trying to continue iteration when the IterationState is already Continue.\n  // In this case, a different ASSERT will be needed to make sure the filter does not try to\n  // continue without adding a body first.\n  //\n  // TODO(yosrym93): Support adding trailers in this case by implementing new functions to inject\n  // trailers, similar to the inject data functions.\n  ContinueAndDontEndStream,\n  // Do not iterate for headers as well as data and trailers for the current filter and the filters\n  // following, and buffer body data for later dispatching. ContinueDecoding() MUST\n  // be called if continued filter iteration is desired.\n  //\n  // Used when a filter wants to stop iteration on data and trailers while waiting for headers'\n  // iteration to resume.\n  //\n  // If buffering the request causes buffered data to exceed the configured buffer limit, a 413 will\n  // be sent to the user. On the response path exceeding buffer limits will result in a 500.\n  //\n  // TODO(soya3129): stop metadata parsing when StopAllIterationAndBuffer is set.\n  StopAllIterationAndBuffer,\n  // Do not iterate for headers as well as data and trailers for the current filter and the filters\n  // following, and buffer body data for later dispatching. continueDecoding() MUST\n  // be called if continued filter iteration is desired.\n  //\n  // Used when a filter wants to stop iteration on data and trailers while waiting for headers'\n  // iteration to resume.\n  //\n  // This will cause the flow of incoming data to cease until continueDecoding() function is called.\n  //\n  // TODO(soya3129): stop metadata parsing when StopAllIterationAndWatermark is set.\n  StopAllIterationAndWatermark,\n};\n\n/**\n * Return codes for encode on informative headers filter invocations.\n */\nenum class Filter1xxHeadersStatus {\n  // Continue filter chain iteration.\n  Continue,\n  // Do not iterate for informative headers on any of the remaining filters in the chain.\n  //\n  // Returning FilterDataStatus::Continue from encodeData() or calling\n  // continueEncoding() MUST be called if continued filter iteration is desired.\n  StopIteration\n};\n\n/**\n * Return codes for encode/decode data filter invocations. The connection manager bases further\n * filter invocations on the return code of the previous filter.\n */\nenum class FilterDataStatus {\n  // Continue filter chain iteration. If headers have not yet been sent to the next filter, they\n  // will be sent first via decodeHeaders()/encodeHeaders(). If data has previously been buffered,\n  // the data in this callback will be added to the buffer before the entirety is sent to the next\n  // filter.\n  Continue,\n  // Do not iterate to any of the remaining filters in the chain, and buffer body data for later\n  // dispatching. Returning FilterDataStatus::Continue from decodeData()/encodeData() or calling\n  // continueDecoding()/continueEncoding() MUST be called if continued filter iteration is desired.\n  //\n  // This should be called by filters which must parse a larger block of the incoming data before\n  // continuing processing and so can not push back on streaming data via watermarks.\n  //\n  // If buffering the request causes buffered data to exceed the configured buffer limit, a 413 will\n  // be sent to the user. On the response path exceeding buffer limits will result in a 500.\n  StopIterationAndBuffer,\n  // Do not iterate to any of the remaining filters in the chain, and buffer body data for later\n  // dispatching. Returning FilterDataStatus::Continue from decodeData()/encodeData() or calling\n  // continueDecoding()/continueEncoding() MUST be called if continued filter iteration is desired.\n  //\n  // This will cause the flow of incoming data to cease until one of the continue.*() functions is",
    "filePath": "/workspace/envoy/http/filter.h",
    "numLines": 120,
    "startLine": 30,
    "totalLines": 1261
  },
  "type": "text"
}

  "file_path": "/workspace/envoy/http/filter.h",
  "limit": 30,

Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "  //\n  // This will cause the flow of incoming data to cease until one of the continue.*() functions is\n  // called.\n  //\n  // This should be returned by filters which can nominally stream data but have a transient back-up\n  // such as the configured delay of the fault filter, or if the router filter is still fetching an\n  // upstream connection.\n  StopIterationAndWatermark,\n  // Do not iterate to any of the remaining filters in the chain, but do not buffer any of the\n  // body data for later dispatching. Returning FilterDataStatus::Continue from\n  // decodeData()/encodeData() or calling continueDecoding()/continueEncoding() MUST be called if\n  // continued filter iteration is desired.\n  //\n  // Note that if a local reply was sent, no further iteration for either data or trailers\n  // for the current filter and the filters following will happen. A local reply can be\n  // triggered via sendLocalReply() or encodeHeaders().\n  StopIterationNoBuffer\n};\n\n/**\n * Return codes for encode/decode trailers filter invocations. The connection manager bases further\n * filter invocations on the return code of the previous filter.\n */\nenum class FilterTrailersStatus {\n  // Continue filter chain iteration.\n  Continue,\n  // Do not iterate to any of the remaining filters in the chain. Calling\n  // continueDecoding()/continueEncoding() MUST be called if continued filter iteration is desired.\n  StopIteration\n};",
    "filePath": "/workspace/envoy/http/filter.h",
    "numLines": 30,
    "startLine": 148,
    "totalLines": 1261

135. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/source/common/router/router.cc",
  "limit": 40,
  "offset": 445
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "Http::FilterHeadersStatus Filter::decodeHeaders(Http::RequestHeaderMap& headers, bool end_stream) {\n  downstream_headers_ = &headers;\n\n  // Extract debug configuration from filter state. This is used further along to determine whether\n  // we should append cluster and host headers to the response, and whether to forward the request\n  // upstream.\n  const StreamInfo::FilterStateSharedPtr& filter_state = callbacks_->streamInfo().filterState();\n  const DebugConfig* debug_config = filter_state->getDataReadOnly<DebugConfig>(DebugConfig::key());\n\n  // TODO: Maybe add a filter API for this.\n  grpc_request_ = Grpc::Common::isGrpcRequestHeaders(headers);\n  exclude_http_code_stats_ = grpc_request_ && config_->suppress_grpc_request_failure_code_stats_;\n\n  // Only increment rq total stat if we actually decode headers here. This does not count requests\n  // that get handled by earlier filters.\n  stats_.rq_total_.inc();\n\n  // Initialize the `modify_headers` function as a no-op (so we don't have to remember to check it\n  // against nullptr before calling it), and feed it behavior later if/when we have cluster info\n  // headers to append.\n  std::function<void(Http::ResponseHeaderMap&)> modify_headers = [](Http::ResponseHeaderMap&) {};\n\n  // Determine if there is a route entry or a direct response for the request.\n  route_ = callbacks_->route();\n  if (!route_) {\n    stats_.no_route_.inc();\n    ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(debug, \"no route match for URL '{}'\", *callbacks_, headers.getPathValue());\n\n    callbacks_->streamInfo().setResponseFlag(StreamInfo::CoreResponseFlag::NoRouteFound);\n    callbacks_->sendLocalReply(Http::Code::NotFound, \"\", modify_headers, absl::nullopt,\n                               StreamInfo::ResponseCodeDetails::get().RouteNotFound);\n    return Http::FilterHeadersStatus::StopIteration;\n  }\n\n  // Determine if there is a direct response for the request.\n  const auto* direct_response = route_->directResponseEntry();\n  if (direct_response != nullptr) {\n    stats_.rq_direct_response_.inc();\n    direct_response->rewritePathHeader(headers, !config_->suppress_envoy_headers_);\n    callbacks_->sendLocalReply(",
    "filePath": "/workspace/source/common/router/router.cc",

the request.\n  route_entry_ = route_->routeEntry();\n  // If there's a route specific limit and it's smaller than general downstream\n  // limits, apply the new cap.\n  retry_shadow_buffer_limit_ =\n      std::min(retry_shadow_buffer_limit_, route_entry_->retryShadowBufferLimit());\n  if (debug_config && debug_config->append_cluster_) {\n    // The cluster name will be appended to any local or upstream responses from this point.\n    modify_headers = [this, debug_config](Http::ResponseHeaderMap& headers) {\n route_entry_->clusterName());\n\n    callbacks_->streamInfo().setResponseFlag(StreamInfo::CoreResponseFlag::NoClusterFound);\n headers.getPathValue());\n\n  if (config_->strict_check_headers_ != nullptr) {\n    for (const auto& header : *config_->strict_check_headers_) {\n      const auto res = Upstream::ThreadLocalCluster* cluster, Http::RequestHeaderMap& headers, bool end_stream,\n    std::function<void(Http::ResponseHeaderMap&)> modify_headers, bool* should_continue_decoding,\n    Upstream::HostConstSharedPtr&& selected_host,\n    absl::optional<std::string> host_selection_details) {\n  const StreamInfo::FilterStateSharedPtr& filter_state = callbacks_->streamInfo().filterState();\n  const DebugConfig* debug_config = filter_state->getDataReadOnly<DebugConfig>(DebugConfig::key());\n  callbacks_->streamInfo().downstreamTiming().setValue(\n      \"envoy.router.host_selection_end_ms\", callbacks_->dispatcher().timeSource().monotonicTime());\n\n  std::unique_ptr<GenericConnPool> generic_conn_pool = createConnPool(*cluster, selected_host);\n  if (!generic_conn_pool) {\n    sendNoHealthyUpstreamResponse(host_selection_details);\n    return Http::FilterHeadersStatus::StopIteration;\n  }\n  Upstream::HostDescriptionConstSharedPtr host = generic_conn_pool->host();\n\n  if (debug_config && debug_config->append_upstream_host_) {\n    // The hostname and address will be appended to any local or upstream responses from this point,\n    // possibly in addition to the cluster name.\n    modify_headers = [modify_headers, debug_config, host](Http::ResponseHeaderMap& headers) {\n      modify_headers(headers);\n      headers.addCopy(\n             Http::Headers::get().EnvoyUpstreamHostAddress),\n                      host->address()->asString());\n    };\n  }\n\n  // If we've been instructed not to forward the request upstream, send an empty local response.\n  if (debug_config && debug_config->do_not_forward_) {\n    modify_headers = [modify_headers, debug_config](Http::ResponseHeaderMap& headers) {\n      modify_headers(headers);\n      headers.addCopy(\n          debug_config->not_forwarded_header_.value_or(Http::Headers::get().EnvoyNotForwarded),\n          \"true\");\n    };\n    callbacks_->sendLocalReply(Http::Code::NoContent, \"\", modify_headers, absl::nullopt, \"\");\n    return Http::FilterHeadersStatus::StopIteration;\n  }\n\n  if (callbacks_->shouldLoadShed()) {\n    callbacks_->sendLocalReply(Http::Code::ServiceUnavailable, \"envoy overloaded\", nullptr,\n                               absl::nullopt, StreamInfo::ResponseCodeDetails::get().Overload);",

    "content": "  ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(debug, \"router decoding headers:\\n{}\", *callbacks_, headers);\n\n  // Hang onto the modify_headers function for later use in handling upstream responses.\n  modify_headers_ = modify_headers;\n\n  const bool can_send_early_data =\n      route_entry_->earlyDataPolicy().allowsEarlyDataForRequest(*downstream_headers_);\n\n  include_timeout_retry_header_in_request_ = route_->virtualHost().includeIsTimeoutRetryHeader();\n\n  // Set initial HTTP/3 use based on the presence of HTTP/1.1 proxy config.\n  // For retries etc, HTTP/3 usability may transition from true to false, but\n  // will never transition from false to true.\n  bool can_use_http3 =\n      !transport_socket_options_ || !transport_socket_options_->http11ProxyInfo().has_value();\n  UpstreamRequestPtr upstream_request = std::make_unique<UpstreamRequest>(\n      *this, std::move(generic_conn_pool), can_send_early_data, can_use_http3,\n      allow_multiplexed_upstream_half_close_ /*enable_half_close*/);\n  LinkedList::moveIntoList(std::move(upstream_request), upstream_requests_);\n  upstream_requests_.front()->acceptHeadersFromRouter(end_stream);\n  if (streaming_shadows_) {\n    // start the shadow streams.\n    for (const auto& shadow_policy_wrapper : active_shadow_policies_) {\n      const auto& shadow_policy = shadow_policy_wrapper.get();\n      const absl::optional<absl::string_view> shadow_cluster_name =\n          getShadowCluster(shadow_policy, *downstream_headers_);\n      if (!shadow_cluster_name.has_value()) {\n        continue;\n      }\n      auto shadow_headers = Http::createHeaderMap<Http::RequestHeaderMapImpl>(*shadow_headers_);\n      const auto options =\n          Http::AsyncClient::RequestOptions()\n              .setTimeout(timeout_.global_timeout_)\n              .setParentSpan(callbacks_->activeSpan())\n              .setChildSpanName(\"mirror\")\n              .setSampled(shadow_policy.traceSampled())\n              .setIsShadow(true)\n              .setIsShadowSuffixDisabled(shadow_policy.disableShadowHostSuffixAppend())\n              .setBufferAccount(callbacks_->account())\n              // A buffer limit of 1 is set in the case that retry_shadow_buffer_limit_ == 0,\n              // because a buffer limit of zero on async clients is interpreted as no buffer limit.\n              .setBufferLimit(1 > retry_shadow_buffer_limit_ ? 1 : retry_shadow_buffer_limit_)\n              .setDiscardResponseBody(true)\n              .setFilterConfig(config_)\n              .setParentContext(Http::AsyncClient::ParentContext{&callbacks_->streamInfo()});\n      if (end_stream) {\n        // This is a header-only request, and can be dispatched immediately to the shadow\n        // without waiting.\n        Http::RequestMessagePtr request(new Http::RequestMessageImpl(\n            Http::createHeaderMap<Http::RequestHeaderMapImpl>(*shadow_headers_)));\n        config_->shadowWriter().shadow(std::string(shadow_cluster_name.value()), std::move(request),\n                                       options);\n      } else {\n        Http::AsyncClient::OngoingRequest* shadow_stream = config_->shadowWriter().streamingShadow(\n            std::string(shadow_cluster_name.value()), std::move(shadow_headers), options);\n        if (shadow_stream != nullptr) {\n          shadow_streams_.insert(shadow_stream);\n          shadow_stream->setDestructorCallback(\n              [this, shadow_stream]() { shadow_streams_.erase(shadow_stream); });\n          shadow_stream->setWatermarkCallbacks(watermark_callbacks_);",

  "file": {
    "content": "}\n\nvoid Filter::onUpstreamHeaders(uint64_t response_code, Http::ResponseHeaderMapPtr&& headers,\n                               UpstreamRequest& upstream_request, bool end_stream) {\n  ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(debug, \"upstream headers complete: end_stream={}\", *callbacks_, end_stream);\n\n  ASSERT(!host_selection_cancelable_);\n\n  modify_headers_(*headers);\n  // When grpc-status appears in response headers, convert grpc-status to HTTP status code\n  // for outlier detection. This does not currently change any stats or logging and does not\n  // handle the case when an error grpc-status is sent as a trailer.\n  absl::optional<Grpc::Status::GrpcStatus> grpc_status;\n  uint64_t grpc_to_http_status = 0;\n  if (grpc_request_) {\n    grpc_status = Grpc::Common::getGrpcStatus(*headers);\n    if (grpc_status.has_value()) {\n      grpc_to_http_status = Grpc::Utility::grpcToHttpStatus(grpc_status.value());\n    }\n  }\n\n  maybeProcessOrcaLoadReport(*headers, upstream_request);\n\n  if (grpc_status.has_value()) {\n    upstream_request.upstreamHost()->outlierDetector().putHttpResponseCode(grpc_to_http_status);\n  } else {\n    upstream_request.upstreamHost()->outlierDetector().putHttpResponseCode(response_code);\n  }\n\n  if (headers->EnvoyImmediateHealthCheckFail() != nullptr) {\n    upstream_request.upstreamHost()->healthChecker().setUnhealthy(\n        Upstream::HealthCheckHostMonitor::UnhealthyType::ImmediateHealthCheckFail);\n  }\n\n  bool could_not_retry = false;\n\n  // Check if this upstream request was already retried, for instance after\n  // hitting a per try timeout. Don't retry it if we already have.\n  if (retry_state_) {\n    if (upstream_request.retried()) {\n      // We already retried this request (presumably for a per try timeout) so\n      // we definitely won't retry it again. Check if we would have retried it\n      // if we could.\n      bool retry_as_early_data; // Not going to be used as we are not retrying.\n      could_not_retry = retry_state_->wouldRetryFromHeaders(*headers, *downstream_headers_,\n                                                            retry_as_early_data) !=\n                        RetryState::RetryDecision::NoRetry;\n    } else {\n      const RetryStatus retry_status = retry_state_->shouldRetryHeaders(\n          *headers, *downstream_headers_,",
    "filePath": "/workspace/source/common/router/router.cc",
    "numLines": 50,
    "startLine": 1650,
    "totalLines": 2351

  "file": {
    "content": "  // TODO(zuercher): If access to response_headers_to_add (at any level) is ever needed outside\n  // Router::Filter we'll need to find a better location for this work. One possibility is to\n  // provide finalizeResponseHeaders functions on the Router::Config and VirtualHost interfaces.\n  route_entry_->finalizeResponseHeaders(*headers, callbacks_->streamInfo());\n\n  downstream_response_started_ = true;\n  final_upstream_request_ = &upstream_request;\n  // Make sure that for request hedging, we end up with the correct final upstream info.\n  callbacks_->streamInfo().setUpstreamInfo(final_upstream_request_->streamInfo().upstreamInfo());\n  resetOtherUpstreams(upstream_request);\n  if (end_stream) {\n    onUpstreamComplete(upstream_request);\n  }\n\n  callbacks_->encodeHeaders(std::move(headers), end_stream,\n                            StreamInfo::ResponseCodeDetails::get().ViaUpstream);\n}\n\nvoid Filter::onUpstreamData(Buffer::Instance& data, UpstreamRequest& upstream_request,\n                            bool end_stream) {\n  // This should be true because when we saw headers we either reset the stream\n  // (hence wouldn't have made it to onUpstreamData) or all other in-flight\n  // streams.\n  ASSERT(upstream_requests_.size() == 1);\n  if (end_stream) {\n    // gRPC request termination without trailers is an error.\n    if (upstream_request.grpcRqSuccessDeferred()) {\n      upstream_request.upstreamHost()->stats().rq_error_.inc();\n    }\n    onUpstreamComplete(upstream_request);",
    "filePath": "/workspace/source/common/router/router.cc",

  "file": {
    "content": "}\n\nvoid UpstreamRequest::onUpstreamHostSelected(Upstream::HostDescriptionConstSharedPtr host,\n                                             bool pool_success) {\n  StreamInfo::UpstreamInfo& upstream_info = *streamInfo().upstreamInfo();\n  upstream_info.setUpstreamHost(host);\n  upstream_host_ = host;\n  parent_.onUpstreamHostSelected(host, pool_success);\n}\n\nvoid UpstreamRequest::acceptHeadersFromRouter(bool end_stream) {\n  ASSERT(!router_sent_end_stream_);\n  router_sent_end_stream_ = end_stream;\n\n  // Make sure that when we are forwarding CONNECT payload we do not do so until\n  // the upstream has accepted the CONNECT request.\n  // This must be done before conn_pool->newStream, as onPoolReady un-pauses for CONNECT\n  // termination.\n  auto* headers = parent_.downstreamHeaders();\n  if (headers->getMethodValue() == Http::Headers::get().MethodValues.Connect) {\n    paused_for_connect_ = true;\n    // If this is a websocket upgrade request, pause the request until the upstream sends\n    // the 101 Switching Protocols response code. Using the else logic here to obey CONNECT\n    // method which is expecting 2xx response.\n  } else if ((Runtime::runtimeFeatureEnabled(\n                 \"envoy.reloadable_features.check_switch_protocol_websocket_handshake\")) &&\n             Http::Utility::isWebSocketUpgradeRequest(*headers)) {\n    paused_for_websocket_ = true;\n  }\n\n  // Kick off creation of the upstream connection immediately upon receiving headers.\n  // In future it may be possible for upstream HTTP filters to delay this, or influence connection\n  // creation but for now optimize for minimal latency and fetch the connection\n  // as soon as possible.\n  conn_pool_->newStream(this);\n\n  if (parent_.config().upstream_log_flush_interval_.has_value()) {\n    upstream_log_flush_timer_ = parent_.callbacks()->dispatcher().createTimer([this]() -> void {\n      // If the request is complete, we've already done the stream-end upstream log, and shouldn't\n      // do the periodic log.\n      if (!streamInfo().requestComplete().has_value()) {\n        upstreamLog(AccessLog::AccessLogType::UpstreamPeriodic);\n        resetUpstreamLogFlushTimer();\n      }\n      // Both downstream and upstream bytes meters may not be initialized when\n      // the timer goes off, e.g. if it takes longer than the interval for a\n      // connection to be initialized; check for nullptr.\n      auto& downstream_bytes_meter = stream_info_.getDownstreamBytesMeter();\n      auto& upstream_bytes_meter = stream_info_.getUpstreamBytesMeter();\n      const SystemTime now = parent_.callbacks()->dispatcher().timeSource().systemTime();",
    "filePath": "/workspace/source/common/router/upstream_request.cc",
    "numLines": 50,
    "startLine": 370,
    "totalLines": 825
  },
  "type": "text"
}

Output

  "file": {
    "content": "  addResponseHeadersStat(headers->byteSize(), headers->size());\n  maybeHandleDeferredReadDisable();\n  parent_.onUpstream1xxHeaders(std::move(headers), *this);\n}\n\n// This is called by the FilterManager when all filters have processed headers. Forward them\n// on to the router.\nvoid UpstreamRequest::decodeHeaders(Http::ResponseHeaderMapPtr&& headers, bool end_stream) {\n  ASSERT(headers.get());\n  ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"end_stream: {}, upstream response headers:\\n{}\", *parent_.callbacks(),\n                   end_stream, *headers);\n  ScopeTrackerScopeState scope(&parent_.callbacks()->scope(), parent_.callbacks()->dispatcher());\n\n  resetPerTryIdleTimer();\n\n  addResponseHeadersStat(headers->byteSize(), headers->size());\n\n  // We drop unsupported 1xx on the floor here. 101 upgrade headers need to be passed to the client\n  // as part of the final response. Most 1xx headers are handled in onUpstream1xxHeaders.\n  //\n  // We could in principle handle other headers here, but this might result in the double invocation\n  // of decodeHeaders() (once for informational, again for non-informational), which is likely an\n  // easy to miss corner case in the filter and HCM contract.\n  //\n  // This filtering is done early in upstream request, unlike 100 coalescing which is performed in\n  // the router filter, since the filtering only depends on the state of a single upstream, and we\n  // don't want to confuse accounting such as onFirstUpstreamRxByteReceived() with informational\n  // headers.\n  const uint64_t response_code = Http::Utility::getResponseStatus(*headers);\n  if (Http::CodeUtility::is1xx(response_code) &&\n      response_code != enumToInt(Http::Code::SwitchingProtocols)) {\n    return;\n  }\n\n  awaiting_headers_ = false;\n  if (span_ != nullptr) {\n    Tracing::HttpTracerUtility::onUpstreamResponseHeaders(*span_, headers.get());\n  }\n  if (!parent_.config().upstream_logs_.empty()) {\n    upstream_headers_ = Http::createHeaderMap<Http::ResponseHeaderMapImpl>(*headers);\n  }\n  stream_info_.setResponseCode(static_cast<uint32_t>(response_code));\n\n  maybeHandleDeferredReadDisable();\n  ASSERT(headers.get());\n\n  parent_.onUpstreamHeaders(response_code, std::move(headers), *this, end_stream);\n}\n\nvoid UpstreamRequest::maybeHandleDeferredReadDisable() {",
    "filePath": "/workspace/source/common/router/upstream_request.cc",
    "numLines": 50,
    "startLine": 260,
    "totalLines": 825
  },
  "type": "text"
}

  "file_path": "/workspace/source/common/router/upstream_codec_filter.h",

Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "#pragma once\n\n#include <chrono>\n#include <cstdint>\n#include <functional>\n#include <memory>\n#include <string>\n\n#include \"envoy/extensions/filters/http/upstream_codec/v3/upstream_codec.pb.h\"\n#include \"envoy/extensions/filters/http/upstream_codec/v3/upstream_codec.pb.validate.h\"\n#include \"envoy/http/conn_pool.h\"\n#include \"envoy/http/filter.h\"\n\n#include \"source/common/common/logger.h\"\n#include \"source/common/config/well_known_names.h\"\n#include \"source/common/runtime/runtime_features.h\"\n#include \"source/extensions/filters/http/common/factory_base.h\"\n\n#include \"absl/status/status.h\"\n\nnamespace Envoy {\nnamespace Router {\n\n// This is the last filter in the upstream HTTP filter chain.\n// It takes request headers/body/data from the filter manager and encodes them to the upstream\n// codec. It also registers the CodecBridge with the upstream stream, and takes response\n// headers/body/data from the upstream stream and sends them to the filter manager.\nclass UpstreamCodecFilter : public Http::StreamDecoderFilter,\n                            public Logger::Loggable<Logger::Id::router>,\n                            public Http::DownstreamWatermarkCallbacks,\n                            public Http::UpstreamCallbacks {\npublic:\n  UpstreamCodecFilter() : bridge_(*this), deferred_reset_status_(absl::OkStatus()) {}\n\n  // Http::DownstreamWatermarkCallbacks\n  void onBelowWriteBufferLowWatermark() override;\n  void onAboveWriteBufferHighWatermark() override;\n\n  // UpstreamCallbacks\n  void onUpstreamConnectionEstablished() override;\n\n  // Http::StreamFilterBase\n  void onDestroy() override { callbacks_->removeDownstreamWatermarkCallbacks(*this); }\n\n  // Http::StreamDecoderFilter\n  Http::FilterHeadersStatus decodeHeaders(Http::RequestHeaderMap& headers,\n                                          bool end_stream) override;\n  Http::FilterDataStatus decodeData(Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream) override;\n  Http::FilterTrailersStatus decodeTrailers(Http::RequestTrailerMap& trailers) override;\n  Http::FilterMetadataStatus decodeMetadata(Http::MetadataMap& metadata_map) override;\n  void setDecoderFilterCallbacks(Http::StreamDecoderFilterCallbacks& callbacks) override;\n\n  // This bridge connects the upstream stream to the filter manager.\n  class CodecBridge : public UpstreamToDownstream {\n  public:\n    CodecBridge(UpstreamCodecFilter& filter) : filter_(filter) {}\n    void decode1xxHeaders(Http::ResponseHeaderMapPtr&& headers) override;\n    void decodeHeaders(Http::ResponseHeaderMapPtr&& headers, bool end_stream) override;\n    void decodeData(Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream) override;\n    void decodeTrailers(Http::ResponseTrailerMapPtr&& trailers) override;\n    void decodeMetadata(Http::MetadataMapPtr&&) override;\n    void dumpState(std::ostream& os, int indent_level) const override;\n    void onResetStream(Http::StreamResetReason reason,\n                       absl::string_view transport_failure_reason) override;\n\n    void onAboveWriteBufferHighWatermark() override {\n      filter_.callbacks_->onDecoderFilterAboveWriteBufferHighWatermark();\n    }\n    void onBelowWriteBufferLowWatermark() override {\n      filter_.callbacks_->onDecoderFilterBelowWriteBufferLowWatermark();\n    }\n    // UpstreamToDownstream\n    const Route& route() const override { return *filter_.callbacks_->route(); }\n    OptRef<const Network::Connection> connection() const override {\n      return filter_.callbacks_->connection();\n    }\n    const Http::ConnectionPool::Instance::StreamOptions& upstreamStreamOptions() const override {\n      return filter_.callbacks_->upstreamCallbacks()->upstreamStreamOptions();\n    }\n\n  private:\n    void maybeEndDecode(bool end_stream);\n    bool seen_1xx_headers_{};\n    UpstreamCodecFilter& filter_;\n  };\n  Http::StreamDecoderFilterCallbacks* callbacks_;\n  CodecBridge bridge_;\n  OptRef<Http::RequestHeaderMap> latched_headers_;\n  absl::Status deferred_reset_status_;\n  absl::optional<bool> latched_end_stream_;\n  // Keep small members (bools and enums) at the end of class, to reduce alignment overhead.\n  bool calling_encode_headers_ = false;\n\nprivate:\n  StreamInfo::UpstreamTiming& upstreamTiming() {\n    return callbacks_->upstreamCallbacks()->upstreamStreamInfo().upstreamInfo()->upstreamTiming();\n  }\n};\n\nclass UpstreamCodecFilterFactory",
    "filePath": "/workspace/source/common/router/upstream_codec_filter.h",
    "numLines": 100,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 128
  },
  "type": "text"
}

  "file": {
    "content": "                                                       upgrade_map, *this, options)\n             ? UpgradeResult::UpgradeAccepted\n             : UpgradeResult::UpgradeRejected;\n}\n\nFilterManager::CreateChainResult\nFilterManager::createFilterChain(const FilterChainFactory& filter_chain_factory) {\n  if (state_.create_chain_result_.created()) {\n    return state_.create_chain_result_;\n  }\n\n  // After the filter chain creation is completed, the filter chain containers will not be\n  // modified. So, we can safely complete the initialization of the filter chain now.\n  Cleanup initialize_filter_chain([this]() {\n    for (auto iter = decoder_filters_.begin(); iter != decoder_filters_.end(); ++iter) {\n      (*iter)->entry_ = iter;\n    }\n    for (auto iter = encoder_filters_.begin(); iter != encoder_filters_.end(); ++iter) {\n      (*iter)->entry_ = iter;\n    }\n  });\n\n  // TODO(wbpcode): reserve memory for filters to avoid frequent reallocation.\n\n  OptRef<DownstreamStreamFilterCallbacks> downstream_callbacks =\n      filter_manager_callbacks_.downstreamCallbacks();\n\n  // This filter chain options is only used for the downstream HTTP filter chains for now. So, try\n  // to set valid initial route only when the downstream callbacks is available.\n  FilterChainOptionsImpl options(downstream_callbacks.has_value() ? streamInfo().route() : nullptr);\n\n  UpgradeResult upgrade = UpgradeResult::UpgradeUnneeded;\n\n  // Only try the upgrade filter chain for downstream filter chains.\n  if (downstream_callbacks.has_value()) {\n    upgrade = createUpgradeFilterChain(filter_chain_factory, options);\n    if (upgrade == UpgradeResult::UpgradeAccepted) {\n      // Upgrade filter chain is created. Return the result directly.\n      state_.create_chain_result_ = CreateChainResult(true, upgrade);\n      return state_.create_chain_result_;\n    }\n    // If the upgrade is unnecessary or the upgrade filter chain is rejected, fall through to\n    // create the default filter chain.\n  }\n\n  state_.create_chain_result_ =\n      CreateChainResult(filter_chain_factory.createFilterChain(*this, options), upgrade);\n  return state_.create_chain_result_;\n}\n\nvoid ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::requestDataDrained() {\n  // If this is called it means the call to requestDataTooLarge() was a\n  // streaming call, or a 413 would have been sent.\n  onDecoderFilterBelowWriteBufferLowWatermark();\n}\n\nvoid ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::onDecoderFilterBelowWriteBufferLowWatermark() {\n  parent_.filter_manager_callbacks_.onDecoderFilterBelowWriteBufferLowWatermark();\n}\n",
    "filePath": "/workspace/source/common/http/filter_manager.cc",
    "numLines": 60,
    "startLine": 1655,

    "content": "void ActiveTcpListener::updateListenerConfig(Network::ListenerConfig& config) {\n  ENVOY_LOG(trace, \"replacing listener \", config_->listenerTag(), \" by \", config.listenerTag());\n  config_ = &config;\n}\n\nvoid ActiveTcpListener::onAccept(Network::ConnectionSocketPtr&& socket) {\n  if (listenerConnectionLimitReached()) {\n    RELEASE_ASSERT(socket->connectionInfoProvider().remoteAddress() != nullptr, \"\");\n    ENVOY_LOG(trace, \"closing connection from {}: listener connection limit reached for {}\",\n              socket->connectionInfoProvider().remoteAddress()->asString(), config_->name());\n    socket->close();\n    stats_.downstream_cx_overflow_.inc();\n    return;\n  }\n\n  onAcceptWorker(std::move(socket), config_->handOffRestoredDestinationConnections(), false);\n}\n\nvoid ActiveTcpListener::onReject(RejectCause cause) {\n  switch (cause) {\n  case RejectCause::GlobalCxLimit:\n    stats_.downstream_global_cx_overflow_.inc();\n    break;\n  case RejectCause::OverloadAction:\n    stats_.downstream_cx_overload_reject_.inc();\n    break;\n  }\n}\n\nvoid ActiveTcpListener::recordConnectionsAcceptedOnSocketEvent(uint32_t connections_accepted) {\n  stats_.connections_accepted_per_socket_event_.recordValue(connections_accepted);\n}\n\nvoid ActiveTcpListener::onAcceptWorker(Network::ConnectionSocketPtr&& socket,\n                                       bool hand_off_restored_destination_connections,\n                                       bool rebalanced) {\n  // Get Round Trip Time\n  absl::optional<std::chrono::milliseconds> t = socket->lastRoundTripTime();\n  if (t.has_value()) {\n    socket->connectionInfoProvider().setRoundTripTime(t.value());\n  }\n\n  if (!rebalanced) {\n    Network::BalancedConnectionHandler& target_handler =\n        connection_balancer_.pickTargetHandler(*this);\n    if (&target_handler != this) {\n      target_handler.post(std::move(socket));\n      return;\n    }\n  }\n\n  auto active_socket = std::make_unique<ActiveTcpSocket>(*this, std::move(socket),\n                                                         hand_off_restored_destination_connections);\n\n  onSocketAccepted(std::move(active_socket));\n}\n\nvoid ActiveTcpListener::pauseListening() {\n  if (listener_ != nullptr) {\n    listener_->disable();",

    "content": "}\n\nvoid FilterManager::decodeHeaders(ActiveStreamDecoderFilter* filter, RequestHeaderMap& headers,\n                                  bool end_stream) {\n  // Headers filter iteration should always start with the next filter if available.\n  StreamDecoderFilters::Iterator entry =\n      commonDecodePrefix(filter, FilterIterationStartState::AlwaysStartFromNext);\n  StreamDecoderFilters::Iterator continue_data_entry = decoder_filters_.end();\n  bool terminal_filter_decoded_end_stream = false;\n  ASSERT(!state_.decoder_filter_chain_complete_ || entry == decoder_filters_.end() ||\n         (*entry)->end_stream_);\n\n  for (; entry != decoder_filters_.end(); entry++) {\n    ENVOY_EXECUTION_SCOPE(trackedStream(), &(*entry)->filter_context_);\n    ASSERT(!(state_.filter_call_state_ & FilterCallState::DecodeHeaders));\n    state_.filter_call_state_ |= FilterCallState::DecodeHeaders;\n    (*entry)->end_stream_ = (end_stream && continue_data_entry == decoder_filters_.end());\n    if ((*entry)->end_stream_) {\n      state_.filter_call_state_ |= FilterCallState::EndOfStream;\n    }\n    FilterHeadersStatus status = (*entry)->decodeHeaders(headers, (*entry)->end_stream_);\n    state_.filter_call_state_ &= ~FilterCallState::DecodeHeaders;\n    if ((*entry)->end_stream_) {\n      state_.filter_call_state_ &= ~FilterCallState::EndOfStream;\n    }\n    if (state_.decoder_filter_chain_aborted_) {\n      executeLocalReplyIfPrepared();\n      ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace,\n                       \"decodeHeaders filter iteration aborted due to local reply: filter={}\",\n                       *this, (*entry)->filter_context_.config_name);\n      status = FilterHeadersStatus::StopIteration;\n    }\n\n    ASSERT(!(status == FilterHeadersStatus::ContinueAndDontEndStream && !(*entry)->end_stream_),\n           \"Filters should not return FilterHeadersStatus::ContinueAndDontEndStream from \"\n static_cast<uint64_t>(status));\n\n    (*entry)->processed_headers_ = true;\n\n    const auto continue_iteration = (*entry)->commonHandleAfterHeadersCallback(status, end_stream);\n
